They ignore\n   905\u2192\t// all ordinary claims to the device with respect to access modes and\n   906\u2192\t// any resource allocations.\n   907\u2192\t//\n   908\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRAAdminAccess\n   909\u2192\t// feature gate. Admin access is disabled if this field is unset or\n   910\u2192\t// set to false, otherwise it is enabled.\n   911\u2192\t//\n   912\u2192\t// +optional\n   913\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAAdminAccess\n   914\u2192\tAdminAccess *bool `json:\"adminAccess,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=adminAccess\"`\n   915\u2192\n   916\u2192\t// If specified, the request's tolerations.\n   917\u2192\t//\n   918\u2192\t// Tolerations for NoSchedule are required to allocate a\n   919\u2192\t// device which has a taint with that effect. The same applies\n   920\u2192\t// to NoExecute.\n   921\u2192\t//\n   922\u2192\t// In addition, should any of the allocated devices get tainted\n   923\u2192\t// with NoExecute after allocation and that effect is not tolerated,\n   924\u2192\t// then all pods consuming the ResourceClaim get deleted to evict\n   925\u2192\t// them. The scheduler will not let new pods reserve the claim while\n   926\u2192\t// it has these tainted devices. Once all pods are evicted, the\n   927\u2192\t// claim will get deallocated.\n   928\u2192\t//\n   929\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n   930\u2192\t//\n   931\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n   932\u2192\t// feature gate.\n   933\u2192\t//\n   934\u2192\t// +optional\n   935\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   936\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n   937\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n   938\u2192\n   939\u2192\t// Capacity define resource requirements against each capacity.\n   940\u2192\t//\n   941\u2192\t// If this field is unset and the device supports multiple allocations,\n   942\u2192\t// the default value will be applied to each capacity according to requestPolicy.\n   943\u2192\t// For the capacity that has no requestPolicy, default is the full capacity value.\n   944\u2192\t//\n   945\u2192\t// Applies to each device allocation.\n   946\u2192\t// If Count > 1,\n   947\u2192\t// the request fails if there aren't enough devices that meet the requirements.\n   948\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is set to All,\n   949\u2192\t// the request fails if there are devices that otherwise match the request,\n   950\u2192\t// and have this capacity, with a value >= the requested amount, but which cannot be allocated to this request.\n   951\u2192\t//\n   952\u2192\t// +optional\n   953\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n   954\u2192\tCapacity *CapacityRequirements `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=capacity\"`\n   955\u2192}\n   956\u2192\n   957\u2192// DeviceSubRequest describes a request for device provided in the\n   958\u2192// claim.spec.devices.requests[].firstAvailable array. Each\n   959\u2192// is typically a request for a single resource like a device, but can\n   960\u2192// also ask for several identical devices.\n   961\u2192//\n   962\u2192// DeviceSubRequest is similar to ExactDeviceRequest, but doesn't expose the\n   963\u2192// AdminAccess field as that one is only supported when requesting a\n   964\u2192// specific device.\n   965\u2192type DeviceSubRequest struct {\n   966\u2192\t// Name can be used to reference this subrequest in the list of constraints\n   967\u2192\t// or the list of configurations for the claim. References must use the\n   968\u2192\t// format <main request>/<subrequest>.\n   969\u2192\t//\n   970\u2192\t// Must be a DNS label.\n   971\u2192\t//\n   972\u2192\t// +required\n   973\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   974\u2192\n   975\u2192\t// DeviceClassName references a specific DeviceClass, which can define\n   976\u2192\t// additional configuration and selectors to be inherited by this\n   977\u2192\t// subrequest.\n   978\u2192\t//\n   979\u2192\t// A class is required. Which classes are available depends on the cluster.\n   980\u2192\t//\n   981\u2192\t// Administrators may use this to restrict which devices may get\n   982\u2192\t// requested by only installing classes with selectors for permitted\n   983\u2192\t// devices. If users are free to request anything without restrictions,\n   984\u2192\t// then administrators can create an empty DeviceClass for users\n   985\u2192\t// to reference.\n   986\u2192\t//\n   987\u2192\t// +required\n   988\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n   989\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n   990\u2192\tDeviceClassName string `json:\"deviceClassName\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceClassName\"`\n   991\u2192\n   992\u2192\t// Selectors define criteria which must be satisfied by a specific\n   993\u2192\t// device in order for that device to be considered for this\n   994\u2192\t// subrequest. All selectors must be satisfied for a device to be\n   995\u2192\t// considered.\n   996\u2192\t//\n   997\u2192\t// +optional\n   998\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   999\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1000\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=selectors\"`\n  1001\u2192\n  1002\u2192\t// AllocationMode and its related fields define how devices are allocated\n  1003\u2192\t// to satisfy this subrequest. Supported values are:\n  1004\u2192\t//\n  1005\u2192\t// - ExactCount: This request is for a specific number of devices.\n  1006\u2192\t//   This is the default. The exact number is provided in the\n  1007\u2192\t//   count field.\n  1008\u2192\t//\n  1009\u2192\t// - All: This subrequest is for all of the matching devices in a pool.\n  1010\u2192\t//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,\n  1011\u2192\t//   unless adminAccess is requested.\n  1012\u2192\t//\n  1013\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is not specified, the default mode is ExactCount. If\n  1014\u2192\t// the mode is ExactCount and count is not specified, the default count is\n  1015\u2192\t// one. Any other subrequests must specify this field.\n  1016\u2192\t//\n  1017\u2192\t// More modes may get added in the future. Clients must refuse to handle\n  1018\u2192\t// requests with unknown modes.\n  1019\u2192\t//\n  1020\u2192\t// +optional\n  1021\u2192\tAllocationMode DeviceAllocationMode `json:\"allocationMode,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=allocationMode\"`\n  1022\u2192\n  1023\u2192\t// Count is used only when the count mode is \"ExactCount\". Must be greater than zero.\n  1024\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is ExactCount and this field is not specified, the default is one.\n  1025\u2192\t//\n  1026\u2192\t// +optional\n  1027\u2192\t// +oneOf=AllocationMode\n  1028\u2192\tCount int64 `json:\"count,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=count\"`\n  1029\u2192\n  1030\u2192\t// If specified, the request's tolerations.\n  1031\u2192\t//\n  1032\u2192\t// Tolerations for NoSchedule are required to allocate a\n  1033\u2192\t// device which has a taint with that effect. The same applies\n  1034\u2192\t// to NoExecute.\n  1035\u2192\t//\n  1036\u2192\t// In addition, should any of the allocated devices get tainted\n  1037\u2192\t// with NoExecute after allocation and that effect is not tolerated,\n  1038\u2192\t// then all pods consuming the ResourceClaim get deleted to evict\n  1039\u2192\t// them. The scheduler will not let new pods reserve the claim while\n  1040\u2192\t// it has these tainted devices. Once all pods are evicted, the\n  1041\u2192\t// claim will get deallocated.\n  1042\u2192\t//\n  1043\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n  1044\u2192\t//\n  1045\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n  1046\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1047\u2192\t//\n  1048\u2192\t// +optional\n  1049\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1050\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n  1051\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n  1052\u2192\n  1053\u2192\t// Capacity define resource requirements against each capacity.\n  1054\u2192\t//\n  1055\u2192\t// If this field is unset and the device supports multiple allocations,\n  1056\u2192\t// the default value will be applied to each capacity according to requestPolicy.\n  1057\u2192\t// For the capacity that has no requestPolicy, default is the full capacity value.\n  1058\u2192\t//\n  1059\u2192\t// Applies to each device allocation.\n  1060\u2192\t// If Count > 1,\n  1061\u2192\t// the request fails if there aren't enough devices that meet the requirements.\n  1062\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is set to All,\n  1063\u2192\t// the request fails if there are devices that otherwise match the request,\n  1064\u2192\t// and have this capacity, with a value >= the requested amount, but which cannot be allocated to this request.\n  1065\u2192\t//\n  1066\u2192\t// +optional\n  1067\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1068\u2192\tCapacity *CapacityRequirements `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=capacity\"`\n  1069\u2192}\n  1070\u2192\n  1071\u2192// CapacityRequirements defines the capacity requirements for a specific device request.\n  1072\u2192type CapacityRequirements struct {\n  1073\u2192\t// Requests represent individual device resource requests for distinct resources,\n  1074\u2192\t// all of which must be provided by the device.\n  1075\u2192\t//\n  1076\u2192\t// This value is used as an additional filtering condition against the available capacity on the device.\n  1077\u2192\t// This is semantically equivalent to a CEL selector with\n  1078\u2192\t// `device.capacity[<domain>].<name>.compareTo(quantity(<request quantity>)) >= 0`.\n  1079\u2192\t// For example, device.capacity['test-driver.cdi.k8s.io'].counters.compareTo(quantity('2')) >= 0.\n  1080\u2192\t//\n  1081\u2192\t// When a requestPolicy is defined, the requested amount is adjusted upward\n  1082\u2192\t// to the nearest valid value based on the policy.\n  1083\u2192\t// If the requested amount cannot be adjusted to a valid value\u2014because it exceeds what the requestPolicy allows\u2014\n  1084\u2192\t// the device is considered ineligible for allocation.\n  1085\u2192\t//\n  1086\u2192\t// For any capacity that is not explicitly requested:\n  1087\u2192\t// - If no requestPolicy is set, the default consumed capacity is equal to the full device capacity\n  1088\u2192\t//   (i.e., the whole device is claimed).\n  1089\u2192\t// - If a requestPolicy is set, the default consumed capacity is determined according to that policy.\n  1090\u2192\t//\n  1091\u2192\t// If the device allows multiple allocation,\n  1092\u2192\t// the aggregated amount across all requests must not exceed the capacity value.\n  1093\u2192\t// The consumed capacity, which may be adjusted based on the requestPolicy if defined,\n  1094\u2192\t// is recorded in the resource claim\u2019s status.devices[*].consumedCapacity field.\n  1095\u2192\t//\n  1096\u2192\t// +optional\n  1097\u2192\tRequests map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=requests,castkey=QualifiedName\"`\n  1098\u2192}\n  1099\u2192\n  1100\u2192const (\n  1101\u2192\tDeviceSelectorsMaxSize             = 32\n  1102\u2192\tFirstAvailableDeviceRequestMaxSize = 8\n  1103\u2192\tDeviceTolerationsMaxLength         = 16\n  1104\u2192)\n  1105\u2192\n  1106\u2192// +enum\n  1107\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1108\u2192type DeviceAllocationMode string\n  1109\u2192\n  1110\u2192// Valid [DeviceRequest.CountMode] values.\n  1111\u2192const (\n  1112\u2192\tDeviceAllocationModeExactCount = DeviceAllocationMode(\"ExactCount\")\n  1113\u2192\tDeviceAllocationModeAll        = DeviceAllocationMode(\"All\")\n  1114\u2192)\n  1115\u2192\n  1116\u2192// DeviceSelector must have exactly one field set.\n  1117\u2192type DeviceSelector struct {\n  1118\u2192\t// CEL contains a CEL expression for selecting a device.\n  1119\u2192\t//\n  1120\u2192\t// +optional\n  1121\u2192\t// +oneOf=SelectorType\n  1122\u2192\tCEL *CELDeviceSelector `json:\"cel,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=cel\"`\n  1123\u2192}\n  1124\u2192\n  1125\u2192// CELDeviceSelector contains a CEL expression for selecting a device.\n  1126\u2192type CELDeviceSelector struct {\n  1127\u2192\t// Expression is a CEL expression which evaluates a single device. It\n  1128\u2192\t// must evaluate to true when the device under consideration satisfies\n  1129\u2192\t// the desired criteria, and false when it does not. Any other result\n  1130\u2192\t// is an error and causes allocation of devices to abort.\n  1131\u2192\t//\n  1132\u2192\t// The expression's input is an object named \"device\", which carries\n  1133\u2192\t// the following properties:\n  1134\u2192\t//  - driver (string): the name of the driver which defines this device.\n  1135\u2192\t//  - attributes (map[string]object): the device's attributes, grouped by prefix\n  1136\u2192\t//    (e.g. device.attributes[\"dra.example.com\"] evaluates to an object with all\n  1137\u2192\t//    of the attributes which were prefixed by \"dra.example.com\".\n  1138\u2192\t//  - capacity (map[string]object): the device's capacities, grouped by prefix.\n  1139\u2192\t//  - allowMultipleAllocations (bool): the allowMultipleAllocations property of the device\n  1140\u2192\t//    (v1.34+ with the DRAConsumableCapacity feature enabled).\n  1141\u2192\t//\n  1142\u2192\t// Example: Consider a device with driver=\"dra.example.com\", which exposes\n  1143\u2192\t// two attributes named \"model\" and \"ext.example.com/family\" and which\n  1144\u2192\t// exposes one capacity named \"modules\". This input to this expression\n  1145\u2192\t// would have the following fields:\n  1146\u2192\t//\n  1147\u2192\t//     device.driver\n  1148\u2192\t//     device.attributes[\"dra.example.com\"].model\n  1149\u2192\t//     device.attributes[\"ext.example.com\"].family\n  1150\u2192\t//     device.capacity[\"dra.example.com\"].modules\n  1151\u2192\t//\n  1152\u2192\t// The device.driver field can be used to check for a specific driver,\n  1153\u2192\t// either as a high-level precondition (i.e. you only want to consider\n  1154\u2192\t// devices from this driver) or as part of a multi-clause expression\n  1155\u2192\t// that is meant to consider devices from different drivers.\n  1156\u2192\t//\n  1157\u2192\t// The value type of each attribute is defined by the device\n  1158\u2192\t// definition, and users who write these expressions must consult the\n  1159\u2192\t// documentation for their specific drivers. The value type of each\n  1160\u2192\t// capacity is Quantity.\n  1161\u2192\t//\n  1162\u2192\t// If an unknown prefix is used as a lookup in either device.attributes\n  1163\u2192\t// or device.capacity, an empty map will be returned. Any reference to\n  1164\u2192\t// an unknown field will cause an evaluation error and allocation to\n  1165\u2192\t// abort.\n  1166\u2192\t//\n  1167\u2192\t// A robust expression should check for the existence of attributes\n  1168\u2192\t// before referencing them.\n  1169\u2192\t//\n  1170\u2192\t// For ease of use, the cel.bind() function is enabled, and can be used\n  1171\u2192\t// to simplify expressions that access multiple attributes with the\n  1172\u2192\t// same domain. For example:\n  1173\u2192\t//\n  1174\u2192\t//     cel.bind(dra, device.attributes[\"dra.example.com\"], dra.someBool && dra.anotherBool)\n  1175\u2192\t//\n  1176\u2192\t// The length of the expression must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki. The\n  1177\u2192\t// cost of evaluating it is also limited based on the estimated number\n  1178\u2192\t// of logical steps.\n  1179\u2192\t//\n  1180\u2192\t// +required\n  1181\u2192\tExpression string `json:\"expression\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=expression\"`\n  1182\u2192}\n  1183\u2192\n  1184\u2192// CELSelectorExpressionMaxCost specifies the cost limit for a single CEL selector\n  1185\u2192// evaluation.\n  1186\u2192//\n  1187\u2192// There is no overall budget for selecting a device, so the actual time\n  1188\u2192// required for that is proportional to the number of CEL selectors and how\n  1189\u2192// often they need to be evaluated, which can vary depending on several factors\n  1190\u2192// (number of devices, cluster utilization, additional constraints).\n  1191\u2192//\n  1192\u2192// Validation against this limit and [CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength] happens\n  1193\u2192// only when setting an expression for the first time or when changing it. If\n  1194\u2192// the limits are changed in a future Kubernetes release, existing users are\n  1195\u2192// guaranteed that existing expressions will continue to be valid.\n  1196\u2192//\n  1197\u2192// However, the kube-scheduler also applies this cost limit at runtime, so it\n  1198\u2192// could happen that a valid expression fails at runtime after an up- or\n  1199\u2192// downgrade. This can also happen without version skew when the cost estimate\n  1200\u2192// underestimated the actual cost. That this might happen is the reason why\n  1201\u2192// kube-scheduler enforces the runtime limit instead of relying on validation.\n  1202\u2192//\n  1203\u2192// According to\n  1204\u2192// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/4aeaf1e99e82da8334c0d6dddd848a194cd44b4f/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/cel/config.go#L20-L22,\n  1205\u2192// this gives roughly 0.1 second for each expression evaluation.\n  1206\u2192// However, this depends on how fast the machine is.\n  1207\u2192const CELSelectorExpressionMaxCost = 1000000\n  1208\u2192\n  1209\u2192// CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength is the maximum length of a CEL selector expression string.\n  1210\u2192const CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength = 10 * 1024\n  1211\u2192\n  1212\u2192// DeviceConstraint must have exactly one field set besides Requests.\n  1213\u2192type DeviceConstraint struct {\n  1214\u2192\t// Requests is a list of the one or more requests in this claim which\n  1215\u2192\t// must co-satisfy this constraint. If a request is fulfilled by\n  1216\u2192\t// multiple devices, then all of the devices must satisfy the\n  1217\u2192\t// constraint. If this is not specified, this constraint applies to all\n  1218\u2192\t// requests in this claim.\n  1219\u2192\t//\n  1220\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1221\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1222\u2192\t// the main request is given, the constraint applies to all subrequests.\n  1223\u2192\t//\n  1224\u2192\t// +optional\n  1225\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1226\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n  1227\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=set\n  1228\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1229\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1230\u2192\n  1231\u2192\t// MatchAttribute requires that all devices in question have this\n  1232\u2192\t// attribute and that its type and value are the same across those\n  1233\u2192\t// devices.\n  1234\u2192\t//\n  1235\u2192\t// For example, if you specified \"dra.example.com/numa\" (a hypothetical example!),\n  1236\u2192\t// then only devices in the same NUMA node will be chosen. A device which\n  1237\u2192\t// does not have that attribute will not be chosen. All devices should\n  1238\u2192\t// use a value of the same type for this attribute because that is part of\n  1239\u2192\t// its specification, but if one device doesn't, then it also will not be\n  1240\u2192\t// chosen.\n  1241\u2192\t//\n  1242\u2192\t// Must include the domain qualifier.\n  1243\u2192\t//\n  1244\u2192\t// +optional\n  1245\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConstraintType\n  1246\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1247\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-resource-fully-qualified-name\n  1248\u2192\tMatchAttribute *FullyQualifiedName `json:\"matchAttribute,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=matchAttribute\"`\n  1249\u2192\n  1250\u2192\t// Potential future extension, not part of the current design:\n  1251\u2192\t// A CEL expression which compares different devices and returns\n  1252\u2192\t// true if they match.\n  1253\u2192\t//\n  1254\u2192\t// Because it would be part of a one-of, old schedulers will not\n  1255\u2192\t// accidentally ignore this additional, for them unknown match\n  1256\u2192\t// criteria.\n  1257\u2192\t//\n  1258\u2192\t// MatchExpression string\n  1259\u2192\n  1260\u2192\t// DistinctAttribute requires that all devices in question have this\n  1261\u2192\t// attribute and that its type and value are unique across those devices.\n  1262\u2192\t//\n  1263\u2192\t// This acts as the inverse of MatchAttribute.\n  1264\u2192\t//\n  1265\u2192\t// This constraint is used to avoid allocating multiple requests to the same device\n  1266\u2192\t// by ensuring attribute-level differentiation.\n  1267\u2192\t//\n  1268\u2192\t// This is useful for scenarios where resource requests must be fulfilled by separate physical devices.\n  1269\u2192\t// For example, a container requests two network interfaces that must be allocated from two different physical NICs.\n  1270\u2192\t//\n  1271\u2192\t// +optional\n  1272\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConstraintType\n  1273\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1274\u2192\tDistinctAttribute *FullyQualifiedName `json:\"distinctAttribute,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=distinctAttribute\"`\n  1275\u2192}\n  1276\u2192\n  1277\u2192// DeviceClaimConfiguration is used for configuration parameters in DeviceClaim.\n  1278\u2192type DeviceClaimConfiguration struct {\n  1279\u2192\t// Requests lists the names of requests where the configuration applies.\n  1280\u2192\t// If empty, it applies to all requests.\n  1281\u2192\t//\n  1282\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1283\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1284\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1285\u2192\t//\n  1286\u2192\t// +optional\n  1287\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1288\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n  1289\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=set\n  1290\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1291\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1292\u2192\n  1293\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1294\u2192}\n  1295\u2192\n  1296\u2192// DeviceConfiguration must have exactly one field set. It gets embedded\n  1297\u2192// inline in some other structs which have other fields, so field names must\n  1298\u2192// not conflict with those.\n  1299\u2192type DeviceConfiguration struct {\n  1300\u2192\t// Opaque provides driver-specific configuration parameters.\n  1301\u2192\t//\n  1302\u2192\t// +optional\n  1303\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConfigurationType\n  1304\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1305\u2192\tOpaque *OpaqueDeviceConfiguration `json:\"opaque,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=opaque\"`\n  1306\u2192}\n  1307\u2192\n  1308\u2192// OpaqueDeviceConfiguration contains configuration parameters for a driver\n  1309\u2192// in a format defined by the driver vendor.\n  1310\u2192type OpaqueDeviceConfiguration struct {\n  1311\u2192\t// Driver is used to determine which kubelet plugin needs\n  1312\u2192\t// to be passed these configuration parameters.\n  1313\u2192\t//\n  1314\u2192\t// An admission policy provided by the driver developer could use this\n  1315\u2192\t// to decide whether it needs to validate them.\n  1316\u2192\t//\n  1317\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1318\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1319\u2192\t//\n  1320\u2192\t// +required\n  1321\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1322\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name-caseless\n  1323\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=driver\"`\n  1324\u2192\n  1325\u2192\t// Parameters can contain arbitrary data. It is the responsibility of\n  1326\u2192\t// the driver developer to handle validation and versioning. Typically this\n  1327\u2192\t// includes self-identification and a version (\"kind\" + \"apiVersion\" for\n  1328\u2192\t// Kubernetes types), with conversion between different versions.\n  1329\u2192\t//\n  1330\u2192\t// The length of the raw data must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki.\n  1331\u2192\t//\n  1332\u2192\t// +required\n  1333\u2192\tParameters runtime.RawExtension `json:\"parameters\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=parameters\"`\n  1334\u2192}\n  1335\u2192\n  1336\u2192// OpaqueParametersMaxLength is the maximum length of the raw data in an\n  1337\u2192// [OpaqueDeviceConfiguration.Parameters] field.\n  1338\u2192const OpaqueParametersMaxLength = 10 * 1024\n  1339\u2192\n  1340\u2192// The ResourceClaim this DeviceToleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n  1341\u2192// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n  1342\u2192type DeviceToleration struct {\n  1343\u2192\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n  1344\u2192\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n  1345\u2192\t// Must be a label name.\n  1346\u2192\t//\n  1347\u2192\t// +optional\n  1348\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1349\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-label-key\n  1350\u2192\tKey string `json:\"key,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\"`\n  1351\u2192\n  1352\u2192\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n  1353\u2192\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n  1354\u2192\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a ResourceClaim can\n  1355\u2192\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n  1356\u2192\t//\n  1357\u2192\t// +optional\n  1358\u2192\t// +default=\"Equal\"\n  1359\u2192\tOperator DeviceTolerationOperator `json:\"operator,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=DeviceTolerationOperator\"`\n  1360\u2192\n  1361\u2192\t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\n  1362\u2192\t// If the operator is Exists, the value must be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\n  1363\u2192\t// Must be a label value.\n  1364\u2192\t//\n  1365\u2192\t// +optional\n  1366\u2192\tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\"`\n  1367\u2192\n  1368\u2192\t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\n  1369\u2192\t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule and NoExecute.\n  1370\u2192\t//\n  1371\u2192\t// +optional\n  1372\u2192\tEffect DeviceTaintEffect `json:\"effect,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=DeviceTaintEffect\"`\n  1373\u2192\n  1374\u2192\t// TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be\n  1375\u2192\t// of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default,\n  1376\u2192\t// it is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and\n  1377\u2192\t// negative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.\n  1378\u2192\t// If larger than zero, the time when the pod needs to be evicted is calculated as <time when\n  1379\u2192\t// taint was adedd> + <toleration seconds>.\n  1380\u2192\t//\n  1381\u2192\t// +optional\n  1382\u2192\tTolerationSeconds *int64 `json:\"tolerationSeconds,omitempty\" protobuf:\"varint,5,opt,name=tolerationSeconds\"`\n  1383\u2192}\n  1384\u2192\n  1385\u2192// A toleration operator is the set of operators that can be used in a toleration.\n  1386\u2192//\n  1387\u2192// +enum\n  1388\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1389\u2192type DeviceTolerationOperator string\n  1390\u2192\n  1391\u2192const (\n  1392\u2192\tDeviceTolerationOpExists DeviceTolerationOperator = \"Exists\"\n  1393\u2192\tDeviceTolerationOpEqual  DeviceTolerationOperator = \"Equal\"\n  1394\u2192)\n  1395\u2192\n  1396\u2192// ResourceClaimStatus tracks whether the resource has been allocated and what\n  1397\u2192// the result of that was.\n  1398\u2192type ResourceClaimStatus struct {\n  1399\u2192\t// Allocation is set once the claim has been allocated successfully.\n  1400\u2192\t//\n  1401\u2192\t// +optional\n  1402\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1403\u2192\t// +k8s:update=NoModify\n  1404\u2192\tAllocation *AllocationResult `json:\"allocation,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=allocation\"`\n  1405\u2192\n  1406\u2192\t// ReservedFor indicates which entities are currently allowed to use\n  1407\u2192\t// the claim. A Pod which references a ResourceClaim which is not\n  1408\u2192\t// reserved for that Pod will not be started. A claim that is in\n  1409\u2192\t// use or might be in use because it has been reserved must not get\n  1410\u2192\t// deallocated.\n  1411\u2192\t//\n  1412\u2192\t// In a cluster with multiple scheduler instances, two pods might get\n  1413\u2192\t// scheduled concurrently by different schedulers. When they reference\n  1414\u2192\t// the same ResourceClaim which already has reached its maximum number\n  1415\u2192\t// of consumers, only one pod can be scheduled.\n  1416\u2192\t//\n  1417\u2192\t// Both schedulers try to add their pod to the claim.status.reservedFor\n  1418\u2192\t// field, but only the update that reaches the API server first gets\n  1419\u2192\t// stored. The other one fails with an error and the scheduler\n  1420\u2192\t// which issued it knows that it must put the pod back into the queue,\n  1421\u2192\t// waiting for the ResourceClaim to become usable again.\n  1422\u2192\t//\n  1423\u2192\t// There can be at most 256 such reservations. This may get increased in\n  1424\u2192\t// the future, but not reduced.\n  1425\u2192\t//\n  1426\u2192\t// +optional\n  1427\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1428\u2192\t// +listMapKey=uid\n  1429\u2192\t// +patchStrategy=merge\n  1430\u2192\t// +patchMergeKey=uid\n  1431\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1432\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=map\n  1433\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=uid\n  1434\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=256\n  1435\u2192\tReservedFor []ResourceClaimConsumerReference `json:\"reservedFor,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=reservedFor\" patchStrategy:\"merge\" patchMergeKey:\"uid\"`\n  1436\u2192\n  1437\u2192\t// DeallocationRequested is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1438\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1439\u2192\t// supported.\n  1440\u2192\t// DeallocationRequested bool `json:\"deallocationRequested,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=deallocationRequested\"`\n  1441\u2192\n  1442\u2192\t// Devices contains the status of each device allocated for this\n  1443\u2192\t// claim, as reported by the driver. This can include driver-specific\n  1444\u2192\t// information. Entries are owned by their respective drivers.\n  1445\u2192\t//\n  1446\u2192\t// +optional\n  1447\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1448\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1449\u2192\t// +listMapKey=driver\n  1450\u2192\t// +listMapKey=device\n  1451\u2192\t// +listMapKey=pool\n  1452\u2192\t// +listMapKey=shareID\n  1453\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1454\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=map\n  1455\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=driver\n  1456\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=device\n  1457\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=pool\n  1458\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=shareID\n  1459\u2192\tDevices []AllocatedDeviceStatus `json:\"devices,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=devices\"`\n  1460\u2192}\n  1461\u2192\n  1462\u2192// ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize is the maximum number of entries in\n  1463\u2192// claim.status.reservedFor.\n  1464\u2192const ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize = 256\n  1465\u2192\n  1466\u2192// ResourceClaimConsumerReference contains enough information to let you\n  1467\u2192// locate the consumer of a ResourceClaim. The user must be a resource in the same\n  1468\u2192// namespace as the ResourceClaim.\n  1469\u2192type ResourceClaimConsumerReference struct {\n  1470\u2192\t// APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced. It is\n  1471\u2192\t// empty for the core API. This matches the group in the APIVersion\n  1472\u2192\t// that is used when creating the resources.\n  1473\u2192\t// +optional\n  1474\u2192\tAPIGroup string `json:\"apiGroup,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=apiGroup\"`\n  1475\u2192\t// Resource is the type of resource being referenced, for example \"pods\".\n  1476\u2192\t// +required\n  1477\u2192\tResource string `json:\"resource\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=resource\"`\n  1478\u2192\t// Name is the name of resource being referenced.\n  1479\u2192\t// +required\n  1480\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=name\"`\n  1481\u2192\t// UID identifies exactly one incarnation of the resource.\n  1482\u2192\t// +required\n  1483\u2192\tUID types.UID `json:\"uid\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,name=uid\"`\n  1484\u2192}\n  1485\u2192\n  1486\u2192// AllocationResult contains attributes of an allocated resource.\n  1487\u2192type AllocationResult struct {\n  1488\u2192\t// Devices is the result of allocating devices.\n  1489\u2192\t//\n  1490\u2192\t// +optional\n  1491\u2192\tDevices DeviceAllocationResult `json:\"devices,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=devices\"`\n  1492\u2192\n  1493\u2192\t// NodeSelector defines where the allocated resources are available. If\n  1494\u2192\t// unset, they are available everywhere.\n  1495\u2192\t//\n  1496\u2192\t// +optional\n  1497\u2192\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n  1498\u2192\n  1499\u2192\t// Controller is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1500\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1501\u2192\t// supported.\n  1502\u2192\t// Controller string `json:\"controller,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=controller\"`\n  1503\u2192\n  1504\u2192\t// AllocationTimestamp stores the time when the resources were allocated.\n  1505\u2192\t// This field is not guaranteed to be set, in which case that time is unknown.\n  1506\u2192\t//\n  1507\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1508\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1509\u2192\t//\n  1510\u2192\t// +optional\n  1511\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1512\u2192\tAllocationTimestamp *metav1.Time `json:\"allocationTimestamp,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=allocationTimestamp\"`\n  1513\u2192}\n  1514\u2192\n  1515\u2192// DeviceAllocationResult is the result of allocating devices.\n  1516\u2192type DeviceAllocationResult struct {\n  1517\u2192\t// Results lists all allocated devices.\n  1518\u2192\t//\n  1519\u2192\t// +optional\n  1520\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1521\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1522\u2192\tResults []DeviceRequestAllocationResult `json:\"results,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=results\"`\n  1523\u2192\n  1524\u2192\t// This field is a combination of all the claim and class configuration parameters.\n  1525\u2192\t// Drivers can distinguish between those based on a flag.\n  1526\u2192\t//\n  1527\u2192\t// This includes configuration parameters for drivers which have no allocated\n  1528\u2192\t// devices in the result because it is up to the drivers which configuration\n  1529\u2192\t// parameters they support. They can silently ignore unknown configuration\n  1530\u2192\t// parameters.\n  1531\u2192\t//\n  1532\u2192\t// +optional\n  1533\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1534\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=64\n  1535\u2192\tConfig []DeviceAllocationConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1536\u2192}\n  1537\u2192\n  1538\u2192// AllocationResultsMaxSize represents the maximum number of\n  1539\u2192// entries in allocation.devices.results.\n  1540\u2192const AllocationResultsMaxSize = 32\n  1541\u2192\n  1542\u2192// DeviceRequestAllocationResult contains the allocation result for one request.\n  1543\u2192type DeviceRequestAllocationResult struct {\n  1544\u2192\t// Request is the name of the request in the claim which caused this\n  1545\u2192\t// device to be allocated. If it references a subrequest in the\n  1546\u2192\t// firstAvailable list on a DeviceRequest, this field must\n  1547\u2192\t// include both the name of the main request and the subrequest\n  1548\u2192\t// using the format <main request>/<subrequest>.\n  1549\u2192\t//\n  1550\u2192\t// Multiple devices may have been allocated per request.\n  1551\u2192\t//\n  1552\u2192\t// +required\n  1553\u2192\tRequest string `json:\"request\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=request\"`\n  1554\u2192\n  1555\u2192\t// Driver specifies the name of the DRA driver whose kubelet\n  1556\u2192\t// plugin should be invoked to process the allocation once the claim is\n  1557\u2192\t// needed on a node.\n  1558\u2192\t//\n  1559\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1560\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1561\u2192\t//\n  1562\u2192\t// +required\n  1563\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name-caseless\n  1564\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1565\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=driver\"`\n  1566\u2192\n  1567\u2192\t// This name together with the driver name and the device name field\n  1568\u2192\t// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).\n  1569\u2192\t//\n  1570\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 253 characters and may contain one or more\n  1571\u2192\t// DNS sub-domains separated by slashes.\n  1572\u2192\t//\n  1573\u2192\t// +required\n  1574\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1575\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-resource-pool-name\n  1576\u2192\tPool string `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=pool\"`\n  1577\u2192\n  1578\u2192\t// Device references one device instance via its name in the driver's\n  1579\u2192\t// resource pool. It must be a DNS label.\n  1580\u2192\t//\n  1581\u2192\t// +required\n  1582\u2192\tDevice string `json:\"device\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=device\"`\n  1583\u2192\n  1584\u2192\t// AdminAccess indicates that this device was allocated for\n  1585\u2192\t// administrative access. See the corresponding request field\n  1586\u2192\t// for a definition of mode.\n  1587\u2192\t//\n  1588\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRAAdminAccess\n  1589\u2192\t// feature gate. Admin access is disabled if this field is unset or\n  1590\u2192\t// set to false, otherwise it is enabled.\n  1591\u2192\t//\n  1592\u2192\t// +optional\n  1593\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAAdminAccess\n  1594\u2192\tAdminAccess *bool `json:\"adminAccess,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=adminAccess\"`\n  1595\u2192\n  1596\u2192\t// A copy of all tolerations specified in the request at the time\n  1597\u2192\t// when the device got allocated.\n  1598\u2192\t//\n  1599\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n  1600\u2192\t//\n  1601\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n  1602\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1603\u2192\t//\n  1604\u2192\t// +optional\n  1605\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1606\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n  1607\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n  1608\u2192\n  1609\u2192\t// BindingConditions contains a copy of the BindingConditions\n  1610\u2192\t// from the corresponding ResourceSlice at the time of allocation.\n  1611\u2192\t//\n  1612\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1613\u2192\t// feature gates.\n  1614\u2192\t//\n  1615\u2192\t// +optional\n  1616\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1617\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1618\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1619\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n  1620\u2192\tBindingConditions []string `json:\"bindingConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,rep,name=bindingConditions\"`\n  1621\u2192\n  1622\u2192\t// BindingFailureConditions contains a copy of the BindingFailureConditions\n  1623\u2192\t// from the corresponding ResourceSlice at the time of allocation.\n  1624\u2192\t//\n  1625\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1626\u2192\t// feature gates.\n  1627\u2192\t//\n  1628\u2192\t// +optional\n  1629\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1630\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1631\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1632\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n  1633\u2192\tBindingFailureConditions []string `json:\"bindingFailureConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,rep,name=bindingFailureConditions\"`\n  1634\u2192\n  1635\u2192\t// ShareID uniquely identifies an individual allocation share of the device,\n  1636\u2192\t// used when the device supports multiple simultaneous allocations.\n  1637\u2192\t// It serves as an additional map key to differentiate concurrent shares\n  1638\u2192\t// of the same device.\n  1639\u2192\t//\n  1640\u2192\t// +optional\n  1641\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1642\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1643\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-uuid\n  1644\u2192\tShareID *types.UID `json:\"shareID,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,9,opt,name=shareID\"`\n  1645\u2192\n  1646\u2192\t// ConsumedCapacity tracks the amount of capacity consumed per device as part of the claim request.\n  1647\u2192\t// The consumed amount may differ from the requested amount: it is rounded up to the nearest valid\n  1648\u2192\t// value based on the device\u2019s requestPolicy if applicable (i.e., may not be less than the requested amount).\n  1649\u2192\t//\n  1650\u2192\t// The total consumed capacity for each device must not exceed the DeviceCapacity's Value.\n  1651\u2192\t//\n  1652\u2192\t// This field is populated only for devices that allow multiple allocations.\n  1653\u2192\t// All capacity entries are included, even if the consumed amount is zero.\n  1654\u2192\t//\n  1655\u2192\t// +optional\n  1656\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1657\u2192\tConsumedCapacity map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity `json:\"consumedCapacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,10,rep,name=consumedCapacity\"`\n  1658\u2192}\n  1659\u2192\n  1660\u2192// DeviceAllocationConfiguration gets embedded in an AllocationResult.\n  1661\u2192type DeviceAllocationConfiguration struct {\n  1662\u2192\t// Source records whether the configuration comes from a class and thus\n  1663\u2192\t// is not something that a normal user would have been able to set\n  1664\u2192\t// or from a claim.\n  1665\u2192\t//\n  1666\u2192\t// +required\n  1667\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1668\u2192\tSource AllocationConfigSource `json:\"source\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=source\"`\n  1669\u2192\n  1670\u2192\t// Requests lists the names of requests where the configuration applies.\n  1671\u2192\t// If empty, its applies to all requests.\n  1672\u2192\t//\n  1673\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1674\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1675\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1676\u2192\t//\n  1677\u2192\t// +optional\n  1678\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1679\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1680\u2192\n  1681\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1682\u2192}\n  1683\u2192\n  1684\u2192// +enum\n  1685\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1686\u2192type AllocationConfigSource string\n  1687\u2192\n  1688\u2192// Valid [DeviceAllocationConfiguration.Source] values.\n  1689\u2192const (\n  1690\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClass AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClass\"\n  1691\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClaim AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClaim\"\n  1692\u2192)\n  1693\u2192\n  1694\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1695\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1696\u2192\n  1697\u2192// ResourceClaimList is a collection of claims.\n  1698\u2192type ResourceClaimList struct {\n  1699\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1700\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1701\u2192\t// +optional\n  1702\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1703\u2192\n  1704\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claims.\n  1705\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaim `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1706\u2192}\n  1707\u2192\n  1708\u2192// +genclient\n  1709\u2192// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n  1710\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1711\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1712\u2192\n  1713\u2192// DeviceClass is a vendor- or admin-provided resource that contains\n  1714\u2192// device configuration and selectors. It can be referenced in\n  1715\u2192// the device requests of a claim to apply these presets.\n  1716\u2192// Cluster scoped.\n  1717\u2192//\n  1718\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1719\u2192// feature gate.\n  1720\u2192type DeviceClass struct {\n  1721\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1722\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1723\u2192\t// +optional\n  1724\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:optional\n  1725\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n  1726\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1727\u2192\n  1728\u2192\t// Spec defines what can be allocated and how to configure it.\n  1729\u2192\t//\n  1730\u2192\t// This is mutable. Consumers have to be prepared for classes changing\n  1731\u2192\t// at any time, either because they get updated or replaced. Claim\n  1732\u2192\t// allocations are done once based on whatever was set in classes at\n  1733\u2192\t// the time of allocation.\n  1734\u2192\t//\n  1735\u2192\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n  1736\u2192\tSpec DeviceClassSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1737\u2192}\n  1738\u2192\n  1739\u2192// DeviceClassSpec is used in a [DeviceClass] to define what can be allocated\n  1740\u2192// and how to configure it.\n  1741\u2192type DeviceClassSpec struct {\n  1742\u2192\t// Each selector must be satisfied by a device which is claimed via this class.\n  1743\u2192\t//\n  1744\u2192\t// +optional\n  1745\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1746\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1747\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1748\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=selectors\"`\n  1749\u2192\n  1750\u2192\t// Config defines configuration parameters that apply to each device that is claimed via this class.\n  1751\u2192\t// Some classses may potentially be satisfied by multiple drivers, so each instance of a vendor\n  1752\u2192\t// configuration applies to exactly one driver.\n  1753\u2192\t//\n  1754\u2192\t// They are passed to the driver, but are not considered while allocating the claim.\n  1755\u2192\t//\n  1756\u2192\t// +optional\n  1757\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1758\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1759\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1760\u2192\tConfig []DeviceClassConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1761\u2192\n  1762\u2192\t// SuitableNodes is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1763\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1764\u2192\t// supported.\n  1765\u2192\t// SuitableNodes *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"suitableNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=suitableNodes\"`\n  1766\u2192\n  1767\u2192\t// ExtendedResourceName is the extended resource name for the devices of this class.\n  1768\u2192\t// The devices of this class can be used to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1769\u2192\t// It has the same format as the name of a pod's extended resource.\n  1770\u2192\t// It should be unique among all the device classes in a cluster.\n  1771\u2192\t// If two device classes have the same name, then the class created later\n  1772\u2192\t// is picked to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1773\u2192\t// If two classes are created at the same time, then the name of the class\n  1774\u2192\t// lexicographically sorted first is picked.\n  1775\u2192\t//\n  1776\u2192\t// This is an alpha field.\n  1777\u2192\t// +optional\n  1778\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAExtendedResource\n  1779\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1780\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-extended-resource-name\n  1781\u2192\tExtendedResourceName *string `json:\"extendedResourceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=extendedResourceName\"`\n  1782\u2192}\n  1783\u2192\n  1784\u2192// DeviceClassConfiguration is used in DeviceClass.\n  1785\u2192type DeviceClassConfiguration struct {\n  1786\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1787\u2192}\n  1788\u2192\n  1789\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1790\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1791\u2192\n  1792\u2192// DeviceClassList is a collection of classes.\n  1793\u2192type DeviceClassList struct {\n  1794\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1795\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1796\u2192\t// +optional\n  1797\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1798\u2192\n  1799\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource classes.\n  1800\u2192\tItems []DeviceClass `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1801\u2192}\n  1802\u2192\n  1803\u2192// +genclient\n  1804\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1805\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1806\u2192\n  1807\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects.\n  1808\u2192//\n  1809\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1810\u2192// feature gate.\n  1811\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplate struct {\n  1812\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1813\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1814\u2192\t// +optional\n  1815\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1816\u2192\n  1817\u2192\t// Describes the ResourceClaim that is to be generated.\n  1818\u2192\t//\n  1819\u2192\t// This field is immutable. A ResourceClaim will get created by the\n  1820\u2192\t// control plane for a Pod when needed and then not get updated\n  1821\u2192\t// anymore.\n  1822\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimTemplateSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1823\u2192}\n  1824\u2192\n  1825\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplateSpec contains the metadata and fields for a ResourceClaim.\n  1826\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplateSpec struct {\n  1827\u2192\t// ObjectMeta may contain labels and annotations that will be copied into the ResourceClaim\n  1828\u2192\t// when creating it. No other fields are allowed and will be rejected during\n  1829\u2192\t// validation.\n  1830\u2192\t// +optional\n  1831\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1832\u2192\n  1833\u2192\t// Spec for the ResourceClaim. The entire content is copied unchanged\n  1834\u2192\t// into the ResourceClaim that gets created from this template. The\n  1835\u2192\t// same fields as in a ResourceClaim are also valid here.\n  1836\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1837\u2192}\n  1838\u2192\n  1839\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1840\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1841\u2192\n  1842\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplateList is a collection of claim templates.\n  1843\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplateList struct {\n  1844\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1845\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1846\u2192\t// +optional\n  1847\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1848\u2192\n  1849\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claim templates.\n  1850\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaimTemplate `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1851\u2192}\n  1852\u2192\n  1853\u2192const (\n  1854\u2192\t// AllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions represents the maximum number of\n  1855\u2192\t// conditions in a device status.\n  1856\u2192\tAllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions int = 8\n  1857\u2192\t// AllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength represents the maximum length of the\n  1858\u2192\t// raw data in the Data field in a device status.\n  1859\u2192\tAllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength int = 10 * 1024\n  1860\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs represents the maximum number of IPs in the networkData\n  1861\u2192\t// field in a device status.\n  1862\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs int = 16\n  1863\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength represents the maximum number of characters\n  1864\u2192\t// for the networkData.interfaceName field in a device status.\n  1865\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength int = 256\n  1866\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength represents the maximum number of characters\n  1867\u2192\t// for the networkData.hardwareAddress field in a device status.\n  1868\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength int = 128\n  1869\u2192)\n  1870\u2192\n  1871\u2192// AllocatedDeviceStatus contains the status of an allocated device, if the\n  1872\u2192// driver chooses to report it. This may include driver-specific information.\n  1873\u2192//\n  1874\u2192// The combination of Driver, Pool, Device, and ShareID must match the corresponding key\n  1875\u2192// in Status.Allocation.Devices.\n  1876\u2192type AllocatedDeviceStatus struct {\n  1877\u2192\t// Driver specifies the name of the DRA driver whose kubelet\n  1878\u2192\t// plugin should be invoked to process the allocation once the claim is\n  1879\u2192\t// needed on a node.\n  1880\u2192\t//\n  1881\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1882\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1883\u2192\t//\n  1884\u2192\t// +required\n  1885\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=driver\"`\n  1886\u2192\n  1887\u2192\t// This name together with the driver name and the device name field\n  1888\u2192\t// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).\n  1889\u2192\t//\n  1890\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 253 characters and may contain one or more\n  1891\u2192\t// DNS sub-domains separated by slashes.\n  1892\u2192\t//\n  1893\u2192\t// +required\n  1894\u2192\tPool string `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=pool\"`\n  1895\u2192\n  1896\u2192\t// Device references one device instance via its name in the driver's\n  1897\u2192\t// resource pool. It must be a DNS label.\n  1898\u2192\t//\n  1899\u2192\t// +required\n  1900\u2192\tDevice string `json:\"device\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=device\"`\n  1901\u2192\n  1902\u2192\t// ShareID uniquely identifies an individual allocation share of the device.\n  1903\u2192\t//\n  1904\u2192\t// +optional\n  1905\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1906\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1907\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-uuid\n  1908\u2192\tShareID *string `json:\"shareID,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=shareID\"`\n  1909\u2192\n  1910\u2192\t// Conditions contains the latest observation of the device's state.\n  1911\u2192\t// If the device has been configured according to the class and claim\n  1912\u2192\t// config references, the `Ready` condition should be True.\n  1913\u2192\t//\n  1914\u2192\t// Must not contain more than 8 entries.\n  1915\u2192\t//\n  1916\u2192\t// +optional\n  1917\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1918\u2192\t// +listMapKey=type\n  1919\u2192\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=conditions\"`\n  1920\u2192\n  1921\u2192\t// Data contains arbitrary driver-specific data.\n  1922\u2192\t//\n  1923\u2192\t// The length of the raw data must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki.\n  1924\u2192\t//\n  1925\u2192\t// +optional\n  1926\u2192\tData *runtime.RawExtension `json:\"data,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=data\"`\n  1927\u2192\n  1928\u2192\t// NetworkData contains network-related information specific to the device.\n  1929\u2192\t//\n  1930\u2192\t// +optional\n  1931\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1932\u2192\tNetworkData *NetworkDeviceData `json:\"networkData,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=networkData\"`\n  1933\u2192}\n  1934\u2192\n  1935\u2192// NetworkDeviceData provides network-related details for the allocated device.\n  1936\u2192// This information may be filled by drivers or other components to configure\n  1937\u2192// or identify the device within a network context.\n  1938\u2192type NetworkDeviceData struct {\n  1939\u2192\t// InterfaceName specifies the name of the network interface associated with\n  1940\u2192\t// the allocated device. This might be the name of a physical or virtual\n  1941\u2192\t// network interface being configured in the pod.\n  1942\u2192\t//\n  1943\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 256 characters.\n  1944\u2192\t//\n  1945\u2192\t// +optional\n  1946\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1947\u2192\t// +k8s:maxLength=256\n  1948\u2192\tInterfaceName string `json:\"interfaceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=interfaceName\"`\n  1949\u2192\n  1950\u2192\t// IPs lists the network addresses assigned to the device's network interface.\n  1951\u2192\t// This can include both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.\n  1952\u2192\t// The IPs are in the CIDR notation, which includes both the address and the\n  1953\u2192\t// associated subnet mask.\n  1954\u2192\t// e.g.: \"192.0.2.5/24\" for IPv4 and \"2001:db8::5/64\" for IPv6.\n  1955\u2192\t//\n  1956\u2192\t// +optional\n  1957\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1958\u2192\tIPs []string `json:\"ips,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=ips\"`\n  1959\u2192\n  1960\u2192\t// HardwareAddress represents the hardware address (e.g. MAC Address) of the device's network interface.\n  1961\u2192\t//\n  1962\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 128 characters.\n  1963\u2192\t//\n  1964\u2192\t// +optional\n  1965\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1966\u2192\t// +k8s:maxLength=128\n  1967\u2192\tHardwareAddress string `json:\"hardwareAddress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=hardwareAddress\"`\n  1968\u2192}\n  1969\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
13. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta1/types.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package v1beta1\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    21\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\"\n    22\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n    26\u2192)\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192const (\n    29\u2192\t// Finalizer is the finalizer that gets set for claims\n    30\u2192\t// which were allocated through a builtin controller.\n    31\u2192\t// Reserved for use by Kubernetes, DRA driver controllers must\n    32\u2192\t// use their own finalizer.\n    33\u2192\tFinalizer = \"resource.kubernetes.io/delete-protection\"\n    34\u2192\t// ExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation is the annotation applied on the generated\n    35\u2192\t// special ResourceClaim. Its single valid value is \"true\".\n    36\u2192\t// This is used only inside the scheduler.\n    37\u2192\tExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation = \"resource.kubernetes.io/extended-resource-claim\"\n    38\u2192\t// Resource device class prefix is for generating implicit extended resource\n    39\u2192\t// name for a device class when its ExtendedResourceName field is not\n    40\u2192\t// specified. The generated name is this prefix + the device class name.\n    41\u2192\t// The generated name may not be a valid extended resource name for use\n    42\u2192\t// in pod.Spec.Resources.Requests, in that case, a valid name has to be specified\n    43\u2192\t// explicitly in device class.\n    44\u2192\tResourceDeviceClassPrefix string = \"deviceclass.resource.kubernetes.io/\"\n    45\u2192)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192// +genclient\n    48\u2192// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n    49\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n    50\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources,\n    53\u2192// managed by a common driver. A pool may span more than one ResourceSlice, and exactly how many\n    54\u2192// ResourceSlices comprise a pool is determined by the driver.\n    55\u2192//\n    56\u2192// At the moment, the only supported resources are devices with attributes and capacities.\n    57\u2192// Each device in a given pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices, must have a unique name.\n    58\u2192// The ResourceSlice in which a device gets published may change over time. The unique identifier\n    59\u2192// for a device is the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>.\n    60\u2192//\n    61\u2192// Whenever a driver needs to update a pool, it increments the pool.Spec.Pool.Generation number\n    62\u2192// and updates all ResourceSlices with that new number and new resource definitions. A consumer\n    63\u2192// must only use ResourceSlices with the highest generation number and ignore all others.\n    64\u2192//\n    65\u2192// When allocating all resources in a pool matching certain criteria or when\n    66\u2192// looking for the best solution among several different alternatives, a\n    67\u2192// consumer should check the number of ResourceSlices in a pool (included in\n    68\u2192// each ResourceSlice) to determine whether its view of a pool is complete and\n    69\u2192// if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.\n    70\u2192//\n    71\u2192// For resources that are not local to a node, the node name is not set. Instead,\n    72\u2192// the driver may use a node selector to specify where the devices are available.\n    73\u2192//\n    74\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n    75\u2192// feature gate.\n    76\u2192type ResourceSlice struct {\n    77\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n    78\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n    79\u2192\t// +optional\n    80\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t// Contains the information published by the driver.\n    83\u2192\t//\n    84\u2192\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n    85\u2192\tSpec ResourceSliceSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192const (\n    89\u2192\t// ResourceSliceSelectorNodeName can be used in a [metav1.ListOptions]\n    90\u2192\t// field selector to filter based on [ResourceSliceSpec.NodeName].\n    91\u2192\tResourceSliceSelectorNodeName = \"spec.nodeName\"\n    92\u2192\t// ResourceSliceSelectorDriver can be used in a [metav1.ListOptions]\n    93\u2192\t// field selector to filter based on [ResourceSliceSpec.Driver].\n    94\u2192\tResourceSliceSelectorDriver = \"spec.driver\"\n    95\u2192)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192// ResourceSliceSpec contains the information published by the driver in one ResourceSlice.\n    98\u2192type ResourceSliceSpec struct {\n    99\u2192\t// Driver identifies the DRA driver providing the capacity information.\n   100\u2192\t// A field selector can be used to list only ResourceSlice\n   101\u2192\t// objects with a certain driver name.\n   102\u2192\t//\n   103\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n   104\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n   105\u2192\t// This field is immutable.\n   106\u2192\t//\n   107\u2192\t// +required\n   108\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=driver\"`\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\t// Pool describes the pool that this ResourceSlice belongs to.\n   111\u2192\t//\n   112\u2192\t// +required\n   113\u2192\tPool ResourcePool `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=pool\"`\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192\t// NodeName identifies the node which provides the resources in this pool.\n   116\u2192\t// A field selector can be used to list only ResourceSlice\n   117\u2192\t// objects belonging to a certain node.\n   118\u2192\t//\n   119\u2192\t// This field can be used to limit access from nodes to ResourceSlices with\n   120\u2192\t// the same node name. It also indicates to autoscalers that adding\n   121\u2192\t// new nodes of the same type as some old node might also make new\n   122\u2192\t// resources available.\n   123\u2192\t//\n   124\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   125\u2192\t// This field is immutable.\n   126\u2192\t//\n   127\u2192\t// +optional\n   128\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   129\u2192\tNodeName string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\t// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,\n   132\u2192\t// when that pool is not limited to a single node.\n   133\u2192\t//\n   134\u2192\t// Must use exactly one term.\n   135\u2192\t//\n   136\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   137\u2192\t//\n   138\u2192\t// +optional\n   139\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   140\u2192\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\t// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the resources in the pool.\n   143\u2192\t//\n   144\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   145\u2192\t//\n   146\u2192\t// +optional\n   147\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   148\u2192\tAllNodes bool `json:\"allNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=allNodes\"`\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192\t// Devices lists some or all of the devices in this pool.\n   151\u2192\t//\n   152\u2192\t// Must not have more than 128 entries.\n   153\u2192\t//\n   154\u2192\t// +optional\n   155\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   156\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   157\u2192\tDevices []Device `json:\"devices\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,name=devices\"`\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192\t// PerDeviceNodeSelection defines whether the access from nodes to\n   160\u2192\t// resources in the pool is set on the ResourceSlice level or on each\n   161\u2192\t// device. If it is set to true, every device defined the ResourceSlice\n   162\u2192\t// must specify this individually.\n   163\u2192\t//\n   164\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   165\u2192\t//\n   166\u2192\t// +optional\n   167\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   168\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   169\u2192\tPerDeviceNodeSelection *bool `json:\"perDeviceNodeSelection,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,name=perDeviceNodeSelection\"`\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\t// SharedCounters defines a list of counter sets, each of which\n   172\u2192\t// has a name and a list of counters available.\n   173\u2192\t//\n   174\u2192\t// The names of the SharedCounters must be unique in the ResourceSlice.\n   175\u2192\t//\n   176\u2192\t// The maximum number of SharedCounters is 32.\n   177\u2192\t//\n   178\u2192\t// +optional\n   179\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   180\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   181\u2192\tSharedCounters []CounterSet `json:\"sharedCounters,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,name=sharedCounters\"`\n   182\u2192}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192// CounterSet defines a named set of counters\n   185\u2192// that are available to be used by devices defined in the\n   186\u2192// ResourceSlice.\n   187\u2192//\n   188\u2192// The counters are not allocatable by themselves, but\n   189\u2192// can be referenced by devices. When a device is allocated,\n   190\u2192// the portion of counters it uses will no longer be available for use\n   191\u2192// by other devices.\n   192\u2192type CounterSet struct {\n   193\u2192\t// Name defines the name of the counter set.\n   194\u2192\t// It must be a DNS label.\n   195\u2192\t//\n   196\u2192\t// +required\n   197\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192\t// Counters defines the set of counters for this CounterSet\n   200\u2192\t// The name of each counter must be unique in that set and must be a DNS label.\n   201\u2192\t//\n   202\u2192\t// The maximum number of counters is 32.\n   203\u2192\t//\n   204\u2192\t// +required\n   205\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n   206\u2192\t// +k8s:eachKey=+k8s:format=k8s-short-name\n   207\u2192\tCounters map[string]Counter `json:\"counters,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=counters\"`\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192// Counter describes a quantity associated with a device.\n   211\u2192type Counter struct {\n   212\u2192\t// Value defines how much of a certain device counter is available.\n   213\u2192\t//\n   214\u2192\t// +required\n   215\u2192\tValue resource.Quantity `json:\"value\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=value\"`\n   216\u2192}\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192// DriverNameMaxLength is the maximum valid length of a driver name in the\n   219\u2192// ResourceSliceSpec and other places. It's the same as for CSI driver names.\n   220\u2192const DriverNameMaxLength = 63\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192// ResourcePool describes the pool that ResourceSlices belong to.\n   223\u2192type ResourcePool struct {\n   224\u2192\t// Name is used to identify the pool. For node-local devices, this\n   225\u2192\t// is often the node name, but this is not required.\n   226\u2192\t//\n   227\u2192\t// It must not be longer than 253 characters and must consist of one or more DNS sub-domains\n   228\u2192\t// separated by slashes. This field is immutable.\n   229\u2192\t//\n   230\u2192\t// +required\n   231\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192\t// Generation tracks the change in a pool over time. Whenever a driver\n   234\u2192\t// changes something about one or more of the resources in a pool, it\n   235\u2192\t// must change the generation in all ResourceSlices which are part of\n   236\u2192\t// that pool. Consumers of ResourceSlices should only consider\n   237\u2192\t// resources from the pool with the highest generation number. The\n   238\u2192\t// generation may be reset by drivers, which should be fine for\n   239\u2192\t// consumers, assuming that all ResourceSlices in a pool are updated to\n   240\u2192\t// match or deleted.\n   241\u2192\t//\n   242\u2192\t// Combined with ResourceSliceCount, this mechanism enables consumers to\n   243\u2192\t// detect pools which are comprised of multiple ResourceSlices and are\n   244\u2192\t// in an incomplete state.\n   245\u2192\t//\n   246\u2192\t// +required\n   247\u2192\tGeneration int64 `json:\"generation\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=generation\"`\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192\t// ResourceSliceCount is the total number of ResourceSlices in the pool at this\n   250\u2192\t// generation number. Must be greater than zero.\n   251\u2192\t//\n   252\u2192\t// Consumers can use this to check whether they have seen all ResourceSlices\n   253\u2192\t// belonging to the same pool.\n   254\u2192\t//\n   255\u2192\t// +required\n   256\u2192\tResourceSliceCount int64 `json:\"resourceSliceCount\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=resourceSliceCount\"`\n   257\u2192}\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192const ResourceSliceMaxSharedCapacity = 128\n   260\u2192const ResourceSliceMaxDevices = 128\n   261\u2192const PoolNameMaxLength = validation.DNS1123SubdomainMaxLength // Same as for a single node name.\n   262\u2192const BindingConditionsMaxSize = 4\n   263\u2192const BindingFailureConditionsMaxSize = 4\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192// Device represents one individual hardware instance that can be selected based\n   266\u2192// on its attributes. Besides the name, exactly one field must be set.\n   267\u2192type Device struct {\n   268\u2192\t// Name is unique identifier among all devices managed by\n   269\u2192\t// the driver in the pool. It must be a DNS label.\n   270\u2192\t//\n   271\u2192\t// +required\n   272\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192\t// Basic defines one device instance.\n   275\u2192\t//\n   276\u2192\t// +optional\n   277\u2192\t// +oneOf=deviceType\n   278\u2192\tBasic *BasicDevice `json:\"basic,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=basic\"`\n   279\u2192}\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192// BasicDevice defines one device instance.\n   282\u2192type BasicDevice struct {\n   283\u2192\t// Attributes defines the set of attributes for this device.\n   284\u2192\t// The name of each attribute must be unique in that set.\n   285\u2192\t//\n   286\u2192\t// The maximum number of attributes and capacities combined is 32.\n   287\u2192\t//\n   288\u2192\t// +optional\n   289\u2192\tAttributes map[QualifiedName]DeviceAttribute `json:\"attributes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=attributes\"`\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192\t// Capacity defines the set of capacities for this device.\n   292\u2192\t// The name of each capacity must be unique in that set.\n   293\u2192\t//\n   294\u2192\t// The maximum number of attributes and capacities combined is 32.\n   295\u2192\t//\n   296\u2192\t// +optional\n   297\u2192\tCapacity map[QualifiedName]DeviceCapacity `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=capacity\"`\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192\t// ConsumesCounters defines a list of references to sharedCounters\n   300\u2192\t// and the set of counters that the device will\n   301\u2192\t// consume from those counter sets.\n   302\u2192\t//\n   303\u2192\t// There can only be a single entry per counterSet.\n   304\u2192\t//\n   305\u2192\t// The total number of device counter consumption entries\n   306\u2192\t// must be <= 32. In addition, the total number in the\n   307\u2192\t// entire ResourceSlice must be <= 1024 (for example,\n   308\u2192\t// 64 devices with 16 counters each).\n   309\u2192\t//\n   310\u2192\t// +optional\n   311\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   312\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   313\u2192\tConsumesCounters []DeviceCounterConsumption `json:\"consumesCounters,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=consumesCounters\"`\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192\t// NodeName identifies the node where the device is available.\n   316\u2192\t//\n   317\u2192\t// Must only be set if Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection is set to true.\n   318\u2192\t// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.\n   319\u2192\t//\n   320\u2192\t// +optional\n   321\u2192\t// +oneOf=DeviceNodeSelection\n   322\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   323\u2192\tNodeName *string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192\t// NodeSelector defines the nodes where the device is available.\n   326\u2192\t//\n   327\u2192\t// Must use exactly one term.\n   328\u2192\t//\n   329\u2192\t// Must only be set if Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection is set to true.\n   330\u2192\t// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.\n   331\u2192\t//\n   332\u2192\t// +optional\n   333\u2192\t// +oneOf=DeviceNodeSelection\n   334\u2192\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192\t// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the device.\n   337\u2192\t//\n   338\u2192\t// Must only be set if Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection is set to true.\n   339\u2192\t// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.\n   340\u2192\t//\n   341\u2192\t// +optional\n   342\u2192\t// +oneOf=DeviceNodeSelection\n   343\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   344\u2192\tAllNodes *bool `json:\"allNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=allNodes\"`\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192\t// If specified, these are the driver-defined taints.\n   347\u2192\t//\n   348\u2192\t// The maximum number of taints is 4.\n   349\u2192\t//\n   350\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n   351\u2192\t// feature gate.\n   352\u2192\t//\n   353\u2192\t// +optional\n   354\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   355\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n   356\u2192\tTaints []DeviceTaint `json:\"taints,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,rep,name=taints\"`\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192\t// BindsToNode indicates if the usage of an allocation involving this device\n   359\u2192\t// has to be limited to exactly the node that was chosen when allocating the claim.\n   360\u2192\t// If set to true, the scheduler will set the ResourceClaim.Status.Allocation.NodeSelector\n   361\u2192\t// to match the node where the allocation was made.\n   362\u2192\t//\n   363\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n   364\u2192\t// feature gates.\n   365\u2192\t//\n   366\u2192\t// +optional\n   367\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n   368\u2192\tBindsToNode *bool `json:\"bindsToNode,omitempty\" protobuf:\"varint,8,opt,name=bindsToNode\"`\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192\t// BindingConditions defines the conditions for proceeding with binding.\n   371\u2192\t// All of these conditions must be set in the per-device status\n   372\u2192\t// conditions with a value of True to proceed with binding the pod to the node\n   373\u2192\t// while scheduling the pod.\n   374\u2192\t//\n   375\u2192\t//
