The maximum number of binding conditions is 4.\n   376\u2192\t//\n   377\u2192\t// The conditions must be a valid condition type string.\n   378\u2192\t//\n   379\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n   380\u2192\t// feature gates.\n   381\u2192\t//\n   382\u2192\t// +optional\n   383\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   384\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n   385\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   386\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n   387\u2192\tBindingConditions []string `json:\"bindingConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,9,rep,name=bindingConditions\"`\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192\t// BindingFailureConditions defines the conditions for binding failure.\n   390\u2192\t// They may be set in the per-device status conditions.\n   391\u2192\t// If any is true, a binding failure occurred.\n   392\u2192\t//\n   393\u2192\t// The maximum number of binding failure conditions is 4.\n   394\u2192\t//\n   395\u2192\t// The conditions must be a valid condition type string.\n   396\u2192\t//\n   397\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n   398\u2192\t// feature gates.\n   399\u2192\t//\n   400\u2192\t// +optional\n   401\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   402\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n   403\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   404\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n   405\u2192\tBindingFailureConditions []string `json:\"bindingFailureConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,10,rep,name=bindingFailureConditions\"`\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192\t// AllowMultipleAllocations marks whether the device is allowed to be allocated to multiple DeviceRequests.\n   408\u2192\t//\n   409\u2192\t// If AllowMultipleAllocations is set to true, the device can be allocated more than once,\n   410\u2192\t// and all of its capacity is consumable, regardless of whether the requestPolicy is defined or not.\n   411\u2192\t//\n   412\u2192\t// +optional\n   413\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n   414\u2192\tAllowMultipleAllocations *bool `json:\"allowMultipleAllocations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,11,opt,name=allowMultipleAllocations\"`\n   415\u2192}\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192// DeviceCounterConsumption defines a set of counters that\n   418\u2192// a device will consume from a CounterSet.\n   419\u2192type DeviceCounterConsumption struct {\n   420\u2192\t// CounterSet is the name of the set from which the\n   421\u2192\t// counters defined will be consumed.\n   422\u2192\t//\n   423\u2192\t// +required\n   424\u2192\tCounterSet string `json:\"counterSet\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=counterSet\"`\n   425\u2192\n   426\u2192\t// Counters defines the counters that will be consumed by the device.\n   427\u2192\t//\n   428\u2192\t// The maximum number counters in a device is 32.\n   429\u2192\t// In addition, the maximum number of all counters\n   430\u2192\t// in all devices is 1024 (for example, 64 devices with\n   431\u2192\t// 16 counters each).\n   432\u2192\t//\n   433\u2192\t// +required\n   434\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n   435\u2192\t// +k8s:eachKey=+k8s:format=k8s-short-name\n   436\u2192\tCounters map[string]Counter `json:\"counters,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=counters\"`\n   437\u2192}\n   438\u2192\n   439\u2192// DeviceCapacity describes a quantity associated with a device.\n   440\u2192type DeviceCapacity struct {\n   441\u2192\t// Value defines how much of a certain capacity that device has.\n   442\u2192\t//\n   443\u2192\t// This field reflects the fixed total capacity and does not change.\n   444\u2192\t// The consumed amount is tracked separately by scheduler\n   445\u2192\t// and does not affect this value.\n   446\u2192\t//\n   447\u2192\t// +required\n   448\u2192\tValue resource.Quantity `json:\"value\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=value\"`\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192\t// RequestPolicy defines how this DeviceCapacity must be consumed\n   451\u2192\t// when the device is allowed to be shared by multiple allocations.\n   452\u2192\t//\n   453\u2192\t// The Device must have allowMultipleAllocations set to true in order to set a requestPolicy.\n   454\u2192\t//\n   455\u2192\t// If unset, capacity requests are unconstrained:\n   456\u2192\t// requests can consume any amount of capacity, as long as the total consumed\n   457\u2192\t// across all allocations does not exceed the device's defined capacity.\n   458\u2192\t// If request is also unset, default is the full capacity value.\n   459\u2192\t//\n   460\u2192\t// +optional\n   461\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n   462\u2192\tRequestPolicy *CapacityRequestPolicy `json:\"requestPolicy,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=requestPolicy\"`\n   463\u2192}\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192// CapacityRequestPolicy defines how requests consume device capacity.\n   466\u2192//\n   467\u2192// Must not set more than one ValidRequestValues.\n   468\u2192type CapacityRequestPolicy struct {\n   469\u2192\t// Default specifies how much of this capacity is consumed by a request\n   470\u2192\t// that does not contain an entry for it in DeviceRequest's Capacity.\n   471\u2192\t//\n   472\u2192\t// +optional\n   473\u2192\tDefault *resource.Quantity `json:\"default\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=default\"`\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192\t// ValidValues defines a set of acceptable quantity values in consuming requests.\n   476\u2192\t//\n   477\u2192\t// Must not contain more than 10 entries.\n   478\u2192\t// Must be sorted in ascending order.\n   479\u2192\t//\n   480\u2192\t// If this field is set,\n   481\u2192\t// Default must be defined and it must be included in ValidValues list.\n   482\u2192\t//\n   483\u2192\t// If the requested amount does not match any valid value but smaller than some valid values,\n   484\u2192\t// the scheduler calculates the smallest valid value that is greater than or equal to the request.\n   485\u2192\t// That is: min(ceil(requestedValue) \u2208 validValues), where requestedValue \u2264 max(validValues).\n   486\u2192\t//\n   487\u2192\t// If the requested amount exceeds all valid values, the request violates the policy,\n   488\u2192\t// and this device cannot be allocated.\n   489\u2192\t//\n   490\u2192\t// +optional\n   491\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   492\u2192\t// +oneOf=ValidRequestValues\n   493\u2192\tValidValues []resource.Quantity `json:\"validValues,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=validValues\"`\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192\t// ValidRange defines an acceptable quantity value range in consuming requests.\n   496\u2192\t//\n   497\u2192\t// If this field is set,\n   498\u2192\t// Default must be defined and it must fall within the defined ValidRange.\n   499\u2192\t//\n   500\u2192\t// If the requested amount does not fall within the defined range, the request violates the policy,\n   501\u2192\t// and this device cannot be allocated.\n   502\u2192\t//\n   503\u2192\t// If the request doesn't contain this capacity entry, Default value is used.\n   504\u2192\t//\n   505\u2192\t// +optional\n   506\u2192\t// +oneOf=ValidRequestValues\n   507\u2192\tValidRange *CapacityRequestPolicyRange `json:\"validRange,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=validRange\"`\n   508\u2192}\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192// CapacityRequestPolicyRange defines a valid range for consumable capacity values.\n   511\u2192//\n   512\u2192//   - If the requested amount is less than Min, it is rounded up to the Min value.\n   513\u2192//   - If Step is set and the requested amount is between Min and Max but not aligned with Step,\n   514\u2192//     it will be rounded up to the next value equal to Min + (n * Step).\n   515\u2192//   - If Step is not set, the requested amount is used as-is if it falls within the range Min to Max (if set).\n   516\u2192//   - If the requested or rounded amount exceeds Max (if set), the request does not satisfy the policy,\n   517\u2192//     and the device cannot be allocated.\n   518\u2192type CapacityRequestPolicyRange struct {\n   519\u2192\t// Min specifies the minimum capacity allowed for a consumption request.\n   520\u2192\t//\n   521\u2192\t// Min must be greater than or equal to zero,\n   522\u2192\t// and less than or equal to the capacity value.\n   523\u2192\t// requestPolicy.default must be more than or equal to the minimum.\n   524\u2192\t//\n   525\u2192\t// +required\n   526\u2192\tMin *resource.Quantity `json:\"min,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=min\"`\n   527\u2192\n   528\u2192\t// Max defines the upper limit for capacity that can be requested.\n   529\u2192\t//\n   530\u2192\t// Max must be less than or equal to the capacity value.\n   531\u2192\t// Min and requestPolicy.default must be less than or equal to the maximum.\n   532\u2192\t//\n   533\u2192\t// +optional\n   534\u2192\tMax *resource.Quantity `json:\"max,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=max\"`\n   535\u2192\n   536\u2192\t// Step defines the step size between valid capacity amounts within the range.\n   537\u2192\t//\n   538\u2192\t// Max (if set) and requestPolicy.default must be a multiple of Step.\n   539\u2192\t// Min + Step must be less than or equal to the capacity value.\n   540\u2192\t//\n   541\u2192\t// +optional\n   542\u2192\tStep *resource.Quantity `json:\"step,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=step\"`\n   543\u2192}\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192// Limit for the sum of the number of entries in both attributes and capacity.\n   546\u2192const ResourceSliceMaxAttributesAndCapacitiesPerDevice = 32\n   547\u2192\n   548\u2192// Limit for the total number of counters in each device.\n   549\u2192const ResourceSliceMaxCountersPerDevice = 32\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192// Limit for the total number of counters defined in devices in\n   552\u2192// a ResourceSlice. We want to allow up to 64 devices to specify\n   553\u2192// up to 16 counters, so the limit for the ResourceSlice will be 1024.\n   554\u2192const ResourceSliceMaxDeviceCountersPerSlice = 1024 // 64 * 16\n   555\u2192\n   556\u2192// QualifiedName is the name of a device attribute or capacity.\n   557\u2192//\n   558\u2192// Attributes and capacities are defined either by the owner of the specific\n   559\u2192// driver (usually the vendor) or by some 3rd party (e.g. the Kubernetes\n   560\u2192// project). Because they are sometimes compared across devices, a given name\n   561\u2192// is expected to mean the same thing and have the same type on all devices.\n   562\u2192//\n   563\u2192// Names must be either a C identifier (e.g. \"theName\") or a DNS subdomain\n   564\u2192// followed by a slash (\"/\") followed by a C identifier\n   565\u2192// (e.g. \"dra.example.com/theName\"). Names which do not include the\n   566\u2192// domain prefix are assumed to be part of the driver's domain. Attributes\n   567\u2192// or capacities defined by 3rd parties must include the domain prefix.\n   568\u2192//\n   569\u2192// The maximum length for the DNS subdomain is 63 characters (same as\n   570\u2192// for driver names) and the maximum length of the C identifier\n   571\u2192// is 32.\n   572\u2192type QualifiedName string\n   573\u2192\n   574\u2192// FullyQualifiedName is a QualifiedName where the domain is set.\n   575\u2192// Format validation cannot be added to this type because one of its usages,\n   576\u2192// DistinctAttribute, is validated conditionally. This conditional validation\n   577\u2192// cannot be expressed declaratively.\n   578\u2192type FullyQualifiedName string\n   579\u2192\n   580\u2192// DeviceMaxDomainLength is the maximum length of the domain prefix in a fully-qualified name.\n   581\u2192const DeviceMaxDomainLength = 63\n   582\u2192\n   583\u2192// DeviceMaxIDLength is the maximum length of the identifier in a device attribute or capacity name (`<domain>/<ID>`).\n   584\u2192const DeviceMaxIDLength = 32\n   585\u2192\n   586\u2192// DeviceAttribute must have exactly one field set.\n   587\u2192type DeviceAttribute struct {\n   588\u2192\t// The Go field names below have a Value suffix to avoid a conflict between the\n   589\u2192\t// field \"String\" and the corresponding method. That method is required.\n   590\u2192\t// The Kubernetes API is defined without that suffix to keep it more natural.\n   591\u2192\n   592\u2192\t// IntValue is a number.\n   593\u2192\t//\n   594\u2192\t// +optional\n   595\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   596\u2192\t// +k8s:unionMember\n   597\u2192\tIntValue *int64 `json:\"int,omitempty\" protobuf:\"varint,2,opt,name=int\"`\n   598\u2192\n   599\u2192\t// BoolValue is a true/false value.\n   600\u2192\t//\n   601\u2192\t// +optional\n   602\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   603\u2192\t// +k8s:unionMember\n   604\u2192\tBoolValue *bool `json:\"bool,omitempty\" protobuf:\"varint,3,opt,name=bool\"`\n   605\u2192\n   606\u2192\t// StringValue is a string. Must not be longer than 64 characters.\n   607\u2192\t//\n   608\u2192\t// +optional\n   609\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   610\u2192\t// +k8s:unionMember\n   611\u2192\tStringValue *string `json:\"string,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=string\"`\n   612\u2192\n   613\u2192\t// VersionValue is a semantic version according to semver.org spec 2.0.0.\n   614\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 64 characters.\n   615\u2192\t//\n   616\u2192\t// +optional\n   617\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   618\u2192\t// +k8s:unionMember\n   619\u2192\tVersionValue *string `json:\"version,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=version\"`\n   620\u2192}\n   621\u2192\n   622\u2192// DeviceAttributeMaxValueLength is the maximum length of a string or version attribute value.\n   623\u2192const DeviceAttributeMaxValueLength = 64\n   624\u2192\n   625\u2192// DeviceTaintsMaxLength is the maximum number of taints per device.\n   626\u2192const DeviceTaintsMaxLength = 4\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192// The device this taint is attached to has the \"effect\" on\n   629\u2192// any claim which does not tolerate the taint and, through the claim,\n   630\u2192// to pods using the claim.\n   631\u2192//\n   632\u2192// +protobuf.options.(gogoproto.goproto_stringer)=false\n   633\u2192type DeviceTaint struct {\n   634\u2192\t// The taint key to be applied to a device.\n   635\u2192\t// Must be a label name.\n   636\u2192\t//\n   637\u2192\t// +required\n   638\u2192\tKey string `json:\"key\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=key\"`\n   639\u2192\n   640\u2192\t// The taint value corresponding to the taint key.\n   641\u2192\t// Must be a label value.\n   642\u2192\t//\n   643\u2192\t// +optional\n   644\u2192\tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=value\"`\n   645\u2192\n   646\u2192\t// The effect of the taint on claims that do not tolerate the taint\n   647\u2192\t// and through such claims on the pods using them.\n   648\u2192\t// Valid effects are NoSchedule and NoExecute. PreferNoSchedule as used for\n   649\u2192\t// nodes is not valid here.\n   650\u2192\t//\n   651\u2192\t// +required\n   652\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n   653\u2192\tEffect DeviceTaintEffect `json:\"effect\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=effect,casttype=DeviceTaintEffect\"`\n   654\u2192\n   655\u2192\t// ^^^^\n   656\u2192\t//\n   657\u2192\t// Implementing PreferNoSchedule would depend on a scoring solution for DRA.\n   658\u2192\t// It might get added as part of that.\n   659\u2192\n   660\u2192\t// TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added.\n   661\u2192\t// Added automatically during create or update if not set.\n   662\u2192\t//\n   663\u2192\t// +optional\n   664\u2192\tTimeAdded *metav1.Time `json:\"timeAdded,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=timeAdded\"`\n   665\u2192\n   666\u2192\t// ^^^\n   667\u2192\t//\n   668\u2192\t// This field was defined as \"It is only written for NoExecute taints.\" for node taints.\n   669\u2192\t// But in practice, Kubernetes never did anything with it (no validation, no defaulting,\n   670\u2192\t// ignored during pod eviction in pkg/controller/tainteviction).\n   671\u2192}\n   672\u2192\n   673\u2192// +enum\n   674\u2192// +k8s:enum\n   675\u2192type DeviceTaintEffect string\n   676\u2192\n   677\u2192const (\n   678\u2192\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule which use a tainted device unless they tolerate the taint,\n   679\u2192\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n   680\u2192\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n   681\u2192\tDeviceTaintEffectNoSchedule DeviceTaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the device taint.\n   684\u2192\tDeviceTaintEffectNoExecute DeviceTaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n   685\u2192)\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n   688\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n   689\u2192\n   690\u2192// ResourceSliceList is a collection of ResourceSlices.\n   691\u2192type ResourceSliceList struct {\n   692\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n   693\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n   694\u2192\t// +optional\n   695\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n   696\u2192\n   697\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource ResourceSlices.\n   698\u2192\tItems []ResourceSlice `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n   699\u2192}\n   700\u2192\n   701\u2192// +genclient\n   702\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n   703\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n   704\u2192// +k8s:supportsSubresource=/status\n   705\u2192\n   706\u2192// ResourceClaim describes a request for access to resources in the cluster,\n   707\u2192// for use by workloads. For example, if a workload needs an accelerator device\n   708\u2192// with specific properties, this is how that request is expressed. The status\n   709\u2192// stanza tracks whether this claim has been satisfied and what specific\n   710\u2192// resources have been allocated.\n   711\u2192//\n   712\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n   713\u2192// feature gate.\n   714\u2192type ResourceClaim struct {\n   715\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n   716\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n   717\u2192\t// +optional\n   718\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n   719\u2192\n   720\u2192\t// Spec describes what is being requested and how to configure it.\n   721\u2192\t// The spec is immutable.\n   722\u2192\t// +k8s:immutable\n   723\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n   724\u2192\n   725\u2192\t// Status describes whether the claim is ready to use and what has been allocated.\n   726\u2192\t// +optional\n   727\u2192\tStatus ResourceClaimStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=status\"`\n   728\u2192}\n   729\u2192\n   730\u2192// ResourceClaimSpec defines what is being requested in a ResourceClaim and how to configure it.\n   731\u2192type ResourceClaimSpec struct {\n   732\u2192\t// Devices defines how to request devices.\n   733\u2192\t//\n   734\u2192\t// +optional\n   735\u2192\tDevices DeviceClaim `json:\"devices\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=devices\"`\n   736\u2192\n   737\u2192\t// Controller is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n   738\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n   739\u2192\t// supported.\n   740\u2192\t// Controller string `json:\"controller,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=controller\"`\n   741\u2192}\n   742\u2192\n   743\u2192// DeviceClaim defines how to request devices with a ResourceClaim.\n   744\u2192type DeviceClaim struct {\n   745\u2192\t// Requests represent individual requests for distinct devices which\n   746\u2192\t// must all be satisfied. If empty, nothing needs to be allocated.\n   747\u2192\t//\n   748\u2192\t// +optional\n   749\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   750\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n   751\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=map\n   752\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=name\n   753\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n   754\u2192\tRequests []DeviceRequest `json:\"requests\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=requests\"`\n   755\u2192\n   756\u2192\t// These constraints must be satisfied by the set of devices that get\n   757\u2192\t// allocated for the claim.\n   758\u2192\t//\n   759\u2192\t// +optional\n   760\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   761\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n   762\u2192\tConstraints []DeviceConstraint `json:\"constraints,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=constraints\"`\n   763\u2192\n   764\u2192\t// This field holds configuration for multiple potential drivers which\n   765\u2192\t// could satisfy requests in this claim. It is ignored while allocating\n   766\u2192\t// the claim.\n   767\u2192\t//\n   768\u2192\t// +optional\n   769\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   770\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n   771\u2192\tConfig []DeviceClaimConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=config\"`\n   772\u2192\n   773\u2192\t// Potential future extension, ignored by older schedulers. This is\n   774\u2192\t// fine because scoring allows users to define a preference, without\n   775\u2192\t// making it a hard requirement.\n   776\u2192\t//\n   777\u2192\t// Score *SomeScoringStruct\n   778\u2192}\n   779\u2192\n   780\u2192const (\n   781\u2192\tDeviceRequestsMaxSize    = AllocationResultsMaxSize\n   782\u2192\tDeviceConstraintsMaxSize = 32\n   783\u2192\tDeviceConfigMaxSize      = 32\n   784\u2192)\n   785\u2192\n   786\u2192// DRAAdminNamespaceLabelKey is a label key used to grant administrative access\n   787\u2192// to certain resource.k8s.io API types within a namespace. When this label is\n   788\u2192// set on a namespace with the value \"true\" (case-sensitive), it allows the use\n   789\u2192// of adminAccess: true in any namespaced resource.k8s.io API types. Currently,\n   790\u2192// this permission applies to ResourceClaim and ResourceClaimTemplate objects.\n   791\u2192const (\n   792\u2192\tDRAAdminNamespaceLabelKey = \"resource.kubernetes.io/admin-access\"\n   793\u2192)\n   794\u2192\n   795\u2192// DeviceRequest is a request for devices required for a claim.\n   796\u2192// This is typically a request for a single resource like a device, but can\n   797\u2192// also ask for several identical devices.\n   798\u2192type DeviceRequest struct {\n   799\u2192\t// Name can be used to reference this request in a pod.spec.containers[].resources.claims\n   800\u2192\t// entry and in a constraint of the claim.\n   801\u2192\t//\n   802\u2192\t// Must be a DNS label and unique among all DeviceRequests in a\n   803\u2192\t// ResourceClaim.\n   804\u2192\t//\n   805\u2192\t// +required\n   806\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   807\u2192\n   808\u2192\t// DeviceClassName references a specific DeviceClass, which can define\n   809\u2192\t// additional configuration and selectors to be inherited by this\n   810\u2192\t// request.\n   811\u2192\t//\n   812\u2192\t// A class is required if no subrequests are specified in the\n   813\u2192\t// firstAvailable list and no class can be set if subrequests\n   814\u2192\t// are specified in the firstAvailable list.\n   815\u2192\t// Which classes are available depends on the cluster.\n   816\u2192\t//\n   817\u2192\t// Administrators may use this to restrict which devices may get\n   818\u2192\t// requested by only installing classes with selectors for permitted\n   819\u2192\t// devices. If users are free to request anything without restrictions,\n   820\u2192\t// then administrators can create an empty DeviceClass for users\n   821\u2192\t// to reference.\n   822\u2192\t//\n   823\u2192\t// +optional\n   824\u2192\t// +oneOf=deviceRequestType\n   825\u2192\tDeviceClassName string `json:\"deviceClassName\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceClassName\"`\n   826\u2192\n   827\u2192\t// Selectors define criteria which must be satisfied by a specific\n   828\u2192\t// device in order for that device to be considered for this\n   829\u2192\t// request. All selectors must be satisfied for a device to be\n   830\u2192\t// considered.\n   831\u2192\t//\n   832\u2192\t// This field can only be set when deviceClassName is set and no subrequests\n   833\u2192\t// are specified in the firstAvailable list.\n   834\u2192\t//\n   835\u2192\t// +optional\n   836\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   837\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n   838\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=selectors\"`\n   839\u2192\n   840\u2192\t// AllocationMode and its related fields define how devices are allocated\n   841\u2192\t// to satisfy this request. Supported values are:\n   842\u2192\t//\n   843\u2192\t// - ExactCount: This request is for a specific number of devices.\n   844\u2192\t//   This is the default. The exact number is provided in the\n   845\u2192\t//   count field.\n   846\u2192\t//\n   847\u2192\t// - All: This request is for all of the matching devices in a pool.\n   848\u2192\t//   At least one device must exist on the node for the allocation to succeed.\n   849\u2192\t//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,\n   850\u2192\t//   unless adminAccess is requested.\n   851\u2192\t//\n   852\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is not specified, the default mode is ExactCount. If\n   853\u2192\t// the mode is ExactCount and count is not specified, the default count is\n   854\u2192\t// one. Any other requests must specify this field.\n   855\u2192\t//\n   856\u2192\t// This field can only be set when deviceClassName is set and no subrequests\n   857\u2192\t// are specified in the firstAvailable list.\n   858\u2192\t//\n   859\u2192\t// More modes may get added in the future. Clients must refuse to handle\n   860\u2192\t// requests with unknown modes.\n   861\u2192\t//\n   862\u2192\t// +optional\n   863\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   864\u2192\tAllocationMode DeviceAllocationMode `json:\"allocationMode,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=allocationMode\"`\n   865\u2192\n   866\u2192\t// Count is used only when the count mode is \"ExactCount\". Must be greater than zero.\n   867\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is ExactCount and this field is not specified, the default is one.\n   868\u2192\t//\n   869\u2192\t// This field can only be set when deviceClassName is set and no subrequests\n   870\u2192\t// are specified in the firstAvailable list.\n   871\u2192\t//\n   872\u2192\t// +optional\n   873\u2192\t// +oneOf=AllocationMode\n   874\u2192\tCount int64 `json:\"count,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=count\"`\n   875\u2192\n   876\u2192\t// AdminAccess indicates that this is a claim for administrative access\n   877\u2192\t// to the device(s). Claims with AdminAccess are expected to be used for\n   878\u2192\t// monitoring or other management services for a device.  They ignore\n   879\u2192\t// all ordinary claims to the device with respect to access modes and\n   880\u2192\t// any resource allocations.\n   881\u2192\t//\n   882\u2192\t// This field can only be set when deviceClassName is set and no subrequests\n   883\u2192\t// are specified in the firstAvailable list.\n   884\u2192\t//\n   885\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRAAdminAccess\n   886\u2192\t// feature gate. Admin access is disabled if this field is unset or\n   887\u2192\t// set to false, otherwise it is enabled.\n   888\u2192\t//\n   889\u2192\t// +optional\n   890\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAAdminAccess\n   891\u2192\tAdminAccess *bool `json:\"adminAccess,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=adminAccess\"`\n   892\u2192\n   893\u2192\t// FirstAvailable contains subrequests, of which exactly one will be\n   894\u2192\t// satisfied by the scheduler to satisfy this request. It tries to\n   895\u2192\t// satisfy them in the order in which they are listed here. So if\n   896\u2192\t// there are two entries in the list, the scheduler will only check\n   897\u2192\t// the second one if it determines that the first one cannot be used.\n   898\u2192\t//\n   899\u2192\t// This field may only be set in the entries of DeviceClaim.Requests.\n   900\u2192\t//\n   901\u2192\t// DRA does not yet implement scoring, so the scheduler will\n   902\u2192\t// select the first set of devices that satisfies all the\n   903\u2192\t// requests in the claim. And if the requirements can\n   904\u2192\t// be satisfied on more than one node, other scheduling features\n   905\u2192\t// will determine which node is chosen. This means that the set of\n   906\u2192\t// devices allocated to a claim might not be the optimal set\n   907\u2192\t// available to the cluster. Scoring will be implemented later.\n   908\u2192\t//\n   909\u2192\t// +optional\n   910\u2192\t// +oneOf=deviceRequestType\n   911\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   912\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPrioritizedList\n   913\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n   914\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=map\n   915\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=name\n   916\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=8\n   917\u2192\tFirstAvailable []DeviceSubRequest `json:\"firstAvailable,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,name=firstAvailable\"`\n   918\u2192\n   919\u2192\t// If specified, the request's tolerations.\n   920\u2192\t//\n   921\u2192\t// Tolerations for NoSchedule are required to allocate a\n   922\u2192\t// device which has a taint with that effect. The same applies\n   923\u2192\t// to NoExecute.\n   924\u2192\t//\n   925\u2192\t// In addition, should any of the allocated devices get tainted\n   926\u2192\t// with NoExecute after allocation and that effect is not tolerated,\n   927\u2192\t// then all pods consuming the ResourceClaim get deleted to evict\n   928\u2192\t// them. The scheduler will not let new pods reserve the claim while\n   929\u2192\t// it has these tainted devices. Once all pods are evicted, the\n   930\u2192\t// claim will get deallocated.\n   931\u2192\t//\n   932\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n   933\u2192\t//\n   934\u2192\t// This field can only be set when deviceClassName is set and no subrequests\n   935\u2192\t// are specified in the firstAvailable list.\n   936\u2192\t//\n   937\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n   938\u2192\t// feature gate.\n   939\u2192\t//\n   940\u2192\t// +optional\n   941\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   942\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n   943\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n   944\u2192\n   945\u2192\t// Capacity define resource requirements against each capacity.\n   946\u2192\t//\n   947\u2192\t// If this field is unset and the device supports multiple allocations,\n   948\u2192\t// the default value will be applied to each capacity according to requestPolicy.\n   949\u2192\t// For the capacity that has no requestPolicy, default is the full capacity value.\n   950\u2192\t//\n   951\u2192\t// Applies to each device allocation.\n   952\u2192\t// If Count > 1,\n   953\u2192\t// the request fails if there aren't enough devices that meet the requirements.\n   954\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is set to All,\n   955\u2192\t// the request fails if there are devices that otherwise match the request,\n   956\u2192\t// and have this capacity, with a value >= the requested amount, but which cannot be allocated to this request.\n   957\u2192\t//\n   958\u2192\t// +optional\n   959\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n   960\u2192\tCapacity *CapacityRequirements `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,9,opt,name=capacity\"`\n   961\u2192}\n   962\u2192\n   963\u2192// DeviceSubRequest describes a request for device provided in the\n   964\u2192// claim.spec.devices.requests[].firstAvailable array. Each\n   965\u2192// is typically a request for a single resource like a device, but can\n   966\u2192// also ask for several identical devices.\n   967\u2192//\n   968\u2192// DeviceSubRequest is similar to Request, but doesn't expose the AdminAccess\n   969\u2192// or FirstAvailable fields, as those can only be set on the top-level request.\n   970\u2192// AdminAccess is not supported for requests with a prioritized list, and\n   971\u2192// recursive FirstAvailable fields are not supported.\n   972\u2192type DeviceSubRequest struct {\n   973\u2192\t// Name can be used to reference this subrequest in the list of constraints\n   974\u2192\t// or the list of configurations for the claim. References must use the\n   975\u2192\t// format <main request>/<subrequest>.\n   976\u2192\t//\n   977\u2192\t// Must be a DNS label.\n   978\u2192\t//\n   979\u2192\t// +required\n   980\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   981\u2192\n   982\u2192\t// DeviceClassName references a specific DeviceClass, which can define\n   983\u2192\t// additional configuration and selectors to be inherited by this\n   984\u2192\t// subrequest.\n   985\u2192\t//\n   986\u2192\t// A class is required. Which classes are available depends on the cluster.\n   987\u2192\t//\n   988\u2192\t// Administrators may use this to restrict which devices may get\n   989\u2192\t// requested by only installing classes with selectors for permitted\n   990\u2192\t// devices. If users are free to request anything without restrictions,\n   991\u2192\t// then administrators can create an empty DeviceClass for users\n   992\u2192\t// to reference.\n   993\u2192\t//\n   994\u2192\t// +required\n   995\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n   996\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n   997\u2192\tDeviceClassName string `json:\"deviceClassName\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceClassName\"`\n   998\u2192\n   999\u2192\t// Selectors define criteria which must be satisfied by a specific\n  1000\u2192\t// device in order for that device to be considered for this\n  1001\u2192\t// subrequest. All selectors must be satisfied for a device to be\n  1002\u2192\t// considered.\n  1003\u2192\t//\n  1004\u2192\t// +optional\n  1005\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1006\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1007\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=selectors\"`\n  1008\u2192\n  1009\u2192\t// AllocationMode and its related fields define how devices are allocated\n  1010\u2192\t// to satisfy this subrequest. Supported values are:\n  1011\u2192\t//\n  1012\u2192\t// - ExactCount: This request is for a specific number of devices.\n  1013\u2192\t//   This is the default. The exact number is provided in the\n  1014\u2192\t//   count field.\n  1015\u2192\t//\n  1016\u2192\t// - All: This subrequest is for all of the matching devices in a pool.\n  1017\u2192\t//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,\n  1018\u2192\t//   unless adminAccess is requested.\n  1019\u2192\t//\n  1020\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is not specified, the default mode is ExactCount. If\n  1021\u2192\t// the mode is ExactCount and count is not specified, the default count is\n  1022\u2192\t// one. Any other subrequests must specify this field.\n  1023\u2192\t//\n  1024\u2192\t// More modes may get added in the future. Clients must refuse to handle\n  1025\u2192\t// requests with unknown modes.\n  1026\u2192\t//\n  1027\u2192\t// +optional\n  1028\u2192\tAllocationMode DeviceAllocationMode `json:\"allocationMode,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=allocationMode\"`\n  1029\u2192\n  1030\u2192\t// Count is used only when the count mode is \"ExactCount\". Must be greater than zero.\n  1031\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is ExactCount and this field is not specified, the default is one.\n  1032\u2192\t//\n  1033\u2192\t// +optional\n  1034\u2192\t// +oneOf=AllocationMode\n  1035\u2192\tCount int64 `json:\"count,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=count\"`\n  1036\u2192\n  1037\u2192\t// If specified, the request's tolerations.\n  1038\u2192\t//\n  1039\u2192\t// Tolerations for NoSchedule are required to allocate a\n  1040\u2192\t// device which has a taint with that effect. The same applies\n  1041\u2192\t// to NoExecute.\n  1042\u2192\t//\n  1043\u2192\t// In addition, should any of the allocated devices get tainted\n  1044\u2192\t// with NoExecute after allocation and that effect is not tolerated,\n  1045\u2192\t// then all pods consuming the ResourceClaim get deleted to evict\n  1046\u2192\t// them. The scheduler will not let new pods reserve the claim while\n  1047\u2192\t// it has these tainted devices. Once all pods are evicted, the\n  1048\u2192\t// claim will get deallocated.\n  1049\u2192\t//\n  1050\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n  1051\u2192\t//\n  1052\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n  1053\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1054\u2192\t//\n  1055\u2192\t// +optional\n  1056\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1057\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n  1058\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n  1059\u2192\n  1060\u2192\t// Capacity define resource requirements against each capacity.\n  1061\u2192\t//\n  1062\u2192\t// If this field is unset and the device supports multiple allocations,\n  1063\u2192\t// the default value will be applied to each capacity according to requestPolicy.\n  1064\u2192\t// For the capacity that has no requestPolicy, default is the full capacity value.\n  1065\u2192\t//\n  1066\u2192\t// Applies to each device allocation.\n  1067\u2192\t// If Count > 1,\n  1068\u2192\t// the request fails if there aren't enough devices that meet the requirements.\n  1069\u2192\t// If AllocationMode is set to All,\n  1070\u2192\t// the request fails if there are devices that otherwise match the request,\n  1071\u2192\t// and have this capacity, with a value >= the requested amount, but which cannot be allocated to this request.\n  1072\u2192\t//\n  1073\u2192\t// +optional\n  1074\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1075\u2192\tCapacity *CapacityRequirements `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,opt,name=capacity\"`\n  1076\u2192}\n  1077\u2192\n  1078\u2192// CapacityRequirements defines the capacity requirements for a specific device request.\n  1079\u2192type CapacityRequirements struct {\n  1080\u2192\t// Requests represent individual device resource requests for distinct resources,\n  1081\u2192\t// all of which must be provided by the device.\n  1082\u2192\t//\n  1083\u2192\t// This value is used as an additional filtering condition against the available capacity on the device.\n  1084\u2192\t// This is semantically equivalent to a CEL selector with\n  1085\u2192\t// `device.capacity[<domain>].<name>.compareTo(quantity(<request quantity>)) >= 0`.\n  1086\u2192\t// For example, device.capacity['test-driver.cdi.k8s.io'].counters.compareTo(quantity('2')) >= 0.\n  1087\u2192\t//\n  1088\u2192\t// When a requestPolicy is defined, the requested amount is adjusted upward\n  1089\u2192\t// to the nearest valid value based on the policy.\n  1090\u2192\t// If the requested amount cannot be adjusted to a valid value\u2014because it exceeds what the requestPolicy allows\u2014\n  1091\u2192\t// the device is considered ineligible for allocation.\n  1092\u2192\t//\n  1093\u2192\t// For any capacity that is not explicitly requested:\n  1094\u2192\t// - If no requestPolicy is set, the default consumed capacity is equal to the full device capacity\n  1095\u2192\t//   (i.e., the whole device is claimed).\n  1096\u2192\t// - If a requestPolicy is set, the default consumed capacity is determined according to that policy.\n  1097\u2192\t//\n  1098\u2192\t// If the device allows multiple allocation,\n  1099\u2192\t// the aggregated amount across all requests must not exceed the capacity value.\n  1100\u2192\t// The consumed capacity, which may be adjusted based on the requestPolicy if defined,\n  1101\u2192\t// is recorded in the resource claim\u2019s status.devices[*].consumedCapacity field.\n  1102\u2192\t//\n  1103\u2192\t// +optional\n  1104\u2192\tRequests map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=requests,castkey=QualifiedName\"`\n  1105\u2192}\n  1106\u2192\n  1107\u2192const (\n  1108\u2192\tDeviceSelectorsMaxSize             = 32\n  1109\u2192\tFirstAvailableDeviceRequestMaxSize = 8\n  1110\u2192\tDeviceTolerationsMaxLength         = 16\n  1111\u2192)\n  1112\u2192\n  1113\u2192// +enum\n  1114\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1115\u2192type DeviceAllocationMode string\n  1116\u2192\n  1117\u2192// Valid [DeviceRequest.CountMode] values.\n  1118\u2192const (\n  1119\u2192\tDeviceAllocationModeExactCount = DeviceAllocationMode(\"ExactCount\")\n  1120\u2192\tDeviceAllocationModeAll        = DeviceAllocationMode(\"All\")\n  1121\u2192)\n  1122\u2192\n  1123\u2192// DeviceSelector must have exactly one field set.\n  1124\u2192type DeviceSelector struct {\n  1125\u2192\t// CEL contains a CEL expression for selecting a device.\n  1126\u2192\t//\n  1127\u2192\t// +optional\n  1128\u2192\t// +oneOf=SelectorType\n  1129\u2192\tCEL *CELDeviceSelector `json:\"cel,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=cel\"`\n  1130\u2192}\n  1131\u2192\n  1132\u2192// CELDeviceSelector contains a CEL expression for selecting a device.\n  1133\u2192type CELDeviceSelector struct {\n  1134\u2192\t// Expression is a CEL expression which evaluates a single device. It\n  1135\u2192\t// must evaluate to true when the device under consideration satisfies\n  1136\u2192\t// the desired criteria, and false when it does not. Any other result\n  1137\u2192\t// is an error and causes allocation of devices to abort.\n  1138\u2192\t//\n  1139\u2192\t// The expression's input is an object named \"device\", which carries\n  1140\u2192\t// the following properties:\n  1141\u2192\t//  - driver (string): the name of the driver which defines this device.\n  1142\u2192\t//  - attributes (map[string]object): the device's attributes, grouped by prefix\n  1143\u2192\t//    (e.g. device.attributes[\"dra.example.com\"] evaluates to an object with all\n  1144\u2192\t//    of the attributes which were prefixed by \"dra.example.com\".\n  1145\u2192\t//  - capacity (map[string]object): the device's capacities, grouped by prefix.\n  1146\u2192\t//  - allowMultipleAllocations (bool): the allowMultipleAllocations property of the device\n  1147\u2192\t//    (v1.34+ with the DRAConsumableCapacity feature enabled).\n  1148\u2192\t//\n  1149\u2192\t// Example: Consider a device with driver=\"dra.example.com\", which exposes\n  1150\u2192\t// two attributes named \"model\" and \"ext.example.com/family\" and which\n  1151\u2192\t// exposes one capacity named \"modules\". This input to this expression\n  1152\u2192\t// would have the following fields:\n  1153\u2192\t//\n  1154\u2192\t//     device.driver\n  1155\u2192\t//     device.attributes[\"dra.example.com\"].model\n  1156\u2192\t//     device.attributes[\"ext.example.com\"].family\n  1157\u2192\t//     device.capacity[\"dra.example.com\"].modules\n  1158\u2192\t//\n  1159\u2192\t// The device.driver field can be used to check for a specific driver,\n  1160\u2192\t// either as a high-level precondition (i.e. you only want to consider\n  1161\u2192\t// devices from this driver) or as part of a multi-clause expression\n  1162\u2192\t// that is meant to consider devices from different drivers.\n  1163\u2192\t//\n  1164\u2192\t// The value type of each attribute is defined by the device\n  1165\u2192\t// definition, and users who write these expressions must consult the\n  1166\u2192\t// documentation for their specific drivers. The value type of each\n  1167\u2192\t// capacity is Quantity.\n  1168\u2192\t//\n  1169\u2192\t// If an unknown prefix is used as a lookup in either device.attributes\n  1170\u2192\t// or device.capacity, an empty map will be returned. Any reference to\n  1171\u2192\t// an unknown field will cause an evaluation error and allocation to\n  1172\u2192\t// abort.\n  1173\u2192\t//\n  1174\u2192\t// A robust expression should check for the existence of attributes\n  1175\u2192\t// before referencing them.\n  1176\u2192\t//\n  1177\u2192\t// For ease of use, the cel.bind() function is enabled, and can be used\n  1178\u2192\t// to simplify expressions that access multiple attributes with the\n  1179\u2192\t// same domain. For example:\n  1180\u2192\t//\n  1181\u2192\t//     cel.bind(dra, device.attributes[\"dra.example.com\"], dra.someBool && dra.anotherBool)\n  1182\u2192\t//\n  1183\u2192\t// The length of the expression must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki. The\n  1184\u2192\t// cost of evaluating it is also limited based on the estimated number\n  1185\u2192\t// of logical steps.\n  1186\u2192\t//\n  1187\u2192\t// +required\n  1188\u2192\tExpression string `json:\"expression\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=expression\"`\n  1189\u2192}\n  1190\u2192\n  1191\u2192// CELSelectorExpressionMaxCost specifies the cost limit for a single CEL selector\n  1192\u2192// evaluation.\n  1193\u2192//\n  1194\u2192// There is no overall budget for selecting a device, so the actual time\n  1195\u2192// required for that is proportional to the number of CEL selectors and how\n  1196\u2192// often they need to be evaluated, which can vary depending on several factors\n  1197\u2192// (number of devices, cluster utilization, additional constraints).\n  1198\u2192//\n  1199\u2192// Validation against this limit and [CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength] happens\n  1200\u2192// only when setting an expression for the first time or when changing it. If\n  1201\u2192// the limits are changed in a future Kubernetes release, existing users are\n  1202\u2192// guaranteed that existing expressions will continue to be valid.\n  1203\u2192//\n  1204\u2192// However, the kube-scheduler also applies this cost limit at runtime, so it\n  1205\u2192// could happen that a valid expression fails at runtime after an up- or\n  1206\u2192// downgrade. This can also happen without version skew when the cost estimate\n  1207\u2192// underestimated the actual cost. That this might happen is the reason why\n  1208\u2192// kube-scheduler enforces the runtime limit instead of relying on validation.\n  1209\u2192//\n  1210\u2192// According to\n  1211\u2192// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/4aeaf1e99e82da8334c0d6dddd848a194cd44b4f/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/cel/config.go#L20-L22,\n  1212\u2192// this gives roughly 0.1 second for each expression evaluation.\n  1213\u2192// However, this depends on how fast the machine is.\n  1214\u2192const CELSelectorExpressionMaxCost = 1000000\n  1215\u2192\n  1216\u2192// CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength is the maximum length of a CEL selector expression string.\n  1217\u2192const CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength = 10 * 1024\n  1218\u2192\n  1219\u2192// DeviceConstraint must have exactly one field set besides Requests.\n  1220\u2192type DeviceConstraint struct {\n  1221\u2192\t// Requests is a list of the one or more requests in this claim which\n  1222\u2192\t// must co-satisfy this constraint. If a request is fulfilled by\n  1223\u2192\t// multiple devices, then all of the devices must satisfy the\n  1224\u2192\t// constraint. If this is not specified, this constraint applies to all\n  1225\u2192\t// requests in this claim.\n  1226\u2192\t//\n  1227\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1228\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1229\u2192\t// the main request is given, the constraint applies to all subrequests.\n  1230\u2192\t//\n  1231\u2192\t// +optional\n  1232\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1233\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n  1234\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=set\n  1235\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1236\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1237\u2192\n  1238\u2192\t// MatchAttribute requires that all devices in question have this\n  1239\u2192\t// attribute and that its type and value are the same across those\n  1240\u2192\t// devices.\n  1241\u2192\t//\n  1242\u2192\t// For example, if you specified \"dra.example.com/numa\" (a hypothetical example!),\n  1243\u2192\t// then only devices in the same NUMA node will be chosen. A device which\n  1244\u2192\t// does not have that attribute will not be chosen. All devices should\n  1245\u2192\t// use a value of the same type for this attribute because that is part of\n  1246\u2192\t// its specification, but if one device doesn't, then it also will not be\n  1247\u2192\t// chosen.\n  1248\u2192\t//\n  1249\u2192\t// Must include the domain qualifier.\n  1250\u2192\t//\n  1251\u2192\t// +optional\n  1252\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConstraintType\n  1253\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1254\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-resource-fully-qualified-name\n  1255\u2192\tMatchAttribute *FullyQualifiedName `json:\"matchAttribute,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=matchAttribute\"`\n  1256\u2192\n  1257\u2192\t// Potential future extension, not part of the current design:\n  1258\u2192\t// A CEL expression which compares different devices and returns\n  1259\u2192\t// true if they match.\n  1260\u2192\t//\n  1261\u2192\t// Because it would be part of a one-of, old schedulers will not\n  1262\u2192\t// accidentally ignore this additional, for them unknown match\n  1263\u2192\t// criteria.\n  1264\u2192\t//\n  1265\u2192\t// MatchExpression string\n  1266\u2192\n  1267\u2192\t// DistinctAttribute requires that all devices in question have this\n  1268\u2192\t// attribute and that its type and value are unique across those devices.\n  1269\u2192\t//\n  1270\u2192\t// This acts as the inverse of MatchAttribute.\n  1271\u2192\t//\n  1272\u2192\t// This constraint is used to avoid allocating multiple requests to the same device\n  1273\u2192\t// by ensuring attribute-level differentiation.\n  1274\u2192\t//\n  1275\u2192\t// This is useful for scenarios where resource requests must be fulfilled by separate physical devices.\n  1276\u2192\t// For example, a container requests two network interfaces that must be allocated from two different physical NICs.\n  1277\u2192\t//\n  1278\u2192\t// +optional\n  1279\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConstraintType\n  1280\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1281\u2192\tDistinctAttribute *FullyQualifiedName `json:\"distinctAttribute,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=distinctAttribute\"`\n  1282\u2192}\n  1283\u2192\n  1284\u2192// DeviceClaimConfiguration is used for configuration parameters in DeviceClaim.\n  1285\u2192type DeviceClaimConfiguration struct {\n  1286\u2192\t// Requests lists the names of requests where the configuration applies.\n  1287\u2192\t// If empty, it applies to all requests.\n  1288\u2192\t//\n  1289\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1290\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1291\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1292\u2192\t//\n  1293\u2192\t// +optional\n  1294\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1295\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n  1296\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=set\n  1297\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1298\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1299\u2192\n  1300\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1301\u2192}\n  1302\u2192\n  1303\u2192// DeviceConfiguration must have exactly one field set. It gets embedded\n  1304\u2192// inline in some other structs which have other fields, so field names must\n  1305\u2192// not conflict with those.\n  1306\u2192type DeviceConfiguration struct {\n  1307\u2192\t// Opaque provides driver-specific configuration parameters.\n  1308\u2192\t//\n  1309\u2192\t// +optional\n  1310\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConfigurationType\n  1311\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1312\u2192\tOpaque *OpaqueDeviceConfiguration `json:\"opaque,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=opaque\"`\n  1313\u2192}\n  1314\u2192\n  1315\u2192// OpaqueDeviceConfiguration contains configuration parameters for a driver\n  1316\u2192// in a format defined by the driver vendor.\n  1317\u2192type OpaqueDeviceConfiguration struct {\n  1318\u2192\t// Driver is used to determine which kubelet plugin needs\n  1319\u2192\t// to be passed these configuration parameters.\n  1320\u2192\t//\n  1321\u2192\t// An admission policy provided by the driver developer could use this\n  1322\u2192\t// to decide whether it needs to validate them.\n  1323\u2192\t//\n  1324\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1325\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1326\u2192\t//\n  1327\u2192\t// +required\n  1328\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1329\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name-caseless\n  1330\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=driver\"`\n  1331\u2192\n  1332\u2192\t// Parameters can contain arbitrary data. It is the responsibility of\n  1333\u2192\t// the driver developer to handle validation and versioning. Typically this\n  1334\u2192\t// includes self-identification and a version (\"kind\" + \"apiVersion\" for\n  1335\u2192\t// Kubernetes types), with conversion between different versions.\n  1336\u2192\t//\n  1337\u2192\t// The length of the raw data must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki.\n  1338\u2192\t//\n  1339\u2192\t// +required\n  1340\u2192\tParameters runtime.RawExtension `json:\"parameters\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=parameters\"`\n  1341\u2192}\n  1342\u2192\n  1343\u2192// OpaqueParametersMaxLength is the maximum length of the raw data in an\n  1344\u2192// [OpaqueDeviceConfiguration.Parameters] field.\n  1345\u2192const OpaqueParametersMaxLength = 10 * 1024\n  1346\u2192\n  1347\u2192// The ResourceClaim this DeviceToleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n  1348\u2192// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n  1349\u2192type DeviceToleration struct {\n  1350\u2192\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n  1351\u2192\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n  1352\u2192\t// Must be a label name.\n  1353\u2192\t//\n  1354\u2192\t// +optional\n  1355\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1356\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-label-key\n  1357\u2192\tKey string `json:\"key,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\"`\n  1358\u2192\n  1359\u2192\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n  1360\u2192\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n  1361\u2192\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a ResourceClaim can\n  1362\u2192\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n  1363\u2192\t//\n  1364\u2192\t// +optional\n  1365\u2192\t// +default=\"Equal\"\n  1366\u2192\tOperator DeviceTolerationOperator `json:\"operator,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=DeviceTolerationOperator\"`\n  1367\u2192\n  1368\u2192\t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\n  1369\u2192\t// If the operator is Exists, the value must be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\n  1370\u2192\t// Must be a label value.\n  1371\u2192\t//\n  1372\u2192\t// +optional\n  1373\u2192\tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\"`\n  1374\u2192\n  1375\u2192\t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\n  1376\u2192\t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule and NoExecute.\n  1377\u2192\t//\n  1378\u2192\t// +optional\n  1379\u2192\tEffect DeviceTaintEffect `json:\"effect,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=DeviceTaintEffect\"`\n  1380\u2192\n  1381\u2192\t// TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be\n  1382\u2192\t// of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default,\n  1383\u2192\t// it is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and\n  1384\u2192\t// negative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.\n  1385\u2192\t// If larger than zero, the time when the pod needs to be evicted is calculated as <time when\n  1386\u2192\t// taint was adedd> + <toleration seconds>.\n  1387\u2192\t//\n  1388\u2192\t// +optional\n  1389\u2192\tTolerationSeconds *int64 `json:\"tolerationSeconds,omitempty\" protobuf:\"varint,5,opt,name=tolerationSeconds\"`\n  1390\u2192}\n  1391\u2192\n  1392\u2192// A toleration operator is the set of operators that can be used in a toleration.\n  1393\u2192//\n  1394\u2192// +enum\n  1395\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1396\u2192type DeviceTolerationOperator string\n  1397\u2192\n  1398\u2192const (\n  1399\u2192\tDeviceTolerationOpExists DeviceTolerationOperator = \"Exists\"\n  1400\u2192\tDeviceTolerationOpEqual  DeviceTolerationOperator = \"Equal\"\n  1401\u2192)\n  1402\u2192\n  1403\u2192// ResourceClaimStatus tracks whether the resource has been allocated and what\n  1404\u2192// the result of that was.\n  1405\u2192type ResourceClaimStatus struct {\n  1406\u2192\t// Allocation is set once the claim has been allocated successfully.\n  1407\u2192\t//\n  1408\u2192\t// +optional\n  1409\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1410\u2192\t// +k8s:update=NoModify\n  1411\u2192\tAllocation *AllocationResult `json:\"allocation,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=allocation\"`\n  1412\u2192\n  1413\u2192\t// ReservedFor indicates which entities are currently allowed to use\n  1414\u2192\t// the claim. A Pod which references a ResourceClaim which is not\n  1415\u2192\t// reserved for that Pod will not be started. A claim that is in\n  1416\u2192\t// use or might be in use because it has been reserved must not get\n  1417\u2192\t// deallocated.\n  1418\u2192\t//\n  1419\u2192\t// In a cluster with multiple scheduler instances, two pods might get\n  1420\u2192\t// scheduled concurrently by different schedulers. When they reference\n  1421\u2192\t// the same ResourceClaim which already has reached its maximum number\n  1422\u2192\t// of consumers, only one pod can be scheduled.\n  1423\u2192\t//\n  1424\u2192\t// Both schedulers try to add their pod to the claim.status.reservedFor\n  1425\u2192\t// field, but only the update that reaches the API server first gets\n  1426\u2192\t// stored. The other one fails with an error and the scheduler\n  1427\u2192\t// which issued it knows that it must put the pod back into the queue,\n  1428\u2192\t// waiting for the ResourceClaim to become usable again.\n  1429\u2192\t//\n  1430\u2192\t// There can be at most 256 such reservations. This may get increased in\n  1431\u2192\t// the future, but not reduced.\n  1432\u2192\t//\n  1433\u2192\t// +optional\n  1434\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1435\u2192\t// +listMapKey=uid\n  1436\u2192\t// +patchStrategy=merge\n  1437\u2192\t// +patchMergeKey=uid\n  1438\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1439\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=map\n  1440\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=uid\n  1441\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=256\n  1442\u2192\tReservedFor []ResourceClaimConsumerReference `json:\"reservedFor,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=reservedFor\" patchStrategy:\"merge\" patchMergeKey:\"uid\"`\n  1443\u2192\n  1444\u2192\t// DeallocationRequested is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1445\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1446\u2192\t// supported.\n  1447\u2192\t// DeallocationRequested bool `json:\"deallocationRequested,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=deallocationRequested\"`\n  1448\u2192\n  1449\u2192\t// Devices contains the status of each device allocated for this\n  1450\u2192\t// claim, as reported by the driver. This can include driver-specific\n  1451\u2192\t// information. Entries are owned by their respective drivers.\n  1452\u2192\t//\n  1453\u2192\t// +optional\n  1454\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1455\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1456\u2192\t// +listMapKey=driver\n  1457\u2192\t// +listMapKey=device\n  1458\u2192\t// +listMapKey=pool\n  1459\u2192\t// +listMapKey=shareID\n  1460\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1461\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=map\n  1462\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=driver\n  1463\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=device\n  1464\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=pool\n  1465\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=shareID\n  1466\u2192\tDevices []AllocatedDeviceStatus `json:\"devices,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=devices\"`\n  1467\u2192}\n  1468\u2192\n  1469\u2192// ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize is the maximum number of entries in\n  1470\u2192// claim.status.reservedFor.\n  1471\u2192const ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize = 256\n  1472\u2192\n  1473\u2192// ResourceClaimConsumerReference contains enough information to let you\n  1474\u2192// locate the consumer of a ResourceClaim. The user must be a resource in the same\n  1475\u2192// namespace as the ResourceClaim.\n  1476\u2192type ResourceClaimConsumerReference struct {\n  1477\u2192\t// APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced. It is\n  1478\u2192\t// empty for the core API. This matches the group in the APIVersion\n  1479\u2192\t// that is used when creating the resources.\n  1480\u2192\t// +optional\n  1481\u2192\tAPIGroup string `json:\"apiGroup,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=apiGroup\"`\n  1482\u2192\t// Resource is the type of resource being referenced, for example \"pods\".\n  1483\u2192\t// +required\n  1484\u2192\tResource string `json:\"resource\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=resource\"`\n  1485\u2192\t// Name is the name of resource being referenced.\n  1486\u2192\t// +required\n  1487\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=name\"`\n  1488\u2192\t// UID identifies exactly one incarnation of the resource.\n  1489\u2192\t// +required\n  1490\u2192\tUID types.UID `json:\"uid\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,name=uid\"`\n  1491\u2192}\n  1492\u2192\n  1493\u2192// AllocationResult contains attributes of an allocated resource.\n  1494\u2192type AllocationResult struct {\n  1495\u2192\t// Devices is the result of allocating devices.\n  1496\u2192\t//\n  1497\u2192\t// +optional\n  1498\u2192\tDevices DeviceAllocationResult `json:\"devices,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=devices\"`\n  1499\u2192\n  1500\u2192\t// NodeSelector defines where the allocated resources are available. If\n  1501\u2192\t// unset, they are available everywhere.\n  1502\u2192\t//\n  1503\u2192\t// +optional\n  1504\u2192\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n  1505\u2192\n  1506\u2192\t// Controller is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1507\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1508\u2192\t// supported.\n  1509\u2192\t// Controller string `json:\"controller,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=controller\"`\n  1510\u2192\n  1511\u2192\t// AllocationTimestamp stores the time when the resources were allocated.\n  1512\u2192\t// This field is not guaranteed to be set, in which case that time is unknown.\n  1513\u2192\t//\n  1514\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1515\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1516\u2192\t//\n  1517\u2192\t// +optional\n  1518\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1519\u2192\tAllocationTimestamp *metav1.Time `json:\"allocationTimestamp,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=allocationTimestamp\"`\n  1520\u2192}\n  1521\u2192\n  1522\u2192// DeviceAllocationResult is the result of allocating devices.\n  1523\u2192type DeviceAllocationResult struct {\n  1524\u2192\t// Results lists all allocated devices.\n  1525\u2192\t//\n  1526\u2192\t// +optional\n  1527\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1528\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1529\u2192\tResults []DeviceRequestAllocationResult `json:\"results,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=results\"`\n  1530\u2192\n  1531\u2192\t// This field is a combination of all the claim and class configuration parameters.\n  1532\u2192\t// Drivers can distinguish between those based on a flag.\n  1533\u2192\t//\n  1534\u2192\t// This includes configuration parameters for drivers which have no allocated\n  1535\u2192\t// devices in the result because it is up to the drivers which configuration\n  1536\u2192\t// parameters they support. They can silently ignore unknown configuration\n  1537\u2192\t// parameters.\n  1538\u2192\t//\n  1539\u2192\t// +optional\n  1540\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1541\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=64\n  1542\u2192\tConfig []DeviceAllocationConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1543\u2192}\n  1544\u2192\n  1545\u2192// AllocationResultsMaxSize represents the maximum number of\n  1546\u2192// entries in allocation.devices.results.\n  1547\u2192const AllocationResultsMaxSize = 32\n  1548\u2192\n  1549\u2192// DeviceRequestAllocationResult contains the allocation result for one request.\n  1550\u2192type DeviceRequestAllocationResult struct {\n  1551\u2192\t// Request is the name of the request in the claim which caused this\n  1552\u2192\t// device to be allocated. If it references a subrequest in the\n  1553\u2192\t// firstAvailable list on a DeviceRequest, this field must\n  1554\u2192\t// include both the name of the main request and the subrequest\n  1555\u2192\t// using the format <main request>/<subrequest>.\n  1556\u2192\t//\n  1557\u2192\t// Multiple devices may have been allocated per request.\n  1558\u2192\t//\n  1559\u2192\t// +required\n  1560\u2192\tRequest string `json:\"request\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=request\"`\n  1561\u2192\n  1562\u2192\t// Driver specifies the name of the DRA driver whose kubelet\n  1563\u2192\t// plugin should be invoked to process the allocation once the claim is\n  1564\u2192\t// needed on a node.\n  1565\u2192\t//\n  1566\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1567\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1568\u2192\t//\n  1569\u2192\t// +required\n  1570\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name-caseless\n  1571\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1572\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=driver\"`\n  1573\u2192\n  1574\u2192\t// This name together with the driver name and the device name field\n  1575\u2192\t// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).\n  1576\u2192\t//\n  1577\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 253 characters and may contain one or more\n  1578\u2192\t// DNS sub-domains separated by slashes.\n  1579\u2192\t//\n  1580\u2192\t// +required\n  1581\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1582\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-resource-pool-name\n  1583\u2192\tPool string `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=pool\"`\n  1584\u2192\n  1585\u2192\t// Device references one device instance via its name in the driver's\n  1586\u2192\t// resource pool. It must be a DNS label.\n  1587\u2192\t//\n  1588\u2192\t// +required\n  1589\u2192\tDevice string `json:\"device\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=device\"`\n  1590\u2192\n  1591\u2192\t// AdminAccess indicates that this device was allocated for\n  1592\u2192\t// administrative access. See the corresponding request field\n  1593\u2192\t// for a definition of mode.\n  1594\u2192\t//\n  1595\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRAAdminAccess\n  1596\u2192\t// feature gate. Admin access is disabled if this field is unset or\n  1597\u2192\t// set to false, otherwise it is enabled.\n  1598\u2192\t//\n  1599\u2192\t// +optional\n  1600\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAAdminAccess\n  1601\u2192\tAdminAccess *bool `json:\"adminAccess,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=adminAccess\"`\n  1602\u2192\n  1603\u2192\t// A copy of all tolerations specified in the request at the time\n  1604\u2192\t// when the device got allocated.\n  1605\u2192\t//\n  1606\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n  1607\u2192\t//\n  1608\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n  1609\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1610\u2192\t//\n  1611\u2192\t// +optional\n  1612\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1613\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n  1614\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=16\n  1615\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n  1616\u2192\n  1617\u2192\t// BindingConditions contains a copy of the BindingConditions\n  1618\u2192\t// from the corresponding ResourceSlice at the time of allocation.\n  1619\u2192\t//\n  1620\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1621\u2192\t// feature gates.\n  1622\u2192\t//\n  1623\u2192\t// +optional\n  1624\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1625\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1626\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1627\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n  1628\u2192\tBindingConditions []string `json:\"bindingConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,rep,name=bindingConditions\"`\n  1629\u2192\n  1630\u2192\t// BindingFailureConditions contains a copy of the BindingFailureConditions\n  1631\u2192\t// from the corresponding ResourceSlice at the time of allocation.\n  1632\u2192\t//\n  1633\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1634\u2192\t// feature gates.\n  1635\u2192\t//\n  1636\u2192\t// +optional\n  1637\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1638\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1639\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1640\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n  1641\u2192\tBindingFailureConditions []string `json:\"bindingFailureConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,rep,name=bindingFailureConditions\"`\n  1642\u2192\n  1643\u2192\t// ShareID uniquely identifies an individual allocation share of the device,\n  1644\u2192\t// used when the device supports multiple simultaneous allocations.\n  1645\u2192\t// It serves as an additional map key to differentiate concurrent shares\n  1646\u2192\t// of the same device.\n  1647\u2192\t//\n  1648\u2192\t// +optional\n  1649\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1650\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1651\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-uuid\n  1652\u2192\tShareID *types.UID `json:\"shareID,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,9,opt,name=shareID\"`\n  1653\u2192\n  1654\u2192\t// ConsumedCapacity tracks the amount of capacity consumed per device as part of the claim request.\n  1655\u2192\t// The consumed amount may differ from the requested amount: it is rounded up to the nearest valid\n  1656\u2192\t// value based on the device\u2019s requestPolicy if applicable (i.e., may not be less than the requested amount).\n  1657\u2192\t//\n  1658\u2192\t// The total consumed capacity for each device must not exceed the DeviceCapacity's Value.\n  1659\u2192\t//\n  1660\u2192\t// This field is populated only for devices that allow multiple allocations.\n  1661\u2192\t// All capacity entries are included, even if the consumed amount is zero.\n  1662\u2192\t//\n  1663\u2192\t// +optional\n  1664\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1665\u2192\tConsumedCapacity map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity `json:\"consumedCapacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,10,rep,name=consumedCapacity\"`\n  1666\u2192}\n  1667\u2192\n  1668\u2192// DeviceAllocationConfiguration gets embedded in an AllocationResult.\n  1669\u2192type DeviceAllocationConfiguration struct {\n  1670\u2192\t// Source records whether the configuration comes from a class and thus\n  1671\u2192\t// is not something that a normal user would have been able to set\n  1672\u2192\t// or from a claim.\n  1673\u2192\t//\n  1674\u2192\t// +required\n  1675\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1676\u2192\tSource AllocationConfigSource `json:\"source\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=source\"`\n  1677\u2192\n  1678\u2192\t// Requests lists the names of requests where the configuration applies.\n  1679\u2192\t// If empty, its applies to all requests.\n  1680\u2192\t//\n  1681\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1682\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1683\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1684\u2192\t//\n  1685\u2192\t// +optional\n  1686\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1687\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1688\u2192\n  1689\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1690\u2192}\n  1691\u2192\n  1692\u2192// +enum\n  1693\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1694\u2192type AllocationConfigSource string\n  1695\u2192\n  1696\u2192// Valid [DeviceAllocationConfiguration.Source] values.\n  1697\u2192const (\n  1698\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClass AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClass\"\n  1699\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClaim AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClaim\"\n  1700\u2192)\n  1701\u2192\n  1702\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1703\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n  1704\u2192\n  1705\u2192// ResourceClaimList is a collection of claims.\n  1706\u2192type ResourceClaimList struct {\n  1707\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1708\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1709\u2192\t// +optional\n  1710\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1711\u2192\n  1712\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claims.\n  1713\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaim `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1714\u2192}\n  1715\u2192\n  1716\u2192// +genclient\n  1717\u2192// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n  1718\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1719\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n  1720\u2192\n  1721\u2192// DeviceClass is a vendor- or admin-provided resource that contains\n  1722\u2192// device configuration and selectors. It can be referenced in\n  1723\u2192// the device requests of a claim to apply these presets.\n  1724\u2192// Cluster scoped.\n  1725\u2192//\n  1726\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1727\u2192// feature gate.\n  1728\u2192type DeviceClass struct {\n  1729\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1730\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1731\u2192\t// +optional\n  1732\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:optional\n  1733\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n  1734\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1735\u2192\n  1736\u2192\t// Spec defines what can be allocated and how to configure it.\n  1737\u2192\t//\n  1738\u2192\t// This is mutable. Consumers have to be prepared for classes changing\n  1739\u2192\t// at any time, either because they get updated or replaced. Claim\n  1740\u2192\t// allocations are done once based on whatever was set in classes at\n  1741\u2192\t// the time of allocation.\n  1742\u2192\t//\n  1743\u2192\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n  1744\u2192\tSpec DeviceClassSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1745\u2192}\n  1746\u2192\n  1747\u2192// DeviceClassSpec is used in a [DeviceClass] to define what can be allocated\n  1748\u2192// and how to configure it.\n  1749\u2192type DeviceClassSpec struct {\n  1750\u2192\t// Each selector must be satisfied by a device which is claimed via this class.\n  1751\u2192\t//\n  1752\u2192\t// +optional\n  1753\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1754\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1755\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1756\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=selectors\"`\n  1757\u2192\n  1758\u2192\t// Config defines configuration parameters that apply to each device that is claimed via this class.\n  1759\u2192\t// Some classses may potentially be satisfied by multiple drivers, so each instance of a vendor\n  1760\u2192\t// configuration applies to exactly one driver.\n  1761\u2192\t//\n  1762\u2192\t// They are passed to the driver, but are not considered while allocating the claim.\n  1763\u2192\t//\n  1764\u2192\t// +optional\n  1765\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1766\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1767\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1768\u2192\tConfig []DeviceClassConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1769\u2192\n  1770\u2192\t// SuitableNodes is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1771\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1772\u2192\t// supported.\n  1773\u2192\t// SuitableNodes *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"suitableNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=suitableNodes\"`\n  1774\u2192\n  1775\u2192\t// ExtendedResourceName is the extended resource name for the devices of this class.\n  1776\u2192\t// The devices of this class can be used to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1777\u2192\t// It has the same format as the name of a pod's extended resource.\n  1778\u2192\t// It should be unique among all the device classes in a cluster.\n  1779\u2192\t// If two device classes have the same name, then the class created later\n  1780\u2192\t// is picked to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1781\u2192\t// If two classes are created at the same time, then the name of the class\n  1782\u2192\t// lexicographically sorted first is picked.\n  1783\u2192\t//\n  1784\u2192\t// This is an alpha field.\n  1785\u2192\t// +optional\n  1786\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAExtendedResource\n  1787\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1788\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-extended-resource-name\n  1789\u2192\tExtendedResourceName *string `json:\"extendedResourceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=extendedResourceName\"`\n  1790\u2192}\n  1791\u2192\n  1792\u2192// DeviceClassConfiguration is used in DeviceClass.\n  1793\u2192type DeviceClassConfiguration struct {\n  1794\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1795\u2192}\n  1796\u2192\n  1797\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1798\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n  1799\u2192\n  1800\u2192// DeviceClassList is a collection of classes.\n  1801\u2192type DeviceClassList struct {\n  1802\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1803\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1804\u2192\t// +optional\n  1805\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1806\u2192\n  1807\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource classes.\n  1808\u2192\tItems []DeviceClass `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1809\u2192}\n  1810\u2192\n  1811\u2192// +genclient\n  1812\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1813\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.32\n  1814\u2192\n  1815\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects.\n  1816\u2192//\n  1817\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1818\u2192// feature gate.\n  1819\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplate struct {\n  1820\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1821\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1822\u2192\t// +optional\n
