If just\n  1284\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1285\u2192\t//\n  1286\u2192\t// +optional\n  1287\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1288\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=atomic\n  1289\u2192\t// +k8s:unique=set\n  1290\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1291\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1292\u2192\n  1293\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1294\u2192}\n  1295\u2192\n  1296\u2192// DeviceConfiguration must have exactly one field set. It gets embedded\n  1297\u2192// inline in some other structs which have other fields, so field names must\n  1298\u2192// not conflict with those.\n  1299\u2192type DeviceConfiguration struct {\n  1300\u2192\t// Opaque provides driver-specific configuration parameters.\n  1301\u2192\t//\n  1302\u2192\t// +optional\n  1303\u2192\t// +oneOf=ConfigurationType\n  1304\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1305\u2192\tOpaque *OpaqueDeviceConfiguration `json:\"opaque,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=opaque\"`\n  1306\u2192}\n  1307\u2192\n  1308\u2192// OpaqueDeviceConfiguration contains configuration parameters for a driver\n  1309\u2192// in a format defined by the driver vendor.\n  1310\u2192type OpaqueDeviceConfiguration struct {\n  1311\u2192\t// Driver is used to determine which kubelet plugin needs\n  1312\u2192\t// to be passed these configuration parameters.\n  1313\u2192\t//\n  1314\u2192\t// An admission policy provided by the driver developer could use this\n  1315\u2192\t// to decide whether it needs to validate them.\n  1316\u2192\t//\n  1317\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1318\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1319\u2192\t//\n  1320\u2192\t// +required\n  1321\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1322\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name-caseless\n  1323\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=driver\"`\n  1324\u2192\n  1325\u2192\t// Parameters can contain arbitrary data. It is the responsibility of\n  1326\u2192\t// the driver developer to handle validation and versioning. Typically this\n  1327\u2192\t// includes self-identification and a version (\"kind\" + \"apiVersion\" for\n  1328\u2192\t// Kubernetes types), with conversion between different versions.\n  1329\u2192\t//\n  1330\u2192\t// The length of the raw data must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki.\n  1331\u2192\t//\n  1332\u2192\t// +required\n  1333\u2192\tParameters runtime.RawExtension `json:\"parameters\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=parameters\"`\n  1334\u2192}\n  1335\u2192\n  1336\u2192// OpaqueParametersMaxLength is the maximum length of the raw data in an\n  1337\u2192// [OpaqueDeviceConfiguration.Parameters] field.\n  1338\u2192const OpaqueParametersMaxLength = 10 * 1024\n  1339\u2192\n  1340\u2192// The ResourceClaim this DeviceToleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n  1341\u2192// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n  1342\u2192type DeviceToleration struct {\n  1343\u2192\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n  1344\u2192\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n  1345\u2192\t// Must be a label name.\n  1346\u2192\t//\n  1347\u2192\t// +optional\n  1348\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1349\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-label-key\n  1350\u2192\tKey string `json:\"key,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\"`\n  1351\u2192\n  1352\u2192\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n  1353\u2192\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n  1354\u2192\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a ResourceClaim can\n  1355\u2192\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n  1356\u2192\t//\n  1357\u2192\t// +optional\n  1358\u2192\t// +default=\"Equal\"\n  1359\u2192\tOperator DeviceTolerationOperator `json:\"operator,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=DeviceTolerationOperator\"`\n  1360\u2192\n  1361\u2192\t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\n  1362\u2192\t// If the operator is Exists, the value must be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\n  1363\u2192\t// Must be a label value.\n  1364\u2192\t//\n  1365\u2192\t// +optional\n  1366\u2192\tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\"`\n  1367\u2192\n  1368\u2192\t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\n  1369\u2192\t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule and NoExecute.\n  1370\u2192\t//\n  1371\u2192\t// +optional\n  1372\u2192\tEffect DeviceTaintEffect `json:\"effect,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=DeviceTaintEffect\"`\n  1373\u2192\n  1374\u2192\t// TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be\n  1375\u2192\t// of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default,\n  1376\u2192\t// it is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and\n  1377\u2192\t// negative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.\n  1378\u2192\t// If larger than zero, the time when the pod needs to be evicted is calculated as <time when\n  1379\u2192\t// taint was adedd> + <toleration seconds>.\n  1380\u2192\t//\n  1381\u2192\t// +optional\n  1382\u2192\tTolerationSeconds *int64 `json:\"tolerationSeconds,omitempty\" protobuf:\"varint,5,opt,name=tolerationSeconds\"`\n  1383\u2192}\n  1384\u2192\n  1385\u2192// A toleration operator is the set of operators that can be used in a toleration.\n  1386\u2192//\n  1387\u2192// +enum\n  1388\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1389\u2192type DeviceTolerationOperator string\n  1390\u2192\n  1391\u2192const (\n  1392\u2192\tDeviceTolerationOpExists DeviceTolerationOperator = \"Exists\"\n  1393\u2192\tDeviceTolerationOpEqual  DeviceTolerationOperator = \"Equal\"\n  1394\u2192)\n  1395\u2192\n  1396\u2192// ResourceClaimStatus tracks whether the resource has been allocated and what\n  1397\u2192// the result of that was.\n  1398\u2192type ResourceClaimStatus struct {\n  1399\u2192\t// Allocation is set once the claim has been allocated successfully.\n  1400\u2192\t//\n  1401\u2192\t// +optional\n  1402\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1403\u2192\t// +k8s:update=NoModify\n  1404\u2192\tAllocation *AllocationResult `json:\"allocation,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=allocation\"`\n  1405\u2192\n  1406\u2192\t// ReservedFor indicates which entities are currently allowed to use\n  1407\u2192\t// the claim. A Pod which references a ResourceClaim which is not\n  1408\u2192\t// reserved for that Pod will not be started. A claim that is in\n  1409\u2192\t// use or might be in use because it has been reserved must not get\n  1410\u2192\t// deallocated.\n  1411\u2192\t//\n  1412\u2192\t// In a cluster with multiple scheduler instances, two pods might get\n  1413\u2192\t// scheduled concurrently by different schedulers. When they reference\n  1414\u2192\t// the same ResourceClaim which already has reached its maximum number\n  1415\u2192\t// of consumers, only one pod can be scheduled.\n  1416\u2192\t//\n  1417\u2192\t// Both schedulers try to add their pod to the claim.status.reservedFor\n  1418\u2192\t// field, but only the update that reaches the API server first gets\n  1419\u2192\t// stored. The other one fails with an error and the scheduler\n  1420\u2192\t// which issued it knows that it must put the pod back into the queue,\n  1421\u2192\t// waiting for the ResourceClaim to become usable again.\n  1422\u2192\t//\n  1423\u2192\t// There can be at most 256 such reservations. This may get increased in\n  1424\u2192\t// the future, but not reduced.\n  1425\u2192\t//\n  1426\u2192\t// +optional\n  1427\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1428\u2192\t// +listMapKey=uid\n  1429\u2192\t// +patchStrategy=merge\n  1430\u2192\t// +patchMergeKey=uid\n  1431\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1432\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=map\n  1433\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=uid\n  1434\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=256\n  1435\u2192\tReservedFor []ResourceClaimConsumerReference `json:\"reservedFor,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=reservedFor\" patchStrategy:\"merge\" patchMergeKey:\"uid\"`\n  1436\u2192\n  1437\u2192\t// DeallocationRequested is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1438\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1439\u2192\t// supported.\n  1440\u2192\t// DeallocationRequested bool `json:\"deallocationRequested,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=deallocationRequested\"`\n  1441\u2192\n  1442\u2192\t// Devices contains the status of each device allocated for this\n  1443\u2192\t// claim, as reported by the driver. This can include driver-specific\n  1444\u2192\t// information. Entries are owned by their respective drivers.\n  1445\u2192\t//\n  1446\u2192\t// +optional\n  1447\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1448\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1449\u2192\t// +listMapKey=driver\n  1450\u2192\t// +listMapKey=device\n  1451\u2192\t// +listMapKey=pool\n  1452\u2192\t// +listMapKey=shareID\n  1453\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1454\u2192\t// +k8s:listType=map\n  1455\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=driver\n  1456\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=device\n  1457\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=pool\n  1458\u2192\t// +k8s:listMapKey=shareID\n  1459\u2192\tDevices []AllocatedDeviceStatus `json:\"devices,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=devices\"`\n  1460\u2192}\n  1461\u2192\n  1462\u2192// ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize is the maximum number of entries in\n  1463\u2192// claim.status.reservedFor.\n  1464\u2192const ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize = 256\n  1465\u2192\n  1466\u2192// ResourceClaimConsumerReference contains enough information to let you\n  1467\u2192// locate the consumer of a ResourceClaim. The user must be a resource in the same\n  1468\u2192// namespace as the ResourceClaim.\n  1469\u2192type ResourceClaimConsumerReference struct {\n  1470\u2192\t// APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced. It is\n  1471\u2192\t// empty for the core API. This matches the group in the APIVersion\n  1472\u2192\t// that is used when creating the resources.\n  1473\u2192\t// +optional\n  1474\u2192\tAPIGroup string `json:\"apiGroup,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=apiGroup\"`\n  1475\u2192\t// Resource is the type of resource being referenced, for example \"pods\".\n  1476\u2192\t// +required\n  1477\u2192\tResource string `json:\"resource\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=resource\"`\n  1478\u2192\t// Name is the name of resource being referenced.\n  1479\u2192\t// +required\n  1480\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=name\"`\n  1481\u2192\t// UID identifies exactly one incarnation of the resource.\n  1482\u2192\t// +required\n  1483\u2192\tUID types.UID `json:\"uid\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,name=uid\"`\n  1484\u2192}\n  1485\u2192\n  1486\u2192// AllocationResult contains attributes of an allocated resource.\n  1487\u2192type AllocationResult struct {\n  1488\u2192\t// Devices is the result of allocating devices.\n  1489\u2192\t//\n  1490\u2192\t// +optional\n  1491\u2192\tDevices DeviceAllocationResult `json:\"devices,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=devices\"`\n  1492\u2192\n  1493\u2192\t// NodeSelector defines where the allocated resources are available. If\n  1494\u2192\t// unset, they are available everywhere.\n  1495\u2192\t//\n  1496\u2192\t// +optional\n  1497\u2192\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n  1498\u2192\n  1499\u2192\t// Controller is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1500\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1501\u2192\t// supported.\n  1502\u2192\t// Controller string `json:\"controller,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=controller\"`\n  1503\u2192\n  1504\u2192\t// AllocationTimestamp stores the time when the resources were allocated.\n  1505\u2192\t// This field is not guaranteed to be set, in which case that time is unknown.\n  1506\u2192\t//\n  1507\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1508\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1509\u2192\t//\n  1510\u2192\t// +optional\n  1511\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1512\u2192\tAllocationTimestamp *metav1.Time `json:\"allocationTimestamp,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=allocationTimestamp\"`\n  1513\u2192}\n  1514\u2192\n  1515\u2192// DeviceAllocationResult is the result of allocating devices.\n  1516\u2192type DeviceAllocationResult struct {\n  1517\u2192\t// Results lists all allocated devices.\n  1518\u2192\t//\n  1519\u2192\t// +optional\n  1520\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1521\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1522\u2192\tResults []DeviceRequestAllocationResult `json:\"results,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=results\"`\n  1523\u2192\n  1524\u2192\t// This field is a combination of all the claim and class configuration parameters.\n  1525\u2192\t// Drivers can distinguish between those based on a flag.\n  1526\u2192\t//\n  1527\u2192\t// This includes configuration parameters for drivers which have no allocated\n  1528\u2192\t// devices in the result because it is up to the drivers which configuration\n  1529\u2192\t// parameters they support. They can silently ignore unknown configuration\n  1530\u2192\t// parameters.\n  1531\u2192\t//\n  1532\u2192\t// +optional\n  1533\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1534\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=64\n  1535\u2192\tConfig []DeviceAllocationConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1536\u2192}\n  1537\u2192\n  1538\u2192// AllocationResultsMaxSize represents the maximum number of\n  1539\u2192// entries in allocation.devices.results.\n  1540\u2192const AllocationResultsMaxSize = 32\n  1541\u2192\n  1542\u2192// DeviceRequestAllocationResult contains the allocation result for one request.\n  1543\u2192type DeviceRequestAllocationResult struct {\n  1544\u2192\t// Request is the name of the request in the claim which caused this\n  1545\u2192\t// device to be allocated. If it references a subrequest in the\n  1546\u2192\t// firstAvailable list on a DeviceRequest, this field must\n  1547\u2192\t// include both the name of the main request and the subrequest\n  1548\u2192\t// using the format <main request>/<subrequest>.\n  1549\u2192\t//\n  1550\u2192\t// Multiple devices may have been allocated per request.\n  1551\u2192\t//\n  1552\u2192\t// +required\n  1553\u2192\tRequest string `json:\"request\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=request\"`\n  1554\u2192\n  1555\u2192\t// Driver specifies the name of the DRA driver whose kubelet\n  1556\u2192\t// plugin should be invoked to process the allocation once the claim is\n  1557\u2192\t// needed on a node.\n  1558\u2192\t//\n  1559\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1560\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1561\u2192\t//\n  1562\u2192\t// +required\n  1563\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-long-name-caseless\n  1564\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1565\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=driver\"`\n  1566\u2192\n  1567\u2192\t// This name together with the driver name and the device name field\n  1568\u2192\t// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).\n  1569\u2192\t//\n  1570\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 253 characters and may contain one or more\n  1571\u2192\t// DNS sub-domains separated by slashes.\n  1572\u2192\t//\n  1573\u2192\t// +required\n  1574\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1575\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-resource-pool-name\n  1576\u2192\tPool string `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=pool\"`\n  1577\u2192\n  1578\u2192\t// Device references one device instance via its name in the driver's\n  1579\u2192\t// resource pool. It must be a DNS label.\n  1580\u2192\t//\n  1581\u2192\t// +required\n  1582\u2192\tDevice string `json:\"device\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=device\"`\n  1583\u2192\n  1584\u2192\t// AdminAccess indicates that this device was allocated for\n  1585\u2192\t// administrative access. See the corresponding request field\n  1586\u2192\t// for a definition of mode.\n  1587\u2192\t//\n  1588\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRAAdminAccess\n  1589\u2192\t// feature gate. Admin access is disabled if this field is unset or\n  1590\u2192\t// set to false, otherwise it is enabled.\n  1591\u2192\t//\n  1592\u2192\t// +optional\n  1593\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAAdminAccess\n  1594\u2192\tAdminAccess *bool `json:\"adminAccess,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=adminAccess\"`\n  1595\u2192\n  1596\u2192\t// A copy of all tolerations specified in the request at the time\n  1597\u2192\t// when the device got allocated.\n  1598\u2192\t//\n  1599\u2192\t// The maximum number of tolerations is 16.\n  1600\u2192\t//\n  1601\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceTaints\n  1602\u2192\t// feature gate.\n  1603\u2192\t//\n  1604\u2192\t// +optional\n  1605\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1606\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceTaints\n  1607\u2192\tTolerations []DeviceToleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n  1608\u2192\n  1609\u2192\t// BindingConditions contains a copy of the BindingConditions\n  1610\u2192\t// from the corresponding ResourceSlice at the time of allocation.\n  1611\u2192\t//\n  1612\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1613\u2192\t// feature gates.\n  1614\u2192\t//\n  1615\u2192\t// +optional\n  1616\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1617\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1618\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1619\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n  1620\u2192\tBindingConditions []string `json:\"bindingConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,rep,name=bindingConditions\"`\n  1621\u2192\n  1622\u2192\t// BindingFailureConditions contains a copy of the BindingFailureConditions\n  1623\u2192\t// from the corresponding ResourceSlice at the time of allocation.\n  1624\u2192\t//\n  1625\u2192\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling the DRADeviceBindingConditions and DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1626\u2192\t// feature gates.\n  1627\u2192\t//\n  1628\u2192\t// +optional\n  1629\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1630\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRADeviceBindingConditions,DRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus\n  1631\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1632\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=4\n  1633\u2192\tBindingFailureConditions []string `json:\"bindingFailureConditions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,rep,name=bindingFailureConditions\"`\n  1634\u2192\n  1635\u2192\t// ShareID uniquely identifies an individual allocation share of the device,\n  1636\u2192\t// used when the device supports multiple simultaneous allocations.\n  1637\u2192\t// It serves as an additional map key to differentiate concurrent shares\n  1638\u2192\t// of the same device.\n  1639\u2192\t//\n  1640\u2192\t// +optional\n  1641\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1642\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1643\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-uuid\n  1644\u2192\tShareID *types.UID `json:\"shareID,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,9,opt,name=shareID\"`\n  1645\u2192\n  1646\u2192\t// ConsumedCapacity tracks the amount of capacity consumed per device as part of the claim request.\n  1647\u2192\t// The consumed amount may differ from the requested amount: it is rounded up to the nearest valid\n  1648\u2192\t// value based on the device\u2019s requestPolicy if applicable (i.e., may not be less than the requested amount).\n  1649\u2192\t//\n  1650\u2192\t// The total consumed capacity for each device must not exceed the DeviceCapacity's Value.\n  1651\u2192\t//\n  1652\u2192\t// This field is populated only for devices that allow multiple allocations.\n  1653\u2192\t// All capacity entries are included, even if the consumed amount is zero.\n  1654\u2192\t//\n  1655\u2192\t// +optional\n  1656\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1657\u2192\tConsumedCapacity map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity `json:\"consumedCapacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,10,rep,name=consumedCapacity\"`\n  1658\u2192}\n  1659\u2192\n  1660\u2192// DeviceAllocationConfiguration gets embedded in an AllocationResult.\n  1661\u2192type DeviceAllocationConfiguration struct {\n  1662\u2192\t// Source records whether the configuration comes from a class and thus\n  1663\u2192\t// is not something that a normal user would have been able to set\n  1664\u2192\t// or from a claim.\n  1665\u2192\t//\n  1666\u2192\t// +required\n  1667\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1668\u2192\tSource AllocationConfigSource `json:\"source\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=source\"`\n  1669\u2192\n  1670\u2192\t// Requests lists the names of requests where the configuration applies.\n  1671\u2192\t// If empty, its applies to all requests.\n  1672\u2192\t//\n  1673\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1674\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1675\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1676\u2192\t//\n  1677\u2192\t// +optional\n  1678\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1679\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1680\u2192\n  1681\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1682\u2192}\n  1683\u2192\n  1684\u2192// +enum\n  1685\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1686\u2192type AllocationConfigSource string\n  1687\u2192\n  1688\u2192// Valid [DeviceAllocationConfiguration.Source] values.\n  1689\u2192const (\n  1690\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClass AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClass\"\n  1691\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClaim AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClaim\"\n  1692\u2192)\n  1693\u2192\n  1694\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1695\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.34\n  1696\u2192\n  1697\u2192// ResourceClaimList is a collection of claims.\n  1698\u2192type ResourceClaimList struct {\n  1699\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1700\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1701\u2192\t// +optional\n  1702\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1703\u2192\n  1704\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claims.\n  1705\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaim `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1706\u2192}\n  1707\u2192\n  1708\u2192// +genclient\n  1709\u2192// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n  1710\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1711\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.34\n  1712\u2192\n  1713\u2192// DeviceClass is a vendor- or admin-provided resource that contains\n  1714\u2192// device configuration and selectors. It can be referenced in\n  1715\u2192// the device requests of a claim to apply these presets.\n  1716\u2192// Cluster scoped.\n  1717\u2192//\n  1718\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1719\u2192// feature gate.\n  1720\u2192type DeviceClass struct {\n  1721\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1722\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1723\u2192\t// +optional\n  1724\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:optional\n  1725\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n  1726\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1727\u2192\n  1728\u2192\t// Spec defines what can be allocated and how to configure it.\n  1729\u2192\t//\n  1730\u2192\t// This is mutable. Consumers have to be prepared for classes changing\n  1731\u2192\t// at any time, either because they get updated or replaced. Claim\n  1732\u2192\t// allocations are done once based on whatever was set in classes at\n  1733\u2192\t// the time of allocation.\n  1734\u2192\t//\n  1735\u2192\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n  1736\u2192\tSpec DeviceClassSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1737\u2192}\n  1738\u2192\n  1739\u2192// DeviceClassSpec is used in a [DeviceClass] to define what can be allocated\n  1740\u2192// and how to configure it.\n  1741\u2192type DeviceClassSpec struct {\n  1742\u2192\t// Each selector must be satisfied by a device which is claimed via this class.\n  1743\u2192\t//\n  1744\u2192\t// +optional\n  1745\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1746\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1747\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1748\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=selectors\"`\n  1749\u2192\n  1750\u2192\t// Config defines configuration parameters that apply to each device that is claimed via this class.\n  1751\u2192\t// Some classses may potentially be satisfied by multiple drivers, so each instance of a vendor\n  1752\u2192\t// configuration applies to exactly one driver.\n  1753\u2192\t//\n  1754\u2192\t// They are passed to the driver, but are not considered while allocating the claim.\n  1755\u2192\t//\n  1756\u2192\t// +optional\n  1757\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1758\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1759\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1760\u2192\tConfig []DeviceClassConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1761\u2192\n  1762\u2192\t// SuitableNodes is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1763\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1764\u2192\t// supported.\n  1765\u2192\t// SuitableNodes *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"suitableNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=suitableNodes\"`\n  1766\u2192\n  1767\u2192\t// ExtendedResourceName is the extended resource name for the devices of this class.\n  1768\u2192\t// The devices of this class can be used to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1769\u2192\t// It has the same format as the name of a pod's extended resource.\n  1770\u2192\t// It should be unique among all the device classes in a cluster.\n  1771\u2192\t// If two device classes have the same name, then the class created later\n  1772\u2192\t// is picked to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1773\u2192\t// If two classes are created at the same time, then the name of the class\n  1774\u2192\t// lexicographically sorted first is picked.\n  1775\u2192\t//\n  1776\u2192\t// This is an alpha field.\n  1777\u2192\t// +optional\n  1778\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAExtendedResource\n  1779\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1780\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-extended-resource-name\n  1781\u2192\tExtendedResourceName *string `json:\"extendedResourceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=extendedResourceName\"`\n  1782\u2192}\n  1783\u2192\n  1784\u2192// DeviceClassConfiguration is used in DeviceClass.\n  1785\u2192type DeviceClassConfiguration struct {\n  1786\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1787\u2192}\n  1788\u2192\n  1789\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1790\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.34\n  1791\u2192\n  1792\u2192// DeviceClassList is a collection of classes.\n  1793\u2192type DeviceClassList struct {\n  1794\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1795\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1796\u2192\t// +optional\n  1797\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1798\u2192\n  1799\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource classes.\n  1800\u2192\tItems []DeviceClass `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1801\u2192}\n  1802\u2192\n  1803\u2192// +genclient\n  1804\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1805\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.34\n  1806\u2192\n  1807\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects.\n  1808\u2192//\n  1809\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1810\u2192// feature gate.\n  1811\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplate struct {\n  1812\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1813\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1814\u2192\t// +optional\n  1815\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1816\u2192\n  1817\u2192\t// Describes the ResourceClaim that is to be generated.\n  1818\u2192\t//\n  1819\u2192\t// This field is immutable. A ResourceClaim will get created by the\n  1820\u2192\t// control plane for a Pod when needed and then not get updated\n  1821\u2192\t// anymore.\n  1822\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimTemplateSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1823\u2192}\n  1824\u2192\n  1825\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplateSpec contains the metadata and fields for a ResourceClaim.\n  1826\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplateSpec struct {\n  1827\u2192\t// ObjectMeta may contain labels and annotations that will be copied into the ResourceClaim\n  1828\u2192\t// when creating it. No other fields are allowed and will be rejected during\n  1829\u2192\t// validation.\n  1830\u2192\t// +optional\n  1831\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1832\u2192\n  1833\u2192\t// Spec for the ResourceClaim. The entire content is copied unchanged\n  1834\u2192\t// into the ResourceClaim that gets created from this template. The\n  1835\u2192\t// same fields as in a ResourceClaim are also valid here.\n  1836\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1837\u2192}\n  1838\u2192\n  1839\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1840\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.34\n  1841\u2192\n  1842\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplateList is a collection of claim templates.\n  1843\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplateList struct {\n  1844\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1845\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1846\u2192\t// +optional\n  1847\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1848\u2192\n  1849\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claim templates.\n  1850\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaimTemplate `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1851\u2192}\n  1852\u2192\n  1853\u2192const (\n  1854\u2192\t// AllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions represents the maximum number of\n  1855\u2192\t// conditions in a device status.\n  1856\u2192\tAllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions int = 8\n  1857\u2192\t// AllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength represents the maximum length of the\n  1858\u2192\t// raw data in the Data field in a device status.\n  1859\u2192\tAllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength int = 10 * 1024\n  1860\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs represents the maximum number of IPs in the networkData\n  1861\u2192\t// field in a device status.\n  1862\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs int = 16\n  1863\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength represents the maximum number of characters\n  1864\u2192\t// for the networkData.interfaceName field in a device status.\n  1865\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength int = 256\n  1866\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength represents the maximum number of characters\n  1867\u2192\t// for the networkData.hardwareAddress field in a device status.\n  1868\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength int = 128\n  1869\u2192)\n  1870\u2192\n  1871\u2192// AllocatedDeviceStatus contains the status of an allocated device, if the\n  1872\u2192// driver chooses to report it. This may include driver-specific information.\n  1873\u2192//\n  1874\u2192// The combination of Driver, Pool, Device, and ShareID must match the corresponding key\n  1875\u2192// in Status.Allocation.Devices.\n  1876\u2192type AllocatedDeviceStatus struct {\n  1877\u2192\t// Driver specifies the name of the DRA driver whose kubelet\n  1878\u2192\t// plugin should be invoked to process the allocation once the claim is\n  1879\u2192\t// needed on a node.\n  1880\u2192\t//\n  1881\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1882\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1883\u2192\t//\n  1884\u2192\t// +required\n  1885\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=driver\"`\n  1886\u2192\n  1887\u2192\t// This name together with the driver name and the device name field\n  1888\u2192\t// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).\n  1889\u2192\t//\n  1890\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 253 characters and may contain one or more\n  1891\u2192\t// DNS sub-domains separated by slashes.\n  1892\u2192\t//\n  1893\u2192\t// +required\n  1894\u2192\tPool string `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=pool\"`\n  1895\u2192\n  1896\u2192\t// Device references one device instance via its name in the driver's\n  1897\u2192\t// resource pool. It must be a DNS label.\n  1898\u2192\t//\n  1899\u2192\t// +required\n  1900\u2192\tDevice string `json:\"device\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=device\"`\n  1901\u2192\n  1902\u2192\t// ShareID uniquely identifies an individual allocation share of the device.\n  1903\u2192\t//\n  1904\u2192\t// +optional\n  1905\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1906\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1907\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-uuid\n  1908\u2192\tShareID *string `json:\"shareID,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=shareID\"`\n  1909\u2192\n  1910\u2192\t// Conditions contains the latest observation of the device's state.\n  1911\u2192\t// If the device has been configured according to the class and claim\n  1912\u2192\t// config references, the `Ready` condition should be True.\n  1913\u2192\t//\n  1914\u2192\t// Must not contain more than 8 entries.\n  1915\u2192\t//\n  1916\u2192\t// +optional\n  1917\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1918\u2192\t// +listMapKey=type\n  1919\u2192\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=conditions\"`\n  1920\u2192\n  1921\u2192\t// Data contains arbitrary driver-specific data.\n  1922\u2192\t//\n  1923\u2192\t// The length of the raw data must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki.\n  1924\u2192\t//\n  1925\u2192\t// +optional\n  1926\u2192\tData *runtime.RawExtension `json:\"data,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=data\"`\n  1927\u2192\n  1928\u2192\t// NetworkData contains network-related information specific to the device.\n  1929\u2192\t//\n  1930\u2192\t// +optional\n  1931\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1932\u2192\tNetworkData *NetworkDeviceData `json:\"networkData,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=networkData\"`\n  1933\u2192}\n  1934\u2192\n  1935\u2192// NetworkDeviceData provides network-related details for the allocated device.\n  1936\u2192// This information may be filled by drivers or other components to configure\n  1937\u2192// or identify the device within a network context.\n  1938\u2192type NetworkDeviceData struct {\n  1939\u2192\t// InterfaceName specifies the name of the network interface associated with\n  1940\u2192\t// the allocated device. This might be the name of a physical or virtual\n  1941\u2192\t// network interface being configured in the pod.\n  1942\u2192\t//\n  1943\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 256 characters.\n  1944\u2192\t//\n  1945\u2192\t// +optional\n  1946\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1947\u2192\t// +k8s:maxLength=256\n  1948\u2192\tInterfaceName string `json:\"interfaceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=interfaceName\"`\n  1949\u2192\n  1950\u2192\t// IPs lists the network addresses assigned to the device's network interface.\n  1951\u2192\t// This can include both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.\n  1952\u2192\t// The IPs are in the CIDR notation, which includes both the address and the\n  1953\u2192\t// associated subnet mask.\n  1954\u2192\t// e.g.: \"192.0.2.5/24\" for IPv4 and \"2001:db8::5/64\" for IPv6.\n  1955\u2192\t//\n  1956\u2192\t// +optional\n  1957\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1958\u2192\tIPs []string `json:\"ips,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=ips\"`\n  1959\u2192\n  1960\u2192\t// HardwareAddress represents the hardware address (e.g. MAC Address) of the device's network interface.\n  1961\u2192\t//\n  1962\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 128 characters.\n  1963\u2192\t//\n  1964\u2192\t// +optional\n  1965\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1966\u2192\t// +k8s:maxLength=128\n  1967\u2192\tHardwareAddress string `json:\"hardwareAddress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=hardwareAddress\"`\n  1968\u2192}\n  1969\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
15. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/dynamicresources.go",
  "limit": 100
}

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package dynamicresources\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"slices\"\n    24\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    25\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    26\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    27\u2192\t\"time\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    30\u2192\tresourceapi \"k8s.io/api/resource/v1\"\n    31\u2192\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n    32\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    33\u2192\tapimeta \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\"\n    35\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/diff\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n 47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/resourceclaim\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured\"\n    49\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    50\u2192\tfwk \"k8s.io/kube-scheduler/framework\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config\"\n    52\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/validation\"\n    53\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/extended\"\n    55\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/feature\"\n    56\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/names\"\n    57\u2192\tschedutil \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/util\"\n    58\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/util/assumecache\"\n    59\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/slice\"\n    60\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n    61\u2192)\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192const (\n    64\u2192\t// Name is the name of the plugin used in Registry and configurations.\n    65\u2192\tName = names.DynamicResources\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\tstateKey fwk.StateKey = Name\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t// specialClaimInMemName is the name of the special resource claim that\n    70\u2192\t// exists only in memory. The claim will get a generated name when it is\n    71\u2192\t// written to API server.\n    72\u2192\t//\n    73\u2192\t// It's intentionally not a valid ResourceClaim name to avoid conflicts with\n    74\u2192\t// some actual ResourceClaim in the apiserver.\n    75\u2192\tspecialClaimInMemName = \"<extended-resources>\"\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\t// BindingTimeoutDefaultSeconds is the default timeout for waiting for\n    78\u2192\t// BindingConditions to be ready.\n    79\u2192\tBindingTimeoutDefaultSeconds = 600\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// AssumeExtendedResourceTimeoutDefaultSeconds is the default timeout for waiting\n    82\u2192\t// for the extended resource claim to be updated in assumed cache.\n    83\u2192\tAssumeExtendedResourceTimeoutDefaultSeconds = 120\n    84\u2192)\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// The state is initialized in PreFilter phase. Because we save the pointer in\n    87\u2192// fwk.CycleState, in the later phases we don't need to call Write method\n    88\u2192// to update the value\n    89\u2192type stateData struct {\n    90\u2192\t// A copy of all claims for the Pod (i.e. 1:1 match with\n    91\u2192\t// pod.Spec.ResourceClaims), initially with the status from the start\n    92\u2192\t// of the scheduling cycle. Each claim instance is read-only because it\n    93\u2192\t// might come from the informer cache. The instances get replaced when\n    94\u2192\t// the plugin itself successfully does an Update.\n    95\u2192\t//\n    96\u2192\t// When the DRAExtendedResource feature is enabled, the special ResourceClaim\n    97\u2192\t// which represents extended resource requests is also stored here.\n    98\u2192\t// The plugin code should treat this field as a black box and only\n    99\u2192\t// access it via its methods, in particular:\n   100\u2192\t// - The all method can be used to iterate over all claims, including\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package stable\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"math\"\n    24\u2192\t\"slices\"\n    25\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    26\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    29\u2192\tresourceapi \"k8s.io/api/resource/v1\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    31\u2192\tdraapi \"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/api\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/cel\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/resourceclaim\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n    37\u2192)\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192type DeviceClassLister = internal.DeviceClassLister\n    40\u2192type Features = internal.Features\n    41\u2192type DeviceID = internal.DeviceID\n    42\u2192type Stats = internal.Stats\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192func MakeDeviceID(driver, pool, device string) DeviceID {\n    45\u2192\treturn internal.MakeDeviceID(driver, pool, device)\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192// SupportedFeatures does not include any additional features.\n    49\u2192// The \"stable\" implementation can only be used if all of those\n    50\u2192// are off.\n    51\u2192//\n    52\u2192// At least that's its purpose conceptually: in practice, all\n    53\u2192// code supporting the additional features is still there.\n    54\u2192// It could get removed.\n    55\u2192var SupportedFeatures = internal.Features{}\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192type Allocator struct {\n    58\u2192\tfeatures         Features\n    59\u2192\tallocatedDevices sets.Set[DeviceID]\n    60\u2192\tclassLister      DeviceClassLister\n    61\u2192\tslices           []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice\n    62\u2192\tcelCache         *cel.Cache\n    63\u2192\t// availableCounters contains the available counters for individual\n    64\u2192\t// ResourceSlices. It acts as a cache that is updated the first time\n    65\u2192\t// the available counters are needed for each ResourceSlice. The information\n    66\u2192\t// about each slice is never updated once set the first time.\n    67\u2192\t// This is computed bsed on information on the Allocator, so it will\n    68\u2192\t// be correct even for multiple usages of the Allocator.\n    69\u2192\t// The keys in the map are ResourceSlice names.\n    70\u2192\t// The allocator might be accessed by different goroutines, so\n    71\u2192\t// access to this map must be synchronized.\n    72\u2192\tavailableCounters map[string]counterSets\n    73\u2192\tmutex             sync.RWMutex\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192// NewAllocator returns an allocator for a certain set of claims or an error if\n    77\u2192// some problem was detected which makes it impossible to allocate claims.\n    78\u2192//\n    79\u2192// The returned Allocator can be used multiple times and is thread-safe.\n    80\u2192func NewAllocator(ctx context.Context,\n    81\u2192\tfeatures Features,\n    82\u2192\tallocatedDevices sets.Set[DeviceID],\n    83\u2192\tclassLister DeviceClassLister,\n    84\u2192\tslices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice,\n    85\u2192\tcelCache *cel.Cache,\n    86\u2192) (*Allocator, error) {\n    87\u2192\treturn &Allocator{\n    88\u2192\t\tfeatures:          features,\n    89\u2192\t\tallocatedDevices:  allocatedDevices,\n    90\u2192\t\tclassLister:       classLister,\n    91\u2192\t\tslices:            slices,\n    92\u2192\t\tcelCache:          celCache,\n    93\u2192\t\tavailableCounters: make(map[string]counterSets),\n    94\u2192\t}, nil\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192func (a *Allocator) Allocate(ctx context.Context, node *v1.Node, claims []*resourceapi.ResourceClaim) (finalResult []resourceapi.AllocationResult, finalErr error) {\n    98\u2192\talloc := &allocator{\n    99\u2192\t\tAllocator:            a,\n   100\u2192\t\tctx:                  ctx, // all methods share the same a and thus ctx\n   101\u2192\t\tlogger:               klog.FromContext(ctx),\n   102\u2192\t\tnode:                 node,\n   103\u2192\t\tclaimsToAllocate:     claims,\n   104\u2192\t\tdeviceMatchesRequest: make(map[matchKey]bool),\n   105\u2192\t\tconstraints:          make([][]constraint, len(claims)),\n   106\u2192\t\tconsumedCounters:     make(map[string]counterSets),\n   107\u2192\t\trequestData:          make(map[requestIndices]requestData),\n   108\u2192\t\tresult:               make([]internalAllocationResult, len(claims)),\n   109\u2192\t}\n   110\u2192\talloc.logger.V(5).Info(\"Starting allocation\", \"numClaims\", len(alloc.claimsToAllocate))\n   111\u2192\tdefer alloc.logger.V(5).Info(\"Done with allocation\", \"success\", len(finalResult) == len(alloc.claimsToAllocate), \"err\", finalErr)\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// First determine all eligible pools.\n   114\u2192\tpools, err := GatherPools(ctx, alloc.slices, node, a.features)\n   115\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   116\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"gather pool information: %w\", err)\n   117\u2192\t}\n   118\u2192\talloc.pools = pools\n   119\u2192\tif loggerV := alloc.logger.V(7); loggerV.Enabled() {\n   120\u2192\t\tloggerV.Info(\"Gathered pool information\", \"numPools\", len(pools), \"pools\", pools)\n   121\u2192\t} else {\n   122\u2192\t\talloc.logger.V(5).Info(\"Gathered pool information\", \"numPools\", len(pools))\n   123\u2192\t}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192\t// We allocate one claim after the other and for each claim, all of\n   126\u2192\t// its requests. For each individual device we pick one possible\n   127\u2192\t// candidate after the other, checking constraints as we go.\n   128\u2192\t// Each chosen candidate is marked as \"in use\" and the process\n   129\u2192\t// continues, recursively. This way, all requests get matched against\n   130\u2192\t// all candidates in all possible orders.\n   131\u2192\t//\n   132\u2192\t// The first full solution is chosen.\n   133\u2192\t//\n   134\u2192\t// In other words, this is an exhaustive search. This is okay because\n   135\u2192\t// it aborts early. Once scoring gets added, more intelligence may be\n   136\u2192\t// needed to avoid trying \"equivalent\" solutions (two identical\n   137\u2192\t// requests, two identical devices, two solutions that are the same in\n   138\u2192\t// practice).\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192\t// This is where we sanity check that we can actually handle the claims\n   141\u2192\t// and their requests. For each claim we determine how many devices\n   142\u2192\t// need to be allocated. If not all can be stored in the result, the\n   143\u2192\t// claim cannot be allocated.\n   144\u2192\tminDevicesTotal := 0\n   145\u2192\tfor claimIndex, claim := range alloc.claimsToAllocate {\n   146\u2192\t\tminDevicesPerClaim := 0\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\t// If we have any any request that wants \"all\" devices, we need to\n   149\u2192\t\t// figure out how much \"all\" is. If some pool is incomplete, we stop\n   150\u2192\t\t// here because allocation cannot succeed. 24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    25\u2192)\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// claimStore manages all ResourceClaims for the pod, whether they were\n    28\u2192// created by the user directly (ResourceClaim) or indirectly (ResourceClaimTemplate)\n    29\u2192// as well as the special ResourceClaim for extended resources.\n    30\u2192//\n    31\u2192// The zero value is usable and empty.\n    32\u2192type claimStore struct {\n    33\u2192\t// claims contains all user-owned claims, optionally followed by the special ResourceClaim for extended resources.\n    34\u2192\tclaims []*resourceapi.ResourceClaim\n    35\u2192\t// numUserOwned is the number of claims without the special ResourceClaim.\n    36\u2192\tnumUserOwned int\n    37\u2192\t// initialExtendedResourceClaimUID is the initial extended resource claim UID from PreFilter phase.\n    38\u2192\t// It is different from the UID set by API server when the in-memory, temproary claim is written to the API server in PreBind phase.\n    39\u2192\tinitialExtendedResourceClaimUID types.UID\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192// newClaimStore stores the list of user-owned claims and the optional claim owned by the scheduler.\n    43\u2192func newClaimStore(claims []*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, extendedResourceClaim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) claimStore {\n    44\u2192\tcs := claimStore{\n    45\u2192\t\tclaims:                          claims,\n    46\u2192\t\tnumUserOwned:                    len(claims),\n    47\u2192\t\tinitialExtendedResourceClaimUID: \"\",\n    48\u2192\t}\n    49\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim != nil {\n    50\u2192\t\tcs.claims = append(cs.claims, extendedResourceClaim)\n    51\u2192\t\tcs.initialExtendedResourceClaimUID = extendedResourceClaim.UID\n    52\u2192\t}\n    53\u2192\treturn cs\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// empty returns true when there are no ResourceClaims.\n    57\u2192func (cs *claimStore) empty() bool {\n    58\u2192\treturn len(cs.claims) == 0\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192// len returns number of all claims, whether they are owned by the user or the scheduler.\n    62\u2192func (cs *claimStore) len() int {\n    63\u2192\treturn len(cs.claims)\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192// all returns an iterator for all claims, whether they are owned by the user\n    67\u2192// or the scheduler. If there is a special ResourceClaim for extended resoures,\n    68\u2192// then it comes last.\n    69\u2192func (cs *claimStore) all() iter.Seq2[int, *resourceapi.ResourceClaim] {\n    70\u2192\treturn func(yield func(int, *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) bool) {\n    71\u2192\t\tfor i, claim := range cs.claims {\n    72\u2192\t\t\tif !yield(i, claim) {\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n    74\u2192\t\t\t}\n    75\u2192\t\t}\n    76\u2192\t}\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// allUserClaims returns an iterator which excludes the special ResourceClaim for extended resoures.\n    80\u2192func (cs *claimStore) allUserClaims() iter.Seq2[int, *resourceapi.ResourceClaim] {\n    81\u2192\treturn func(yield func(int, *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) bool) {\n    82\u2192\t\tfor i, claim := range cs.claims[0:cs.numUserOwned] {\n    83\u2192\t\t\tif !yield(i, claim) {\n    84\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n    85\u2192\t\t\t}\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192// toAllocate returns an iterator for all claims which have no allocation result.\n    91\u2192// the index returned is the original index in the underlying claim store, it\n    92\u2192// may not be sequentially numbered (e.g. 0, 1, 2 ...).\n    93\u2192func (cs *claimStore) toAllocate() iter.Seq2[int, *resourceapi.ResourceClaim] {\n    94\u2192\treturn func(yield func(int, *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) bool) {\n    95\u2192\t\tfor i, claim := range cs.claims {\n    96\u2192\t\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil {\n    97\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    98\u2192\t\t\t}\n    99\u2192\t\t\tif !yield(i, claim) {\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2024 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package stable\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    24\u2192\tresourceapi \"k8s.io/api/resource/v1\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-helpers/scheduling/corev1/nodeaffinity\"\n    27\u2192\tdraapi \"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/api\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n    29\u2192)\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192func nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, nodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector) (bool, error) {\n    32\u2192\tswitch {\n    33\u2192\tcase nodeNameToMatch != \"\":\n    34\u2192\t\treturn node != nil && node.Name == nodeNameToMatch, nil\n    35\u2192\tcase allNodesMatch:\n    36\u2192\t\treturn true, nil\n    37\u2192\tcase nodeSelector != nil:\n    38\u2192\t\tselector, err := nodeaffinity.NewNodeSelector(nodeSelector)\n    39\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    40\u2192\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse node selector %s: %w\", nodeSelector.String(), err)\n    41\u2192\t\t}\n    42\u2192\t\treturn selector.Match(node), nil\n    43\u2192\t}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\treturn false, nil\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192type poolIdentifier struct {\n    49\u2192\tdriver, pool string\n    50\u2192}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// GatherPools collects information about all resource pools which provide\n    53\u2192// devices that are accessible from the given node.\n    54\u2192//\n    55\u2192// Out-dated slices are silently ignored. Pools may be incomplete (not all\n    56\u2192// required slices available) or invalid (for example, device names not unique).\n    57\u2192// Both is recorded in the result.\n    58\u2192func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node *v1.Node, features Features) ([]*Pool, error) {\n    59\u2192\tslicesByPool := make(map[poolIdentifier][]*resourceapi.ResourceSlice)\n    60\u2192\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n    61\u2192\t\tpoolID := poolIdentifier{\n    62\u2192\t\t\tdriver: slice.Spec.Driver,\n    63\u2192\t\t\tpool:   slice.Spec.Pool.Name,\n    64\u2192\t\t}\n    65\u2192\t\tslicesByPool[poolID] = append(slicesByPool[poolID], slice)\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// We need to check whether a pool is complete while we have all\n    69\u2192\t// the slices. Once we discard slices that don't target the node, we\n    70\u2192\t// no longer have the information needed to find out.\n    71\u2192\tincompletePools := sets.New[poolIdentifier]()\n    72\u2192\tfor poolID, slices := range slicesByPool {\n    73\u2192\t\tcomplete := true\n    74\u2192\t\tsliceCount := len(slices)\n    75\u2192\t\tgeneration := slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation\n    76\u2192\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n    77\u2192\t\t\t// If the number of slices in the pool specified in any of the slices\n    78\u2192\t\t\t// doesn't match what we found, the pool is most likely being updated\n    79\u2192\t\t\t// by the controller.\n    80\u2192\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount != int64(sliceCount) {\n    81\u2192\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n    82\u2192\t\t\t}\n    83\u2192\t\t\t// If the generation of the pool isn't the same across all slices,\n    84\u2192\t\t\t// the pool is most likely being updated by the controller. We can't\n    85\u2192\t\t\t// allocate devices from it.\n    86\u2192\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation != generation {\n    87\u2192\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n    88\u2192\t\t\t}\n    89\u2192\t\t}\n    90\u2192\t\t// We still need to keep incomplete pools, since we need to make sure\n    91\u2192\t\t// all devices available on a node is considered for allocationMode All.\n    92\u2192\t\tif !complete {\n    93\u2192\t\t\tincompletePools.Insert(poolID)\n    94\u2192\t\t}\n    95\u2192\t}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n    98\u2192\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n    99\u2192\t\tif !features.PartitionableDevices && slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n   100\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   101\u2192\t\t}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\t\tif nodeName, allNodes := ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.NodeName, \"\"), ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.AllNodes, false); nodeName != \"\" || allNodes || slice.Spec.NodeSelector != nil {\n   104\u2192\t\t\tmatch, err := nodeMatches(node, nodeName, allNodes, slice.Spec.NodeSelector)\n   105\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   106\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to perform node selection for slice %s: %w\", slice.Name, err)\n   107\u2192\t\t\t}\n   108\u2192\t\t\tif match {\n   109\u2192\t\t\t\tif err := addSlice(pools, slice); err != nil {\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to add node slice %s: %w\", slice.Name, err)\n   111\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   112\u2192\t\t\t}\n   113\u2192\t\t} else if ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection, false) {\n   114\u2192\t\t\tfor _, device := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n   115\u2192\t\t\t\tmatch, err := nodeMatches(node, ptr.Deref(device.NodeName, \"\"), ptr.Deref(device.AllNodes, false), device.NodeSelector)\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to perform node selection for device %s in slice %s: %w\",\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tdevice.String(), slice.Name, err)\n   119\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   120\u2192\t\t\t\tif match {\n   121\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif err := addSlice(pools, slice); err != nil {\n   122\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to add node slice %s: %w\", slice.Name, err)\n   123\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   124\u2192\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n   125\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   126\u2192\t\t\t}\n   127\u2192\t\t} else {\n   128\u2192\t\t\t// Nothing known was set. This must be some future, unknown extension,\n   129\u2192\t\t\t// so we don't know how to handle it. We may still be able to allocated from\n   130\u2192\t\t\t// other pools, so we continue.\n   131\u2192\t\t\t//\n   132\u2192\t\t\t// TODO (eventually): let caller decide how to report this to the user. Warning\n   133\u2192\t\t\t// about it for every slice on each scheduling attempt would be too noisy, but\n   134\u2192\t\t\t// perhaps once per run would be useful?\n   135\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   136\u2192\t\t}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192\t}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192\t// Find incomplete pools and flatten into a single slice.\n   141\u2192\tresult := make([]*Pool, 0, len(pools))\n   142\u2192\tfor _, pool := range pools {\n   143\u2192\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = incompletePools.Has(poolIdentifier{driver: pool.Driver.String(), pool: pool.Pool.String()})\n   144\u2192\t\tpool.IsInvalid, pool.InvalidReason = poolIsInvalid(pool)\n   145\u2192\t\tresult = append(result, pool)\n   146\u2192\t}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\treturn result, nil\n   149\u2192}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192func addSlice(pools map[PoolID]*Pool, s *resourceapi.ResourceSlice) error {\n   152\u2192\tvar slice draapi.ResourceSlice\n   153\u2192\tif err := draapi.Convert_v1_ResourceSlice_To_api_ResourceSlice(s, &slice, nil); err != nil {\n   154\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"convert ResourceSlice: %w\", err)\n   155\u2192\t}\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192\tid := PoolID{Driver: slice.Spec.Driver, Pool: slice.Spec.Pool.Name}\n   158\u2192\tpool := pools[id]\n   159\u2192\tif pool == nil {\n   160\u2192\t\t// New pool.\n   161\u2192\t\tpool = &Pool{\n   162\u2192\t\t\tPoolID: id,\n   163\u2192\t\t\tSlices: []*draapi.ResourceSlice{&slice},\n   164\u2192\t\t}\n   165\u2192\t\tpools[id] = pool\n   166\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation < pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation {\n   170\u2192\t\t// Out-dated.\n   171\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   172\u2192\t}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation > pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation {\n   175\u2192\t\t// Newer, replaces all old slices.\n   176\u2192\t\tpool.Slices = nil\n   177\u2192\t}\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192\t// Add to pool.\n   180\u2192\tpool.Slices = append(pool.Slices, &slice)\n   181\u2192\treturn nil\n   182\u2192}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192func poolIsInvalid(pool *Pool) (bool, string) {\n   185\u2192\tdevices := sets.New[draapi.UniqueString]()\n   186\u2192\tfor _, slice := range pool.Slices {\n   187\u2192\t\tfor _, device := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n   188\u2192\t\t\tif devices.Has(device.Name) {\n   189\u2192\t\t\t\treturn true, fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate device name %s\", device.Name)\n   190\u2192\t\t\t}\n   191\u2192\t\t\tdevices.Insert(device.Name)\n   192\u2192\t\t}\n   193\u2192\t}\n   194\u2192\treturn false, \"\"\n   195\u2192}\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192type Pool struct {\n   198\u2192\tPoolID\n   199\u2192\tIsIncomplete  bool\n   200\u2192\tIsInvalid     bool\n   201\u2192\tInvalidReason string\n   202\u2192\tSlices        []*draapi.ResourceSlice\n   203\u2192}\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192type PoolID struct {\n   206\u2192\tDriver, Pool draapi.UniqueString\n   207\u2192}\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192func (p PoolID) String() string {\n   210\u2192\treturn p.Driver.String() + \"/\" + p.Pool.String()\n   211\u2192}\n   212\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   395\u2192\tcase resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount:\n   396\u2192\t\tnumDevices := request.count()\n   397\u2192\t\tif numDevices > math.MaxInt {\n   398\u2192\t\t\t// Allowed by API validation, but doesn't make sense.\n   399\u2192\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: exact count %d is too large\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), numDevices)\n   400\u2192\t\t}\n   401\u2192\t\trequestData.numDevices = int(numDevices)\n   402\u2192\tcase resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeAll:\n   403\u2192\t\t// If we have any any request that wants \"all\" devices, we need to\n   404\u2192\t\t// figure out how much \"all\" is. If some pool is incomplete, we stop\n   405\u2192\t\t// here because allocation cannot succeed. Once we do scoring, we should\n   406\u2192\t\t// stop in all cases, not just when \"all\" devices are needed, because\n   407\u2192\t\t// pulling from an incomplete might not pick the best solution and it's\n   408\u2192\t\t// better to wait. This does not matter yet as long the incomplete pool\n   409\u2192\t\t// has some matching device.\n   410\u2192\t\trequestData.allDevices = make([]deviceWithID, 0, resourceapi.AllocationResultsMaxSize)\n   411\u2192\t\tfor _, pool := range pools {\n   412\u2192\t\t\tif pool.IsIncomplete {\n   413\u2192\t\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently being updated\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), pool.PoolID)\n   414\u2192\t\t\t}\n   415\u2192\t\t\tif pool.IsInvalid {\n   416\u2192\t\t\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently invalid\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), pool.PoolID)\n   417\u2192\t\t\t}\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192\t\t\tfor _, slice := range pool.Slices {\n   420\u2192\t\t\t\tfor deviceIndex := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n   421\u2192\t\t\t\t\tselectable, err := alloc.isSelectable(requestKey, requestData, slice, deviceIndex)\n   422\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   423\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\treturn requestData, err\n   424\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   425\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif selectable {\n   426\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tdevice := deviceWithID{\n   427\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tid:     DeviceID{Driver: slice.Spec.Driver, Pool: slice.Spec.Pool.Name, Device: slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex].Name},\n   428\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDevice: &slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex],\n   429\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tslice:  slice,\n   430\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   431\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\trequestData.allDevices = append(requestData.allDevices, device)\n   432\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   433\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   434\u2192\t\t\t}\n   435\u2192\t\t}\n   436\u2192\t\trequestData.numDevices = len(requestData.allDevices)\n   437\u2192\t\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Request for 'all' devices\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"request\", request.name(), \"numDevicesPerRequest\", requestData.numDevices)\n   438\u2192\tdefault:\n   439\u2192\t\treturn requestData, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s, request %s: unsupported count mode %s\", klog.KObj(claim), request.name(), request.allocationMode())\n   440\u2192\t}\n   441\u2192\treturn requestData, nil\n   442\u2192}\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192// errStop is a special error that gets returned by allocateOne if it detects\n   445\u2192// that allocation cannot succeed.\n   446\u2192var errStop = errors.New(\"stop allocation\")\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192// allocator is used while an [Allocator.Allocate] is running. Only a single\n   449\u2192// goroutine works with it, so there is no need for locking.\n   450\u2192type allocator struct {\n   451\u2192\t*Allocator\n   452\u2192\tctx                  context.Context\n   453\u2192\tlogger               klog.Logger\n   454\u2192\tnode                 *v1.Node\n   455\u2192\tclaimsToAllocate     []*resourceapi.ResourceClaim\n   456\u2192\tpools                []*Pool\n   457\u2192\tdeviceMatchesRequest map[matchKey]bool\n   458\u2192\tconstraints          [][]constraint // one list of constraints per claim\n   459\u2192\t// consumedCounters keeps track of the counters consumed by all devices\n   460\u2192\t// that are in the process of being allocated.\n   461\u2192\t// The keys in the map are ResourceSlice names.\n   462\u2192\tconsumedCounters map[string]counterSets\n   463\u2192\trequestData      map[requestIndices]requestData // one entry per request with no subrequests and one entry per subrequest\n   464\u2192\t// allocatingDevices tracks which devices will be newly allocated for a\n   465\u2192\t// particular attempt to find a solution. The map is indexed by device\n   466\u2192\t// and its values represent for which of a pod's claims the device will\n   467\u2192\t// be allocated.\n   468\u2192\t// Claims are identified by their index in claimsToAllocate.\n   469\u2192\tallocatingDevices map[DeviceID]sets.Set[int]\n   470\u2192\tresult            []internalAllocationResult\n   471\u2192}\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192// counterSets is a map with the name of counter sets to the counters in\n   474\u2192// the set.\n   475\u2192type counterSets map[draapi.UniqueString]map[string]resourceapi.Counter\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192// matchKey identifies a device/request pair.\n   478\u2192type matchKey struct {\n   479\u2192\tDeviceID\n   480\u2192\trequestIndices\n   481\u2192}\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192// requestIndices identifies one specific request\n   484\u2192// or subrequest by three properties:\n   485\u2192//\n   486\u2192// - claimIndex: The index of the claim in the requestData map.\n   487\u2192// - requestIndex: The index of the request in the claim.\n   488\u2192// - subRequestIndex: The index of the subrequest in the parent request.\n   489\u2192type requestIndices struct {\n   490\u2192\tclaimIndex, requestIndex int\n   491\u2192\tsubRequestIndex          int\n   492\u2192}\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192// deviceIndices identifies one specific required device inside\n   495\u2192// a request or subrequest of a certain claim.\n   496\u2192type deviceIndices struct {\n   497\u2192\tclaimIndex      int // The index of the claim in the allocator.\n   498\u2192\trequestIndex    int // The index of the request in the claim.\n   499\u2192\tsubRequestIndex int // The index of the subrequest within the request (ignored if subRequest is false).\n   500\u2192\tdeviceIndex     int // The index of a device within a request or subrequest.\n   501\u2192}\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192type requestData struct {\n   504\u2192\t// The request or subrequest which needs to be allocated.\n   505\u2192\t// Never nil.\n   506\u2192\trequest requestAccessor\n   507\u2192\t// The parent of a subrequest, nil if not a subrequest.\n   508\u2192\tparentRequest requestAccessor\n   509\u2192\tclass         *resourceapi.DeviceClass\n   510\u2192\tnumDevices    int\n   511\u2192\n   512\u2192\t// selectedSubRequestIndex is set for the entry with requestIndices.subRequestIndex == 0.\n   513\u2192\t// It is the index of the subrequest which got picked during allocation.\n   514\u2192\tselectedSubRequestIndex int\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192\t// pre-determined set of devices for allocating \"all\" devices\n   517\u2192\tallDevices []deviceWithID\n   518\u2192}\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192type deviceWithID struct {\n   521\u2192\t*draapi.Device\n   522\u2192\tid    DeviceID\n   523\u2192\tslice *draapi.ResourceSlice\n   524\u2192}\n   525\u2192\n   526\u2192type internalAllocationResult struct {\n   527\u2192\tdevices []internalDeviceResult\n   528\u2192}\n   529\u2192\n   530\u2192type internalDeviceResult struct {\n   531\u2192\t*draapi.Device\n   532\u2192\trequest       string // name of the request (if no subrequests) or the subrequest\n   533\u2192\tparentRequest string // name of the request which contains the subrequest, empty otherwise\n   534\u2192\tid            DeviceID\n   535\u2192\tslice         *draapi.ResourceSlice\n   536\u2192\tadminAccess   *bool\n   537\u2192}\n   538\u2192\n   539\u2192func (i internalDeviceResult) requestName() string {\n   540\u2192\tif i.parentRequest == \"\" {\n   541\u2192\t\treturn i.request\n   542\u2192\t}\n   543\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", i.parentRequest, i.request)\n   544\u2192}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you "   730\u2192\t\t\t}\n   731\u2192\t\t}\n   732\u2192\t\t// This is unreachable, so no need to have a return statement here.\n   733\u2192\t}\n   734\u2192\n   735\u2192\t// Look up the current request that we are attempting to satisfy. This can\n   736\u2192\t// be either a request or a subrequest.\n   737\u2192\trequest := requestData.request\n   738\u2192\tdoAllDevices := request.allocationMode() == resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeAll\n   739\u2192\n   740\u2192\t// At least one device is required for 'All' allocation mode.\n   741\u2192\tif doAllDevices && len(requestData.allDevices) == 0 {\n   742\u2192\t\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Allocation for 'all' devices didn't succeed: no devices found\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"request\", requestData.request.name())\n   743\u2192\t\treturn false, nil\n   744\u2192\t}\n   745\u2192\n   746\u2192\t// We already know how many devices per request are needed.\n   747\u2192\tif r.deviceIndex >= requestData.numDevices {\n   748\u2192\t\t// Done with request, continue with next one. We have completed the work for\n   749\u2192\t\t// the request or subrequest, so we can no longer be allocating devices for\n   750\u2192\t\t// a subrequest.\n   751\u2192\t\treturn alloc.allocateOne(deviceIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex + 1}, false)\n   752\u2192\t}\n   753\u2192\n   754\u2192\t// We can calculate this by adding the number of already allocated devices with the number\n   755\u2192\t// of devices in the current request, and then finally subtract the deviceIndex since we\n   756\u2192\t// don't want to double count any devices already allocated for the current request.\n   757\u2192\tnumDevicesAfterAlloc := len(alloc.result[r.claimIndex].devices) + requestData.numDevices - r.deviceIndex\n   758\u2192\tif numDevicesAfterAlloc > resourceapi.AllocationResultsMaxSize {\n   759\u2192\t\t// Don't return an error here since we want to keep searching for\n   760\u2192\t\t// a solution that works.\n   761\u2192\t\treturn false, nil\n   762\u2192\t}\n   763\u2192\n   764\u2192\talloc.logger.V(6).Info(\"Allocating one device\", \"currentClaim\", r.claimIndex, \"totalClaims\", len(alloc.claimsToAllocate), \"currentRequest\", r.requestIndex, \"currentSubRequest\", r.subRequestIndex, \"totalRequestsPerClaim\", len(claim.Spec.Devices.Requests), \"currentDevice\", r.deviceIndex, \"devicesPerRequest\", requestData.numDevices, \"allDevices\", doAllDevices, \"adminAccess\", request.adminAccess())\n   765\u2192\tif doAllDevices {\n   766\u2192\t\t// For \"all\" devices we already know which ones we need. We\n   767\u2192\t\t// just need to check whether we can use them.\n   768\u2192\t\tdeviceWithID := requestData.allDevices[r.deviceIndex]\n   769\u2192\t\tsuccess, deallocate, err := alloc.allocateDevice(r, deviceWithID, true)\n   770\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   771\u2192\t\t\treturn false, err\n   772\u2192\t\t}\n   773\u2192\t\tif !success {\n   774\u2192\t\t\t// The order in which we allocate \"all\" devices doesn't matter,\n   775\u2192\t\t\t// so we only try with the one which was up next. If we couldn't\n   776\u2192\t\t\t// get all of them, then there is no solution and we have to stop.\n   777\u2192\t\t\treturn false, nil\n   778\u2192\t\t}\n   779\u2192\t\tdone, err := alloc.allocateOne(deviceIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex, deviceIndex: r.deviceIndex + 1}, allocateSubRequest)\n   780\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   781\u2192\t\t\treturn false, err\n   782\u2192\t\t}\n   783\u2192\t\tif !done {\n   784\u2192\t\t\t// Backtrack.\n   785\u2192\t\t\tdeallocate()\n   786\u2192\t\t\treturn false, nil\n   787\u2192\t\t}\n   788\u2192\t\treturn done, nil\n   789\u2192\t}\n   790\u2192\n   791\u2192\t// We need to find suitable devices.\n   792\u2192\tfor _, pool := range alloc.pools {\n   793\u2192\t\t// If the pool is not valid, then fail now. It's okay when pools of one driver\n   794\u2192\t\t// are invalid if we allocate from some other pool, but it's not safe to\n   795\u2192\t\t// allocated from an invalid pool.\n   796\u2192\t\tif pool.IsInvalid {\n   797\u2192\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pool %s is invalid: %s\", pool.Pool, pool.InvalidReason)\n   798\u2192\t\t}\n   799\u2192\t\tfor _, slice := range pool.Slices {\n   800\u2192\t\t\tfor deviceIndex := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n   801\u2192\t\t\t\tdeviceID := DeviceID{Driver: pool.Driver, Pool: pool.Pool, Device: slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex].Name}\n   802\u2192\n   803\u2192\t\t\t\t// Checking for \"in use\" is cheap and thus gets done first.\n   804\u2192\t\t\t\tif request.adminAccess() && alloc.allocatingDeviceForClaim(deviceID, r.claimIndex) {\n   805\u2192\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device in use in same claim\", \"device\", deviceID)\n   806\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   807\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   808\u2192\t\t\t\tif !request.adminAccess() && alloc.deviceInUse(deviceID) {\n   809\u2192\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device in use\", \"device\", deviceID)\n   810\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   811\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   812\u2192\n   813\u2192\t\t\t\t// Next check selectors.\n   814\u2192\t\t\t\trequestKey := requestIndices{claimIndex: r.claimIndex, requestIndex: r.requestIndex, subRequestIndex: r.subRequestIndex}\n   815\u2192\t\t\t\tselectable, err := alloc.isSelectable(requestKey, requestData, slice, deviceIndex)\n   816\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   817\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n   818\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   819\u2192\t\t\t\tif !selectable {\n   820\u2192\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device not selectable\", \"device\", deviceID)\n   821\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   822\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   823\u2192\n   824\u2192\t\t\t\t// Finally treat as allocated and move on to the next device.\n   825\u2192\t\t\t\tdevice := deviceWithID{\n   826\u2192\t\t\t\t\tid:     deviceID,\n   827\u2192\t\t\t\t\tDevice: &slice.Spec.Devices[deviceIndex],\n   828\u2192\t\t\t\t\tslice:  slice,\n   829\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   830\u2192\t\t\t\tallocated, deallocate, err := alloc.allocateDevice(r, device, false)\n   831\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   832\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n   833\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   834\u2192\t\t\t\tif !allocated {\n   835\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// In use or constraint violated...\n   836\u2192\t\t\t\t\talloc.logger.V(7).Info(\"Device not usable\", \"device\", deviceID)\n   837\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   838\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   839\u2192\t\t\t\tdeviceKey := deviceIndices{\n   840\u2192\t\t\t\t\tclaimIndex:      r.claimIndex,\n   841\u2192\t\t\t\t\trequestIndex:    r.requestIndex,\n   842\u2192\t\t\t\t\tsubRequestIndex: r.subRequestIndex,\n   843\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdeviceIndex:     r.deviceIndex + 1,\n   844\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   845\u2192\t\t\t\tdone, err := alloc.allocateOne(deviceKey, allocateSubRequest)\n   846\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   847\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n   848\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   849\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
