"f56e2545f09 DRA: integration: test implicit resources\nef68c5f91c4 test: integration: refactor DRA extended resource test\nbf7aed20399 DRA: add scheduler_perf benchmark for implicit resources\n14c6d99b8e9 DRA: Add integration tests for Partitionable Devices\na841d114205 Merge pull request #136619 from pohly/dra-allocator-promotion\ncabdd596c2a Merge pull request #136683 from pohly/dra-integration-timeouts\n47c3b10e8de DRA integration: increase timeout, II\naf6acb8016a Merge pull request #136624 from pohly/dra-integration-timeouts\nb96dd6d2f8e DRA integration: increase timeout\n858d26fd450 DRA structured: remove obsolete, confusing comment\n5c192392900 DRA allocator: promote experimental -> incubating -> stable\n9a1f9cbab6f Merge pull request #136541 from bart0sh/PR220-kubelet-dra-improve-contextual-logging\n0ac5e229e14 test(ut/dra): add unit test for pod requesting prepared and new claims\nfe7f31bdcb1 test(e2e/dra): add test for pod requesting allocated and new claims\n3b4e56e09c5 kubelet: DRA: plugin: set logging prefix"

b/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/experimental/pools_experimental.go\n@@ -45,6 +45,10 @@ func nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, node\n \treturn false, nil\n }\n \n+type poolIdentifier struct {\n+\tdriver, pool string\n+}\n+\n // GatherPools collects information about all resource pools which provide\n // devices that are accessible from the given node.\n //\n@@ -52,9 +56,46 @@ func nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, node\n // required slices available) or invalid (for example, device names not unique).\n // Both is recorded in the result.\n func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node *v1.Node, features Features) ([]*Pool, error) {\n+\tslicesByPool := make(map[poolIdentifier][]*resourceapi.ResourceSlice)\n+\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\tpoolID := poolIdentifier{\n+\t\t\tdriver: slice.Spec.Driver,\n+\t\t\tpool:   slice.Spec.Pool.Name,\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tslicesByPool[poolID] = append(slicesByPool[poolID], slice)\n+\t}\n+\n+\t// We need to check whether a pool is complete while we have all\n+\t// the slices. Once we discard slices that don't target the node, we\n+\t// no longer have the information needed to find out.\n+\tincompletePools := sets.New[poolIdentifier]()\n+\tfor poolID, slices := range slicesByPool {\n+\t\tcomplete := true\n+\t\tsliceCount := len(slices)\n+\t\tgeneration := slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation\n+\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\t\t// If the number of slices in the pool specified in any of the slices\n+\t\t\t// doesn't match what we found, the pool is most likely being updated\n+\t\t\t// by the controller.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount != int64(sliceCount) {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// If the generation of the pool isn't the same across all slices,\n+\t\t\t// the pool is most likely being updated by the controller. We can't\n+\t\t\t// allocate devices from it.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation != generation {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\t// We still need to keep incomplete pools, since we need to make sure\n+\t\t// all devices available on a node is considered for allocationMode All.\n+\t\tif !complete {\n+\t\t\tincompletePools.Insert(poolID)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\n \tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n \tvar slicesWithBindingConditions []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice\n-\n \tfor _, slice := range slices {\n \t\tif !features.PartitionableDevices && slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n \t\t\tcontinue\n@@ -121,7 +162,7 @@ func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node\n \tresult := make([]*Pool, 0, len(pools))\n \tvar resultWithBindingConditions []*Pool\n \tfor _, pool := range pools {\n-\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = int64(len(pool.Slices)) != pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount\n+\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = incompletePools.Has(poolIdentifier{driver: pool.Driver.String(), pool: pool.Pool.String()})\n \t\tpool.IsInvalid, pool.InvalidReason = poolIsInvalid(pool)\n \t\t// if pool has binding conditions, add the pool to the end of the result\n \t\tif poolHasBindingConditions(*pool) {\ndiff --git a/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/incubating/pools_incubating.go node\n \treturn false, nil\n }\n \n+type poolIdentifier struct {\n+\tdriver, pool string\n+}\n+\n // GatherPools collects information about all resource pools which provide\n // devices that are accessible from the given node.\n //\n@@ -52,8 +56,45 @@ func nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, node\n // required slices available) or invalid (for example, device names not unique).\n // Both is recorded in the result.\n func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node *v1.Node, features Features) ([]*Pool, error) {\n-\tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n+\tslicesByPool := make(map[poolIdentifier][]*resourceapi.ResourceSlice)\n+\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\tpoolID := poolIdentifier{\n+\t\t\tdriver: slice.Spec.Driver,\n+\t\t\tpool:   slice.Spec.Pool.Name,\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tslicesByPool[poolID] = append(slicesByPool[poolID], slice)\n+\t}\n \n+\t// We need to check whether a pool is complete while we have all\n+\t// the slices. Once we discard slices that don't target the node, we\n+\t// no longer have the information needed to find out.\n+\tincompletePools := sets.New[poolIdentifier]()\n+\tfor poolID, slices := range slicesByPool {\n+\t\tcomplete := true\n+\t\tsliceCount := len(slices)\n+\t\tgeneration := slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation\n+\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\t\t// If the number of slices in the pool specified in any of the slices\n+\t\t\t// doesn't match what we found, the pool is most likely being updated\n+\t\t\t// by the controller.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount != int64(sliceCount) {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// If the generation of the pool isn't the same across all slices,\n+\t\t\t// the pool is most likely being updated by the controller. We can't\n+\t\t\t// allocate devices from it.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation != generation {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\t// We still need to keep incomplete pools, since we need to make sure\n+\t\t// all devices available on a node is considered for allocationMode All.\n+\t\tif !complete {\n+\t\t\tincompletePools.Insert(poolID)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\n+\tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n \tfor _, slice := range slices {\n \t\tif !features.PartitionableDevices && slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n \t\t\tcontinue\n@@ -99,7 +140,7 @@ func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node\n \t// Find incomplete pools and flatten into a single slice.\n \tresult := make([]*Pool, 0, len(pools))\n \tfor _, pool := range pools {\n-\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = int64(len(pool.Slices)) != pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount\n+\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = incompletePools.Has(poolIdentifier{driver: pool.Driver.String(), pool: pool.Pool.String()})\n \t\tpool.IsInvalid, pool.InvalidReason = poolIsInvalid(pool)\n \t\tresult = append(result, pool)\n \t}\ndiff --git a/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go b/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go\nindex 57dbf31260e..2941a4819c8 100644\n--- a/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go\n+++ b/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go\n@@ -45,6 +45,10 @@ func nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, node\n \treturn false, nil"
36. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Analyze downstream consumers and performance implications",
  "prompt": "Continue the investigation by analyzing:\n\n1. **Downstream Consumers:** Find where device allocations are consumed by:\n   - Kubelet - how does it use allocated devices?\n   - Device plugins - how do they interact with the scheduler?\n   - Pod admission controllers\n   - Search for \"DeviceAllocationMode\" or \"AllocationMode\" usage in kubelet code\n\n2. **Scheduler Hot Paths:** In the DRA scheduler plugin:\n   - What are the performance-critical paths?\n   - How does device filtering/matching work?\n   - Are there any caching mechanisms?\n   - Lines where AllocationMode.All is checked or used in allocation loops\n\n3. **API Changes and Validation:** Look for:\n   - Validation code that enforces single-node restrictions on AllocationMode.All\n   - Where these validation rules might be (API validation, webhook validation)\n   - Deprecation or feature gate handling\n   - Any compatibility concerns\n\n4. **ResourceClaim and Device Request Flow:** Trace how:\n   - ResourceClaim flows from user pod to scheduler\n   - How DeviceRequest with AllocationMode affects scheduling\n   - Where the actual device assignment happens\n\nFocus on files in: pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/, pkg/kubelet/, staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a3d3a09",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Now let me create a comprehensive summary document based on my findings:\n\n## COMPREHENSIVE INVESTIGATION REPORT: Device Allocation and AllocationMode.All\n\nBased on my extensive code analysis of the Kubernetes DRA (Dynamic Resource Allocation) codebase, here's a detailed investigation of the downstream consumers, scheduler hot paths, API validation, and ResourceClaim flow:\n\n---\n\n### 1. DOWNSTREAM CONSUMERS: How Device Allocations Are Consumed\n\n#### **1.1 Kubelet Integration (pkg/kubelet/allocation/)**\n\nThe kubelet consumes device allocations through the **AllocationManager** (`allocation_manager.go`):\n\n- **Manager Interface** provides methods for:\n  - `GetContainerResourceAllocation()` - Retrieves allocated resources per container\n  - `UpdatePodFromAllocation()` - Updates pod specs with allocation data\n  - `SetAllocatedResources()` - Checkpoints allocated resources to pods\n  - `AddPod()` - Pod admission with resource availability checking\n  - `RemovePod()` - Cleanup of pod allocations\n\n- **State Tracking**: Uses checkpoint-based state management (`state/` directory) to persist allocations across kubelet restarts\n- **Pod-to-Allocation Mapping**: Maintains allocation state indexed by pod UID and container name\n\n#### **1.2 Scheduler Integration (pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/)**\n\nThe DRA scheduler plugin manages allocations through:\n\n- **allocatedDevices** (`allocateddevices.go`): Caches all allocated devices across the cluster\n  - Uses `DeviceID` (driver/pool/device tuple) as keys\n  - For consumable capacity features, also tracks `SharedDeviceID` (adds ShareID) and consumed capacities\n  - Thread-safe with RWMutex for concurrent read access during filtering\n\n- **Filter Phase** (`dynamicresources.go:884-1019`):\n  - Calls `state.allocator.Allocate()` to attempt device allocation per node\n  - Caches results in `nodeAllocations` map (keyed by node name)\n  - Returns Unschedulable if any claims can't be allocated on the node\n\n- **Reserve Phase** (`dynamicresources.go:1085-1181`):\n  - Signals pending allocations via `SignalClaimPendingAllocation()`\n  - Stores allocation results in claim status\n\n- **PreBind Phase** (`dynamicresources.go:1290-1348`):\n  - Actually reserves claims by updating ResourceClaim status\n  - Handles binding conditions if device attachment is required\n  - Creates extended resource claims for DRA-backed extended resources\n\n#### **1.3 Device Plugins & Plugin Controllers**\n\n- Allocation is primarily **scheduler-driven** for DRA claims\n- Device plugins interact via:\n  - **ResourceSlice API**: Report available devices with attributes/capacities\n  - **ResourceClaim webhooks**: Can validate/mutate claims\n  - **Kubelet plugins**: Process device configuration and setup via device plugin protocol\n\n---\n\n### 2. SCHEDULER HOT PATHS AND PERFORMANCE\n\n#### **2.1 Performance-Critical Paths in DRA Scheduler**\n\n**PreFilter (allocator setup)**:\n```go\n// pk/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/dynamicresources.go:627-679\n- GatherAllocatedState() - Lists all allocated devices globally\n- structured.NewAllocator() - Creates allocator per scheduling cycle\n- Allocator caches CEL expressions for device selectors\n```\n\n**Filter (per-node allocation)**:\n```go\n// Line 964: state.allocator.Allocate(ctx, node, claimsToAllocate)\n- For each pod-node pair, allocates devices\n- Validates device selectors via CEL expressions\n- Checks remaining device capacity\n- Returns all-or-nothing allocation (transaction-like)\n```\n\n**Caching Mechanisms**:\n- **CEL Expression Cache** (`celCache` with 10-item LRU in dynamicresources.go:201)\n- **nodeAllocations Map** (line 678): Caches Filter phase results per node\n- **Allocated Devices Cache** (`allocatedDevices` singleton): Global device allocation state\n\n#### **2.2 AllocationMode.All - Special Case Performance Impact**\n\n**Before Change (Commit 2e534d6da10 - REVERTED)**:\n- AllocationMode.All was only allowed for **single-node pools**\n- Pool completeness checked: `pool.IsIncomplete = int64(len(pool.Slices)) != pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount`\n- Only filters pool slices matching the target node \u2192 incomplete pools rejected for AllocationMode.All\n\n**After Change (2e534d6da10)**:\n- Allowed AllocationMode.All from **multi-node pools**\n- Pool completeness now checked globally across all slices (before node filtering)\n- Introduced `slicesByPool` map to track pool state across all nodes\n- Checks pool generation consistency\n\n**Performance Regression (Commit a0e500ace39 - REVERT)**:\n- The change was **reverted** due to **performance regression**\n- Root issue: Gathering pool information across all nodes before filtering is expensive\n- Needs a different approach (currently under investigation)\n\n---\n\n### 3. API VALIDATION AND ALLOCATION MODE RESTRICTIONS\n\n#### **3.1 Validation Code Structure**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go`\n\n**validateDeviceAllocationMode()** (lines 268-286):\n```go\nfunc validateDeviceAllocationMode(deviceAllocationMode resource.DeviceAllocationMode, count int64, allocModeFldPath, countFldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n    var allErrs field.ErrorList\n    switch deviceAllocationMode {\n    case resource.DeviceAllocationModeAll:\n        if count != 0 {\n            // Error: count must not be specified\n        }\n    case resource.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount:\n        if count <= 0 {\n            // Error: count must be greater than zero\n        }\n    default:\n        // Not supported\n    }\n    return allErrs\n}\n```\n\n**Key Validation Rules**:\n1. **For DeviceAllocationModeAll**:\n   - `count` field MUST be 0 (line 272-274)\n   - ONLY devices in single-node pools can be allocated this way (enforced in allocator, not validation)\n   - All matching devices in pool must be available\n\n2. **For DeviceAllocationModeExactCount**:\n   - `count` field MUST be > 0 (line 276-278)\n   - Allocates exactly N matching devices\n\n#### **3.2 Where Validation Happens**\n\n- **API Server** (`ValidateResourceClaim` - line 109): Called on create/update\n- **Validation Webhook** (optional): CRD validation rules (in `zz_generated.validations.go`)\n- **Scheduler** (Filter phase): No additional validation, trusts API server\n\n#### **3.3 Multi-Node Restriction Status**\n\n**Current State (as of HEAD)**:\n- NO validation rule explicitly prevents AllocationMode.All from multi-node pools at API level\n- Restriction is **enforced in allocator logic** (in structured/internal/*/pools_*.go)\n- **WAS LIFTED** in commit 2e534d6da10 but **REVERTED** in a0e500ace39\n\n**Allocator-Level Enforcement**:\nThe allocator marks pools as \"incomplete\" if they span multiple nodes, and AllocationMode.All rejects incomplete pools (this logic was modified but reverted).\n\n---\n\n### 4. RESOURCE CLAIM AND DEVICE REQUEST FLOW\n\n#### **4.1 Pod to Scheduler Pipeline**\n\n```\nPod.Spec.ResourceClaims[] \n    \u2193\n[DynamicResourceAllocation admission controller]\n    \u2193 (creates ResourceClaim if from template)\nResourceClaim.Spec.Devices.Requests[]\n    \u2193\n[Scheduler PreFilter: pl.podResourceClaims()]\n    \u2193 (validates classes exist)\n[Scheduler Filter: state.allocator.Allocate()]\n    \u2193 (finds matching devices)\nResourceClaim.Status.Allocation.Devices.Results[]\n    \u2193 (per-device allocation)\n[Scheduler Reserve: pl.SignalClaimPendingAllocation()]\n    \u2193 (marks as \"in-flight\")\n[Scheduler PreBind: pl.bindClaim()]\n    \u2193 (updates claim status)\nResourceClaim.Status.ReservedFor[] + Allocation\n    \u2193\n[Kubelet: UpdatePodFromAllocation()]\n    \u2193 (reads allocation)\nPod.Spec.Containers[].Resources.Allocated\n```\n\n#### **4.2 Device Request Structure**\n\n```go\ntype DeviceRequest struct {\n    Name string\n    Exactly *ExactDeviceRequest  // One of these two\n    FirstAvailable []DeviceSubRequest\n}\n\ntype ExactDeviceRequest struct {\n    DeviceClassName string\n    Selectors []DeviceSelector  // CEL expressions\n    AllocationMode DeviceAllocationMode  // \"All\" or \"ExactCount\"\n    Count int64  // Only for ExactCount\n    AdminAccess *bool\n    Tolerations []DeviceToleration\n}\n```\n\n#### **4.3 Allocation Result Binding**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types.go` (lines 1486-1658)\n\n```go\ntype AllocationResult struct {\n    Devices DeviceAllocationResult\n    NodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector  // Restricts to nodes with allocated devices\n}\n\ntype DeviceRequestAllocationResult struct {\n    Request string  // \"req0\" or \"req0/subreq1\"\n    Driver string\n    Pool string\n    Device string\n    AdminAccess *bool\n    Tolerations []DeviceToleration\n    BindingConditions []string\n    ShareID *types.UID  // For consumable capacity\n    ConsumedCapacity map[QualifiedName]resource.Quantity\n}\n```\n\n**Allocation Flow**:\n1. Allocator selects devices matching selectors\n2. For AllocationMode.All: allocates ALL matching devices on target node\n3. For AllocationMode.ExactCount: allocates exactly N matching devices\n4. Returns NodeSelector restricting claim usage to allocated node\n5. Kubelet reads allocation and configures container\n\n---\n\n### 5. KEY FINDINGS AND IMPLICATIONS\n\n#### **Major Insight: AllocationMode.All and Multi-Node Pools**\n\nThe commit history reveals a **known limitation**:\n\n1. **Original Design**: AllocationMode.All only for single-node pools\n   - Simpler allocation logic\n   - No performance overhead\n\n2. **Expansion Attempt (2e534d6da10)**:\n   - Tried to support multi-node pools with AllocationMode.All\n   - Required global pool state tracking across nodes\n   - **Caused performance regression** (allocating on every node becomes expensive)\n\n3. **Current Status (a0e500ace39 - HEAD)**:\n   - Reverted the change\n   - Back to single-node-only restriction\n   - \"A different fix is needed\"\n   - Indicates this is an active development area\n\n#### **Performance-Critical Path: Pool Gathering**\n\nThe **GatherPools()** function in allocator is critical:\n- Called during Filter phase for every pod-node pair\n- Must determine which devices are available on the node\n- For multi-node pools with AllocationMode.All, needs to know the global pool state\n- Previous optimization attempted to track this globally, but it caused regression\n\n#### **No API-Level Multi-Node Restriction**\n\n**Important**: There is **NO API validation** preventing AllocationMode.All on multi-node pools. The restriction is **algorithm-level** (allocator rejects incomplete pools for AllocationMode.All).\n\nThis means:\n- Users CAN create ResourceClaims with AllocationMode.All on multi-node pools\n- Scheduler will reject allocation as \"incomplete pool\"\n- Pod becomes unschedulable\n\n---\n\n### 6. FILES SUMMARY\n\n**Key Files Analyzed**:\n\n| File | Role |\n|------|------|\n| `pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go` | API validation (count field) |\n| `pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/dynamicresources.go` | Scheduler hot path (Filter/Reserve/PreBind) |\n| `pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/allocateddevices.go` | Allocated device cache (thread-safe) |\n| `staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/*/pools_*.go` | Allocator pool gathering (multi-node restriction enforced here) |\n| `pkg/kubelet/allocation/allocation_manager.go` | Kubelet allocation tracking |\n| `staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types.go` | AllocationResult API schema |\n\n**Performance-Critical Code**:\n- DRA Scheduler Filter: 1000+ lines, O(devices \u00d7 selectors) complexity\n- Pool gathering: GatherPools() must check device validity for each node\n- CEL evaluation: Cached but evaluated per device per request\n\nThis investigation reveals a system designed for single-node optimization with ongoing work to extend multi-node support without performance regression.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Continue the investigation by analyzing:\n\n1. **Downstream Consumers:** Find where device allocations are consumed by:\n   - Kubelet - how does it use allocated devices?\n   - Device plugins - how do they interact with the scheduler?\n   - Pod admission controllers\n   - Search for \"DeviceAllocationMode\" or \"AllocationMode\" usage in kubelet code\n\n2. **Scheduler Hot Paths:** In the DRA scheduler plugin:\n   - What are the performance-critical paths?\n   - How does device filtering/matching work?\n   - Are there any caching mechanisms?\n   - Lines where AllocationMode.All is checked or used in allocation loops\n\n3. **API Changes and Validation:** Look for:\n   - Validation code that enforces single-node restrictions on AllocationMode.All\n   - Where these validation rules might be (API validation, webhook validation)\n   - Deprecation or feature gate handling\n   - Any compatibility concerns\n\n4. **ResourceClaim and Device Request Flow:** Trace how:\n   - ResourceClaim flows from user pod to scheduler\n   - How DeviceRequest with AllocationMode affects scheduling\n   - Where the actual device assignment happens\n\nFocus on files in: pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/, pkg/kubelet/, staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/",
  "status": "completed",
  "totalDurationMs": 62014,
  "totalTokens": 132600,
  "totalToolUseCount": 24,
  "usage": {

      "web_search_requests": 0
    },
    "service_tier": "standard"
  }
}

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package validation\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"encoding/json\"\n    22\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    23\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    24\u2192\t\"regexp\"\n    25\u2192\t\"slices\"\n    26\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    27\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    32\u2192\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/operation\"\n    34\u2192\tapiresource \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validate\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validate/content\"\n    37\u2192\tapimachineryvalidation \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\"\n    38\u2192\tmetav1validation \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/validation\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/cel\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/cel/environment\"\n    46\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    47\u2192\tdracel \"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/cel\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured\"\n    49\u2192\tv1helper \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\"\n    50\u2192\tcorevalidation \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/validation\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/resource\"\n    52\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n    53\u2192)\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192// ResourceNormalizationRules handles the structural differences between v1beta1\n    56\u2192// (flattened fields) and v1/v1beta2 (fields under 'exactly') for validation error paths.\n    57\u2192var ResourceNormalizationRules = []field.NormalizationRule{\n    58\u2192\t{\n    59\u2192\t\tRegexp:      regexp.MustCompile(`spec.devices\\.requests\\[(\\d+)\\]\\.(deviceClassName|selectors|allocationMode|count|adminAccess|tolerations)`),\n    60\u2192\t\tReplacement: \"spec.devices.requests[$1].exactly.$2\",\n    61\u2192\t},\n    62\u2192\t{\n    63\u2192\t\t// This v1beta1 'basic' to flattened rule is to support ResourceSlice\n    64\u2192\t\tRegexp:      regexp.MustCompile(`spec.devices\\[(\\d+)\\]\\.basic\\.`),\n    65\u2192\t\tReplacement: \"spec.devices[$1].\",\n    66\u2192\t},\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192var (\n    70\u2192\t// validateResourceDriverName reuses the validation of a CSI driver because\n    71\u2192\t// the allowed values are exactly the same.\n    72\u2192\tvalidateDriverName      = corevalidation.ValidateCSIDriverName\n    73\u2192\tvalidateDeviceName      = corevalidation.ValidateDNS1123Label\n    74\u2192\tvalidateDeviceClassName = corevalidation.ValidateDNS1123Subdomain\n    75\u2192\tvalidateRequestName     = corevalidation.ValidateDNS1123Label\n    76\u2192\tvalidateCounterName     = corevalidation.ValidateDNS1123Label\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\t// this is the max length limit for domain/ID\n    79\u2192\tattributeAndCapacityMaxKeyLength = resource.DeviceMaxDomainLength + 1 + resource.DeviceMaxIDLength\n    80\u2192)\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192func validatePoolName(name string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n    83\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n    84\u2192\tif name == \"\" {\n    85\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"\"))\n    86\u2192\t} else {\n    87\u2192\t\tif len(name) > resource.PoolNameMaxLength {\n    88\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath, \"\" /*unused*/, resource.PoolNameMaxLength).WithOrigin(\"format=k8s-resource-pool-name\"))\n    89\u2192\t\t}\n    90\u2192\t\tparts := strings.Split(name, \"/\")\n    91\u2192\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n    92\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, corevalidation.ValidateDNS1123Subdomain(part, fldPath).WithOrigin(\"format=k8s-resource-pool-name\")...)\n    93\u2192\t\t}\n    94\u2192\t}\n    95\u2192\treturn allErrs\n    96\u2192}\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192func validateUID(uid string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n    99\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   100\u2192\tif err := uuid.Validate(uid); err != nil {\n   101\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, uid, fmt.Sprintf(\"error validating uid: %v\", err)))\n   102\u2192\t} else if len(uid) != 36 || uid != strings.ToLower(uid) {\n   103\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, uid, \"uid must be in RFC 4122 normalized form, `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx` with lowercase hexadecimal characters\"))\n   104\u2192\t}\n   105\u2192\treturn allErrs.WithOrigin(\"format=k8s-uuid\")\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192// ValidateResourceClaim validates a ResourceClaim.\n   109\u2192func ValidateResourceClaim(resourceClaim *resource.ResourceClaim) field.ErrorList {\n   110\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMeta(&resourceClaim.ObjectMeta, true, corevalidation.ValidateResourceClaimName, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   111\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourceClaimSpec(&resourceClaim.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\"), false)...)\n   112\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   113\u2192}\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192// ValidateResourceClaimUpdate tests if an update to ResourceClaim is valid.\n   116\u2192func ValidateResourceClaimUpdate(resourceClaim, oldClaim *resource.ResourceClaim) field.ErrorList {\n   117\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMeta(&resourceClaim.ObjectMeta, true, corevalidation.ValidateResourceClaimName, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   118\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, corevalidation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(&resourceClaim.ObjectMeta, &oldClaim.ObjectMeta, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))...)\n   119\u2192\t// The spec is immutable. On update, we only check for immutability.\n   120\u2192\t// Re-validating other fields is skipped because the user cannot change them;\n   121\u2192\t// the only actionable error is for the immutability violation.\n   122\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(resourceClaim.Spec, oldClaim.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\")).WithOrigin(\"immutable\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n   123\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   124\u2192}\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192// ValidateResourceClaimStatusUpdate tests if an update to the status of a ResourceClaim is valid.\n   127\u2192func ValidateResourceClaimStatusUpdate(resourceClaim, oldClaim *resource.ResourceClaim) field.ErrorList {\n   128\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(&resourceClaim.ObjectMeta, &oldClaim.ObjectMeta, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   129\u2192\trequestNames := gatherRequestNames(&resourceClaim.Spec.Devices)\n   130\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourceClaimStatusUpdate(&resourceClaim.Status, &oldClaim.Status, resourceClaim.DeletionTimestamp != nil, requestNames, field.NewPath(\"status\"))...)\n   131\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   132\u2192}\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192func validateResourceClaimSpec(spec *resource.ResourceClaimSpec, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   135\u2192\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n   136\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceClaim(&spec.Devices, fldPath.Child(\"devices\"), stored)...)\n   137\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   138\u2192}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192func validateDeviceClaim(deviceClaim *resource.DeviceClaim, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   141\u2192\tallErrs := field.ErrorList{}\n   142\u2192\trequestNames := gatherRequestNames(deviceClaim)\n   143\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(deviceClaim.Requests, resource.DeviceRequestsMaxSize,\n   144\u2192\t\tfunc(request resource.DeviceRequest, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   145\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceRequest(request, fldPath, stored)\n   146\u2192\t\t},\n   147\u2192\t\tfunc(request resource.DeviceRequest) string {\n   148\u2192\t\t\treturn request.Name\n   149\u2192\t\t},\n   150\u2192\t\tfldPath.Child(\"requests\"), sizeCovered, uniquenessCovered)...)\n   151\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(deviceClaim.Constraints, resource.DeviceConstraintsMaxSize,\n   152\u2192\t\tfunc(constraint resource.DeviceConstraint, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   153\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceConstraint(constraint, fldPath, requestNames)\n   154\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"constraints\"), sizeCovered)...)\n   155\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(deviceClaim.Config, resource.DeviceConfigMaxSize,\n   156\u2192\t\tfunc(config resource.DeviceClaimConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   157\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceClaimConfiguration(config, fldPath, requestNames, stored)\n   158\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"config\"), sizeCovered)...)\n   159\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   160\u2192}\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192type requestNames map[string]sets.Set[string]\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192func (r requestNames) Has(s string) bool {\n   165\u2192\tsegments := strings.Split(s, \"/\")\n   166\u2192\t// If there are more than one / in the string, we\n   167\u2192\t// know there can't be any match.\n   168\u2192\tif len(segments) > 2 {\n   169\u2192\t\treturn false\n   170\u2192\t}\n   171\u2192\t// If the first segment doesn't have a match, we\n   172\u2192\t// don't need to check the other one.\n   173\u2192\tsubRequestNames, found := r[segments[0]]\n   174\u2192\tif !found {\n   175\u2192\t\treturn false\n   176\u2192\t}\n   177\u2192\tif len(segments) == 1 {\n   178\u2192\t\treturn true\n   179\u2192\t}\n   180\u2192\t// If the first segment matched and we have another one,\n   181\u2192\t// check for a match for that too.\n   182\u2192\treturn subRequestNames.Has(segments[1])\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192func gatherRequestNames(deviceClaim *resource.DeviceClaim) requestNames {\n   186\u2192\trequestNames := make(requestNames)\n   187\u2192\tfor _, request := range deviceClaim.Requests {\n   188\u2192\t\tif len(request.FirstAvailable) == 0 {\n   189\u2192\t\t\trequestNames[request.Name] = nil\n   190\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   191\u2192\t\t}\n   192\u2192\t\tsubRequestNames := sets.New[string]()\n   193\u2192\t\tfor _, subRequest := range request.FirstAvailable {\n   194\u2192\t\t\tsubRequestNames.Insert(subRequest.Name)\n   195\u2192\t\t}\n   196\u2192\t\trequestNames[request.Name] = subRequestNames\n   197\u2192\t}\n   198\u2192\treturn requestNames\n   199\u2192}\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192func gatherAllocatedDevices(allocationResult *resource.DeviceAllocationResult) sets.Set[structured.SharedDeviceID] {\n   202\u2192\tallocatedDevices := sets.New[structured.SharedDeviceID]()\n   203\u2192\tfor _, result := range allocationResult.Results {\n   204\u2192\t\tdeviceName := result.Device\n   205\u2192\t\tdeviceID := structured.MakeDeviceID(result.Driver, result.Pool, deviceName)\n   206\u2192\t\tsharedDeviceID := structured.MakeSharedDeviceID(deviceID, result.ShareID)\n   207\u2192\t\tallocatedDevices.Insert(sharedDeviceID)\n   208\u2192\t}\n   209\u2192\treturn allocatedDevices\n   210\u2192}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192func validateDeviceRequest(request resource.DeviceRequest, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   213\u2192\tallErrs := validateRequestName(request.Name, fldPath.Child(\"name\"))\n   214\u2192\tnumDeviceRequestType := 0\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192\thasFirstAvailable := len(request.FirstAvailable) > 0\n   217\u2192\thasExactly := request.Exactly != nil\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192\tif hasFirstAvailable {\n   220\u2192\t\tnumDeviceRequestType++\n   221\u2192\t}\n   222\u2192\tif hasExactly {\n   223\u2192\t\tnumDeviceRequestType++\n   224\u2192\t}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\tswitch {\n   227\u2192\tcase numDeviceRequestType == 0:\n   228\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"exactly one of `exactly` or `firstAvailable` is required\"))\n   229\u2192\tcase numDeviceRequestType > 1:\n   230\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, nil, \"exactly one of `exactly` or `firstAvailable` is required, but multiple fields are set\"))\n   231\u2192\tcase hasFirstAvailable:\n   232\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(request.FirstAvailable, resource.FirstAvailableDeviceRequestMaxSize,\n   233\u2192\t\t\tfunc(subRequest resource.DeviceSubRequest, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   234\u2192\t\t\t\treturn validateDeviceSubRequest(subRequest, fldPath, stored)\n   235\u2192\t\t\t},\n   236\u2192\t\t\tfunc(subRequest resource.DeviceSubRequest) string {\n   237\u2192\t\t\t\treturn subRequest.Name\n   238\u2192\t\t\t},\n   239\u2192\t\t\tfldPath.Child(\"firstAvailable\"), sizeCovered, uniquenessCovered)...)\n   240\u2192\tcase hasExactly:\n   241\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateExactDeviceRequest(*request.Exactly, fldPath.Child(\"exactly\"), stored)...)\n   242\u2192\t}\n   243\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   244\u2192}\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192func validateDeviceSubRequest(subRequest resource.DeviceSubRequest, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   247\u2192\tallErrs := validateRequestName(subRequest.Name, fldPath.Child(\"name\"))\n   248\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceClass(subRequest.DeviceClassName, fldPath.Child(\"deviceClassName\")).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n   249\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSelectorSlice(subRequest.Selectors, fldPath.Child(\"selectors\"), stored)...)\n   250\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceAllocationMode(subRequest.AllocationMode, subRequest.Count, fldPath.Child(\"allocationMode\"), fldPath.Child(\"count\"))...)\n   251\u2192\tfor i, toleration := range subRequest.Tolerations {\n   252\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceToleration(toleration, fldPath.Child(\"tolerations\").Index(i))...)\n   253\u2192\t}\n   254\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   255\u2192}\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192func validateExactDeviceRequest(request resource.ExactDeviceRequest, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   258\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   259\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceClass(request.DeviceClassName, fldPath.Child(\"deviceClassName\"))...)\n   260\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSelectorSlice(request.Selectors, fldPath.Child(\"selectors\"), stored)...)\n   261\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceAllocationMode(request.AllocationMode, request.Count, fldPath.Child(\"allocationMode\"), fldPath.Child(\"count\"))...)\n   262\u2192\tfor i, toleration := range request.Tolerations {\n   263\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceToleration(toleration, fldPath.Child(\"tolerations\").Index(i))...)\n   264\u2192\t}\n   265\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   266\u2192}\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192func validateDeviceAllocationMode(deviceAllocationMode resource.DeviceAllocationMode, count int64, allocModeFldPath, countFldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   269\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   270\u2192\tswitch deviceAllocationMode {\n   271\u2192\tcase resource.DeviceAllocationModeAll:\n   272\u2192\t\tif count != 0 {\n   273\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(countFldPath, count, fmt.Sprintf(\"must not be specified when allocationMode is '%s'\", deviceAllocationMode)))\n   274\u2192\t\t}\n   275\u2192\tcase resource.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount:\n   276\u2192\t\tif count <= 0 {\n   277\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(countFldPath, count, \"must be greater than zero\"))\n   278\u2192\t\t}\n   279\u2192\tdefault:\n   280\u2192\t\t// NOTE: Declarative validation does not (yet) enforce the real requiredness of this field\n   281\u2192\t\t// because v1beta1 uses a bespoke form of union which makes this field truly optional\n   282\u2192\t\t// in some cases and truly required in others. DO NOT REMOVE THIS CODE UNLESS THAT IS RESOLVED.\n   283\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(allocModeFldPath, deviceAllocationMode, []resource.DeviceAllocationMode{resource.DeviceAllocationModeAll, resource.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount}).MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n   284\u2192\t}\n   285\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   286\u2192}\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192func validateDeviceClass(deviceClass string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   289\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   290\u2192\tif deviceClass == \"\" {\n   291\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"\"))\n   292\u2192\t} else {\n   293\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceClassName(deviceClass, fldPath)...)\n   294\u2192\t}\n   295\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   296\u2192}\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192func validateSelectorSlice(selectors []resource.DeviceSelector, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   299\u2192\treturn validateSlice(selectors, resource.DeviceSelectorsMaxSize,\n   300\u2192\t\tfunc(selector resource.DeviceSelector, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   301\u2192\t\t\treturn validateSelector(selector, fldPath, stored)\n   302\u2192\t\t},\n   303\u2192\t\tfldPath, sizeCovered)\n   304\u2192}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192func validateSelector(selector resource.DeviceSelector, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   307\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   308\u2192\tif selector.CEL == nil {\n   309\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"cel\"), \"\"))\n   310\u2192\t} else {\n   311\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateCELSelector(*selector.CEL, fldPath.Child(\"cel\"), stored)...)\n   312\u2192\t}\n   313\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   314\u2192}\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192func validateCELSelector(celSelector resource.CELDeviceSelector, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   317\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   318\u2192\tenvType := environment.NewExpressions\n   319\u2192\tif stored {\n   320\u2192\t\tenvType = environment.StoredExpressions\n   321\u2192\t}\n   322\u2192\tif len(celSelector.Expression) > resource.CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength {\n   323\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath.Child(\"expression\"), \"\" /*unused*/, resource.CELSelectorExpressionMaxLength))\n   324\u2192\t\t// Don't bother compiling too long expressions.\n   325\u2192\t\treturn allErrs\n   326\u2192\t}\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192\tresult := dracel.GetCompiler(dracel.Features{EnableConsumableCapacity: utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.DRAConsumableCapacity)}).CompileCELExpression(celSelector.Expression, dracel.Options{EnvType: &envType})\n   329\u2192\tif result.Error != nil {\n   330\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, convertCELErrorToValidationError(fldPath.Child(\"expression\"), celSelector.Expression, result.Error))\n   331\u2192\t} else if result.MaxCost > resource.CELSelectorExpressionMaxCost {\n   332\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"expression\"), \"too complex, exceeds cost limit\"))\n   333\u2192\t}\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   336\u2192}\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192func convertCELErrorToValidationError(fldPath *field.Path, expression string, err error) *field.Error {\n   339\u2192\tvar celErr *cel.Error\n   340\u2192\tif errors.As(err, &celErr) {\n   341\u2192\t\tswitch celErr.Type {\n   342\u2192\t\tcase cel.ErrorTypeRequired:\n   343\u2192\t\t\treturn field.Required(fldPath, celErr.Detail)\n   344\u2192\t\tcase cel.ErrorTypeInvalid:\n   345\u2192\t\t\treturn field.Invalid(fldPath, expression, celErr.Detail)\n   346\u2192\t\tcase cel.ErrorTypeInternal:\n   347\u2192\t\t\treturn field.InternalError(fldPath, celErr)\n   348\u2192\t\t}\n   349\u2192\t}\n   350\u2192\treturn field.InternalError(fldPath, fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported error type: %w\", err))\n   351\u2192}\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192func validateDeviceConstraint(constraint resource.DeviceConstraint, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames) field.ErrorList {\n   354\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   355\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(constraint.Requests, resource.DeviceRequestsMaxSize,\n   356\u2192\t\tfunc(name string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   357\u2192\t\t\treturn validateRequestNameRef(name, fldPath, requestNames)\n   358\u2192\t\t},\n   359\u2192\t\tstringKey, fldPath.Child(\"requests\"), sizeCovered, uniquenessCovered)...)\n   360\u2192\tif constraint.MatchAttribute != nil {\n   361\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateFullyQualifiedName(*constraint.MatchAttribute, fldPath.Child(\"matchAttribute\")).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n   362\u2192\t} else if constraint.DistinctAttribute != nil {\n   363\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateFullyQualifiedName(*constraint.DistinctAttribute, fldPath.Child(\"distinctAttribute\"))...)\n   364\u2192\t} else if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.DRAConsumableCapacity) {\n   365\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, `exactly one of \"matchAttribute\" or \"distinctAttribute\" is required, but multiple fields are set`))\n   366\u2192\t} else {\n   367\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"matchAttribute\"), \"\"))\n   368\u2192\t}\n   369\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   370\u2192}\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192func validateDeviceClaimConfiguration(config resource.DeviceClaimConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   373\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   374\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(config.Requests, resource.DeviceRequestsMaxSize,\n   375\u2192\t\tfunc(name string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   376\u2192\t\t\treturn validateRequestNameRef(name, fldPath, requestNames)\n   377\u2192\t\t}, stringKey, fldPath.Child(\"requests\"), sizeCovered, uniquenessCovered)...)\n   378\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceConfiguration(config.DeviceConfiguration, fldPath, stored)...)\n   379\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   380\u2192}\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192func validateRequestNameRef(name string, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames) field.ErrorList {\n   383\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   384\u2192\tsegments := strings.Split(name, \"/\")\n   385\u2192\tif len(segments) > 2 {\n   386\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, name, \"must be the name of a request in the claim or the name of a request and a subrequest separated by '/'\"))\n   387\u2192\t\treturn allErrs\n   388\u2192\t}\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\tfor i := range segments {\n   391\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRequestName(segments[i], fldPath)...)\n   392\u2192\t}\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192\tif !requestNames.Has(name) {\n   395\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, name, \"must be the name of a request in the claim or the name of a request and a subrequest separated by '/'\"))\n   396\u2192\t}\n   397\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   398\u2192}\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192func validateDeviceConfiguration(config resource.DeviceConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   401\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   402\u2192\tif config.Opaque == nil {\n   403\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"opaque\"), \"\"))\n   404\u2192\t} else {\n   405\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateOpaqueConfiguration(*config.Opaque, fldPath.Child(\"opaque\"), stored)...)\n   406\u2192\t}\n   407\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   408\u2192}\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192func validateOpaqueConfiguration(config resource.OpaqueDeviceConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   411\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   412\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDriverName(config.Driver, fldPath.Child(\"driver\"), corevalidation.RequiredCovered, corevalidation.FormatCovered)...)\n   413\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRawExtension(config.Parameters, fldPath.Child(\"parameters\"), stored, resource.OpaqueParametersMaxLength)...)\n   414\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   415\u2192}\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192func validateResourceClaimStatusUpdate(status, oldStatus *resource.ResourceClaimStatus, claimDeleted bool, requestNames requestNames, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   418\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   419\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(status.ReservedFor, resource.ResourceClaimReservedForMaxSize,\n   420\u2192\t\tvalidateResourceClaimUserReference,\n   421\u2192\t\tfunc(consumer resource.ResourceClaimConsumerReference) types.UID { return consumer.UID },\n   422\u2192\t\tfldPath.Child(\"reservedFor\"), sizeCovered, uniquenessCovered)...)\n   423\u2192\n   424\u2192\tvar allocatedDevices sets.Set[structured.SharedDeviceID]\n   425\u2192\tif status.Allocation != nil {\n   426\u2192\t\tallocatedDevices = gatherAllocatedDevices(&status.Allocation.Devices)\n   427\u2192\t}\n   428\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(status.Devices, -1,\n   429\u2192\t\tfunc(device resource.AllocatedDeviceStatus, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   430\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceStatus(device, fldPath, allocatedDevices)\n   431\u2192\t\t},\n   432\u2192\t\tfunc(device resource.AllocatedDeviceStatus) structured.SharedDeviceID {\n   433\u2192\t\t\tdeviceID := structured.MakeDeviceID(device.Driver, device.Pool, device.Device)\n   434\u2192\t\t\treturn structured.MakeSharedDeviceID(deviceID, (*types.UID)(device.ShareID))\n   435\u2192\t\t},\n   436\u2192\t\tfldPath.Child(\"devices\"), uniquenessCovered)...)\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192\t// Now check for invariants that must be valid for a ResourceClaim.\n   439\u2192\tif len(status.ReservedFor) > 0 {\n   440\u2192\t\tif status.Allocation == nil {\n   441\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"reservedFor\"), \"may not be specified when `allocated` is not set\"))\n   442\u2192\t\t} else {\n   443\u2192\t\t\t// Items may be removed from ReservedFor while the claim is meant to be deallocated,\n   444\u2192\t\t\t// but not added.\n   445\u2192\t\t\tif claimDeleted {\n   446\u2192\t\t\t\toldSet := sets.New(oldStatus.ReservedFor...)\n   447\u2192\t\t\t\tnewSet := sets.New(status.ReservedFor...)\n   448\u2192\t\t\t\tnewItems := newSet.Difference(oldSet)\n   449\u2192\t\t\t\tif len(newItems) > 0 {\n   450\u2192\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"reservedFor\"), \"new entries may not be added while `deallocationRequested` or `deletionTimestamp` are set\"))\n   451\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   452\u2192\t\t\t}\n   453\u2192\t\t}\n   454\u2192\t}\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192\t// Updates to a populated status.Allocation are not allowed.\n   457\u2192\t// Unmodified fields don't need to be validated again and,\n   458\u2192\t// in this particular case, must not be validated again because\n   459\u2192\t// validation for new results is tighter than it was before.\n   460\u2192\tif oldStatus.Allocation != nil && status.Allocation != nil {\n   461\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(status.Allocation, oldStatus.Allocation, fldPath.Child(\"allocation\")).WithOrigin(\"update\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n   462\u2192\t} else if status.Allocation != nil {\n   463\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateAllocationResult(status.Allocation, fldPath.Child(\"allocation\"), requestNames, false)...)\n   464\u2192\t}\n   465\u2192\n   466\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   467\u2192}\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192func validateResourceClaimUserReference(ref resource.ResourceClaimConsumerReference, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   470\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   471\u2192\tif ref.Resource == \"\" {\n   472\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"resource\"), \"\"))\n   473\u2192\t}\n   474\u2192\tif ref.Name == \"\" {\n   475\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"name\"), \"\"))\n   476\u2192\t}\n   477\u2192\tif ref.UID == \"\" {\n   478\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"uid\"), \"\"))\n   479\u2192\t}\n   480\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   481\u2192}\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192// validateAllocationResult enforces constraints for *new* results, which in at\n   484\u2192// least one case (admin access) are more strict than before. Therefore it\n   485\u2192// may not be called to re-validate results which were stored earlier.\n   486\u2192func validateAllocationResult(allocation *resource.AllocationResult, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   487\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   488\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceAllocationResult(allocation.Devices, fldPath.Child(\"devices\"), requestNames, stored)...)\n   489\u2192\tif allocation.NodeSelector != nil {\n   490\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, corevalidation.ValidateNodeSelector(allocation.NodeSelector, false, fldPath.Child(\"nodeSelector\"))...)\n   491\u2192\t}\n   492\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   493\u2192}\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192func validateDeviceAllocationResult(allocation resource.DeviceAllocationResult, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   496\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   497\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(allocation.Results, resource.AllocationResultsMaxSize,\n   498\u2192\t\tfunc(result resource.DeviceRequestAllocationResult, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   499\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceRequestAllocationResult(result, fldPath, requestNames)\n   500\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"results\"), sizeCovered)...)\n   501\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(allocation.Config, 2*resource.DeviceConfigMaxSize, /* class + claim */\n   502\u2192\t\tfunc(config resource.DeviceAllocationConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   503\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceAllocationConfiguration(config, fldPath, requestNames, stored)\n   504\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"config\"), sizeCovered)...)\n   505\u2192\n   506\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   507\u2192}\n   508\u2192\n   509\u2192func validateDeviceRequestAllocationResult(result resource.DeviceRequestAllocationResult, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames) field.ErrorList {\n   510\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   511\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRequestNameRef(result.Request, fldPath.Child(\"request\"), requestNames)...)\n   512\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDriverName(result.Driver, fldPath.Child(\"driver\"), corevalidation.RequiredCovered, corevalidation.FormatCovered)...)\n   513\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validatePoolName(result.Pool, fldPath.Child(\"pool\")).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n   514\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceName(result.Device, fldPath.Child(\"device\"))...)\n   515\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceBindingParameters(result.BindingConditions, result.BindingFailureConditions, fldPath)...)\n   516\u2192\tif result.ShareID != nil {\n   517\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateUID(string(*result.ShareID), fldPath.Child(\"shareID\")).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n   518\u2192\t}\n   519\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   520\u2192}\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192func validateDeviceAllocationConfiguration(config resource.DeviceAllocationConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path, requestNames requestNames, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   523\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   524\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateAllocationConfigSource(config.Source, fldPath.Child(\"source\"))...)\n   525\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(config.Requests, resource.DeviceRequestsMaxSize,\n   526\u2192\t\tfunc(name string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   527\u2192\t\t\treturn validateRequestNameRef(name, fldPath, requestNames)\n   528\u2192\t\t}, stringKey, fldPath.Child(\"requests\"))...)\n   529\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceConfiguration(config.DeviceConfiguration, fldPath, stored)...)\n   530\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   531\u2192}\n   532\u2192\n   533\u2192func validateAllocationConfigSource(source resource.AllocationConfigSource, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   534\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   535\u2192\tswitch source {\n   536\u2192\tcase \"\":\n   537\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n   538\u2192\tcase resource.AllocationConfigSourceClaim, resource.AllocationConfigSourceClass:\n   539\u2192\tdefault:\n   540\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath, source, []resource.AllocationConfigSource{resource.AllocationConfigSourceClaim, resource.AllocationConfigSourceClass}).MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n   541\u2192\t}\n   542\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   543\u2192}\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192// ValidateDeviceClass validates a DeviceClass.\n   546\u2192func ValidateDeviceClass(class *resource.DeviceClass) field.ErrorList {\n   547\u2192\tvalidateClassName := func(fldPath *field.Path, name string) field.ErrorList {\n   548\u2192\t\t// validate.LongName doesn't respect operation type currently (CREATE or UPDATE)\n   549\u2192\t\t// so it is ok to use operation.Operation{} here\n   550\u2192\t\treturn validate.LongName(context.Background(), operation.Operation{}, fldPath, &name, nil).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()\n   551\u2192\t}\n   552\u2192\tallErrs := field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n   554\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   555\u2192}\n   556\u2192\n   557\u2192// ValidateDeviceClassUpdate tests if an update to DeviceClass is valid.\n   558\u2192func ValidateDeviceClassUpdate(class, oldClass *resource.DeviceClass) field.ErrorList {\n   559\u2192\tvalidateClassName := func(fldPath *field.Path, name string) field.ErrorList {\n   560\u2192\t\treturn validateDeviceClassSpec(&class.Spec, &oldClass.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n   566\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   567\u2192}\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192func validateDeviceClassSpec(spec, oldSpec *resource.DeviceClassSpec, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   570\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   571\u2192\t// If the selectors are exactly as before, we treat the CEL expressions as \"stored\".\n   572\u2192\t// Any change, including merely reordering selectors, triggers validation as new\n   573\u2192\t// expressions.\n   574\u2192\tstored := false\n   575\u2192\tif oldSpec != nil {\n   576\u2192\t\tstored = apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(spec.Selectors, oldSpec.Selectors)\n   577\u2192\t}\n   578\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(spec.Selectors, resource.DeviceSelectorsMaxSize,\n   579\u2192\t\tfunc(selector resource.DeviceSelector, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   580\u2192\t\t\treturn validateSelector(selector, fldPath, stored)\n
