586\u2192\t\tstored = apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(spec.Config, oldSpec.Config)\n   587\u2192\t}\n   588\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(spec.Config, resource.DeviceConfigMaxSize,\n   589\u2192\t\tfunc(config resource.DeviceClassConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   590\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDeviceClassConfiguration(config, fldPath, stored)\n   591\u2192\t\t},\n   592\u2192\t\tfldPath.Child(\"config\"), sizeCovered)...)\n   593\u2192\tif spec.ExtendedResourceName != nil && !v1helper.IsExtendedResourceName(corev1.ResourceName(*spec.ExtendedResourceName)) {\n   594\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"extendedResourceName\"), *spec.ExtendedResourceName,\n   595\u2192\t\t\t\"must be a valid extended resource name\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative().WithOrigin(\"format=k8s-extended-resource-name\"))\n   596\u2192\t}\n   597\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   598\u2192}\n   599\u2192\n   600\u2192func validateDeviceClassConfiguration(config resource.DeviceClassConfiguration, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   601\u2192\treturn validateDeviceConfiguration(config.DeviceConfiguration, fldPath, stored)\n   602\u2192}\n   603\u2192\n   604\u2192// ValidateResourceClaimTemplate validates a ResourceClaimTemplate.\n   605\u2192func ValidateResourceClaimTemplate(template *resource.ResourceClaimTemplate) field.ErrorList {\n   606\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMeta(&template.ObjectMeta, true, corevalidation.ValidateResourceClaimTemplateName, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   607\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourceClaimTemplateSpec(&template.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\"), false)...)\n   608\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   609\u2192}\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192func validateResourceClaimTemplateSpec(spec *resource.ResourceClaimTemplateSpec, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool) field.ErrorList {\n   612\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateTemplateObjectMeta(&spec.ObjectMeta, fldPath.Child(\"metadata\"))\n   613\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourceClaimSpec(&spec.Spec, fldPath.Child(\"spec\"), stored)...)\n   614\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   615\u2192}\n   616\u2192\n   617\u2192// ValidateResourceClaimTemplateUpdate tests if an update to template is valid.\n   618\u2192func ValidateResourceClaimTemplateUpdate(template, oldTemplate *resource.ResourceClaimTemplate) field.ErrorList {\n   619\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(&template.ObjectMeta, &oldTemplate.ObjectMeta, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   620\u2192\t// The spec is immutable. On update, we only check for immutability.\n   621\u2192\t// Re-validating other fields is skipped because the user cannot change them;\n   622\u2192\t// the only actionable error is for the immutability violation.\n   623\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(template.Spec, oldTemplate.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n   624\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   625\u2192}\n   626\u2192\n   627\u2192func validateNodeName(name string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   628\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   629\u2192\tfor _, msg := range corevalidation.ValidateNodeName(name, false) {\n   630\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, name, msg))\n   631\u2192\t}\n   632\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   633\u2192}\n   634\u2192\n   635\u2192// ValidateResourceSlice tests if a ResourceSlice object is valid.\n   636\u2192func ValidateResourceSlice(slice *resource.ResourceSlice) field.ErrorList {\n   637\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMeta(&slice.ObjectMeta, false, apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   638\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourceSliceSpec(&slice.Spec, nil, field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n   639\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   640\u2192}\n   641\u2192\n   642\u2192// ValidateResourceSliceUpdate tests if a ResourceSlice update is valid.\n   643\u2192func ValidateResourceSliceUpdate(resourceSlice, oldResourceSlice *resource.ResourceSlice) field.ErrorList {\n   644\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(&resourceSlice.ObjectMeta, &oldResourceSlice.ObjectMeta, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n   645\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourceSliceSpec(&resourceSlice.Spec, &oldResourceSlice.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n   646\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   647\u2192}\n   648\u2192\n   649\u2192func validateResourceSliceSpec(spec, oldSpec *resource.ResourceSliceSpec, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   650\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   651\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDriverName(spec.Driver, fldPath.Child(\"driver\"))...)\n   652\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateResourcePool(spec.Pool, fldPath.Child(\"pool\"))...)\n   653\u2192\tif oldSpec != nil {\n   654\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(spec.Pool.Name, oldSpec.Pool.Name, fldPath.Child(\"pool\", \"name\"))...)\n   655\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(spec.Driver, oldSpec.Driver, fldPath.Child(\"driver\"))...)\n   656\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, apimachineryvalidation.ValidateImmutableField(spec.NodeName, oldSpec.NodeName, fldPath.Child(\"nodeName\"))...)\n   657\u2192\t}\n   658\u2192\n   659\u2192\tsetFields := make([]string, 0, 4)\n   660\u2192\tif spec.NodeName != nil {\n   661\u2192\t\tif *spec.NodeName != \"\" {\n   662\u2192\t\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`nodeName`\")\n   663\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateNodeName(*spec.NodeName, fldPath.Child(\"nodeName\"))...)\n   664\u2192\t\t} else {\n   665\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"nodeName\"), *spec.NodeName,\n   666\u2192\t\t\t\t\"must be either unset or set to a non-empty string\"))\n   667\u2192\t\t}\n   668\u2192\t}\n   669\u2192\tif spec.NodeSelector != nil {\n   670\u2192\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`nodeSelector`\")\n   671\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, corevalidation.ValidateNodeSelector(spec.NodeSelector, false, fldPath.Child(\"nodeSelector\"))...)\n   672\u2192\t\tif len(spec.NodeSelector.NodeSelectorTerms) != 1 {\n   673\u2192\t\t\t// This additional constraint simplifies merging of different selectors\n   674\u2192\t\t\t// when devices are allocated from different slices.\n   675\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"nodeSelector\", \"nodeSelectorTerms\"), spec.NodeSelector.NodeSelectorTerms, \"must have exactly one node selector term\"))\n   676\u2192\t\t}\n   677\u2192\t}\n   678\u2192\n   679\u2192\tif spec.AllNodes != nil {\n   680\u2192\t\tif *spec.AllNodes {\n   681\u2192\t\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`allNodes`\")\n   682\u2192\t\t} else {\n   683\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"allNodes\"), *spec.AllNodes,\n   684\u2192\t\t\t\t\"must be either unset or set to true\"))\n   685\u2192\t\t}\n   686\u2192\t}\n   687\u2192\n   688\u2192\tif spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n   689\u2192\t\tif *spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection {\n   690\u2192\t\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`perDeviceNodeSelection`\")\n   691\u2192\t\t} else {\n   692\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"perDeviceNodeSelection\"), *spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection,\n   693\u2192\t\t\t\t\"must be either unset or set to true\"))\n   694\u2192\t\t}\n   695\u2192\t}\n   696\u2192\n   697\u2192\tswitch len(setFields) {\n   698\u2192\tcase 0:\n   699\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"exactly one of `nodeName`, `nodeSelector`, `allNodes`, `perDeviceNodeSelection` is required\"))\n   700\u2192\tcase 1:\n   701\u2192\tdefault:\n   702\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(setFields, \", \")),\n   703\u2192\t\t\t\"exactly one of `nodeName`, `nodeSelector`, `allNodes`, `perDeviceNodeSelection` is required, but multiple fields are set\"))\n   704\u2192\t}\n   705\u2192\n   706\u2192\tsharedCounterToCounterNames := gatherSharedCounterCounterNames(spec.SharedCounters)\n   707\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(spec.Devices, resource.ResourceSliceMaxDevices,\n   708\u2192\t\tfunc(device resource.Device, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   709\u2192\t\t\treturn validateDevice(device, fldPath, sharedCounterToCounterNames, spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection)\n   710\u2192\t\t},\n   711\u2192\t\tfunc(device resource.Device) string {\n   712\u2192\t\t\treturn device.Name\n   713\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"devices\"))...)\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192\t// Size limit for total number of counters in devices enforced here.\n   716\u2192\tnumDeviceCounters := 0\n   717\u2192\tfor _, device := range spec.Devices {\n   718\u2192\t\tfor _, c := range device.ConsumesCounters {\n   719\u2192\t\t\tnumDeviceCounters += len(c.Counters)\n   720\u2192\t\t}\n   721\u2192\t}\n   722\u2192\tif numDeviceCounters > resource.ResourceSliceMaxDeviceCountersPerSlice {\n   723\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"devices\"), numDeviceCounters, fmt.Sprintf(\"the total number of counters in devices must not exceed %d\", resource.ResourceSliceMaxDeviceCountersPerSlice)))\n   724\u2192\t}\n   725\u2192\n   726\u2192\t// Size limit for all shared counters enforced here.\n   727\u2192\tnumCounters := 0\n   728\u2192\tfor _, set := range spec.SharedCounters {\n   729\u2192\t\tnumCounters += len(set.Counters)\n   730\u2192\t}\n   731\u2192\tif numCounters > resource.ResourceSliceMaxSharedCounters {\n   732\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"sharedCounters\"), numCounters, fmt.Sprintf(\"the total number of shared counters must not exceed %d\", resource.ResourceSliceMaxSharedCounters)))\n   733\u2192\t}\n   734\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(spec.SharedCounters, -1,\n   735\u2192\t\tvalidateCounterSet,\n   736\u2192\t\tfunc(counterSet resource.CounterSet) string {\n   737\u2192\t\t\treturn counterSet.Name\n   738\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"sharedCounters\"))...)\n   739\u2192\n   740\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   741\u2192}\n   742\u2192\n   743\u2192func validateCounterSet(counterSet resource.CounterSet, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   744\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   745\u2192\tif counterSet.Name == \"\" {\n   746\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"name\"), \"\"))\n   747\u2192\t} else {\n   748\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateCounterName(counterSet.Name, fldPath.Child(\"name\"))...)\n   749\u2192\t}\n   750\u2192\tif len(counterSet.Counters) == 0 {\n   751\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"counters\"), \"\"))\n   752\u2192\t} else {\n   753\u2192\t\t// The size limit is enforced for across all sets by the caller.\n   754\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateMap(counterSet.Counters, -1, validation.DNS1123LabelMaxLength,\n   755\u2192\t\t\tvalidateCounterName, validateDeviceCounter, fldPath.Child(\"counters\"), keysCovered)...)\n   756\u2192\t}\n   757\u2192\n   758\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   759\u2192}\n   760\u2192\n   761\u2192func gatherSharedCounterCounterNames(sharedCounters []resource.CounterSet) map[string]sets.Set[string] {\n   762\u2192\tsharedCounterToCounterMap := make(map[string]sets.Set[string])\n   763\u2192\tfor _, counterSet := range sharedCounters {\n   764\u2192\t\tcounterNames := sets.New[string]()\n   765\u2192\t\tfor counterName := range counterSet.Counters {\n   766\u2192\t\t\tcounterNames.Insert(counterName)\n   767\u2192\t\t}\n   768\u2192\t\tsharedCounterToCounterMap[counterSet.Name] = counterNames\n   769\u2192\t}\n   770\u2192\treturn sharedCounterToCounterMap\n   771\u2192}\n   772\u2192\n   773\u2192func validateResourcePool(pool resource.ResourcePool, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   774\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   775\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validatePoolName(pool.Name, fldPath.Child(\"name\"))...)\n   776\u2192\tif pool.ResourceSliceCount <= 0 {\n   777\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"resourceSliceCount\"), pool.ResourceSliceCount, \"must be greater than zero\"))\n   778\u2192\t}\n   779\u2192\tif pool.Generation < 0 {\n   780\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"generation\"), pool.Generation, \"must be greater than or equal to zero\"))\n   781\u2192\t}\n   782\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   783\u2192}\n   784\u2192\n   785\u2192func validateDevice(device resource.Device, fldPath *field.Path, sharedCounterToCounterNames map[string]sets.Set[string], perDeviceNodeSelection *bool) field.ErrorList {\n   786\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   787\u2192\tallowMultipleAllocations := device.AllowMultipleAllocations != nil && *device.AllowMultipleAllocations\n   788\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceName(device.Name, fldPath.Child(\"name\"))...)\n   789\u2192\t// Warn about exceeding the maximum length only once. If any individual\n   790\u2192\t// field is too large, then so is the combination.\n   791\u2192\tattributeAndCapacityLength := len(device.Attributes) + len(device.Capacity)\n   792\u2192\tif attributeAndCapacityLength > resource.ResourceSliceMaxAttributesAndCapacitiesPerDevice {\n   793\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, attributeAndCapacityLength, fmt.Sprintf(\"the total number of attributes and capacities must not exceed %d\", resource.ResourceSliceMaxAttributesAndCapacitiesPerDevice)))\n   794\u2192\t}\n   795\u2192\n   796\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateMap(device.Attributes, -1, attributeAndCapacityMaxKeyLength, validateQualifiedName, validateDeviceAttribute, fldPath.Child(\"attributes\"))...)\n   797\u2192\tif allowMultipleAllocations {\n   798\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateMap(device.Capacity, -1, attributeAndCapacityMaxKeyLength, validateQualifiedName, validateMultiAllocatableDeviceCapacity, fldPath.Child(\"capacity\"))...)\n   799\u2192\t} else {\n   800\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateMap(device.Capacity, -1, attributeAndCapacityMaxKeyLength, validateQualifiedName, validateSingleAllocatableDeviceCapacity, fldPath.Child(\"capacity\"))...)\n   801\u2192\t}\n   802\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(device.Taints, resource.DeviceTaintsMaxLength, validateDeviceTaint, fldPath.Child(\"taints\"))...)\n   803\u2192\n   804\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(device.ConsumesCounters, -1,\n   805\u2192\t\tvalidateDeviceCounterConsumption,\n   806\u2192\t\tfunc(deviceCapacityConsumption resource.DeviceCounterConsumption) string {\n   807\u2192\t\t\treturn deviceCapacityConsumption.CounterSet\n   808\u2192\t\t}, fldPath.Child(\"consumesCounters\"))...)\n   809\u2192\n   810\u2192\tvar countersLength int\n   811\u2192\tfor _, set := range device.ConsumesCounters {\n   812\u2192\t\tcountersLength += len(set.Counters)\n   813\u2192\t}\n   814\u2192\tif countersLength > resource.ResourceSliceMaxCountersPerDevice {\n   815\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, countersLength, fmt.Sprintf(\"the total number of counters must not exceed %d\", resource.ResourceSliceMaxCountersPerDevice)))\n   816\u2192\t}\n   817\u2192\n   818\u2192\tfor i, deviceCounterConsumption := range device.ConsumesCounters {\n   819\u2192\t\tif capacityNames, exists := sharedCounterToCounterNames[deviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet]; exists {\n   820\u2192\t\t\tfor capacityName := range deviceCounterConsumption.Counters {\n   821\u2192\t\t\t\tif !capacityNames.Has(string(capacityName)) {\n   822\u2192\t\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"consumesCounters\").Index(i).Child(\"counters\"),\n   823\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tcapacityName, \"must reference a counter defined in the ResourceSlice sharedCounters\"))\n   824\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   825\u2192\t\t\t}\n   826\u2192\t\t} else {\n   827\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"consumesCounters\").Index(i).Child(\"counterSet\"),\n   828\u2192\t\t\t\tdeviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet, \"must reference a counterSet defined in the ResourceSlice sharedCounters\"))\n   829\u2192\t\t}\n   830\u2192\t}\n   831\u2192\n   832\u2192\tif perDeviceNodeSelection != nil && *perDeviceNodeSelection {\n   833\u2192\t\tsetFields := make([]string, 0, 3)\n   834\u2192\t\tif device.NodeName != nil {\n   835\u2192\t\t\tif len(*device.NodeName) != 0 {\n   836\u2192\t\t\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`nodeName`\")\n   837\u2192\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateNodeName(*device.NodeName, fldPath.Child(\"nodeName\"))...)\n   838\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   839\u2192\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"nodeName\"), *device.NodeName, \"must not be empty\"))\n   840\u2192\t\t\t}\n   841\u2192\n   842\u2192\t\t}\n   843\u2192\t\tif device.NodeSelector != nil {\n   844\u2192\t\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`nodeSelector`\")\n   845\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, corevalidation.ValidateNodeSelector(device.NodeSelector, false, fldPath.Child(\"nodeSelector\"))...)\n   846\u2192\t\t}\n   847\u2192\t\tif device.AllNodes != nil {\n   848\u2192\t\t\tif *device.AllNodes {\n   849\u2192\t\t\t\tsetFields = append(setFields, \"`allNodes`\")\n   850\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   851\u2192\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"allNodes\"), *device.AllNodes, \"must be either unset or set to true\"))\n   852\u2192\t\t\t}\n   853\u2192\t\t}\n   854\u2192\t\tswitch len(setFields) {\n   855\u2192\t\tcase 0:\n   856\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"exactly one of `nodeName`, `nodeSelector`, or `allNodes` is required when `perDeviceNodeSelection` is set to true in the ResourceSlice spec\"))\n   857\u2192\t\tcase 1:\n   858\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   859\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(setFields, \", \")), \"exactly one of `nodeName`, `nodeSelector`, or `allNodes` is required when `perDeviceNodeSelection` is set to true in the ResourceSlice spec\"))\n   860\u2192\t\t}\n   861\u2192\t} else if (perDeviceNodeSelection == nil || !*perDeviceNodeSelection) && (device.NodeName != nil || device.NodeSelector != nil || device.AllNodes != nil) {\n   862\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, nil, \"`nodeName`, `nodeSelector` and `allNodes` can only be set if `perDeviceNodeSelection` is set to true in the ResourceSlice spec\"))\n   863\u2192\t}\n   864\u2192\n   865\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceBindingParameters(device.BindingConditions, device.BindingFailureConditions, fldPath)...)\n   866\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   867\u2192}\n   868\u2192\n   869\u2192func validateDeviceCounterConsumption(deviceCounterConsumption resource.DeviceCounterConsumption, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   870\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   871\u2192\n   872\u2192\tif len(deviceCounterConsumption.CounterSet) == 0 {\n   873\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"counterSet\"), \"\"))\n   874\u2192\t}\n   875\u2192\tif len(deviceCounterConsumption.Counters) == 0 {\n   876\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"counters\"), \"\"))\n   877\u2192\t} else {\n   878\u2192\t\t// The size limit is enforced for the entire device.\n   879\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateMap(deviceCounterConsumption.Counters, -1, validation.DNS1123LabelMaxLength,\n   880\u2192\t\t\tvalidateCounterName, validateDeviceCounter, fldPath.Child(\"counters\"), keysCovered)...)\n   881\u2192\t}\n   882\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   883\u2192}\n   884\u2192\n   885\u2192var (\n   886\u2192\tnumericIdentifier = `(0|[1-9]\\d*)`\n   887\u2192\n   888\u2192\tpreReleaseIdentifier = `(0|[1-9]\\d*|\\d*[a-zA-Z-][0-9a-zA-Z-]*)`\n   889\u2192\n   890\u2192\tbuildIdentifier = `[0-9a-zA-Z-]+`\n   891\u2192\n   892\u2192\tsemverRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^` +\n   893\u2192\n   894\u2192\t\t// dot-separated version segments (e.g. 1.2.3)\n   895\u2192\t\tnumericIdentifier + `\\.` + numericIdentifier + `\\.` + numericIdentifier +\n   896\u2192\n   897\u2192\t\t// optional dot-separated prerelease segments (e.g. -alpha.PRERELEASE.1)\n   898\u2192\t\t`(-` + preReleaseIdentifier + `(\\.` + preReleaseIdentifier + `)*)?` +\n   899\u2192\n   900\u2192\t\t// optional dot-separated build identifier segments (e.g. +build.id.20240305)\n   901\u2192\t\t`(\\+` + buildIdentifier + `(\\.` + buildIdentifier + `)*)?` +\n   902\u2192\t\t`$`)\n   903\u2192)\n   904\u2192\n   905\u2192func validateDeviceAttribute(attribute resource.DeviceAttribute, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   906\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   907\u2192\tnumFields := 0\n   908\u2192\tif attribute.BoolValue != nil {\n   909\u2192\t\tnumFields++\n   910\u2192\t}\n   911\u2192\tif attribute.IntValue != nil {\n   912\u2192\t\tnumFields++\n   913\u2192\t}\n   914\u2192\tif attribute.StringValue != nil {\n   915\u2192\t\tnumFields++\n   916\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceAttributeStringValue(attribute.StringValue, fldPath.Child(\"string\"))...)\n   917\u2192\t}\n   918\u2192\tif attribute.VersionValue != nil {\n   919\u2192\t\tnumFields++\n   920\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceAttributeVersionValue(attribute.VersionValue, fldPath.Child(\"version\"))...)\n   921\u2192\t}\n   922\u2192\n   923\u2192\tswitch numFields {\n   924\u2192\tcase 0:\n   925\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, \"\", \"exactly one value must be specified\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n   926\u2192\tcase 1:\n   927\u2192\t\t// Okay.\n   928\u2192\tdefault:\n   929\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, attribute, \"exactly one value must be specified\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n   930\u2192\t}\n   931\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   932\u2192}\n   933\u2192\n   934\u2192func validateDeviceAttributeStringValue(value *string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   935\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   936\u2192\tif len(*value) > resource.DeviceAttributeMaxValueLength {\n   937\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath, \"\" /*unused*/, resource.DeviceAttributeMaxValueLength))\n   938\u2192\t}\n   939\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   940\u2192}\n   941\u2192\n   942\u2192func validateDeviceAttributeVersionValue(value *string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   943\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   944\u2192\tif !semverRe.MatchString(*value) {\n   945\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, *value, \"must be a string compatible with semver.org spec 2.0.0\"))\n   946\u2192\t}\n   947\u2192\tif len(*value) > resource.DeviceAttributeMaxValueLength {\n   948\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath, \"\" /*unused*/, resource.DeviceAttributeMaxValueLength))\n   949\u2192\t}\n   950\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   951\u2192}\n   952\u2192\n   953\u2192// validateMultiAllocatableDeviceCapacity must check requestPolicy in consumable capacity.\n   954\u2192func validateMultiAllocatableDeviceCapacity(capacity resource.DeviceCapacity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   955\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   956\u2192\tif capacity.RequestPolicy != nil {\n   957\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs,\n   958\u2192\t\t\tvalidateRequestPolicy(capacity.Value, capacity.RequestPolicy, fldPath.Child(\"requestPolicy\"))...)\n   959\u2192\t}\n   960\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   961\u2192}\n   962\u2192\n   963\u2192// validateSingleAllocatableDeviceCapacity must not allow consumable capacity.\n   964\u2192func validateSingleAllocatableDeviceCapacity(capacity resource.DeviceCapacity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   965\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   966\u2192\tif capacity.RequestPolicy != nil {\n   967\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs,\n   968\u2192\t\t\tfield.Forbidden(fldPath.Child(\"requestPolicy\"), \"allowMultipleAllocations must be true\"))\n   969\u2192\t}\n   970\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   971\u2192}\n   972\u2192\n   973\u2192// validateRequestPolicy validates at most one of ValidRequestValues can be defined.\n   974\u2192// If any ValidRequestValues are defined, Default must also be defined and valid.\n   975\u2192func validateRequestPolicy(maxCapacity apiresource.Quantity, policy *resource.CapacityRequestPolicy, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   976\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   977\u2192\tif len(policy.ValidValues) > 0 && policy.ValidRange != nil {\n   978\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Forbidden(fldPath, `exactly one policy can be specified, cannot specify \"validValues\" and \"validRange\" at the same time`))\n   979\u2192\t} else {\n   980\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateValidRequestValues(maxCapacity, policy, fldPath)...)\n   981\u2192\t}\n   982\u2192\treturn allErrs\n   983\u2192}\n   984\u2192\n   985\u2192func validateValidRequestValues(maxCapacity apiresource.Quantity, policy *resource.CapacityRequestPolicy, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n   986\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n   987\u2192\tswitch {\n   988\u2192\tcase len(policy.ValidValues) > 0:\n   989\u2192\t\tif policy.Default == nil {\n   990\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"default\"), \"required when validValues is defined\"))\n   991\u2192\t\t} else {\n   992\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRequestPolicyValidValues(*policy.Default, maxCapacity, policy.ValidValues, fldPath.Child(\"validValues\"))...)\n   993\u2192\t\t}\n   994\u2192\tcase policy.ValidRange != nil:\n   995\u2192\t\tif policy.Default == nil {\n   996\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"default\"), \"required when validRange is defined\"))\n   997\u2192\t\t} else {\n   998\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRequestPolicyRange(*policy.Default, maxCapacity, *policy.ValidRange, fldPath.Child(\"validRange\"))...)\n   999\u2192\t\t}\n  1000\u2192\t}\n  1001\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1002\u2192}\n  1003\u2192\n  1004\u2192func validateRequestPolicyValidValues(defaultValue apiresource.Quantity, maxCapacity apiresource.Quantity, validValues []apiresource.Quantity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1005\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1006\u2192\tfoundDefault := false\n  1007\u2192\n  1008\u2192\t// Check if validValues is sorted in ascending order\n  1009\u2192\tfor i := range len(validValues) - 1 {\n  1010\u2192\t\tif validValues[i].Cmp(validValues[i+1]) > 0 {\n  1011\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(\n  1012\u2192\t\t\t\tfldPath.Index(i+1),\n  1013\u2192\t\t\t\tvalidValues[i+1].String(),\n  1014\u2192\t\t\t\t\"values must be sorted in ascending order\"))\n  1015\u2192\t\t}\n  1016\u2192\t}\n  1017\u2192\n  1018\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(validValues, resource.CapacityRequestPolicyDiscreteMaxOptions,\n  1019\u2192\t\tfunc(option apiresource.Quantity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1020\u2192\t\t\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1021\u2192\t\t\tif option.Cmp(maxCapacity) > 0 {\n  1022\u2192\t\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, option.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"option is larger than capacity value: %s\", maxCapacity.String())))\n  1023\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1024\u2192\t\t\tif option.Cmp(defaultValue) == 0 {\n  1025\u2192\t\t\t\tfoundDefault = true\n  1026\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1027\u2192\t\t\treturn allErrs\n  1028\u2192\t\t}, quantityKey, fldPath)...)\n  1029\u2192\tif !foundDefault {\n  1030\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, defaultValue.String(), \"default value is not valid according to the requestPolicy\"))\n  1031\u2192\t}\n  1032\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1033\u2192}\n  1034\u2192\n  1035\u2192func validateRequestPolicyRange(defaultValue apiresource.Quantity, maxCapacity apiresource.Quantity, valueRange resource.CapacityRequestPolicyRange, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1036\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1037\u2192\tif valueRange.Min == nil {\n  1038\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"min\"), \"required when validRange is defined\"))\n  1039\u2192\t\treturn allErrs\n  1040\u2192\t}\n  1041\u2192\tif valueRange.Min.Cmp(maxCapacity) > 0 {\n  1042\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"min\"), valueRange.Min.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"min is larger than capacity value: %s\", maxCapacity.String())))\n  1043\u2192\t}\n  1044\u2192\tif defaultValue.Cmp(*valueRange.Min) < 0 {\n  1045\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"min\"), defaultValue.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"default is less than min: %s\", valueRange.Min.String())))\n  1046\u2192\t}\n  1047\u2192\tif valueRange.Max != nil {\n  1048\u2192\t\tif valueRange.Min.Cmp(*valueRange.Max) > 0 {\n  1049\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"max\"), valueRange.Min.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"min is larger than max: %s\", valueRange.Max.String())))\n  1050\u2192\t\t}\n  1051\u2192\t\tif valueRange.Max.Cmp(maxCapacity) > 0 {\n  1052\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"max\"), valueRange.Max.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"max is larger than capacity value: %s\", maxCapacity.String())))\n  1053\u2192\t\t}\n  1054\u2192\t\tif defaultValue.Cmp(*valueRange.Max) > 0 {\n  1055\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"max\"), defaultValue.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"default is more than max: %s\", valueRange.Max.String())))\n  1056\u2192\t\t}\n  1057\u2192\t}\n  1058\u2192\tif valueRange.Step != nil {\n  1059\u2192\t\tadded := valueRange.Min.DeepCopy()\n  1060\u2192\t\tadded.Add(*valueRange.Step)\n  1061\u2192\t\tif added.Cmp(maxCapacity) > 0 {\n  1062\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"step\"), valueRange.Step.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"one step %s is larger than capacity value: %s\", added.String(), maxCapacity.String())))\n  1063\u2192\t\t}\n  1064\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRequestPolicyRangeStep(defaultValue, *valueRange.Min, *valueRange.Step, fldPath.Child(\"step\"))...)\n  1065\u2192\t\tif valueRange.Max != nil {\n  1066\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRequestPolicyRangeStep(*valueRange.Max, *valueRange.Min, *valueRange.Step, fldPath.Child(\"step\"))...)\n  1067\u2192\t\t}\n  1068\u2192\t}\n  1069\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1070\u2192}\n  1071\u2192\n  1072\u2192func validateRequestPolicyRangeStep(value, min, step apiresource.Quantity, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1073\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1074\u2192\tstepVal := step.Value()\n  1075\u2192\tminVal := min.Value()\n  1076\u2192\tval := value.Value()\n  1077\u2192\tadded := (val - minVal)\n  1078\u2192\tif added%stepVal != 0 {\n  1079\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, value.String(), fmt.Sprintf(\"value is not a multiple of a given step (%s) from (%s)\", step.String(), min.String())))\n  1080\u2192\t}\n  1081\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1082\u2192}\n  1083\u2192\n  1084\u2192func validateDeviceCounter(counter resource.Counter, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1085\u2192\t// Any parsed quantity is valid.\n  1086\u2192\treturn nil\n  1087\u2192}\n  1088\u2192\n  1089\u2192func validateQualifiedName(name resource.QualifiedName, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1090\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1091\u2192\tparts := strings.Split(string(name), \"/\")\n  1092\u2192\tswitch len(parts) {\n  1093\u2192\tcase 1:\n  1094\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateCIdentifier(parts[0], fldPath)...)\n  1095\u2192\tcase 2:\n  1096\u2192\t\tif len(parts[0]) == 0 {\n  1097\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, \"\", \"the domain must not be empty\"))\n  1098\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1099\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDriverName(parts[0], fldPath)...)\n  1100\u2192\t\t}\n  1101\u2192\t\tif len(parts[1]) == 0 {\n  1102\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, \"\", \"the name must not be empty\"))\n  1103\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1104\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateCIdentifier(parts[1], fldPath)...)\n  1105\u2192\t\t}\n  1106\u2192\t\t// TODO: This validation is incomplete. It should reject qualified names\n  1107\u2192\t\t// that contain more than one slash. Currently, names like \"a/b/c\" are not\n  1108\u2192\t\t// handled and are implicitly accepted.\n  1109\u2192\t\t//\n  1110\u2192\t\t// This needs to be fixed in two places:\n  1111\u2192\t\t// 1. Here in this function.\n  1112\u2192\t\t// 2. In the corresponding declarative validation utility `resourcesQualifiedName`\n  1113\u2192\t\t//    in `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validate/strfmt.go`.\n  1114\u2192\t\t//\n  1115\u2192\t\t// The fix should be introduced carefully, possibly using ratcheting to avoid\n  1116\u2192\t\t// breaking existing, non-compliant objects.\n  1117\u2192\t}\n  1118\u2192\n  1119\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1120\u2192}\n  1121\u2192\n  1122\u2192func validateFullyQualifiedName(name resource.FullyQualifiedName, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1123\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1124\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateQualifiedName(resource.QualifiedName(name), fldPath)...)\n  1125\u2192\t// validateQualifiedName checks that both parts are valid.\n  1126\u2192\t// What we need to enforce here is that there really is a domain.\n  1127\u2192\tif !strings.Contains(string(name), \"/\") {\n  1128\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, name, \"a fully qualified name must be a domain and a name separated by a slash\"))\n  1129\u2192\t}\n  1130\u2192\treturn allErrs.WithOrigin(\"format=k8s-resource-fully-qualified-name\")\n  1131\u2192}\n  1132\u2192\n  1133\u2192func validateCIdentifier(id string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1134\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1135\u2192\tif len(id) > resource.DeviceMaxIDLength {\n  1136\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath, \"\" /*unused*/, resource.DeviceMaxIDLength))\n  1137\u2192\t}\n  1138\u2192\tfor _, msg := range content.IsCIdentifier(id) {\n  1139\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, id, msg))\n  1140\u2192\t}\n  1141\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1142\u2192}\n  1143\u2192\n  1144\u2192// validationOption is an option for validation.\n  1145\u2192type validationOption int\n  1146\u2192\n  1147\u2192const (\n  1148\u2192\t// The validation of each item is covered by declarative validation.\n  1149\u2192\titemsCovered validationOption = iota\n  1150\u2192\t// The list size check is covered by declarative validation.\n  1151\u2192\tsizeCovered\n  1152\u2192\t// The uniqueness check is covered by declarative validation.\n  1153\u2192\tuniquenessCovered\n  1154\u2192\t// key validation is covered by declarative validation.\n  1155\u2192\tkeysCovered\n  1156\u2192)\n  1157\u2192\n  1158\u2192// validateSlice ensures that a slice does not exceed a certain maximum size\n  1159\u2192// and that all entries are valid.\n  1160\u2192// A negative maxSize disables the length check.\n  1161\u2192func validateSlice[T any](slice []T, maxSize int, validateItem func(T, *field.Path) field.ErrorList, fldPath *field.Path, opts ...validationOption) field.ErrorList {\n  1162\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1163\u2192\tfor i, item := range slice {\n  1164\u2192\t\tidxPath := fldPath.Index(i)\n  1165\u2192\t\terrs := validateItem(item, idxPath)\n  1166\u2192\t\tif slices.Contains(opts, itemsCovered) {\n  1167\u2192\t\t\terrs = errs.MarkCoveredByDeclarative()\n  1168\u2192\t\t}\n  1169\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, errs...)\n  1170\u2192\t}\n  1171\u2192\tif maxSize >= 0 && len(slice) > maxSize {\n  1172\u2192\t\t// Dumping the entire field into the error message is likely to be too long,\n  1173\u2192\t\t// in particular when it is already beyond the maximum size. Instead this\n  1174\u2192\t\t// just shows the number of entries.\n  1175\u2192\t\terr := field.TooMany(fldPath, len(slice), maxSize).WithOrigin(\"maxItems\")\n  1176\u2192\t\tif slices.Contains(opts, sizeCovered) {\n  1177\u2192\t\t\terr = err.MarkCoveredByDeclarative()\n  1178\u2192\t\t}\n  1179\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, err)\n  1180\u2192\t}\n  1181\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1182\u2192}\n  1183\u2192\n  1184\u2192// validateSet ensures that a slice contains no duplicates, does not\n  1185\u2192// exceed a certain maximum size and that all entries are valid.\n  1186\u2192func validateSet[T any, K comparable](slice []T, maxSize int, validateItem func(item T, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList, itemKey func(T) K, fldPath *field.Path, opts ...validationOption) field.ErrorList {\n  1187\u2192\tallErrs := validateSlice(slice, maxSize, validateItem, fldPath, opts...)\n  1188\u2192\n  1189\u2192\tallItems := sets.New[K]()\n  1190\u2192\tfor i, item := range slice {\n  1191\u2192\t\tidxPath := fldPath.Index(i)\n  1192\u2192\t\tkey := itemKey(item)\n  1193\u2192\t\tchildPath := idxPath\n  1194\u2192\t\tif allItems.Has(key) {\n  1195\u2192\t\t\terr := field.Duplicate(childPath, key)\n  1196\u2192\t\t\tif slices.Contains(opts, uniquenessCovered) {\n  1197\u2192\t\t\t\terr = err.MarkCoveredByDeclarative()\n  1198\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1199\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, err)\n  1200\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1201\u2192\t\t\tallItems.Insert(key)\n  1202\u2192\t\t}\n  1203\u2192\t}\n  1204\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1205\u2192}\n  1206\u2192\n  1207\u2192// stringKey uses the item itself as a key for validateSet.\n  1208\u2192func stringKey(item string) string {\n  1209\u2192\treturn item\n  1210\u2192}\n  1211\u2192\n  1212\u2192// quantityKey uses the item itself as a key for validateSet.\n  1213\u2192func quantityKey(item apiresource.Quantity) string {\n  1214\u2192\treturn strconv.FormatInt(item.Value(), 10)\n  1215\u2192}\n  1216\u2192\n  1217\u2192// validateMap validates keys, items and the maximum length of a map.\n  1218\u2192// A negative maxSize disables the length check.\n  1219\u2192//\n  1220\u2192// Keys larger than truncateKeyLen get truncated in the middle. A very\n  1221\u2192// small limit gets increased because it is okay to include more details.\n  1222\u2192// This is not used for validation of keys, which has to be done by\n  1223\u2192// the callback function.\n  1224\u2192func validateMap[K ~string, T any](m map[K]T, maxSize, truncateKeyLen int, validateKey func(K, *field.Path) field.ErrorList, validateItem func(T, *field.Path) field.ErrorList, fldPath *field.Path, opts ...validationOption) field.ErrorList {\n  1225\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1226\u2192\tif maxSize >= 0 && len(m) > maxSize {\n  1227\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooMany(fldPath, len(m), maxSize))\n  1228\u2192\t}\n  1229\u2192\tfor key, item := range m {\n  1230\u2192\t\tkeyPath := fldPath.Key(truncateIfTooLong(string(key), truncateKeyLen))\n  1231\u2192\n  1232\u2192\t\tkeyValidationErrors := validateKey(key, fldPath)\n  1233\u2192\t\tif slices.Contains(opts, keysCovered) {\n  1234\u2192\t\t\tkeyValidationErrors = keyValidationErrors.MarkCoveredByDeclarative()\n  1235\u2192\t\t}\n  1236\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, keyValidationErrors...)\n  1237\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateItem(item, keyPath)...)\n  1238\u2192\t}\n  1239\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1240\u2192}\n  1241\u2192\n  1242\u2192func truncateIfTooLong(str string, maxLen int) string {\n  1243\u2192\t// The caller was overly restrictive. Increase the length to something reasonable\n  1244\u2192\t// (https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/127511#discussion_r1826206362).\n  1245\u2192\tif maxLen < 16 {\n  1246\u2192\t\tmaxLen = 16\n  1247\u2192\t}\n  1248\u2192\tif len(str) <= maxLen {\n  1249\u2192\t\treturn str\n  1250\u2192\t}\n  1251\u2192\tellipsis := \"...\"\n  1252\u2192\tremaining := maxLen - len(ellipsis)\n  1253\u2192\treturn str[0:(remaining+1)/2] + ellipsis + str[len(str)-remaining/2:]\n  1254\u2192}\n  1255\u2192\n  1256\u2192func validateDeviceStatus(device resource.AllocatedDeviceStatus, fldPath *field.Path, allocatedDevices sets.Set[structured.SharedDeviceID]) field.ErrorList {\n  1257\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1258\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDriverName(device.Driver, fldPath.Child(\"driver\"))...)\n  1259\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validatePoolName(device.Pool, fldPath.Child(\"pool\"))...)\n  1260\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceName(device.Device, fldPath.Child(\"device\"))...)\n  1261\u2192\tif device.ShareID != nil {\n  1262\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateUID(*device.ShareID, fldPath.Child(\"shareID\")).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n  1263\u2192\t}\n  1264\u2192\tdeviceID := structured.MakeDeviceID(device.Driver, device.Pool, device.Device)\n  1265\u2192\tsharedDeviceID := structured.MakeSharedDeviceID(deviceID, (*types.UID)(device.ShareID))\n  1266\u2192\tif !allocatedDevices.Has(sharedDeviceID) {\n  1267\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, sharedDeviceID, \"must be an allocated device in the claim\"))\n  1268\u2192\t}\n  1269\u2192\tif len(device.Conditions) > resource.AllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions {\n  1270\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooMany(fldPath.Child(\"conditions\"), len(device.Conditions), resource.AllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions))\n  1271\u2192\t}\n  1272\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, metav1validation.ValidateConditions(device.Conditions, fldPath.Child(\"conditions\"))...)\n  1273\u2192\tif device.Data != nil && len(device.Data.Raw) > 0 { // Data is an optional field.\n  1274\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateRawExtension(*device.Data, fldPath.Child(\"data\"), false, resource.AllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength)...)\n  1275\u2192\t}\n  1276\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateNetworkDeviceData(device.NetworkData, fldPath.Child(\"networkData\"))...)\n  1277\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1278\u2192}\n  1279\u2192\n  1280\u2192// validateRawExtension validates RawExtension as in https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/125549/\n  1281\u2192func validateRawExtension(rawExtension runtime.RawExtension, fldPath *field.Path, stored bool, rawExtensionMaxLength int) field.ErrorList {\n  1282\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1283\u2192\tvar v any\n  1284\u2192\tif len(rawExtension.Raw) == 0 {\n  1285\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"\"))\n  1286\u2192\t} else if !stored && len(rawExtension.Raw) > rawExtensionMaxLength {\n  1287\u2192\t\t// Don't even bother with parsing when too large.\n  1288\u2192\t\t// Only applies on create. Existing parameters are grand-fathered in\n  1289\u2192\t\t// because the limit was introduced in 1.32. This also means that it\n  1290\u2192\t\t// can be changed in the future.\n  1291\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath, \"\" /* unused */, rawExtensionMaxLength))\n  1292\u2192\t} else if err := json.Unmarshal(rawExtension.Raw, &v); err != nil {\n  1293\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, \"<value omitted>\", fmt.Sprintf(\"error parsing data as JSON: %v\", err.Error())))\n  1294\u2192\t} else if v == nil {\n  1295\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath, \"\"))\n  1296\u2192\t} else if _, isObject := v.(map[string]any); !isObject {\n  1297\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, \"<value omitted>\", \"must be a valid JSON object\"))\n  1298\u2192\t}\n  1299\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1300\u2192}\n  1301\u2192\n  1302\u2192func validateNetworkDeviceData(networkDeviceData *resource.NetworkDeviceData, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1303\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1304\u2192\tif networkDeviceData == nil {\n  1305\u2192\t\treturn allErrs\n  1306\u2192\t}\n  1307\u2192\n  1308\u2192\tif len(networkDeviceData.InterfaceName) > resource.NetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength {\n  1309\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath.Child(\"interfaceName\"), \"\" /* unused */, resource.NetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength).WithOrigin(\"maxLength\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n  1310\u2192\t}\n  1311\u2192\n  1312\u2192\tif len(networkDeviceData.HardwareAddress) > resource.NetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength {\n  1313\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.TooLong(fldPath.Child(\"hardwareAddress\"), \"\" /* unused */, resource.NetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength).WithOrigin(\"maxLength\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n  1314\u2192\t}\n  1315\u2192\n  1316\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSet(networkDeviceData.IPs, resource.NetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs,\n  1317\u2192\t\tfunc(address string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1318\u2192\t\t\treturn validation.IsValidInterfaceAddress(fldPath, address)\n  1319\u2192\t\t}, stringKey, fldPath.Child(\"ips\"))...)\n  1320\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1321\u2192}\n  1322\u2192\n  1323\u2192// ValidateDeviceTaintRule tests if a DeviceTaintRule object is valid.\n  1324\u2192func ValidateDeviceTaintRule(deviceTaint *resource.DeviceTaintRule) field.ErrorList {\n  1325\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMeta(&deviceTaint.ObjectMeta, false, apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n  1326\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceTaintRuleSpec(&deviceTaint.Spec, nil, field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n  1327\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1328\u2192}\n  1329\u2192\n  1330\u2192// ValidateDeviceTaintRuleUpdate tests if a DeviceTaintRule update is valid.\n  1331\u2192func ValidateDeviceTaintRuleUpdate(deviceTaint, oldDeviceTaint *resource.DeviceTaintRule) field.ErrorList {\n  1332\u2192\tallErrs := corevalidation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(&deviceTaint.ObjectMeta, &oldDeviceTaint.ObjectMeta, field.NewPath(\"metadata\"))\n  1333\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceTaintRuleSpec(&deviceTaint.Spec, &oldDeviceTaint.Spec, field.NewPath(\"spec\"))...)\n  1334\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1335\u2192}\n  1336\u2192\n  1337\u2192func validateDeviceTaintRuleSpec(spec, oldSpec *resource.DeviceTaintRuleSpec, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1338\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1339\u2192\n  1340\u2192\tvar oldFilter *resource.DeviceTaintSelector\n  1341\u2192\tif oldSpec != nil {\n  1342\u2192\t\toldFilter = oldSpec.DeviceSelector // +k8s:verify-mutation:reason=clone\n  1343\u2192\t}\n  1344\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceTaintSelector(spec.DeviceSelector, oldFilter, fldPath.Child(\"deviceSelector\"))...)\n  1345\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceTaint(spec.Taint, fldPath.Child(\"taint\"))...)\n  1346\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1347\u2192}\n  1348\u2192\n  1349\u2192func validateDeviceTaintSelector(filter, oldFilter *resource.DeviceTaintSelector, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1350\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1351\u2192\n  1352\u2192\tif filter == nil {\n  1353\u2192\t\treturn allErrs\n  1354\u2192\t}\n  1355\u2192\tif filter.DeviceClassName != nil {\n  1356\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceClassName(*filter.DeviceClassName, fldPath.Child(\"deviceClassName\"))...)\n  1357\u2192\t}\n  1358\u2192\tif filter.Driver != nil {\n  1359\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDriverName(*filter.Driver, fldPath.Child(\"driver\"))...)\n  1360\u2192\t}\n  1361\u2192\tif filter.Pool != nil {\n  1362\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validatePoolName(*filter.Pool, fldPath.Child(\"pool\"))...)\n  1363\u2192\t}\n  1364\u2192\tif filter.Device != nil {\n  1365\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateDeviceName(*filter.Device, fldPath.Child(\"device\"))...)\n  1366\u2192\t}\n  1367\u2192\n  1368\u2192\t// If the selectors are exactly as before, we treat the CEL expressions as \"stored\".\n  1369\u2192\t// Any change, including merely reordering selectors, triggers validation as new\n  1370\u2192\t// expressions.\n  1371\u2192\tstored := false\n  1372\u2192\tif oldFilter != nil {\n  1373\u2192\t\tstored = apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(filter.Selectors, oldFilter.Selectors)\n  1374\u2192\t}\n  1375\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(filter.Selectors, resource.DeviceSelectorsMaxSize,\n  1376\u2192\t\tfunc(selector resource.DeviceSelector, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1377\u2192\t\t\treturn validateSelector(selector, fldPath, stored)\n  1378\u2192\t\t},\n  1379\u2192\t\tfldPath.Child(\"selectors\"))...)\n  1380\u2192\n  1381\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1382\u2192}\n  1383\u2192\n  1384\u2192var validDeviceTolerationOperators = []resource.DeviceTolerationOperator{resource.DeviceTolerationOpEqual, resource.DeviceTolerationOpExists}\n  1385\u2192var validDeviceTaintEffects = sets.New(resource.DeviceTaintEffectNoSchedule, resource.DeviceTaintEffectNoExecute)\n  1386\u2192\n  1387\u2192func validateDeviceTaint(taint resource.DeviceTaint, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1388\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1389\u2192\n  1390\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, metav1validation.ValidateLabelName(taint.Key, fldPath.Child(\"key\"))...) // Includes checking for non-empty.\n  1391\u2192\tif taint.Value != \"\" {\n  1392\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateLabelValue(taint.Value, fldPath.Child(\"value\"))...)\n  1393\u2192\t}\n  1394\u2192\tswitch {\n  1395\u2192\tcase taint.Effect == \"\":\n  1396\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"effect\"), \"\").MarkCoveredByDeclarative()) // Required in a taint.\n  1397\u2192\tcase !validDeviceTaintEffects.Has(taint.Effect):\n  1398\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath.Child(\"effect\"), taint.Effect, sets.List(validDeviceTaintEffects)).MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n  1399\u2192\t}\n  1400\u2192\n  1401\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1402\u2192}\n  1403\u2192\n  1404\u2192func validateDeviceToleration(toleration resource.DeviceToleration, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1405\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1406\u2192\n  1407\u2192\tif toleration.Key != \"\" {\n  1408\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, metav1validation.ValidateLabelName(toleration.Key, fldPath.Child(\"key\")).MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)\n  1409\u2192\t}\n  1410\u2192\tswitch toleration.Operator {\n  1411\u2192\tcase resource.DeviceTolerationOpExists:\n  1412\u2192\t\tif toleration.Value != \"\" {\n  1413\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"value\"), toleration.Value, \"must be empty for operator `Exists`\"))\n  1414\u2192\t\t}\n  1415\u2192\tcase resource.DeviceTolerationOpEqual:\n  1416\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateLabelValue(toleration.Value, fldPath.Child(\"value\"))...)\n  1417\u2192\tcase \"\":\n  1418\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Required(fldPath.Child(\"operator\"), \"\"))\n  1419\u2192\tdefault:\n  1420\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath.Child(\"operator\"), toleration.Operator, validDeviceTolerationOperators).MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n  1421\u2192\t}\n  1422\u2192\tswitch {\n  1423\u2192\tcase toleration.Effect == \"\":\n  1424\u2192\t\t// Optional in a toleration.\n  1425\u2192\tcase !validDeviceTaintEffects.Has(toleration.Effect):\n  1426\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.NotSupported(fldPath.Child(\"effect\"), toleration.Effect, sets.List(validDeviceTaintEffects)).MarkCoveredByDeclarative())\n  1427\u2192\t}\n  1428\u2192\n  1429\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1430\u2192}\n  1431\u2192\n  1432\u2192func validateLabelValue(value string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1433\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1434\u2192\n  1435\u2192\t// There's no metav1validation.ValidateLabelValue.\n  1436\u2192\tfor _, msg := range validation.IsValidLabelValue(value) {\n  1437\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, value, msg))\n  1438\u2192\t}\n  1439\u2192\n  1440\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1441\u2192}\n  1442\u2192\n  1443\u2192func validateDeviceBindingParameters(bindingConditions, bindingFailureConditions []string, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n  1444\u2192\tvar allErrs field.ErrorList\n  1445\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(bindingConditions, resource.BindingConditionsMaxSize,\n  1446\u2192\t\tmetav1validation.ValidateLabelName, fldPath.Child(\"bindingConditions\"), sizeCovered)...)\n  1447\u2192\n  1448\u2192\tallErrs = append(allErrs, validateSlice(bindingFailureConditions, resource.BindingFailureConditionsMaxSize,\n  1449\u2192\t\tmetav1validation.ValidateLabelName, fldPath.Child(\"bindingFailureConditions\"), sizeCovered)...)\n  1450\u2192\n  1451\u2192\t// ensure BindingConditions and BindingFailureConditions contain no duplicate items and do not overlap with each other\n  1452\u2192\tconditionsSet := sets.New[string]()\n  1453\u2192\tfor i, condition := range bindingConditions {\n  1454\u2192\t\tif conditionsSet.Has(condition) {\n  1455\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Duplicate(fldPath.Child(\"bindingConditions\").Index(i), condition))\n  1456\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1457\u2192\t\t\tconditionsSet.Insert(condition)\n  1458\u2192\t\t}\n  1459\u2192\t}\n  1460\u2192\tfailureConditionsSet := sets.New[string]()\n  1461\u2192\tfor i, condition := range bindingFailureConditions {\n  1462\u2192\t\tif failureConditionsSet.Has(condition) {\n  1463\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Duplicate(fldPath.Child(\"bindingFailureConditions\").Index(i), condition))\n  1464\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1465\u2192\t\t\tfailureConditionsSet.Insert(condition)\n  1466\u2192\t\t}\n  1467\u2192\t\tif sets.New(bindingConditions...).Has(condition) {\n  1468\u2192\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"bindingFailureConditions\").Index(i), condition, \"bindingFailureConditions must not overlap with bindingConditions\"))\n  1469\u2192\t\t}\n  1470\u2192\t}\n  1471\u2192\n  1472\u2192\tif len(bindingConditions) == 0 && len(bindingFailureConditions) > 0 {\n  1473\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"bindingConditions\"), bindingConditions, \"bindingConditions are required to use bindingFailureConditions\"))\n  1474\u2192\t}\n  1475\u2192\tif len(bindingFailureConditions) == 0 && len(bindingConditions) > 0 {\n  1476\u2192\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath.Child(\"bindingFailureConditions\"), bindingFailureConditions, \"bindingFailureConditions are required to use bindingConditions\"))\n  1477\u2192\t}\n  1478\u2192\n  1479\u2192\treturn allErrs\n  1480\u2192}\n  1481\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
45. Read @ -

  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/dynamicresources.go"

53\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/extended\"\n    55\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/feature\"\n    56\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/names\"\n    57\u2192\tschedutil \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/util\"\n    58\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/util/assumecache\"\n    59\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/slice\"\n    60\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n    61\u2192)\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192const (\n    64\u2192\t// Name is the name of the plugin used in Registry and configurations.\n    65\u2192\tName = names.DynamicResources\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\tstateKey fwk.StateKey = Name\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t// specialClaimInMemName is the name of the special resource claim that\n    70\u2192\t// exists only in memory. The claim will get a generated name when it is\n    71\u2192\t// written to API server.\n    72\u2192\t//\n    73\u2192\t// It's intentionally not a valid ResourceClaim name to avoid conflicts with\n    74\u2192\t// some actual ResourceClaim in the apiserver.\n    75\u2192\tspecialClaimInMemName = \"<extended-resources>\"\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\t// BindingTimeoutDefaultSeconds is the default timeout for waiting for\n    78\u2192\t// BindingConditions to be ready.\n    79\u2192\tBindingTimeoutDefaultSeconds = 600\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// AssumeExtendedResourceTimeoutDefaultSeconds is the default timeout for waiting\n    82\u2192\t// for the extended resource claim to be updated in assumed cache.\n    83\u2192\tAssumeExtendedResourceTimeoutDefaultSeconds = 120\n    84\u2192)\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// The state is initialized in PreFilter phase. Because we save the pointer in\n    87\u2192// fwk.CycleState, in the later phases we don't need to call Write method\n    88\u2192// to update the value\n    89\u2192type stateData struct {\n    90\u2192\t// A copy of all claims for the Pod (i.e. 1:1 match with\n    91\u2192\t// pod.Spec.ResourceClaims), initially with the status from the start\n    92\u2192\t// of the scheduling cycle. Each claim instance is read-only because it\n    93\u2192\t// might come from the informer cache. The instances get replaced when\n    94\u2192\t// the plugin itself successfully does an Update.\n    95\u2192\t//\n    96\u2192\t// When the DRAExtendedResource feature is enabled, the special ResourceClaim\n    97\u2192\t// which represents extended resource requests is also stored here.\n    98\u2192\t// The plugin code should treat this field as a black box and only\n    99\u2192\t// access it via its methods, in particular:\n   100\u2192\t// - The all method can be used to iterate over all claims, including\n   101\u2192\t//   the special one.\n   102\u2192\t// - The allUserClaims method excludes the special one.\n   103\u2192\t//\n   104\u2192\t// Empty if the Pod has no claims and no special claim for extended\n   105\u2192\t// resources backed by DRA, in which case the plugin has no work to do for\n   106\u2192\t// the Pod.\n   107\u2192\tclaims claimStore\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192\t// draExtendedResource stores data for extended resources backed by DRA.\n   110\u2192\t// It will remain empty when the DRAExtendedResource feature is disabled.\n   111\u2192\tdraExtendedResource draExtendedResource\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// Allocator handles claims with structured parameters, which is all of them nowadays.\n   114\u2192\tallocator structured.Allocator\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// mutex must be locked while accessing any of the fields below.\n   117\u2192\tmutex sync.Mutex\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t// The indices of all claims that:\n   120\u2192\t// - are allocated\n   121\u2192\t// - were not available on at least one node\n   122\u2192\t//\n   123\u2192\t// Set in parallel during Filter, so write access there must be\n   124\u2192\t// protected by the mutex. Used by PostFilter.\n   125\u2192\tunavailableClaims sets.Set[int]\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// informationsForClaim has one entry for each claim in claims.\n   128\u2192\tinformationsForClaim []informationForClaim\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192\t// nodeAllocations caches the result of Filter for the nodes, its key is node name.\n   131\u2192\tnodeAllocations map[string]nodeAllocation\n   132\u2192}\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192func (d *stateData) Clone() fwk.StateData {\n   135\u2192\treturn d\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192// draExtendedResource stores data for extended resources backed by DRA.\n   139\u2192// It will remain empty when the DRAExtendedResource feature is disabled.\n   140\u2192type draExtendedResource struct {\n   141\u2192\t// May have extended resource backed by DRA.\n   142\u2192\tpodScalarResources map[v1.ResourceName]int64\n   143\u2192\t// The mapping of extended resource to device class name\n   144\u2192\tresourceToDeviceClass map[v1.ResourceName]string\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192type informationForClaim struct {\n   148\u2192\t// Node selector based on the claim status if allocated.\n   149\u2192\tavailableOnNodes *nodeaffinity.NodeSelector\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t// Set by Reserved, published by PreBind, empty if nothing had to be allocated.\n   152\u2192\tallocation *resourceapi.AllocationResult\n   153\u2192}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192// nodeAllocation holds the allocation results and extended resource claim per node.\n   156\u2192type nodeAllocation struct {\n   157\u2192\t// allocationResults has the allocation results, matching the order of\n   158\u2192\t// claims which had to be allocated.\n   159\u2192\tallocationResults []resourceapi.AllocationResult\n   160\u2192\t// extendedResourceClaim has the special claim for extended resource backed by DRA\n   161\u2192\t// created during Filter for the nodes.\n   162\u2192\textendedResourceClaim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim\n   163\u2192}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192// DynamicResources is a plugin that ensures that ResourceClaims are allocated.\n   166\u2192type DynamicResources struct {\n   167\u2192\tenabled       bool\n   168\u2192\tfts           feature.Features\n   169\u2192\tfilterTimeout time.Duration\n   170\u2192\tfh            fwk.Handle\n   171\u2192\tclientset     kubernetes.Interface\n   172\u2192\tcelCache      *cel.Cache\n   173\u2192\tdraManager    fwk.SharedDRAManager\n   174\u2192}\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192// New initializes a new plugin and returns it.\n   177\u2192func New(ctx context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, fh fwk.Handle, fts feature.Features) (fwk.Plugin, error) {\n   178\u2192\tif !fts.EnableDynamicResourceAllocation {\n   179\u2192\t\t// Disabled, won't do anything.\n   180\u2192\t\treturn &DynamicResources{}, nil\n   181\u2192\t}\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192\targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.DynamicResourcesArgs)\n   184\u2192\tif !ok {\n   185\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"got args of type %T, want *DynamicResourcesArgs\", plArgs)\n   186\u2192\t}\n   187\u2192\tif err := validation.ValidateDynamicResourcesArgs(nil, args, fts); err != nil {\n   188\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   189\u2192\t}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192\tpl := &DynamicResources{\n   192\u2192\t\tenabled:       true,\n   193\u2192\t\tfts:           fts,\n   194\u2192\t\tfilterTimeout: ptr.Deref(args.FilterTimeout, metav1.Duration{}).Duration,\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\t\tfh:        fh,\n   197\u2192\t\tclientset: fh.ClientSet(),\n   198\u2192\t\t// This is a LRU cache for compiled CEL expressions. The most\n   199\u2192\t\t// recent 10 of them get reused across different scheduling\n   200\u2192\t\t// cycles.\n   201\u2192\t\tcelCache:   cel.NewCache(10, cel.Features{EnableConsumableCapacity: fts.EnableDRAConsumableCapacity}),\n   202\u2192\t\tdraManager: fh.SharedDRAManager(),\n   203\u2192\t}\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192\treturn pl, nil\n   206\u2192}\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192var _ fwk.PreEnqueuePlugin = &DynamicResources{}\n   209\u2192var _ fwk.PreFilterPlugin = &DynamicResources{}\n   210\u2192var _ fwk.FilterPlugin = &DynamicResources{}\n   211\u2192var _ fwk.PostFilterPlugin = &DynamicResources{}\n   212\u2192var _ fwk.ReservePlugin = &DynamicResources{}\n   213\u2192var _ fwk.EnqueueExtensions = &DynamicResources{}\n   214\u2192var _ fwk.PreBindPlugin = &DynamicResources{}\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\n   217\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) Name() string {\n   218\u2192\treturn Name\n   219\u2192}\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n   222\u2192// failed by this plugin schedulable.\n   223\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) EventsToRegister(_ context.Context) ([]fwk.ClusterEventWithHint, error) {\n   224\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n   225\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   226\u2192\t}\n   227\u2192\t// A resource might depend on node labels for topology filtering.\n   228\u2192\t// A new or updated node may make pods schedulable.\n   229\u2192\t//\n   230\u2192\t// A note about UpdateNodeTaint event:\n   231\u2192\t// Ideally, it's supposed to register only Add | UpdateNodeLabel because UpdateNodeTaint will never change the result from this plugin.\n   232\u2192\t// But, we may miss Node/Add event due to preCheck, and we decided to register UpdateNodeTaint | UpdateNodeLabel for all plugins registering Node/Add.\n   233\u2192\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/109437\n   234\u2192\tnodeActionType := fwk.Add | fwk.UpdateNodeLabel | fwk.UpdateNodeTaint | fwk.UpdateNodeAllocatable\n   235\u2192\tif pl.fts.EnableSchedulingQueueHint {\n   236\u2192\t\t// When QHint is enabled, the problematic preCheck is already removed, and we can remove UpdateNodeTaint.\n   237\u2192\t\tnodeActionType = fwk.Add | fwk.UpdateNodeLabel | fwk.UpdateNodeAllocatable\n   238\u2192\t}\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192\tevents := []fwk.ClusterEventWithHint{\n   241\u2192\t\t{Event: fwk.ClusterEvent{Resource: fwk.Node, ActionType: nodeActionType}},\n   242\u2192\t\t// Allocation is tracked in ResourceClaims, so any changes may make the pods schedulable.\n   243\u2192\t\t{Event: fwk.ClusterEvent{Resource: fwk.ResourceClaim, ActionType: fwk.Add | fwk.Update}, QueueingHintFn: pl.isSchedulableAfterClaimChange},\n   244\u2192\t\t// Adding the ResourceClaim name to the pod status makes pods waiting for their ResourceClaim schedulable.\n   245\u2192\t\t{Event: fwk.ClusterEvent{Resource: fwk.Pod, ActionType: fwk.UpdatePodGeneratedResourceClaim}, QueueingHintFn: pl.isSchedulableAfterPodChange},\n   246\u2192\t\t// A pod might be waiting for a class to get created or modified.\n   247\u2192\t\t{Event: fwk.ClusterEvent{Resource: fwk.DeviceClass, ActionType: fwk.Add | fwk.Update}},\n   248\u2192\t\t// Adding or updating a ResourceSlice might make a pod schedulable because new resources became available.\n   249\u2192\t\t{Event: fwk.ClusterEvent{Resource: fwk.ResourceSlice, ActionType: fwk.Add | fwk.Update}},\n   250\u2192\t}\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192\treturn events, nil\n   253\u2192}\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192// PreEnqueue checks if there are known reasons why a pod currently cannot be\n   256\u2192// scheduled. When this fails, one of the registered events can trigger another\n   257\u2192// attempt.\n   258\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) PreEnqueue(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) (status *fwk.Status) {\n   259\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n   260\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   261\u2192\t}\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192\tif err := pl.foreachPodResourceClaim(pod, nil); err != nil {\n   264\u2192\t\treturn statusUnschedulable(klog.FromContext(ctx), err.Error())\n   265\u2192\t}\n   266\u2192\treturn nil\n   267\u2192}\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192// isSchedulableAfterClaimChange is invoked for add and update claim events reported by\n   270\u2192// an informer. It checks whether that change made a previously unschedulable\n   271\u2192// pod schedulable. It errs on the side of letting a pod scheduling attempt\n   272\u2192// happen. The delete claim event will not invoke it, so newObj will never be nil.\n   273\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) isSchedulableAfterClaimChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (fwk.QueueingHint, error) {\n   274\u2192\toriginalClaim, modifiedClaim, err := schedutil.As[*resourceapi.ResourceClaim](oldObj, newObj)\n   275\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   276\u2192\t\t// Shouldn't happen.\n   277\u2192\t\treturn fwk.Queue, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected object in isSchedulableAfterClaimChange: %w\", err)\n   278\u2192\t}\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192\tusesClaim := false\n   281\u2192\tif err := pl.foreachPodResourceClaim(pod, func(_ string, claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) {\n   282\u2192\t\tif claim.UID == modifiedClaim.UID {\n   283\u2192\t\t\tusesClaim = true\n   284\u2192\t\t}\n   285\u2192\t}); err != nil {\n   286\u2192\t\t// This is not an unexpected error: we know that\n   287\u2192\t\t// foreachPodResourceClaim only returns errors for \"not\n   288\u2192\t\t// schedulable\".\n   289\u2192\t\tif loggerV := logger.V(6); loggerV.Enabled() {\n   290\u2192\t\t\towner := metav1.GetControllerOf(modifiedClaim)\n   291\u2192\t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"pod is not schedulable after resource claim change\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim), \"claimOwner\", owner, \"reason\", err.Error())\n   292\u2192\t\t}\n   293\u2192\t\treturn fwk.QueueSkip, nil\n   294\u2192\t}\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192\tif originalClaim != nil &&\n   297\u2192\t\toriginalClaim.Status.Allocation != nil &&\n   298\u2192\t\tmodifiedClaim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n   299\u2192\t\t// A claim with structured parameters was deallocated. This might have made\n   300\u2192\t\t// resources available for other pods.\n   301\u2192\t\tlogger.V(6).Info(\"claim with structured parameters got deallocated\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim))\n   302\u2192\t\treturn fwk.Queue, nil\n   303\u2192\t}\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192\tif !usesClaim {\n   306\u2192\t\t// This was not the claim the pod was waiting for.\n   307\u2192\t\tlogger.V(6).Info(\"unrelated claim got modified\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim))\n   308\u2192\t\treturn fwk.QueueSkip, nil\n   309\u2192\t}\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192\tif originalClaim == nil {\n   312\u2192\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"claim for pod got created\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim))\n   313\u2192\t\treturn fwk.Queue, nil\n   314\u2192\t}\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. If the entire\n   317\u2192\t// status is as before, then something else must have changed\n   318\u2192\t// and we don't care. What happens in practice is that the\n   319\u2192\t// resource driver adds the finalizer.\n   320\u2192\tif apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(&originalClaim.Status, &modifiedClaim.Status) {\n   321\u2192\t\tif loggerV := logger.V(7); loggerV.Enabled() {\n   322\u2192\t\t\t// Log more information.\n   323\u2192\t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"claim for pod got modified where the pod doesn't care\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim), \"diff\", diff.Diff(originalClaim, modifiedClaim))\n   324\u2192\t\t} else {\n   325\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(6).Info(\"claim for pod got modified where the pod doesn't care\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim))\n   326\u2192\t\t}\n   327\u2192\t\treturn fwk.QueueSkip, nil\n   328\u2192\t}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"status of claim for pod got updated\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim\", klog.KObj(modifiedClaim))\n   331\u2192\treturn fwk.Queue, nil\n   332\u2192}\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192// isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked for update pod events reported by\n   335\u2192// an informer. It checks whether that change adds the ResourceClaim(s) that the\n   336\u2192// pod has been waiting for.\n   337\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (fwk.QueueingHint, error) {\n   338\u2192\t_, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](nil, newObj)\n   339\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   340\u2192\t\t// Shouldn't happen.\n   341\u2192\t\treturn fwk.Queue, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected object in isSchedulableAfterClaimChange: %w\", err)\n   342\u2192\t}\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192\tif pod.UID != modifiedPod.UID {\n   345\u2192\t\tlogger.V(7).Info(\"pod is not schedulable after change in other pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n   346\u2192\t\treturn fwk.QueueSkip, nil\n   347\u2192\t}\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192\tif err := pl.foreachPodResourceClaim(modifiedPod, nil); err != nil {\n   350\u2192\t\t// This is not an unexpected error: we know that\n   351\u2192\t\t// foreachPodResourceClaim only returns errors for \"not\n   352\u2192\t\t// schedulable\".\n   353\u2192\t\tlogger.V(6).Info(\"pod is not schedulable after being updated\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n   354\u2192\t\treturn fwk.QueueSkip, nil\n   355\u2192\t}\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"pod got updated and is schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n   358\u2192\treturn fwk.Queue, nil\n   359\u2192}\n   360\u2192\n   361\u2192// podResourceClaims returns the ResourceClaims for all pod.Spec.PodResourceClaims.\n   362\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) podResourceClaims(pod *v1.Pod) ([]*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, error) {\n   363\u2192\tclaims := make([]*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, 0, len(pod.Spec.ResourceClaims))\n   364\u2192\tif err := pl.foreachPodResourceClaim(pod, func(_ string, claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) {\n   365\u2192\t\t// We store the pointer as returned by the lister. The\n   366\u2192\t\t// assumption is that if a claim gets modified while our code\n   367\u2192\t\t// runs, the cache will store a new pointer, not mutate the\n   368\u2192\t\t// existing object that we point to here.\n   369\u2192\t\tclaims = append(claims, claim)\n   370\u2192\t}); err != nil {\n   371\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   372\u2192\t}\n   373\u2192\treturn claims, nil\n   374\u2192}\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192// foreachPodResourceClaim checks that each ResourceClaim for the pod exists.\n   377\u2192// It calls an optional handler for those claims that it finds.\n   378\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) foreachPodResourceClaim(pod *v1.Pod, cb func(podResourceName string, claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim)) error {\n   379\u2192\tfor _, resource := range pod.Spec.ResourceClaims {\n   380\u2192\t\tclaimName, mustCheckOwner, err := resourceclaim.Name(pod, &resource)\n   381\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   382\u2192\t\t\treturn err\n   383\u2192\t\t}\n   384\u2192\t\t// The claim name might be nil if no underlying resource claim\n   385\u2192\t\t// was generated for the referenced claim. There are valid use\n   386\u2192\t\t// cases when this might happen, so we simply skip it.\n   387\u2192\t\tif claimName == nil {\n   388\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   389\u2192\t\t}\n   390\u2192\t\tclaim, err := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().Get(pod.Namespace, *claimName)\n   391\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   392\u2192\t\t\treturn err\n   393\u2192\t\t}\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192\t\tif claim.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n   396\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resourceclaim %q is being deleted\", claim.Name)\n   397\u2192\t\t}\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192\t\tif mustCheckOwner {\n   400\u2192\t\t\tif err := resourceclaim.IsForPod(pod, claim); err != nil {\n   401\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n   402\u2192\t\t\t}\n   403\u2192\t\t}\n   404\u2192\t\tif cb != nil {\n   405\u2192\t\t\tcb(resource.Name, claim)\n   406\u2192\t\t}\n   407\u2192\t}\n   408\u2192\treturn nil\n   409\u2192}\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192// hasDeviceClassMappedExtendedResource returns true when the given resource list has an extended resource, that has\n   412\u2192// a mapping to a device class.\n   413\u2192func hasDeviceClassMappedExtendedResource(reqs v1.ResourceList, deviceClassMapping map[v1.ResourceName]string) bool {\n   414\u2192\tfor rName, rValue := range reqs {\n   415\u2192\t\tif rValue.IsZero() {\n   416\u2192\t\t\t// We only care about the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n   417\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   418\u2192\t\t}\n   419\u2192\t\tif schedutil.IsDRAExtendedResourceName(rName) {\n   420\u2192\t\t\t_, ok := deviceClassMapping[rName]\n   421\u2192\t\t\tif ok {\n   422\u2192\t\t\t\treturn true\n   423\u2192\t\t\t}\n   424\u2192\t\t}\n   425\u2192\t}\n   426\u2192\treturn false\n   427\u2192}\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192// findExtendedResourceClaim looks for the extended resource claim, i.e., the claim with special annotation\n   430\u2192// set to \"true\", and with the pod as owner. It must be called with all ResourceClaims in the cluster.\n   431\u2192// The returned ResourceClaim is read-only.\n   432\u2192func findExtendedResourceClaim(pod *v1.Pod, resourceClaims []*resourceapi.ResourceClaim) *resourceapi.ResourceClaim {\n   433\u2192\tfor _, c := range resourceClaims {\n   434\u2192\t\tif c.Annotations[resourceapi.ExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation] == \"true\" {\n   435\u2192\t\t\tfor _, or := range c.OwnerReferences {\n   436\u2192\t\t\t\tif or.Name == pod.Name && *or.Controller && or.UID == pod.UID {\n   437\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn c\n   438\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   439\u2192\t\t\t}\n   440\u2192\t\t}\n   441\u2192\t}\n   442\u2192\treturn nil\n   443\u2192}\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192// preFilterExtendedResources checks if there is any extended resource in the\n   446\u2192// pod requests that has a device class mapping, i.e., there is a device class\n   447\u2192// that has spec.ExtendedResourceName or its implicit extended resource name\n   448\u2192// matching the given extended resource in that pod requests.\n   449\u2192//\n   450\u2192// It looks for the special resource claim for the pod created from prior scheduling\n   451\u2192// cycle. If not found, it creates the special claim with no Requests in the Spec,\n   452\u2192// with a temporary UID, and the specialClaimInMemName name.\n   453\u2192// Either way, the special claim is stored in state.claims.\n   454\u2192//\n   455\u2192// In addition, draExtendedResource is also stored in the cycle state.\n   456\u2192//\n   457\u2192// It returns the special ResourceClaim and an error status. It returns nil for both\n   458\u2192// if the feature is disabled or not required for the Pod.\n   459\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) preFilterExtendedResources(pod *v1.Pod, logger klog.Logger, s *stateData) (*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, *fwk.Status) {\n   460\u2192\tif !pl.fts.EnableDRAExtendedResource {\n   461\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   462\u2192\t}\n   463\u2192\n   464\u2192\tdeviceClassMapping, err := extended.DeviceClassMapping(pl.draManager)\n   465\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   466\u2192\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err, \"retrieving extended resource to DeviceClass mapping\")\n   467\u2192\t}\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192\treqs := resourcehelper.PodRequests(pod, resourcehelper.PodResourcesOptions{})\n   470\u2192\thasExtendedResource := hasDeviceClassMappedExtendedResource(reqs, deviceClassMapping)\n   471\u2192\tif !hasExtendedResource {\n   472\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   473\u2192\t}\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192\ts.draExtendedResource.resourceToDeviceClass = deviceClassMapping\n   476\u2192\tr := framework.NewResource(reqs)\n   477\u2192\ts.draExtendedResource.podScalarResources = r.ScalarResources\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192\tresourceClaims, err := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().List()\n   480\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   481\u2192\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err, \"listing ResourceClaims\")\n   482\u2192\t}\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192\t// Check if the special resource claim has been created from prior scheduling cycle.\n   485\u2192\t//\n   486\u2192\t// If it was already allocated earlier, that allocation might not be valid anymore.\n   487\u2192\t// We could try to check that, but it depends on various factors that are difficult to\n   488\u2192\t// cover (basically needs to replicate allocator logic) and if it turns out that the\n   489\u2192\t// allocation is stale, we would have to schedule with those allocated devices not\n   490\u2192\t// available for a new allocation. This situation should be rare (= binding failure),\n   491\u2192\t// so we solve it via brute-force\n   492\u2192\t// - Kick off deallocation in the background.\n   493\u2192\t// - Mark the pod as unschedulable. Successful deallocation will make it schedulable again.\n   494\u2192\textendedResourceClaim := findExtendedResourceClaim(pod, resourceClaims)\n   495\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim == nil {\n   496\u2192\t\t// Create one special claim for all extended resources backed by DRA in the Pod.\n   497\u2192\t\t// Create the ResourceClaim with pod as owner, with a generated name that uses\n   498\u2192\t\t// <pod name>-extended-resources- as base.
