The final name will get truncated if it\n   499\u2192\t\t// would be too long.\n   500\u2192\t\textendedResourceClaim = &resourceapi.ResourceClaim{\n   501\u2192\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n   502\u2192\t\t\t\tNamespace: pod.Namespace,\n   503\u2192\t\t\t\tName:      specialClaimInMemName,\n   504\u2192\t\t\t\t// fake temporary UID for use in SignalClaimPendingAllocation\n   505\u2192\t\t\t\tUID:          types.UID(uuid.NewUUID()),\n   506\u2192\t\t\t\tGenerateName: pod.Name + \"-extended-resources-\",\n   507\u2192\t\t\t\tOwnerReferences: []metav1.OwnerReference{\n   508\u2192\t\t\t\t\t{\n   509\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: \"v1\",\n   510\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tKind:       \"Pod\",\n   511\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tName:       pod.Name,\n   512\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tUID:        pod.UID,\n   513\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tController: ptr.To(true),\n   514\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n   515\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   516\u2192\t\t\t\tAnnotations: map[string]string{\n   517\u2192\t\t\t\t\tresourceapi.ExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation: \"true\",\n   518\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   519\u2192\t\t\t},\n   520\u2192\t\t\tSpec: resourceapi.ResourceClaimSpec{},\n   521\u2192\t\t}\n   522\u2192\t}\n   523\u2192\treturn extendedResourceClaim, nil\n   524\u2192}\n   525\u2192\n   526\u2192// PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point to check if pod has all\n   527\u2192// immediate claims bound. UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is returned if\n   528\u2192// the pod cannot be scheduled at the moment on any node.\n   529\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, state fwk.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []fwk.NodeInfo) (*fwk.PreFilterResult, *fwk.Status) {\n   530\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n   531\u2192\t\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Skip)\n   532\u2192\t}\n   533\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n   534\u2192\n   535\u2192\t// If the pod does not reference any claim, we don't need to do\n   536\u2192\t// anything for it. We just initialize an empty state to record that\n   537\u2192\t// observation for the other functions. This gets updated below\n   538\u2192\t// if we get that far.\n   539\u2192\ts := &stateData{}\n   540\u2192\tstate.Write(stateKey, s)\n   541\u2192\n   542\u2192\tuserClaims, err := pl.podResourceClaims(pod)\n   543\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   544\u2192\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, err.Error())\n   545\u2192\t}\n   546\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"pod resource claims\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"resourceclaims\", klog.KObjSlice(userClaims))\n   547\u2192\textendedResourceClaim, status := pl.preFilterExtendedResources(pod, logger, s)\n   548\u2192\tif status != nil {\n   549\u2192\t\treturn nil, status\n   550\u2192\t}\n   551\u2192\tclaims := newClaimStore(userClaims, extendedResourceClaim)\n   552\u2192\n   553\u2192\t// This check covers user and extended ResourceClaim.\n   554\u2192\tif claims.empty() {\n   555\u2192\t\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Skip)\n   556\u2192\t}\n   557\u2192\n   558\u2192\t// Counts all claims which the scheduler needs to allocate itself.\n   559\u2192\tnumClaimsToAllocate := 0\n   560\u2192\ts.informationsForClaim = make([]informationForClaim, claims.len())\n   561\u2192\tfor index, claim := range claims.all() {\n   562\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil &&\n   563\u2192\t\t\t!resourceclaim.CanBeReserved(claim) &&\n   564\u2192\t\t\t!resourceclaim.IsReservedForPod(pod, claim) {\n   565\u2192\t\t\t// Resource is in use. The pod has to wait.\n   566\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, \"resourceclaim in use\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   567\u2192\t\t}\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil {\n   570\u2192\t\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation.NodeSelector != nil {\n   571\u2192\t\t\t\tnodeSelector, err := nodeaffinity.NewNodeSelector(claim.Status.Allocation.NodeSelector)\n   572\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   573\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n   574\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   575\u2192\t\t\t\ts.informationsForClaim[index].availableOnNodes = nodeSelector\n   576\u2192\t\t\t}\n   577\u2192\t\t} else {\n   578\u2192\t\t\tnumClaimsToAllocate++\n   579\u2192\n   580\u2192\t\t\t// Allocation in flight? Better wait for that\n   581\u2192\t\t\t// to finish, see inFlightAllocations\n   582\u2192\t\t\t// documentation for details.\n   583\u2192\t\t\tif pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().ClaimHasPendingAllocation(claim.UID) {\n   584\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, fmt.Sprintf(\"resource claim %s is in the process of being allocated\", klog.KObj(claim)))\n   585\u2192\t\t\t}\n   586\u2192\n   587\u2192\t\t\t// Continue without validating the special claim for extended resource backed by DRA.\n   588\u2192\t\t\t// For the claim template, it is not allocated yet at this point, and it does not have a spec.\n   589\u2192\t\t\t// For the claim from prior scheduling cycle, leave it to Filter Phase to validate.\n   590\u2192\t\t\tif claim == extendedResourceClaim {\n   591\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   592\u2192\t\t\t}\n   593\u2192\n   594\u2192\t\t\t// Check all requests and device classes. If a class\n   595\u2192\t\t\t// does not exist, scheduling cannot proceed, no matter\n   596\u2192\t\t\t// how the claim is being allocated.\n   597\u2192\t\t\t//\n   598\u2192\t\t\t// When using a control plane controller, a class might\n   599\u2192\t\t\t// have a node filter. This is useful for trimming the\n   600\u2192\t\t\t// initial set of potential nodes before we ask the\n   601\u2192\t\t\t// driver(s) for information about the specific pod.\n   602\u2192\t\t\tfor _, request := range claim.Spec.Devices.Requests {\n   603\u2192\t\t\t\t// The requirements differ depending on whether the request has a list of\n   604\u2192\t\t\t\t// alternative subrequests defined in the firstAvailable field.\n   605\u2192\t\t\t\tswitch {\n   606\u2192\t\t\t\tcase request.Exactly != nil:\n   607\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif status := pl.validateDeviceClass(logger, request.Exactly.DeviceClassName, request.Name); status != nil {\n   608\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, status\n   609\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   610\u2192\t\t\t\tcase len(request.FirstAvailable) > 0:\n   611\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif !pl.fts.EnableDRAPrioritizedList {\n   612\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, fmt.Sprintf(\"resource claim %s, request %s: has subrequests, but the DRAPrioritizedList feature is disabled\", klog.KObj(claim), request.Name))\n   613\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   614\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfor _, subRequest := range request.FirstAvailable {\n   615\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tqualRequestName := strings.Join([]string{request.Name, subRequest.Name}, \"/\")\n   616\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tif status := pl.validateDeviceClass(logger, subRequest.DeviceClassName, qualRequestName); status != nil {\n   617\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, status\n   618\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   619\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   620\u2192\t\t\t\tdefault:\n   621\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, fmt.Sprintf(\"resource claim %s, request %s: unknown request type\", klog.KObj(claim), request.Name))\n   622\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   623\u2192\t\t\t}\n   624\u2192\t\t}\n   625\u2192\t}\n   626\u2192\n   627\u2192\tif numClaimsToAllocate > 0 {\n   628\u2192\t\tif loggerV := logger.V(5); loggerV.Enabled() {\n   629\u2192\t\t\tclaimsToAllocate := make([]*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, 0, claims.len())\n   630\u2192\t\t\tfor _, claim := range claims.toAllocate() {\n   631\u2192\t\t\t\tclaimsToAllocate = append(claimsToAllocate, claim)\n   632\u2192\t\t\t}\n   633\u2192\t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"Preparing allocation with structured parameters\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"resourceclaims\", klog.KObjSlice(claimsToAllocate))\n   634\u2192\t\t}\n   635\u2192\n   636\u2192\t\t// Doing this over and over again for each pod could be avoided\n   637\u2192\t\t// by setting the allocator up once and then keeping it up-to-date\n   638\u2192\t\t// as changes are observed.\n   639\u2192\t\t//\n   640\u2192\t\t// But that would cause problems for using the plugin in the\n   641\u2192\t\t// Cluster Autoscaler. If this step here turns out to be\n   642\u2192\t\t// expensive, we may have to maintain and update state more\n   643\u2192\t\t// persistently.\n   644\u2192\t\t//\n   645\u2192\t\t// Claims (and thus their devices) are treated as \"allocated\" if they are in the assume cache\n   646\u2192\t\t// or currently their allocation is in-flight. This does not change\n   647\u2192\t\t// during filtering, so we can determine that once.\n   648\u2192\t\tvar allocatedState *structured.AllocatedState\n   649\u2192\t\tif pl.fts.EnableDRAConsumableCapacity {\n   650\u2192\t\t\tallocatedState, err = pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().GatherAllocatedState()\n   651\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   652\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n   653\u2192\t\t\t}\n   654\u2192\t\t\tif allocatedState == nil {\n   655\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, errors.New(\"nil allocated state\"))\n   656\u2192\t\t\t}\n   657\u2192\t\t} else {\n   658\u2192\t\t\tallocatedDevices, err := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().ListAllAllocatedDevices()\n   659\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   660\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n   661\u2192\t\t\t}\n   662\u2192\t\t\tallocatedState = &structured.AllocatedState{\n   663\u2192\t\t\t\tAllocatedDevices:         allocatedDevices,\n   664\u2192\t\t\t\tAllocatedSharedDeviceIDs: sets.New[structured.SharedDeviceID](),\n   665\u2192\t\t\t\tAggregatedCapacity:       structured.NewConsumedCapacityCollection(),\n   666\u2192\t\t\t}\n   667\u2192\t\t}\n   668\u2192\t\tslices, err := pl.draManager.ResourceSlices().ListWithDeviceTaintRules()\n   669\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   670\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n   671\u2192\t\t}\n   672\u2192\t\tfeatures := allocatorFeatures(pl.fts)\n   673\u2192\t\tallocator, err := structured.NewAllocator(ctx, features, *allocatedState, pl.draManager.DeviceClasses(), slices, pl.celCache)\n   674\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   675\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n   676\u2192\t\t}\n   677\u2192\t\ts.allocator = allocator\n   678\u2192\t\ts.nodeAllocations = make(map[string]nodeAllocation)\n   679\u2192\t}\n   680\u2192\ts.claims = claims\n   681\u2192\treturn nil, nil\n   682\u2192}\n   683\u2192\n   684\u2192func allocatorFeatures(fts feature.Features) structured.Features {\n   685\u2192\treturn structured.Features{\n   686\u2192\t\tAdminAccess:            fts.EnableDRAAdminAccess,\n   687\u2192\t\tPrioritizedList:        fts.EnableDRAPrioritizedList,\n   688\u2192\t\tPartitionableDevices:   fts.EnableDRAPartitionableDevices,\n   689\u2192\t\tDeviceTaints:           fts.EnableDRADeviceTaints,\n   690\u2192\t\tDeviceBindingAndStatus: fts.EnableDRADeviceBindingConditions && fts.EnableDRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus,\n   691\u2192\t\tConsumableCapacity:     fts.EnableDRAConsumableCapacity,\n   692\u2192\t}\n   693\u2192}\n   694\u2192\n   695\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) validateDeviceClass(logger klog.Logger, deviceClassName, requestName string) *fwk.Status {\n   696\u2192\tif deviceClassName == \"\" {\n   697\u2192\t\treturn statusError(logger, fmt.Errorf(\"request %s: unsupported request type\", requestName))\n   698\u2192\t}\n   699\u2192\n   700\u2192\t_, err := pl.draManager.DeviceClasses().Get(deviceClassName)\n   701\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   702\u2192\t\t// If the class cannot be retrieved, allocation cannot proceed.\n   703\u2192\t\tif apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\n   704\u2192\t\t\t// Here we mark the pod as \"unschedulable\", so it'll sleep in\n   705\u2192\t\t\t// the unscheduleable queue until a DeviceClass event occurs.\n   706\u2192\t\t\treturn statusUnschedulable(logger, fmt.Sprintf(\"request %s: device class %s does not exist\", requestName, deviceClassName))\n   707\u2192\t\t}\n   708\u2192\t}\n   709\u2192\treturn nil\n   710\u2192}\n   711\u2192\n   712\u2192// PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\n   713\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) PreFilterExtensions() fwk.PreFilterExtensions {\n   714\u2192\treturn nil\n   715\u2192}\n   716\u2192\n   717\u2192func getStateData(cs fwk.CycleState) (*stateData, error) {\n   718\u2192\tstate, err := cs.Read(stateKey)\n   719\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   720\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   721\u2192\t}\n   722\u2192\ts, ok := state.(*stateData)\n   723\u2192\tif !ok {\n   724\u2192\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unable to convert state into stateData\")\n   725\u2192\t}\n   726\u2192\treturn s, nil\n   727\u2192}\n   728\u2192\n   729\u2192// filterExtendedResources computes the special claim's Requests based on the\n   730\u2192// node's Allocatable. It returns the special claim updated to match what needs\n   731\u2192// to be allocated through DRA for the node or nil if nothing needs to be allocated.\n   732\u2192//\n   733\u2192// It returns an error when the pod's extended resource requests cannot be allocated\n   734\u2192// from node's Allocatable, nor matching any device class's explicit or implicit\n   735\u2192// ExtendedResourceName.\n   736\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) filterExtendedResources(state *stateData, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo fwk.NodeInfo, logger klog.Logger) (*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, *fwk.Status) {\n   737\u2192\textendedResourceClaim := state.claims.extendedResourceClaim()\n   738\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim == nil {\n   739\u2192\t\t// Nothing to do.\n   740\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   741\u2192\t}\n   742\u2192\n   743\u2192\t// The claim is from the prior scheduling cycle, return unschedulable such that it can be\n   744\u2192\t// deleted at the PostFilter phase, and retry anew.\n   745\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim.Spec.Devices.Requests != nil {\n   746\u2192\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, \"cannot schedule extended resource claim\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(nodeInfo.Node()), \"claim\", klog.KObj(extendedResourceClaim))\n   747\u2192\t}\n   748\u2192\n   749\u2192\textendedResources := make(map[v1.ResourceName]int64)\n   750\u2192\thasExtendedResource := false\n   751\u2192\tfor rName, rQuant := range state.draExtendedResource.podScalarResources {\n   752\u2192\t\tif !schedutil.IsDRAExtendedResourceName(rName) {\n   753\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   754\u2192\t\t}\n   755\u2192\t\t// Skip in case request quantity is zero\n   756\u2192\t\tif rQuant == 0 {\n   757\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   758\u2192\t\t}\n   759\u2192\n   760\u2192\t\tallocatable, okScalar := nodeInfo.GetAllocatable().GetScalarResources()[rName]\n   761\u2192\t\t_, okDynamic := state.draExtendedResource.resourceToDeviceClass[rName]\n   762\u2192\t\tif okDynamic {\n   763\u2192\t\t\tif allocatable > 0 {\n   764\u2192\t\t\t\t// node provides the resource via device plugin\n   765\u2192\t\t\t\textendedResources[rName] = 0\n   766\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   767\u2192\t\t\t\t// node needs to provide the resource via DRA\n   768\u2192\t\t\t\textendedResources[rName] = rQuant\n   769\u2192\t\t\t\thasExtendedResource = true\n   770\u2192\t\t\t}\n   771\u2192\t\t} else if !okScalar {\n   772\u2192\t\t\t// has request neither provided by device plugin, nor backed by DRA,\n   773\u2192\t\t\t// hence the pod does not fit the node.\n   774\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, statusUnschedulable(logger, \"cannot fit resource\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(nodeInfo.Node()), \"resource\", rName)\n   775\u2192\t\t}\n   776\u2192\t}\n   777\u2192\n   778\u2192\t// No extended resources backed by DRA on this node.\n   779\u2192\t// The pod may have extended resources, but they are all backed by device\n   780\u2192\t// plugin, hence the noderesources plugin should have checked if the node\n   781\u2192\t// can fit the pod.\n   782\u2192\t// This dynamic resources plugin Filter phase has nothing left to do.\n   783\u2192\tif state.claims.noUserClaim() && !hasExtendedResource {\n   784\u2192\t\t// It cannot be allocated when reaching here, as the claim from prior scheduling cycle\n   785\u2192\t\t// would return unschedulable earlier in this function.\n   786\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   787\u2192\t}\n   788\u2192\n   789\u2192\t// Each node needs its own, potentially different variant of the claim.\n   790\u2192\tnodeExtendedResourceClaim := extendedResourceClaim.DeepCopy()\n   791\u2192\tnodeExtendedResourceClaim.Spec.Devices.Requests = createDeviceRequests(pod, extendedResources, state.draExtendedResource.resourceToDeviceClass)\n   792\u2192\n   793\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim.Status.Allocation != nil {\n   794\u2192\t\t// If it is already allocated, then we cannot simply allocate it again.\n   795\u2192\t\t//\n   796\u2192\t\t// It cannot be allocated when reaching here, as the claim found from prior scheduling cycle\n   797\u2192\t\t// would return unschedulable earlier in this function.\n   798\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   799\u2192\t}\n   800\u2192\n   801\u2192\treturn nodeExtendedResourceClaim, nil\n   802\u2192}\n   803\u2192\n   804\u2192// createDeviceRequests computes the special claim's Requests based on the pod's extended resources\n   805\u2192// that are not satisfied by the node's Allocatable.\n   806\u2192//\n   807\u2192// the device request name has the format: container-%d-request-%d,\n   808\u2192// the first %d is the container's index in the pod's initContainer and containers\n   809\u2192// the second %d is the extended resource's index in that container's sorted resource requests.\n   810\u2192func createDeviceRequests(pod *v1.Pod, extendedResources map[v1.ResourceName]int64, deviceClassMapping map[v1.ResourceName]string) []resourceapi.DeviceRequest {\n   811\u2192\tvar deviceRequests []resourceapi.DeviceRequest\n   812\u2192\t// Creating the extended resource claim's Requests by\n   813\u2192\t// iterating over the containers, and the resources in the containers,\n   814\u2192\t// and create one request per <container, extended resource>.\n   815\u2192\n   816\u2192\t// pod level resources currently have only cpu and memory, they are not considered here for now.\n   817\u2192\t// if extended resources are added to pod level resources in the future, they need to be\n   818\u2192\t// supported separately.\n   819\u2192\tcontainers := slices.Clone(pod.Spec.InitContainers)\n   820\u2192\tcontainers = append(containers, pod.Spec.Containers...)\n   821\u2192\tfor r := range extendedResources {\n   822\u2192\t\tfor i, c := range containers {\n   823\u2192\t\t\tcreqs := c.Resources.Requests\n   824\u2192\t\t\tif creqs == nil {\n   825\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   826\u2192\t\t\t}\n   827\u2192\t\t\tvar rQuant resource.Quantity\n   828\u2192\t\t\tvar ok bool\n   829\u2192\t\t\tif rQuant, ok = creqs[r]; !ok {\n   830\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   831\u2192\t\t\t}\n   832\u2192\t\t\tcrq, ok := (&rQuant).AsInt64()\n   833\u2192\t\t\tif !ok || crq == 0 {\n   834\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   835\u2192\t\t\t}\n   836\u2192\t\t\tclassName, ok := deviceClassMapping[r]\n   837\u2192\t\t\t// skip if the request does not map to a device class\n   838\u2192\t\t\tif !ok || className == \"\" {\n   839\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   840\u2192\t\t\t}\n   841\u2192\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(creqs))\n   842\u2192\t\t\tfor k := range creqs {\n   843\u2192\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k.String())\n   844\u2192\t\t\t}\n   845\u2192\t\t\t// resource requests in a container is a map, their names must\n   846\u2192\t\t\t// be sorted to determine the resource's index order.\n   847\u2192\t\t\tslice.SortStrings(keys)\n   848\u2192\t\t\tridx := 0\n   849\u2192\t\t\tfor j := range keys {\n   850\u2192\t\t\t\tif keys[j] == r.String() {\n   851\u2192\t\t\t\t\tridx = j\n   852\u2192\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n   853\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   854\u2192\t\t\t}\n   855\u2192\t\t\t// i is the index of the container if the list of initContainers + containers.\n   856\u2192\t\t\t// ridx is the index of the extended resource request in the sorted all requests in the container.\n   857\u2192\t\t\t// crq is the quantity of the extended resource request.\n   858\u2192\t\t\tdeviceRequests = append(deviceRequests,\n   859\u2192\t\t\t\tresourceapi.DeviceRequest{\n   860\u2192\t\t\t\t\tName: fmt.Sprintf(\"container-%d-request-%d\", i, ridx), // need to be container name index - extended resource name index\n   861\u2192\t\t\t\t\tExactly: &resourceapi.ExactDeviceRequest{\n   862\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tDeviceClassName: className, // map external resource name -> device class name\n   863\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n   864\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tCount:           crq,\n   865\u2192\t\t\t\t\t},\n   866\u2192\t\t\t\t})\n   867\u2192\t\t}\n   868\u2192\t}\n   869\u2192\tsort.Slice(deviceRequests, func(i, j int) bool {\n   870\u2192\t\treturn deviceRequests[i].Name < deviceRequests[j].Name\n   871\u2192\t})\n   872\u2192\treturn deviceRequests\n   873\u2192}\n   874\u2192\n   875\u2192// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n   876\u2192// It evaluates if a pod can fit due to the resources it requests,\n   877\u2192// for both allocated and unallocated claims.\n   878\u2192//\n   879\u2192// For claims that are bound, then it checks that the node affinity is\n   880\u2192// satisfied by the given node.\n   881\u2192//\n   882\u2192// For claims that are unbound, it checks whether the claim might get allocated\n   883\u2192// for the node.\n   884\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) Filter(ctx context.Context, cs fwk.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo fwk.NodeInfo) *fwk.Status {\n   885\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n   886\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   887\u2192\t}\n   888\u2192\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n   889\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   890\u2192\t\treturn statusError(klog.FromContext(ctx), err)\n   891\u2192\t}\n   892\u2192\tif state.claims.empty() {\n   893\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   894\u2192\t}\n   895\u2192\n   896\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n   897\u2192\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n   898\u2192\tnodeExtendedResourceClaim, status := pl.filterExtendedResources(state, pod, nodeInfo, logger)\n   899\u2192\tif status != nil {\n   900\u2192\t\treturn status\n   901\u2192\t}\n   902\u2192\t// The pod has no user claim, it may have extended resource claim that is satisfied by device plugin.\n   903\u2192\t// Then there is nothing left to do for this plugin.\n   904\u2192\tif nodeExtendedResourceClaim == nil && state.claims.noUserClaim() {\n   905\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   906\u2192\t}\n   907\u2192\n   908\u2192\tvar unavailableClaims []int\n   909\u2192\tfor index, claim := range state.claims.all() {\n   910\u2192\t\tlogger.V(10).Info(\"filtering based on resource claims of the pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(node), \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   911\u2192\n   912\u2192\t\t// This node selector only gets set if the claim is allocated.\n   913\u2192\t\tif nodeSelector := state.informationsForClaim[index].availableOnNodes; nodeSelector != nil && !nodeSelector.Match(node) {\n   914\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"allocation's node selector does not match\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(node), \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   915\u2192\t\t\tunavailableClaims = append(unavailableClaims, index)\n   916\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   917\u2192\t\t}\n   918\u2192\n   919\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n   920\u2192\t\t\t// The claim is not allocated yet, don't have to check\n   921\u2192\t\t\t// anything else.\n   922\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   923\u2192\t\t}\n   924\u2192\n   925\u2192\t\t// The claim is allocated, check whether it is ready for binding.\n   926\u2192\t\tif pl.fts.EnableDRADeviceBindingConditions && pl.fts.EnableDRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus {\n   927\u2192\t\t\tready, err := pl.isClaimReadyForBinding(claim)\n   928\u2192\t\t\t// If the claim is not ready yet (ready false, no error) and binding has timed out\n   929\u2192\t\t\t// or binding has failed (err non-nil), then the scheduler should consider deallocating this\n   930\u2192\t\t\t// claim in PostFilter to unblock trying other devices.\n   931\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil || !ready && pl.isClaimTimeout(claim) {\n   932\u2192\t\t\t\tunavailableClaims = append(unavailableClaims, index)\n   933\u2192\t\t\t}\n   934\u2192\t\t}\n   935\u2192\t}\n   936\u2192\n   937\u2192\t// Use allocator to check the node and cache the result in case that the node is picked.\n   938\u2192\tvar allocations []resourceapi.AllocationResult\n   939\u2192\tif state.allocator != nil {\n   940\u2192\t\tallocCtx := ctx\n   941\u2192\t\tif loggerV := logger.V(5); loggerV.Enabled() {\n   942\u2192\t\t\tallocCtx = klog.NewContext(allocCtx, klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \"node\", klog.KObj(node)))\n   943\u2192\t\t}\n   944\u2192\n   945\u2192\t\t// Apply timeout to the operation?\n   946\u2192\t\tif pl.fts.EnableDRASchedulerFilterTimeout && pl.filterTimeout > 0 {\n   947\u2192\t\t\tc, cancel := context.WithTimeout(allocCtx, pl.filterTimeout)\n   948\u2192\t\t\tdefer cancel()\n   949\u2192\t\t\tallocCtx = c\n   950\u2192\t\t}\n   951\u2192\n   952\u2192\t\t// Check which claims need to be allocated.\n   953\u2192\t\t//\n   954\u2192\t\t// This replaces the special ResourceClaim for extended resources with one\n   955\u2192\t\t// matching the node.\n   956\u2192\t\tclaimsToAllocate := make([]*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, 0, state.claims.len())\n   957\u2192\t\textendedResourceClaim := state.claims.extendedResourceClaim()\n   958\u2192\t\tfor _, claim := range state.claims.toAllocate() {\n   959\u2192\t\t\tif claim == extendedResourceClaim && nodeExtendedResourceClaim != nil {\n   960\u2192\t\t\t\tclaim = nodeExtendedResourceClaim\n   961\u2192\t\t\t}\n   962\u2192\t\t\tclaimsToAllocate = append(claimsToAllocate, claim)\n   963\u2192\t\t}\n   964\u2192\t\ta, err := state.allocator.Allocate(allocCtx, node, claimsToAllocate)\n   965\u2192\t\tswitch {\n   966\u2192\t\tcase errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded):\n   967\u2192\t\t\treturn statusUnschedulable(logger, \"timed out trying to allocate devices\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(node), \"resourceclaims\", klog.KObjSlice(claimsToAllocate))\n   968\u2192\t\tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\n   969\u2192\t\t\treturn statusUnschedulable(logger, fmt.Sprintf(\"asked by caller to stop allocating devices: %v\", context.Cause(ctx)), \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(node), \"resourceclaims\", klog.KObjSlice(claimsToAllocate))\n   970\u2192\t\tcase err != nil:\n   971\u2192\t\t\t// This should only fail if there is something wrong with the claim or class.\n   972\u2192\t\t\t// Return an error to abort scheduling of it.\n   973\u2192\t\t\t//\n   974\u2192\t\t\t// This will cause retries. It would be slightly nicer to mark it as unschedulable\n   975\u2192\t\t\t// *and* abort scheduling. Then only cluster event for updating the claim or class\n   976\u2192\t\t\t// with the broken CEL expression would trigger rescheduling.\n   977\u2192\t\t\t//\n   978\u2192\t\t\t// But we cannot do both. As this shouldn't occur often, aborting like this is\n   979\u2192\t\t\t// better than the more complicated alternative (return Unschedulable here, remember\n   980\u2192\t\t\t// the error, then later raise it again later if needed).\n   981\u2192\t\t\treturn statusError(logger, err, \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(node), \"resourceclaims\", klog.KObjSlice(claimsToAllocate))\n   982\u2192\t\t}\n   983\u2192\t\t// Check for exact length just to be sure. In practice this is all-or-nothing.\n   984\u2192\t\tif len(a) != len(claimsToAllocate) {\n   985\u2192\t\t\treturn statusUnschedulable(logger, \"cannot allocate all claims\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(node), \"resourceclaims\", klog.KObjSlice(claimsToAllocate))\n   986\u2192\t\t}\n   987\u2192\t\t// Reserve uses this information.\n   988\u2192\t\tallocations = a\n   989\u2192\t}\n   990\u2192\n   991\u2192\t// Store information in state while holding the mutex.\n   992\u2192\tif state.allocator != nil || len(unavailableClaims) > 0 {\n   993\u2192\t\tstate.mutex.Lock()\n   994\u2192\t\tdefer state.mutex.Unlock()\n   995\u2192\t}\n   996\u2192\n   997\u2192\tif len(unavailableClaims) > 0 {\n   998\u2192\t\t// Remember all unavailable claims. This might be observed\n   999\u2192\t\t// concurrently, so we have to lock the state before writing.\n  1000\u2192\n  1001\u2192\t\tif state.unavailableClaims == nil {\n  1002\u2192\t\t\tstate.unavailableClaims = sets.New[int]()\n  1003\u2192\t\t}\n  1004\u2192\n  1005\u2192\t\tfor _, index := range unavailableClaims {\n  1006\u2192\t\t\tstate.unavailableClaims.Insert(index)\n  1007\u2192\t\t}\n  1008\u2192\t\treturn statusUnschedulable(logger, \"resourceclaim not available on the node\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n  1009\u2192\t}\n  1010\u2192\n  1011\u2192\tif state.allocator != nil {\n  1012\u2192\t\tstate.nodeAllocations[node.Name] = nodeAllocation{\n  1013\u2192\t\t\tallocationResults:     allocations,\n  1014\u2192\t\t\textendedResourceClaim: nodeExtendedResourceClaim,\n  1015\u2192\t\t}\n  1016\u2192\t}\n  1017\u2192\n  1018\u2192\treturn nil\n  1019\u2192}\n  1020\u2192\n  1021\u2192// isSpecialClaimName return true when the name is the specialClaimInMemName.\n  1022\u2192func isSpecialClaimName(name string) bool {\n  1023\u2192\treturn name == specialClaimInMemName\n  1024\u2192}\n  1025\u2192\n  1026\u2192// PostFilter checks whether there are allocated claims that could get\n  1027\u2192// deallocated to help get the Pod schedulable. If yes, it picks one and\n  1028\u2192// requests its deallocation.  This only gets called when filtering found no\n  1029\u2192// suitable node.\n  1030\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) PostFilter(ctx context.Context, cs fwk.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap fwk.NodeToStatusReader) (*fwk.PostFilterResult, *fwk.Status) {\n  1031\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n  1032\u2192\t\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Unschedulable, \"plugin disabled\")\n  1033\u2192\t}\n  1034\u2192\n  1035\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n  1036\u2192\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n  1037\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1038\u2192\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n  1039\u2192\t}\n  1040\u2192\t// If a Pod doesn't have any resource claims attached to it, there is no need for further processing.\n  1041\u2192\t// Thus we provide a fast path for this case to avoid unnecessary computations.\n  1042\u2192\tif state.claims.empty() {\n  1043\u2192\t\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Unschedulable, \"no new claims to deallocate\")\n  1044\u2192\t}\n  1045\u2192\textendedResourceClaim := state.claims.extendedResourceClaim()\n  1046\u2192\n  1047\u2192\t// Iterating over a map is random. This is intentional here, we want to\n  1048\u2192\t// pick one claim randomly because there is no better heuristic.\n  1049\u2192\tfor index := range state.unavailableClaims {\n  1050\u2192\t\tclaim := state.claims.get(index)\n  1051\u2192\t\tif claim == extendedResourceClaim {\n  1052\u2192\t\t\tif extendedResourceClaim != nil && !isSpecialClaimName(extendedResourceClaim.Name) {\n  1053\u2192\t\t\t\t// Handled below.\n  1054\u2192\t\t\t\tbreak\n  1055\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1056\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n  1057\u2192\t\t}\n  1058\u2192\n  1059\u2192\t\tif len(claim.Status.ReservedFor) == 0 ||\n  1060\u2192\t\t\tlen(claim.Status.ReservedFor) == 1 && claim.Status.ReservedFor[0].UID == pod.UID {\n  1061\u2192\t\t\tclaim := claim.DeepCopy()\n  1062\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Status.ReservedFor = nil\n  1063\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Status.Allocation = nil\n  1064\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Status.Devices = nil\n  1065\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Deallocation of ResourceClaim\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n  1066\u2192\t\t\tif _, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).UpdateStatus(ctx, claim, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {\n  1067\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n  1068\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1069\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Unschedulable, \"deallocation of ResourceClaim completed\")\n  1070\u2192\t\t}\n  1071\u2192\t}\n  1072\u2192\n  1073\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim != nil && !isSpecialClaimName(extendedResourceClaim.Name) {\n  1074\u2192\t\t// If the special resource claim for extended resource backed by DRA\n  1075\u2192\t\t// is reserved or allocated at prior scheduling cycle, then it should be deleted.\n  1076\u2192\t\textendedResourceClaim := extendedResourceClaim.DeepCopy()\n  1077\u2192\t\tif err := pl.deleteClaim(ctx, extendedResourceClaim, logger); err != nil {\n  1078\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, statusError(logger, err)\n  1079\u2192\t\t}\n  1080\u2192\t\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Unschedulable, \"deletion of ResourceClaim completed\")\n  1081\u2192\t}\n  1082\u2192\treturn nil, fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Unschedulable, \"still not schedulable\")\n  1083\u2192}\n  1084\u2192\n  1085\u2192// Reserve reserves claims for the pod.\n  1086\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) Reserve(ctx context.Context, cs fwk.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (status *fwk.Status) {\n  1087\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n  1088\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1089\u2192\t}\n  1090\u2192\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n  1091\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1092\u2192\t\treturn statusError(klog.FromContext(ctx), err)\n  1093\u2192\t}\n  1094\u2192\tif state.claims.empty() {\n  1095\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1096\u2192\t}\n  1097\u2192\n  1098\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n  1099\u2192\n  1100\u2192\tnumClaimsWithAllocator := 0\n  1101\u2192\tfor _, claim := range state.claims.all() {\n  1102\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil {\n  1103\u2192\t\t\t// Allocated, but perhaps not reserved yet. We checked in PreFilter that\n  1104\u2192\t\t\t// the pod could reserve the claim. Instead of reserving here by\n  1105\u2192\t\t\t// updating the ResourceClaim status, we assume that reserving\n  1106\u2192\t\t\t// will work and only do it for real during binding. If it fails at\n  1107\u2192\t\t\t// that time, some other pod was faster and we have to try again.\n  1108\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n  1109\u2192\t\t}\n  1110\u2192\n  1111\u2192\t\tnumClaimsWithAllocator++\n  1112\u2192\t}\n  1113\u2192\n  1114\u2192\tif numClaimsWithAllocator == 0 {\n  1115\u2192\t\t// Nothing left to do.\n  1116\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1117\u2192\t}\n  1118\u2192\textendedResourceClaim := state.claims.extendedResourceClaim()\n  1119\u2192\tnumClaimsToAllocate := 0\n  1120\u2192\tneedToAllocateUserClaims := false\n  1121\u2192\tfor _, claim := range state.claims.toAllocate() {\n  1122\u2192\t\tnumClaimsToAllocate++\n  1123\u2192\t\tif claim != extendedResourceClaim {\n  1124\u2192\t\t\tneedToAllocateUserClaims = true\n  1125\u2192\t\t}\n  1126\u2192\t}\n  1127\u2192\n  1128\u2192\t// Prepare allocation of claims handled by the schedulder.\n  1129\u2192\tif state.allocator != nil {\n  1130\u2192\t\t// Entries in these two slices match each other.\n  1131\u2192\t\tallocations, ok := state.nodeAllocations[nodeName]\n  1132\u2192\t\tif !ok || len(allocations.allocationResults) == 0 {\n  1133\u2192\t\t\t// This can happen only when claimsToAllocate has a single special claim template for extended resource backed by DRA,\n  1134\u2192\t\t\t// But it is satisfied by the node with device plugin, hence no DRA allocation.\n  1135\u2192\t\t\tif !needToAllocateUserClaims {\n  1136\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil\n  1137\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1138\u2192\n  1139\u2192\t\t\t// We checked before that the node is suitable. This shouldn't have failed,\n  1140\u2192\t\t\t// so treat this as an error.\n  1141\u2192\t\t\treturn statusError(logger, errors.New(\"claim allocation not found for node\"))\n  1142\u2192\t\t}\n  1143\u2192\n  1144\u2192\t\t// Sanity check: do we have results for all pending claims?\n  1145\u2192\t\tif len(allocations.allocationResults) != numClaimsToAllocate ||\n  1146\u2192\t\t\tlen(allocations.allocationResults) != numClaimsWithAllocator {\n  1147\u2192\t\t\treturn statusError(logger, fmt.Errorf(\"internal error, have %d allocations, %d claims to allocate, want %d claims\", len(allocations.allocationResults), numClaimsToAllocate, numClaimsWithAllocator))\n  1148\u2192\t\t}\n  1149\u2192\n  1150\u2192\t\tallocIndex := 0\n  1151\u2192\t\tfor index, claim := range state.claims.toAllocate() {\n  1152\u2192\t\t\t// The index returned is the original index in the underlying claim store, it\n  1153\u2192\t\t\t// may not be sequentially numbered (e.g. 0, 1, 2 ...).\n  1154\u2192\t\t\tallocation := &allocations.allocationResults[allocIndex]\n  1155\u2192\t\t\tstate.informationsForClaim[index].allocation = allocation\n  1156\u2192\n  1157\u2192\t\t\tif claim == extendedResourceClaim {\n  1158\u2192\t\t\t\t// replace the special claim template for extended\n  1159\u2192\t\t\t\t// resource backed by DRA with the real instantiated claim.\n  1160\u2192\t\t\t\tclaim = allocations.extendedResourceClaim\n  1161\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1162\u2192\n  1163\u2192\t\t\t// Strictly speaking, we don't need to store the full modified object.\n  1164\u2192\t\t\t// The allocation would be enough. The full object is useful for\n  1165\u2192\t\t\t// debugging, testing and the allocator, so let's make it realistic.\n  1166\u2192\t\t\tclaim = claim.DeepCopy()\n  1167\u2192\t\t\tif !slices.Contains(claim.Finalizers, resourceapi.Finalizer) {\n  1168\u2192\t\t\t\tclaim.Finalizers = append(claim.Finalizers, resourceapi.Finalizer)\n  1169\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1170\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Status.Allocation = allocation\n  1171\u2192\t\t\terr := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().SignalClaimPendingAllocation(claim.UID, claim)\n  1172\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n  1173\u2192\t\t\t\treturn statusError(logger, fmt.Errorf(\"internal error, couldn't signal allocation for claim %s\", claim.Name))\n  1174\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1175\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Reserved resource in allocation result\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"allocation\", klog.Format(allocation))\n  1176\u2192\t\t\tallocIndex++\n  1177\u2192\t\t}\n  1178\u2192\t}\n  1179\u2192\n  1180\u2192\treturn nil\n  1181\u2192}\n  1182\u2192\n  1183\u2192// Unreserve clears the ReservedFor field for all claims.\n  1184\u2192// It's idempotent, and does nothing if no state found for the given pod.\n  1185\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) Unreserve(ctx context.Context, cs fwk.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) {\n  1186\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n  1187\u2192\t\treturn\n  1188\u2192\t}\n  1189\u2192\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n  1190\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1191\u2192\t\treturn\n  1192\u2192\t}\n  1193\u2192\tif state.claims.empty() {\n  1194\u2192\t\treturn\n  1195\u2192\t}\n  1196\u2192\n  1197\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n  1198\u2192\n  1199\u2192\t// we process user claims here first, extendedResourceClaim if any is handled below.\n  1200\u2192\tfor _, claim := range state.claims.allUserClaims() {\n  1201\u2192\t\t// If allocation was in-flight, then it's not anymore and we need to revert the\n  1202\u2192\t\t// claim object in the assume cache to what it was before.\n  1203\u2192\t\tif deleted := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().RemoveClaimPendingAllocation(claim.UID); deleted {\n  1204\u2192\t\t\tpl.draManager.ResourceClaims().AssumedClaimRestore(claim.Namespace, claim.Name)\n  1205\u2192\t\t}\n  1206\u2192\n  1207\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil &&\n  1208\u2192\t\t\tresourceclaim.IsReservedForPod(pod, claim) {\n  1209\u2192\t\t\t// Remove pod from ReservedFor. A strategic-merge-patch is used\n  1210\u2192\t\t\t// because that allows removing an individual entry without having\n  1211\u2192\t\t\t// the latest ResourceClaim.\n  1212\u2192\t\t\tpatch := fmt.Sprintf(`{\"metadata\": {\"uid\": %q}, \"status\": { \"reservedFor\": [ {\"$patch\": \"delete\", \"uid\": %q} ] }}`,\n  1213\u2192\t\t\t\tclaim.UID,\n  1214\u2192\t\t\t\tpod.UID,\n  1215\u2192\t\t\t)\n  1216\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"unreserve\", \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n  1217\u2192\t\t\tclaim, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Patch(ctx, claim.Name, types.StrategicMergePatchType, []byte(patch), metav1.PatchOptions{}, \"status\")\n  1218\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n  1219\u2192\t\t\t\t// We will get here again when pod scheduling is retried.\n  1220\u2192\t\t\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"unreserve\", \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n  1221\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1222\u2192\t\t}\n  1223\u2192\t}\n  1224\u2192\n  1225\u2192\textendedResourceClaim := state.claims.extendedResourceClaim()\n  1226\u2192\tif extendedResourceClaim == nil {\n  1227\u2192\t\t// there is no extended resource claim\n  1228\u2192\t\treturn\n  1229\u2192\t}\n  1230\u2192\n  1231\u2192\tif deleted := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().RemoveClaimPendingAllocation(state.claims.getInitialExtendedResourceClaimUID()); deleted {\n  1232\u2192\t\tpl.draManager.ResourceClaims().AssumedClaimRestore(extendedResourceClaim.Namespace, extendedResourceClaim.Name)\n  1233\u2192\t}\n  1234\u2192\tif isSpecialClaimName(extendedResourceClaim.Name) {\n  1235\u2192\t\t// In memory temporary extended resource claim does not need to be deleted\n  1236\u2192\t\treturn\n  1237\u2192\t}\n  1238\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"delete extended resource backed by DRA\", \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(extendedResourceClaim), \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"claim.UID\", extendedResourceClaim.UID)\n  1239\u2192\textendedResourceClaim = extendedResourceClaim.DeepCopy()\n  1240\u2192\tif err := pl.deleteClaim(ctx, extendedResourceClaim, logger); err != nil {\n  1241\u2192\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"delete\", \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(extendedResourceClaim))\n  1242\u2192\t}\n  1243\u2192}\n  1244\u2192\n  1245\u2192// deleteClaim deletes the claim after removing the finalizer from the claim, if there is any.\n  1246\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) deleteClaim(ctx context.Context, claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim, logger klog.Logger) error {\n  1247\u2192\trefreshClaim := false\n  1248\u2192\tretryErr := retry.RetryOnConflict(retry.DefaultRetry, func() error {\n  1249\u2192\t\tif refreshClaim {\n  1250\u2192\t\t\tupdatedClaim, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Get(ctx, claim.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})\n  1251\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n  1252\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"get resourceclaim %s/%s: %w\", claim.Namespace, claim.Name, err)\n  1253\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1254\u2192\t\t\tclaim = updatedClaim\n  1255\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1256\u2192\t\t\trefreshClaim = true\n  1257\u2192\t\t}\n  1258\u2192\t\t// Remove the finalizer to unblock removal first.\n  1259\u2192\t\tbuiltinControllerFinalizer := slices.Index(claim.Finalizers, resourceapi.Finalizer)\n  1260\u2192\t\tif builtinControllerFinalizer >= 0 {\n  1261\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Finalizers = slices.Delete(claim.Finalizers, builtinControllerFinalizer, builtinControllerFinalizer+1)\n  1262\u2192\t\t}\n  1263\u2192\n  1264\u2192\t\t_, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Update(ctx, claim, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\n  1265\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n  1266\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"update resourceclaim %s/%s: %w\", claim.Namespace, claim.Name, err)\n  1267\u2192\t\t}\n  1268\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1269\u2192\t})\n  1270\u2192\tif retryErr != nil {\n  1271\u2192\t\treturn retryErr\n  1272\u2192\t}\n  1273\u2192\n  1274\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Delete\", \"resourceclaim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n  1275\u2192\terr := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Delete(ctx, claim.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n  1276\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1277\u2192\t\treturn err\n  1278\u2192\t}\n  1279\u2192\treturn nil\n  1280\u2192}\n  1281\u2192\n  1282\u2192// PreBind gets called in a separate goroutine after it has been determined\n  1283\u2192// that the pod should get bound to this node. Because Reserve did not actually\n  1284\u2192// reserve claims, we need to do it now. For claims with the builtin controller,\n  1285\u2192// we also handle the allocation.\n  1286\u2192//\n  1287\u2192// If anything fails, we return an error and\n  1288\u2192// the pod will have to go into the backoff queue. The scheduler will call\n  1289\u2192// Unreserve as part of the error handling.\n  1290\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) PreBind(ctx context.Context, cs fwk.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) *fwk.Status {\n  1291\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n  1292\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1293\u2192\t}\n  1294\u2192\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n  1295\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1296\u2192\t\treturn statusError(klog.FromContext(ctx), err)\n  1297\u2192\t}\n  1298\u2192\tif state.claims.empty() {\n  1299\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1300\u2192\t}\n  1301\u2192\n  1302\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n  1303\u2192\n  1304\u2192\tfor index, claim := range state.claims.all() {\n  1305\u2192\t\tif !resourceclaim.IsReservedForPod(pod, claim) {\n  1306\u2192\t\t\tclaim, err := pl.bindClaim(ctx, state, index, pod, nodeName)\n  1307\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n  1308\u2192\t\t\t\treturn statusError(logger, err)\n  1309\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1310\u2192\t\t\t// Updated here such that Unreserve can work with patched claim.\n  1311\u2192\t\t\tstate.claims.set(index, claim)\n  1312\u2192\t\t}\n  1313\u2192\t}\n  1314\u2192\n  1315\u2192\tif !pl.fts.EnableDRADeviceBindingConditions || !pl.fts.EnableDRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus {\n  1316\u2192\t\t// If we don't have binding conditions, we can return early.\n  1317\u2192\t\t// The claim is now reserved for the pod and the scheduler can proceed with binding.\n  1318\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1319\u2192\t}\n  1320\u2192\n  1321\u2192\t// We need to check if the device is attached to the node.\n  1322\u2192\tneedToWait := hasBindingConditions(state)\n  1323\u2192\n  1324\u2192\t// If no device needs to be prepared, we can return early.\n  1325\u2192\tif !needToWait {\n  1326\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1327\u2192\t}\n  1328\u2192\n  1329\u2192\t// We need to wait for the device to be attached to the node.\n  1330\u2192\tpl.fh.EventRecorder().Eventf(pod, nil, v1.EventTypeNormal, \"BindingConditionsPending\", \"Scheduling\", \"waiting for binding conditions for device on node %s\", nodeName)\n  1331\u2192\terr = wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second, time.Duration(BindingTimeoutDefaultSeconds)*time.Second, true,\n  1332\u2192\t\tfunc(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n  1333\u2192\t\t\treturn pl.isPodReadyForBinding(state)\n  1334\u2192\t\t})\n  1335\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1336\u2192\t\tif errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) {\n  1337\u2192\t\t\terr = errors.New(\"device binding timeout\")\n  1338\u2192\t\t}\n  1339\u2192\t\t// Returning an error here causes another scheduling attempt.\n  1340\u2192\t\t// In that next attempt, PreFilter will detect the timeout or\n  1341\u2192\t\t// error and try to recover.\n  1342\u2192\t\treturn statusError(logger, err)\n  1343\u2192\t}\n  1344\u2192\n  1345\u2192\t// If we get here, we know that reserving the claim for\n  1346\u2192\t// the pod worked and we can proceed with binding it.\n  1347\u2192\treturn nil\n  1348\u2192}\n  1349\u2192\n  1350\u2192// PreBindPreFlight is called before PreBind, and determines whether PreBind is going to do something for this pod, or not.\n  1351\u2192// It just checks state.claims to determine whether there are any claims and hence the plugin has to handle them at PreBind.\n  1352\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) PreBindPreFlight(ctx context.Context, cs fwk.CycleState, p *v1.Pod, nodeName string) *fwk.Status {\n  1353\u2192\tif !pl.enabled {\n  1354\u2192\t\treturn fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Skip)\n  1355\u2192\t}\n  1356\u2192\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n  1357\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1358\u2192\t\treturn statusError(klog.FromContext(ctx), err)\n  1359\u2192\t}\n  1360\u2192\tif state.claims.empty() {\n  1361\u2192\t\treturn fwk.NewStatus(fwk.Skip)\n  1362\u2192\t}\n  1363\u2192\treturn nil\n  1364\u2192}\n  1365\u2192\n  1366\u2192// createRequestMappings creates the requestMappings for the special extended resource claim.\n  1367\u2192// For each device request in the claim, it finds the container name, and\n  1368\u2192// the extended resource name in that container matching the device request.\n  1369\u2192// the device request name has the format: container-%d-request-%d,\n  1370\u2192// the first %d is the container's index in the pod's initContainer and containers\n  1371\u2192// the second %d is the extended resource's index in that container's sorted resource requests.\n  1372\u2192func createRequestMappings(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim, pod *v1.Pod) []v1.ContainerExtendedResourceRequest {\n  1373\u2192\tvar cer []v1.ContainerExtendedResourceRequest\n  1374\u2192\tdeviceReqNames := make([]string, 0, len(claim.Spec.Devices.Requests))\n  1375\u2192\tfor _, r := range claim.Spec.Devices.Requests {\n  1376\u2192\t\tdeviceReqNames = append(deviceReqNames, r.Name)\n  1377\u2192\t}\n  1378\u2192\t// pod level resources currently have only cpu and memory, they are not considered here for now.\n  1379\u2192\t// if extended resources are added to pod level resources in the future, they need to be\n  1380\u2192\t// supported separately.\n  1381\u2192\tcontainers := slices.Clone(pod.Spec.InitContainers)\n  1382\u2192\tcontainers = append(containers, pod.Spec.Containers...)\n  1383\u2192\tfor i, c := range containers {\n  1384\u2192\t\tcreqs := c.Resources.Requests\n  1385\u2192\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(creqs))\n  1386\u2192\t\tfor k := range creqs {\n  1387\u2192\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k.String())\n  1388\u2192\t\t}\n  1389\u2192\t\t// resource requests in a container is a map, their names must\n  1390\u2192\t\t// be sorted to determine the resource's index order.\n  1391\u2192\t\tslice.SortStrings(keys)\n  1392\u2192\t\tfor rName := range creqs {\n  1393\u2192\t\t\tridx := 0\n  1394\u2192\t\t\tfor j := range keys {\n  1395\u2192\t\t\t\tif keys[j] == rName.String() {\n  1396\u2192\t\t\t\t\tridx = j\n  1397\u2192\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n  1398\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1399\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1400\u2192\t\t\tfor _, devReqName := range deviceReqNames {\n  1401\u2192\t\t\t\t// During filter phase, device request name is set to be\n  1402\u2192\t\t\t\t// container name index \"-\" extended resource name index\n  1403\u2192\t\t\t\tif fmt.Sprintf(\"container-%d-request-%d\", i, ridx) == devReqName {\n  1404\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcer = append(cer,\n  1405\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tv1.ContainerExtendedResourceRequest{\n  1406\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tContainerName: c.Name,\n  1407\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tResourceName:  rName.String(),\n  1408\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRequestName:   devReqName,\n  1409\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n  1410\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1411\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1412\u2192\t\t}\n  1413\u2192\t}\n  1414\u2192\treturn cer\n  1415\u2192}\n  1416\u2192\n  1417\u2192// bindClaim gets called by PreBind for claim which is not reserved for the pod yet.\n  1418\u2192// It might not even be allocated. bindClaim then ensures that the allocation\n  1419\u2192// and reservation are recorded. This finishes the work started in Reserve.\n  1420\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) bindClaim(ctx context.Context, state *stateData, index int, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (*resourceapi.ResourceClaim, error) {\n  1421\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n  1422\u2192\tclaim := state.claims.get(index)\n  1423\u2192\tallocation := state.informationsForClaim[index].allocation\n  1424\u2192\tisExtendedResourceClaim := false\n  1425\u2192\tif claim == state.claims.extendedResourceClaim() {\n  1426\u2192\t\t// extended resource requests satisfied by device plugin\n  1427\u2192\t\tif allocation == nil && claim.Spec.Devices.Requests == nil {\n  1428\u2192\t\t\treturn claim, nil\n  1429\u2192\t\t}\n  1430\u2192\t\tisExtendedResourceClaim = true\n  1431\u2192\t}\n  1432\u2192\tclaimUIDs := []types.UID{claim.UID}\n  1433\u2192\tresourceClaimModified := false\n  1434\u2192\tdefer func() {\n  1435\u2192\t\t// The scheduler was handling allocation. Now that has\n  1436\u2192\t\t// completed, either successfully or with a failure.\n  1437\u2192\t\tif resourceClaimModified {\n  1438\u2192\t\t\tif isExtendedResourceClaim {\n  1439\u2192\t\t\t\t// Unlike other claims, extended resource claim is created in API server below.\n  1440\u2192\t\t\t\t// AssumeClaimAfterAPICall returns ErrNotFound when the informer update has not reached assumed cache yet.\n  1441\u2192\t\t\t\t// Hence we must poll and wait for it.\n  1442\u2192\t\t\t\tpollErr := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(ctx, 1*time.Second, time.Duration(AssumeExtendedResourceTimeoutDefaultSeconds)*time.Second, true,\n  1443\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfunc(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n  1444\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tif err := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().AssumeClaimAfterAPICall(claim); err != nil {\n  1445\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, assumecache.ErrNotFound) {\n  1446\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn false, nil\n  1447\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n  1448\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Claim not stored in assume cache\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"err\", err)\n  1449\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn false, err\n  1450\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n  1451\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n  1452\u2192\t\t\t\t\t})\n  1453\u2192\t\t\t\tif pollErr != nil {\n  1454\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Claim not stored in assume cache after retries\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim), \"err\", pollErr)\n  1455\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1456\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n  1457\u2192\t\t\t\t// This can fail, but only for reasons that are okay (concurrent delete or update).\n  1458\u2192\t\t\t\t// Shouldn't happen in this case.\n  1459\u2192\t\t\t\tif err := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().AssumeClaimAfterAPICall(claim); err != nil {\n  1460\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Claim not stored in assume cache\", \"err\", err)\n  1461\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1462\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1463\u2192\t\t}\n  1464\u2192\t\tif allocation != nil {\n  1465\u2192\t\t\tfor _, claimUID := range claimUIDs {\n  1466\u2192\t\t\t\tpl.draManager.ResourceClaims().RemoveClaimPendingAllocation(claimUID)\n  1467\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1468\u2192\t\t}\n  1469\u2192\t}()\n  1470\u2192\n  1471\u2192\t// Create the special claim for extended resource backed by DRA\n  1472\u2192\tif isExtendedResourceClaim && isSpecialClaimName(claim.Name) {\n  1473\u2192\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"preparing to create claim for extended resources\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", nodeName, \"resourceclaim\", klog.Format(claim))\n  1474\u2192\t\t// Replace claim template with instantiated claim for the node.\n  1475\u2192\t\tif nodeAllocation, ok := state.nodeAllocations[nodeName]; ok && nodeAllocation.extendedResourceClaim != nil {\n  1476\u2192\t\t\tclaim = nodeAllocation.extendedResourceClaim.DeepCopy()\n  1477\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1478\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"extended resource claim not found for node %s\", nodeName)\n  1479\u2192\t\t}\n  1480\u2192\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"create claim for extended resources\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", nodeName, \"resourceclaim\", klog.Format(claim))\n  1481\u2192\t\t// Clear fields which must or can not be set during creation.\n  1482\u2192\t\tclaim.Status.Allocation = nil\n  1483\u2192\t\tclaim.Name = \"\"\n  1484\u2192\t\tclaim.UID = \"\"\n  1485\u2192\t\tvar err error\n  1486\u2192\t\tclaim, err = pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Create(ctx, claim, metav1.CreateOptions{})\n  1487\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n  1488\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"create claim for extended resources %v: %w\", klog.KObj(claim), err)\n  1489\u2192\t\t}\n  1490\u2192\t\tresourceClaimModified = true\n  1491\u2192\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"created claim for extended resources\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", nodeName, \"resourceclaim\", klog.Format(claim))\n  1492\u2192\n  1493\u2192\t\t// Track the actual extended ResourceClaim from now.\n  1494\u2192\t\t// Relevant if we need to delete again in Unreserve.\n  1495\u2192\t\tif err := state.claims.updateExtendedResourceClaim(claim); err != nil {\n  1496\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"internal error: update extended ResourceClaim: %w\", err)\n  1497\u2192\t\t}\n  1498\u2192\t}\n  1499\u2192\n  1500\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"preparing claim status update\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(state.claims.get(index)), \"allocation\", klog.Format(allocation))\n  1501\u2192\n  1502\u2192\t// We may run into a ResourceVersion conflict because there may be some\n  1503\u2192\t// benign concurrent changes. In that case we get the latest claim and\n  1504\u2192\t// try again.\n  1505\u2192\trefreshClaim := false\n  1506\u2192\tclaim = claim.DeepCopy()\n  1507\u2192\tretryErr := retry.RetryOnConflict(retry.DefaultRetry, func() error {\n  1508\u2192\t\tif refreshClaim {\n  1509\u2192\t\t\tupdatedClaim, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Get(ctx, claim.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})\n  1510\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n  1511\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"get updated claim %s after conflict: %w\", klog.KObj(claim), err)\n  1512\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1513\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"retrying update after conflict\", \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n  1514\u2192\t\t\tclaim = updatedClaim\n  1515\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1516\u2192\t\t\t// All future retries must get a new claim first.\n  1517\u2192\t\t\trefreshClaim = true\n  1518\u2192\t\t}\n  1519\u2192\n  1520\u2192\t\tif claim.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n  1521\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s got deleted in the meantime\", klog.KObj(claim))\n  1522\u2192\t\t}\n  1523\u2192\n  1524\u2192\t\t// Do we need to store an allocation result from Reserve?\n  1525\u2192\t\tif allocation != nil {\n  1526\u2192\t\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil {\n  1527\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s got allocated elsewhere in the meantime\", klog.KObj(claim))\n  1528\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1529\u2192\n  1530\u2192\t\t\t// The finalizer needs to be added in a normal update.\n  1531\u2192\t\t\t// If we were interrupted in the past, it might already be set and we simply continue.\n  1532\u2192\t\t\tif !slices.Contains(claim.Finalizers, resourceapi.Finalizer) {\n  1533\u2192\t\t\t\tclaim.Finalizers = append(claim.Finalizers, resourceapi.Finalizer)\n  1534\u2192\t\t\t\tupdatedClaim, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).Update(ctx, claim, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\n  1535\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n  1536\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"add finalizer to claim %s: %w\", klog.KObj(claim), err)\n  1537\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1538\u2192\t\t\t\tclaim = updatedClaim\n  1539\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1540\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Status.Allocation = allocation\n  1541\u2192\t\t}\n  1542\u2192\n  1543\u2192\t\t// We can simply try to add the pod here without checking\n  1544\u2192\t\t// preconditions. The apiserver will tell us with a\n  1545\u2192\t\t// non-conflict error if this isn't possible.\n  1546\u2192\t\tclaim.Status.ReservedFor = append(claim.Status.ReservedFor, resourceapi.ResourceClaimConsumerReference{Resource: \"pods\", Name: pod.Name, UID: pod.UID})\n  1547\u2192\t\tif pl.fts.EnableDRADeviceBindingConditions && pl.fts.EnableDRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus && claim.Status.Allocation.AllocationTimestamp == nil {\n  1548\u2192\t\t\tclaim.Status.Allocation.AllocationTimestamp = &metav1.Time{Time: time.Now()}\n  1549\u2192\t\t}\n  1550\u2192\t\tupdatedClaim, err := pl.clientset.ResourceV1().ResourceClaims(claim.Namespace).UpdateStatus(ctx, claim, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\n  1551\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n  1552\u2192\t\t\tif allocation != nil {\n  1553\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"add allocation and reservation to claim %s: %w\", klog.KObj(claim), err)\n  1554\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1555\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"add reservation to claim %s: %w\", klog.KObj(claim), err)\n  1556\u2192\t\t}\n  1557\u2192\t\tclaim = updatedClaim\n  1558\u2192\t\tresourceClaimModified = true\n  1559\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1560\u2192\t})\n  1561\u2192\n  1562\u2192\tif retryErr != nil {\n  1563\u2192\t\treturn nil, retryErr\n  1564\u2192\t}\n  1565\u2192\n  1566\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"reserved\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", nodeName, \"resourceclaim\", klog.Format(claim))\n  1567\u2192\n  1568\u2192\t// Patch the pod status with the new information about the generated\n  1569\u2192\t// special resource claim.\n  1570\u2192\tif isExtendedResourceClaim {\n  1571\u2192\t\tcer := createRequestMappings(claim, pod)\n  1572\u2192\t\tpodStatusCopy := pod.Status.DeepCopy()\n  1573\u2192\t\tpodStatusCopy.ExtendedResourceClaimStatus = &v1.PodExtendedResourceClaimStatus{\n  1574\u2192\t\t\tRequestMappings:   cer,\n  1575\u2192\t\t\tResourceClaimName: claim.Name,\n  1576\u2192\t\t}\n  1577\u2192\t\terr := schedutil.PatchPodStatus(ctx, pl.clientset, pod.Name, pod.Namespace, &pod.Status, podStatusCopy)\n  1578\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n  1579\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"update pod %s/%s ExtendedResourceClaimStatus: %w\", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err)\n  1580\u2192\t\t}\n  1581\u2192\t}\n  1582\u2192\n  1583\u2192\treturn claim, nil\n  1584\u2192}\n  1585\u2192\n  1586\u2192// isClaimReadyForBinding checks whether a given resource claim is\n  1587\u2192// ready for binding.\n  1588\u2192// It returns an error if the claim is not ready for binding.\n  1589\u2192// It returns true if (and only if) all binding conditions are true,\n  1590\u2192// and no binding failure conditions are true,\n  1591\u2192// which includes the case that there are no binding conditions.\n  1592\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) isClaimReadyForBinding(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) (bool, error) {\n  1593\u2192\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n  1594\u2192\t\treturn false, nil\n  1595\u2192\t}\n  1596\u2192\tfor _, deviceRequest := range claim.Status.Allocation.Devices.Results {\n  1597\u2192\t\tif len(deviceRequest.BindingConditions) == 0 {\n  1598\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n  1599\u2192\t\t}\n  1600\u2192\t\tdeviceStatus := getAllocatedDeviceStatus(claim, &deviceRequest)\n  1601\u2192\t\tif deviceStatus == nil {\n  1602\u2192\t\t\treturn false, nil\n  1603\u2192\t\t}\n  1604\u2192\t\tfor _, cond := range deviceRequest.BindingFailureConditions {\n  1605\u2192\t\t\tfailedCond := apimeta.FindStatusCondition(deviceStatus.Conditions, cond)\n  1606\u2192\t\t\tif failedCond != nil && failedCond.Status == metav1.ConditionTrue {\n  1607\u2192\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s binding failed: reason=%s, message=%q\",\n  1608\u2192\t\t\t\t\tclaim.Name,\n  1609\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfailedCond.Reason,\n  1610\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfailedCond.Message)\n  1611\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1612\u2192\t\t}\n  1613\u2192\t\tfor _, cond := range deviceRequest.BindingConditions {\n  1614\u2192\t\t\tif !apimeta.IsStatusConditionTrue(deviceStatus.Conditions, cond) {\n  1615\u2192\t\t\t\treturn false, nil\n  1616\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1617\u2192\t\t}\n  1618\u2192\t}\n  1619\u2192\treturn true, nil\n  1620\u2192}\n  1621\u2192\n  1622\u2192// isClaimTimeout checks whether a given resource claim has\n  1623\u2192// reached the binding timeout.\n  1624\u2192// It returns true if the binding timeout is reached.\n  1625\u2192// It returns false if the binding timeout is not reached.\n  1626\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) isClaimTimeout(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) bool {\n  1627\u2192\tif !pl.fts.EnableDRADeviceBindingConditions || !pl.fts.EnableDRAResourceClaimDeviceStatus {\n  1628\u2192\t\treturn false\n  1629\u2192\t}\n  1630\u2192\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil || claim.Status.Allocation.AllocationTimestamp == nil {\n  1631\u2192\t\treturn false\n  1632\u2192\t}\n  1633\u2192\t// check if the binding timeout is reached\n  1634\u2192\tfor _, deviceRequest := range claim.Status.Allocation.Devices.Results {\n  1635\u2192\t\tif deviceRequest.BindingConditions == nil {\n  1636\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n  1637\u2192\t\t}\n  1638\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation.AllocationTimestamp.Add(time.Duration(BindingTimeoutDefaultSeconds) * time.Second).Before(time.Now()) {\n  1639\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n  1640\u2192\t\t}\n  1641\u2192\t}\n  1642\u2192\treturn false\n  1643\u2192}\n  1644\u2192\n  1645\u2192// isPodReadyForBinding checks the binding status of devices within the given state claims.\n  1646\u2192// It returns true if (and only if) all binding conditions are true,\n  1647\u2192// and no binding failure conditions are true,\n  1648\u2192// which includes the case when there are no binding conditions.\n  1649\u2192// It returns an error if any binding failure condition is set.\n  1650\u2192func (pl *DynamicResources) isPodReadyForBinding(state *stateData) (bool, error) {\n  1651\u2192\tfor claimIndex, claim := range state.claims.all() {\n  1652\u2192\t\tclaim, err := pl.draManager.ResourceClaims().Get(claim.Namespace, claim.Name)\n  1653\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n  1654\u2192\t\t\treturn false, err\n  1655\u2192\t\t}\n  1656\u2192\t\tstate.claims.set(claimIndex, claim)\n  1657\u2192\t\tready, err := pl.isClaimReadyForBinding(claim)\n  1658\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n  1659\u2192\t\t\treturn false, err\n  1660\u2192\t\t}\n  1661\u2192\t\tif !ready {\n  1662\u2192\t\t\tif pl.isClaimTimeout(claim) {\n  1663\u2192\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"claim %s binding timeout\", claim.Name)\n  1664\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1665\u2192\t\t\treturn false, nil\n  1666\u2192\t\t}\n  1667\u2192\t}\n  1668\u2192\treturn true, nil\n  1669\u2192}\n  1670\u2192\n  1671\u2192// hasBindingConditions checks whether any of the claims in the state\n  1672\u2192// has binding conditions.\n  1673\u2192// It returns true if at least one claim has binding conditions.\n  1674\u2192// It returns false if no claim has binding conditions.\n  1675\u2192func hasBindingConditions(state *stateData) bool {\n  1676\u2192\tfor _, claim := range state.claims.all() {\n  1677\u2192\t\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n  1678\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n  1679\u2192\t\t}\n  1680\u2192\t\tfor _, device := range claim.Status.Allocation.Devices.Results {\n  1681\u2192\t\t\tif len(device.BindingConditions) > 0 {\n  1682\u2192\t\t\t\treturn true\n  1683\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1684\u2192\t\t}\n  1685\u2192\t}\n  1686\u2192\treturn false\n  1687\u2192}\n  1688\u2192\n  1689\u2192// statusUnschedulable ensures that there is a log message associated with the\n  1690\u2192// line where the status originated.\n  1691\u2192func statusUnschedulable(logger klog.Logger, reason string, kv ...interface{}) *fwk.Status {\n  1692\u2192\tif loggerV := logger.V(5); loggerV.Enabled() {\n  1693\u2192\t\thelper, loggerV := loggerV.WithCallStackHelper()\n  1694\u2192\t\thelper()\n  1695\u2192\t\tkv = append(kv, \"reason\", reason)\n  1696\u2192\t\t// nolint: logcheck // warns because it cannot check key/values\n  1697\u2192\t\tloggerV.Info(\"pod unschedulable\", kv...)\n  1698\u2192\t}\n  1699\u2192\treturn fwk.NewStatus(fwk.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, reason)\n  1700\u2192}\n  1701\u2192\n  1702\u2192// statusError ensures that there is a log message associated with the\n  1703\u2192// line where the error originated.\n  1704\u2192func statusError(logger klog.Logger, err error, kv ...interface{}) *fwk.Status {\n  1705\u2192\tif loggerV := logger.V(5); loggerV.Enabled() {\n  1706\u2192\t\thelper, loggerV := loggerV.WithCallStackHelper()\n  1707\u2192\t\thelper()\n  1708\u2192\t\t// nolint: logcheck // warns because it cannot check key/values\n  1709\u2192\t\tloggerV.Error(err, \"dynamic resource plugin failed\", kv...)\n  1710\u2192\t}\n  1711\u2192\treturn fwk.AsStatus(err)\n  1712\u2192}\n  1713\u2192\n  1714\u2192func getAllocatedDeviceStatus(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim, deviceRequest *resourceapi.DeviceRequestAllocationResult) *resourceapi.AllocatedDeviceStatus {\n  1715\u2192\tfor _, device := range claim.Status.Devices {\n  1716\u2192\t\tif deviceRequest.Device == device.Device && deviceRequest.Driver == device.Driver && deviceRequest.Pool == device.Pool {\n  1717\u2192\t\t\treturn &device\n  1718\u2192\t\t}\n  1719\u2192\t}\n  1720\u2192\treturn nil\n  1721\u2192}\n  1722\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
46. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2025 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package v1beta2\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    21\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\"\n    22\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n    26\u2192)\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192const (\n    29\u2192\t// Finalizer is the finalizer that gets set for claims\n    30\u2192\t// which were allocated through a builtin controller.\n    31\u2192\t// Reserved for use by Kubernetes, DRA driver controllers must\n    32\u2192\t// use their own finalizer.\n    33\u2192\tFinalizer = \"resource.kubernetes.io/delete-protection\"\n    34\u2192\t// ExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation is the annotation applied on the generated\n    35\u2192\t// special ResourceClaim. Its single valid value is \"true\".\n    36\u2192\t// This is used only inside the scheduler.\n    37\u2192\tExtendedResourceClaimAnnotation = \"resource.kubernetes.io/extended-resource-claim\"\n    38\u2192\t// Resource device class prefix is for generating implicit extended resource\n    39\u2192\t// name for a device class when its ExtendedResourceName field is not\n    40\u2192\t// specified. The generated name is this prefix + the device class name.\n    41\u2192\t// The generated name may not be a valid extended resource name for use\n    42\u2192\t// in pod.Spec.Resources.Requests, in that case, a valid name has to be specified\n    43\u2192\t// explicitly in device class.\n    44\u2192\tResourceDeviceClassPrefix string = \"deviceclass.resource.kubernetes.io/\"\n    45\u2192)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192// +genclient\n    48\u2192// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n    49\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n    50\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources,\n    53\u2192// managed by a common driver. A pool may span more than one ResourceSlice, and exactly how many\n    54\u2192// ResourceSlices comprise a pool is determined by the driver.\n    55\u2192//\n    56\u2192// At the moment, the only supported resources are devices with attributes and capacities.\n    57\u2192// Each device in a given pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices, must have a unique name.\n    58\u2192// The ResourceSlice in which a device gets published may change over time. The unique identifier\n    59\u2192// for a device is the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>.\n    60\u2192//\n    61\u2192// Whenever a driver needs to update a pool, it increments the pool.Spec.Pool.Generation number\n    62\u2192// and updates all ResourceSlices with that new number and new resource definitions. A consumer\n    63\u2192// must only use ResourceSlices with the highest generation number and ignore all others.\n    64\u2192//\n    65\u2192// When allocating all resources in a pool matching certain criteria or when\n    66\u2192// looking for the best solution among several different alternatives, a\n    67\u2192// consumer should check the number of ResourceSlices in a pool (included in\n    68\u2192// each ResourceSlice) to determine whether its view of a pool is complete and\n    69\u2192// if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.\n    70\u2192//\n    71\u2192// For resources that are not local to a node, the node name is not set. Instead,\n    72\u2192// the driver may use a node selector to specify where the devices are available.\n    73\u2192//\n    74\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n    75\u2192// feature gate.\n    76\u2192type ResourceSlice struct {\n    77\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n    78\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n    79\u2192\t// +optional\n    80\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192\t// Contains the information published by the driver.\n    83\u2192\t//\n    84\u2192\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n    85\u2192\tSpec ResourceSliceSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192const (\n    89\u2192\t// ResourceSliceSelectorNodeName can be used in a [metav1.ListOptions]\n    90\u2192\t// field selector to filter based on [ResourceSliceSpec.NodeName].\n    91\u2192\tResourceSliceSelectorNodeName = \"spec.nodeName\"\n    92\u2192\t// ResourceSliceSelectorDriver can be used in a [metav1.ListOptions]\n    93\u2192\t// field selector to filter based on [ResourceSliceSpec.Driver].\n    94\u2192\tResourceSliceSelectorDriver = \"spec.driver\"\n    95\u2192)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192// ResourceSliceSpec contains the information published by the driver in one ResourceSlice.\n    98\u2192type ResourceSliceSpec struct {\n    99\u2192\t// Driver identifies the DRA driver providing the capacity information.\n   100\u2192\t// A field selector can be used to list only ResourceSlice\n   101\u2192\t// objects with a certain driver name.\n   102\u2192\t//\n   103\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n   104\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n   105\u2192\t// This field is immutable.\n   106\u2192\t//\n   107\u2192\t// +required\n   108\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=driver\"`\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\t// Pool describes the pool that this ResourceSlice belongs to.\n   111\u2192\t//\n   112\u2192\t// +required\n   113\u2192\tPool ResourcePool `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=pool\"`\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192\t// NodeName identifies the node which provides the resources in this pool.\n   116\u2192\t// A field selector can be used to list only ResourceSlice\n   117\u2192\t// objects belonging to a certain node.\n   118\u2192\t//\n   119\u2192\t// This field can be used to limit access from nodes to ResourceSlices with\n   120\u2192\t// the same node name. It also indicates to autoscalers that adding\n   121\u2192\t// new nodes of the same type as some old node might also make new\n   122\u2192\t// resources available.\n   123\u2192\t//\n   124\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   125\u2192\t// This field is immutable.\n   126\u2192\t//\n   127\u2192\t// +optional\n   128\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   129\u2192\tNodeName *string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\t// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,\n   132\u2192\t// when that pool is not limited to a single node.\n   133\u2192\t//\n   134\u2192\t// Must use exactly one term.\n   135\u2192\t//\n   136\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   137\u2192\t//\n   138\u2192\t// +optional\n   139\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   140\u2192\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\t// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the resources in the pool.\n   143\u2192\t//\n   144\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   145\u2192\t//\n   146\u2192\t// +optional\n   147\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   148\u2192\tAllNodes *bool `json:\"allNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=allNodes\"`\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192\t// Devices lists some or all of the devices in this pool.\n   151\u2192\t//\n   152\u2192\t// Must not have more than 128 entries.\n   153\u2192\t//\n   154\u2192\t// +optional\n   155\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   156\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n   157\u2192\tDevices []Device `json:\"devices\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,name=devices\"`\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192\t// PerDeviceNodeSelection defines whether the access from nodes to\n   160\u2192\t// resources in the pool is set on the ResourceSlice level or on each\n   161\u2192\t// device. If it is set to true, every device defined the ResourceSlice\n   162\u2192\t// must specify this individually.\n   163\u2192\t//\n   164\u2192\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n   165\u2192\t//\n   166\u2192\t// +optional\n   167\u2192\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n   168\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   169\u2192\tPerDeviceNodeSelection *bool `json:\"perDeviceNodeSelection,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,name=perDeviceNodeSelection\"`\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\t// SharedCounters defines a list of counter sets, each of which\n   172\u2192\t// has a name and a list of counters available.\n   173\u2192\t//\n   174\u2192\t// The names of the SharedCounters must be unique in the ResourceSlice.\n   175\u2192\t//\n   176\u2192\t// The maximum number of counters in all sets is 32.\n   177\u2192\t//\n   178\u2192\t// +optional\n   179\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n   180\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n   181\u2192\tSharedCounters []CounterSet `json:\"sharedCounters,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,name=sharedCounters\"`\n   182\u2192}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192// CounterSet defines a named set of counters\n   185\u2192// that are available to be used by devices defined in the\n   186\u2192// ResourceSlice.\n   187\u2192//\n   188\u2192// The counters are not allocatable by themselves, but\n   189\u2192// can be referenced by devices. When a device is allocated,\n   190\u2192// the portion of counters it uses will no longer be available for use\n   191\u2192// by other devices.\n   192\u2192type CounterSet struct {\n   193\u2192\t// Name defines the name of the counter set.\n   194\u2192\t// It must be a DNS label.\n   195\u2192\t//\n   196\u2192\t// +required\n   197\u2192\tName string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192\t// Counters defines the set of counters for this CounterSet\n   200\u2192\t// The name of each counter must be unique in that set and must be a DNS label.\n   201\u2192\t//\n   202\u2192\t// The maximum number of counters in all sets is 32.\n   203\u2192\t//\n   204\u2192\t// +required\n   205\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n   206\u2192\t// +k8s:eachKey=+k8s:format=k8s-short-name\n   207\u2192\tCounters map[string]Counter `json:\"counters,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=counters\"`\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192// DriverNameMaxLength is the maximum valid length of a driver name in the\n   211\u2192// ResourceSliceSpec and other places.
