`json:\"consumedCapacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,10,rep,name=consumedCapacity\"`\n  1658\u2192}\n  1659\u2192\n  1660\u2192// DeviceAllocationConfiguration gets embedded in an AllocationResult.\n  1661\u2192type DeviceAllocationConfiguration struct {\n  1662\u2192\t// Source records whether the configuration comes from a class and thus\n  1663\u2192\t// is not something that a normal user would have been able to set\n  1664\u2192\t// or from a claim.\n  1665\u2192\t//\n  1666\u2192\t// +required\n  1667\u2192\t// +k8s:required\n  1668\u2192\tSource AllocationConfigSource `json:\"source\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=source\"`\n  1669\u2192\n  1670\u2192\t// Requests lists the names of requests where the configuration applies.\n  1671\u2192\t// If empty, its applies to all requests.\n  1672\u2192\t//\n  1673\u2192\t// References to subrequests must include the name of the main request\n  1674\u2192\t// and may include the subrequest using the format <main request>[/<subrequest>]. If just\n  1675\u2192\t// the main request is given, the configuration applies to all subrequests.\n  1676\u2192\t//\n  1677\u2192\t// +optional\n  1678\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1679\u2192\tRequests []string `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=requests\"`\n  1680\u2192\n  1681\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1682\u2192}\n  1683\u2192\n  1684\u2192// +enum\n  1685\u2192// +k8s:enum\n  1686\u2192type AllocationConfigSource string\n  1687\u2192\n  1688\u2192// Valid [DeviceAllocationConfiguration.Source] values.\n  1689\u2192const (\n  1690\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClass AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClass\"\n  1691\u2192\tAllocationConfigSourceClaim AllocationConfigSource = \"FromClaim\"\n  1692\u2192)\n  1693\u2192\n  1694\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1695\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1696\u2192\n  1697\u2192// ResourceClaimList is a collection of claims.\n  1698\u2192type ResourceClaimList struct {\n  1699\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1700\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1701\u2192\t// +optional\n  1702\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1703\u2192\n  1704\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claims.\n  1705\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaim `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1706\u2192}\n  1707\u2192\n  1708\u2192// +genclient\n  1709\u2192// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n  1710\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1711\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1712\u2192\n  1713\u2192// DeviceClass is a vendor- or admin-provided resource that contains\n  1714\u2192// device configuration and selectors. It can be referenced in\n  1715\u2192// the device requests of a claim to apply these presets.\n  1716\u2192// Cluster scoped.\n  1717\u2192//\n  1718\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1719\u2192// feature gate.\n  1720\u2192type DeviceClass struct {\n  1721\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1722\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1723\u2192\t// +optional\n  1724\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:optional\n  1725\u2192\t// +k8s:subfield(name)=+k8s:format=k8s-long-name\n  1726\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1727\u2192\n  1728\u2192\t// Spec defines what can be allocated and how to configure it.\n  1729\u2192\t//\n  1730\u2192\t// This is mutable. Consumers have to be prepared for classes changing\n  1731\u2192\t// at any time, either because they get updated or replaced. Claim\n  1732\u2192\t// allocations are done once based on whatever was set in classes at\n  1733\u2192\t// the time of allocation.\n  1734\u2192\t//\n  1735\u2192\t// Changing the spec automatically increments the metadata.generation number.\n  1736\u2192\tSpec DeviceClassSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1737\u2192}\n  1738\u2192\n  1739\u2192// DeviceClassSpec is used in a [DeviceClass] to define what can be allocated\n  1740\u2192// and how to configure it.\n  1741\u2192type DeviceClassSpec struct {\n  1742\u2192\t// Each selector must be satisfied by a device which is claimed via this class.\n  1743\u2192\t//\n  1744\u2192\t// +optional\n  1745\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1746\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1747\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1748\u2192\tSelectors []DeviceSelector `json:\"selectors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=selectors\"`\n  1749\u2192\n  1750\u2192\t// Config defines configuration parameters that apply to each device that is claimed via this class.\n  1751\u2192\t// Some classses may potentially be satisfied by multiple drivers, so each instance of a vendor\n  1752\u2192\t// configuration applies to exactly one driver.\n  1753\u2192\t//\n  1754\u2192\t// They are passed to the driver, but are not considered while allocating the claim.\n  1755\u2192\t//\n  1756\u2192\t// +optional\n  1757\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1758\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1759\u2192\t// +k8s:maxItems=32\n  1760\u2192\tConfig []DeviceClassConfiguration `json:\"config,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=config\"`\n  1761\u2192\n  1762\u2192\t// SuitableNodes is tombstoned since Kubernetes 1.32 where\n  1763\u2192\t// it got removed. May be reused once decoding v1alpha3 is no longer\n  1764\u2192\t// supported.\n  1765\u2192\t// SuitableNodes *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"suitableNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=suitableNodes\"`\n  1766\u2192\n  1767\u2192\t// ExtendedResourceName is the extended resource name for the devices of this class.\n  1768\u2192\t// The devices of this class can be used to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1769\u2192\t// It has the same format as the name of a pod's extended resource.\n  1770\u2192\t// It should be unique among all the device classes in a cluster.\n  1771\u2192\t// If two device classes have the same name, then the class created later\n  1772\u2192\t// is picked to satisfy a pod's extended resource requests.\n  1773\u2192\t// If two classes are created at the same time, then the name of the class\n  1774\u2192\t// lexicographically sorted first is picked.\n  1775\u2192\t//\n  1776\u2192\t// This is an alpha field.\n  1777\u2192\t// +optional\n  1778\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAExtendedResource\n  1779\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1780\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-extended-resource-name\n  1781\u2192\tExtendedResourceName *string `json:\"extendedResourceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=extendedResourceName\"`\n  1782\u2192}\n  1783\u2192\n  1784\u2192// DeviceClassConfiguration is used in DeviceClass.\n  1785\u2192type DeviceClassConfiguration struct {\n  1786\u2192\tDeviceConfiguration `json:\",inline\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=deviceConfiguration\"`\n  1787\u2192}\n  1788\u2192\n  1789\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1790\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1791\u2192\n  1792\u2192// DeviceClassList is a collection of classes.\n  1793\u2192type DeviceClassList struct {\n  1794\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1795\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1796\u2192\t// +optional\n  1797\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1798\u2192\n  1799\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource classes.\n  1800\u2192\tItems []DeviceClass `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1801\u2192}\n  1802\u2192\n  1803\u2192// +genclient\n  1804\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1805\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1806\u2192\n  1807\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects.\n  1808\u2192//\n  1809\u2192// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n  1810\u2192// feature gate.\n  1811\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplate struct {\n  1812\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1813\u2192\t// Standard object metadata\n  1814\u2192\t// +optional\n  1815\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1816\u2192\n  1817\u2192\t// Describes the ResourceClaim that is to be generated.\n  1818\u2192\t//\n  1819\u2192\t// This field is immutable. A ResourceClaim will get created by the\n  1820\u2192\t// control plane for a Pod when needed and then not get updated\n  1821\u2192\t// anymore.\n  1822\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimTemplateSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1823\u2192}\n  1824\u2192\n  1825\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplateSpec contains the metadata and fields for a ResourceClaim.\n  1826\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplateSpec struct {\n  1827\u2192\t// ObjectMeta may contain labels and annotations that will be copied into the ResourceClaim\n  1828\u2192\t// when creating it. No other fields are allowed and will be rejected during\n  1829\u2192\t// validation.\n  1830\u2192\t// +optional\n  1831\u2192\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1832\u2192\n  1833\u2192\t// Spec for the ResourceClaim. The entire content is copied unchanged\n  1834\u2192\t// into the ResourceClaim that gets created from this template. The\n  1835\u2192\t// same fields as in a ResourceClaim are also valid here.\n  1836\u2192\tSpec ResourceClaimSpec `json:\"spec\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=spec\"`\n  1837\u2192}\n  1838\u2192\n  1839\u2192// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n  1840\u2192// +k8s:prerelease-lifecycle-gen:introduced=1.33\n  1841\u2192\n  1842\u2192// ResourceClaimTemplateList is a collection of claim templates.\n  1843\u2192type ResourceClaimTemplateList struct {\n  1844\u2192\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n  1845\u2192\t// Standard list metadata\n  1846\u2192\t// +optional\n  1847\u2192\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\"`\n  1848\u2192\n  1849\u2192\t// Items is the list of resource claim templates.\n  1850\u2192\tItems []ResourceClaimTemplate `json:\"items\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\"`\n  1851\u2192}\n  1852\u2192\n  1853\u2192const (\n  1854\u2192\t// AllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions represents the maximum number of\n  1855\u2192\t// conditions in a device status.\n  1856\u2192\tAllocatedDeviceStatusMaxConditions int = 8\n  1857\u2192\t// AllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength represents the maximum length of the\n  1858\u2192\t// raw data in the Data field in a device status.\n  1859\u2192\tAllocatedDeviceStatusDataMaxLength int = 10 * 1024\n  1860\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs represents the maximum number of IPs in the networkData\n  1861\u2192\t// field in a device status.\n  1862\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataMaxIPs int = 16\n  1863\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength represents the maximum number of characters\n  1864\u2192\t// for the networkData.interfaceName field in a device status.\n  1865\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataInterfaceNameMaxLength int = 256\n  1866\u2192\t// NetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength represents the maximum number of characters\n  1867\u2192\t// for the networkData.hardwareAddress field in a device status.\n  1868\u2192\tNetworkDeviceDataHardwareAddressMaxLength int = 128\n  1869\u2192)\n  1870\u2192\n  1871\u2192// AllocatedDeviceStatus contains the status of an allocated device, if the\n  1872\u2192// driver chooses to report it. This may include driver-specific information.\n  1873\u2192//\n  1874\u2192// The combination of Driver, Pool, Device, and ShareID must match the corresponding key\n  1875\u2192// in Status.Allocation.Devices.\n  1876\u2192type AllocatedDeviceStatus struct {\n  1877\u2192\t// Driver specifies the name of the DRA driver whose kubelet\n  1878\u2192\t// plugin should be invoked to process the allocation once the claim is\n  1879\u2192\t// needed on a node.\n  1880\u2192\t//\n  1881\u2192\t// Must be a DNS subdomain and should end with a DNS domain owned by the\n  1882\u2192\t// vendor of the driver. It should use only lower case characters.\n  1883\u2192\t//\n  1884\u2192\t// +required\n  1885\u2192\tDriver string `json:\"driver\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=driver\"`\n  1886\u2192\n  1887\u2192\t// This name together with the driver name and the device name field\n  1888\u2192\t// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).\n  1889\u2192\t//\n  1890\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 253 characters and may contain one or more\n  1891\u2192\t// DNS sub-domains separated by slashes.\n  1892\u2192\t//\n  1893\u2192\t// +required\n  1894\u2192\tPool string `json:\"pool\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=pool\"`\n  1895\u2192\n  1896\u2192\t// Device references one device instance via its name in the driver's\n  1897\u2192\t// resource pool. It must be a DNS label.\n  1898\u2192\t//\n  1899\u2192\t// +required\n  1900\u2192\tDevice string `json:\"device\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=device\"`\n  1901\u2192\n  1902\u2192\t// ShareID uniquely identifies an individual allocation share of the device.\n  1903\u2192\t//\n  1904\u2192\t// +optional\n  1905\u2192\t// +featureGate=DRAConsumableCapacity\n  1906\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1907\u2192\t// +k8s:format=k8s-uuid\n  1908\u2192\tShareID *string `json:\"shareID,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=shareID\"`\n  1909\u2192\n  1910\u2192\t// Conditions contains the latest observation of the device's state.\n  1911\u2192\t// If the device has been configured according to the class and claim\n  1912\u2192\t// config references, the `Ready` condition should be True.\n  1913\u2192\t//\n  1914\u2192\t// Must not contain more than 8 entries.\n  1915\u2192\t//\n  1916\u2192\t// +optional\n  1917\u2192\t// +listType=map\n  1918\u2192\t// +listMapKey=type\n  1919\u2192\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=conditions\"`\n  1920\u2192\n  1921\u2192\t// Data contains arbitrary driver-specific data.\n  1922\u2192\t//\n  1923\u2192\t// The length of the raw data must be smaller or equal to 10 Ki.\n  1924\u2192\t//\n  1925\u2192\t// +optional\n  1926\u2192\tData *runtime.RawExtension `json:\"data,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=data\"`\n  1927\u2192\n  1928\u2192\t// NetworkData contains network-related information specific to the device.\n  1929\u2192\t//\n  1930\u2192\t// +optional\n  1931\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1932\u2192\tNetworkData *NetworkDeviceData `json:\"networkData,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=networkData\"`\n  1933\u2192}\n  1934\u2192\n  1935\u2192// NetworkDeviceData provides network-related details for the allocated device.\n  1936\u2192// This information may be filled by drivers or other components to configure\n  1937\u2192// or identify the device within a network context.\n  1938\u2192type NetworkDeviceData struct {\n  1939\u2192\t// InterfaceName specifies the name of the network interface associated with\n  1940\u2192\t// the allocated device. This might be the name of a physical or virtual\n  1941\u2192\t// network interface being configured in the pod.\n  1942\u2192\t//\n  1943\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 256 characters.\n  1944\u2192\t//\n  1945\u2192\t// +optional\n  1946\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1947\u2192\t// +k8s:maxLength=256\n  1948\u2192\tInterfaceName string `json:\"interfaceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=interfaceName\"`\n  1949\u2192\n  1950\u2192\t// IPs lists the network addresses assigned to the device's network interface.\n  1951\u2192\t// This can include both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.\n  1952\u2192\t// The IPs are in the CIDR notation, which includes both the address and the\n  1953\u2192\t// associated subnet mask.\n  1954\u2192\t// e.g.: \"192.0.2.5/24\" for IPv4 and \"2001:db8::5/64\" for IPv6.\n  1955\u2192\t//\n  1956\u2192\t// +optional\n  1957\u2192\t// +listType=atomic\n  1958\u2192\tIPs []string `json:\"ips,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=ips\"`\n  1959\u2192\n  1960\u2192\t// HardwareAddress represents the hardware address (e.g. MAC Address) of the device's network interface.\n  1961\u2192\t//\n  1962\u2192\t// Must not be longer than 128 characters.\n  1963\u2192\t//\n  1964\u2192\t// +optional\n  1965\u2192\t// +k8s:optional\n  1966\u2192\t// +k8s:maxLength=128\n  1967\u2192\tHardwareAddress string `json:\"hardwareAddress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=hardwareAddress\"`\n  1968\u2192}\n  1969\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:280:\t\t// NOTE: Declarative validation does not (yet) enforce the real requiredness of this field\npkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:483:// validateAllocationResult enforces constraints for *new* results, which in at\npkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:715:\t// Size limit for total number of counters in devices enforced here.\npkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:726:\t// Size limit for all shared counters enforced here.\npkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:753:\t\t// The size limit is enforced for across all sets by the caller.\npkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:878:\t\t// The size limit is enforced for the entire device.\npkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go:1126:\t// What we need to enforce here is that there really is a domain.\n\n[Showing results with pagination = limit: 30, offset: 0]"

{
  "context": 3,
  "head_limit": 80,
  "output_mode": "content",
  "path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1beta2/types.go",
  "pattern": "NodeSelector|nodeSelection|per.*device|node.*select"

Output
"69-// if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.\n70-//\n71-// For resources that are not local to a node, the node name is not set. Instead,\n72:// the driver may use a node selector to specify where the devices are available.\n73-//\n74-// This is an alpha type and requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation\n75-// feature gate.\n--\n121-\t// new nodes of the same type as some old node might also make new\n122-\t// resources available.\n123-\t//\n124:\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n125-\t// This field is immutable.\n126-\t//\n127-\t// +optional\n128-\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n129-\tNodeName *string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n130-\n131:\t// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,\n132-\t// when that pool is not limited to a single node.\n133-\t//\n134-\t// Must use exactly one term.\n135-\t//\n136:\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n137-\t//\n138-\t// +optional\n139-\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n140:\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n141-\n142-\t// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the resources in the pool.\n143-\t//\n144:\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n145-\t//\n146-\t// +optional\n147-\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n--\n161-\t// device. If it is set to true, every device defined the ResourceSlice\n162-\t// must specify this individually.\n163-\t//\n164:\t// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.\n165-\t//\n166-\t// +optional\n167-\t// +oneOf=NodeSelection\n--\n302-\t// NodeName identifies the node where the device is available.\n303-\t//\n304-\t// Must only be set if Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection is set to true.\n305:\t// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.\n306-\t//\n307-\t// +optional\n308-\t// +oneOf=DeviceNodeSelection\n309-\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n310-\tNodeName *string `json:\"nodeName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=nodeName\"`\n311-\n312:\t// NodeSelector defines the nodes where the device is available.\n313-\t//\n314-\t// Must use exactly one term.\n315-\t//\n316-\t// Must only be set if Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection is set to true.\n317:\t// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.\n318-\t//\n319-\t// +optional\n320-\t// +oneOf=DeviceNodeSelection\n321-\t// +featureGate=DRAPartitionableDevices\n322:\tNodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=nodeSelector\"`\n323-\n324-\t// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the device.\n325-\t//\n326-\t// Must only be set if Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection is set to true.\n327:\t// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.\n328-\t//\n329-\t// +optional\n330-\t// +oneOf=DeviceNodeSelection\n--\n345-\n346-\t// BindsToNode indicates if the usage of an allocation involving this device\n347-\t// has to be limited to exactly the node that was chosen when allocating the claim.\n348:\t// If set to true, the scheduler will set the ResourceClaim.Status.Allocation.NodeSelector\n349-\t// to match the node where the allocation was made.\n350-\t//\n\n[Showing results with pagination = limit: 80, offset: 0]"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2024 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package dynamicresources\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192\tresourceapi \"k8s.io/api/resource/v1\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    24\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n    29\u2192\tschedutil \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/util\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n    31\u2192)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// foreachAllocatedDevice invokes the provided callback for each\n    34\u2192// device in the claim's allocation result which was allocated\n    35\u2192// exclusively for the claim.\n    36\u2192//\n    37\u2192// Devices allocated with admin access can be shared with other\n    38\u2192// claims and are skipped without invoking the callback.\n    39\u2192//\n    40\u2192// foreachAllocatedDevice does nothing if the claim is not allocated.\n    41\u2192func foreachAllocatedDevice(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim,\n    42\u2192\tdedicatedDeviceCallback func(deviceID structured.DeviceID),\n    43\u2192\tenabledConsumableCapacity bool,\n    44\u2192\tsharedDeviceCallback func(structured.SharedDeviceID),\n    45\u2192\tconsumedCapacityCallback func(structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity)) {\n    46\u2192\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n    47\u2192\t\treturn\n    48\u2192\t}\n    49\u2192\tfor _, result := range claim.Status.Allocation.Devices.Results {\n    50\u2192\t\t// Kubernetes 1.31 did not set this, 1.32 always does.\n    51\u2192\t\t// Supporting 1.31 is not worth the additional code that\n    52\u2192\t\t// would have to be written (= looking up in request) because\n    53\u2192\t\t// it is extremely unlikely that there really is a result\n    54\u2192\t\t// that still exists in a cluster from 1.31 where this matters.\n    55\u2192\t\tif ptr.Deref(result.AdminAccess, false) {\n    56\u2192\t\t\t// Is not considered as allocated.\n    57\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    58\u2192\t\t}\n    59\u2192\t\tdeviceID := structured.MakeDeviceID(result.Driver, result.Pool, result.Device)\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t\t// None of the users of this helper need to abort iterating,\n    62\u2192\t\t// therefore it's not supported as it only would add overhead.\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t\t// Execute sharedDeviceCallback and consumedCapacityCallback correspondingly\n    65\u2192\t\t// if DRAConsumableCapacity feature is enabled\n    66\u2192\t\tif enabledConsumableCapacity {\n    67\u2192\t\t\tshared := result.ShareID != nil\n    68\u2192\t\t\tif shared {\n    69\u2192\t\t\t\tsharedDeviceID := structured.MakeSharedDeviceID(deviceID, result.ShareID)\n    70\u2192\t\t\t\tsharedDeviceCallback(sharedDeviceID)\n    71\u2192\t\t\t\tif result.ConsumedCapacity != nil {\n    72\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdeviceConsumedCapacity := structured.NewDeviceConsumedCapacity(deviceID, result.ConsumedCapacity)\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\t\tconsumedCapacityCallback(deviceConsumedCapacity)\n    74\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n    75\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    76\u2192\t\t\t}\n    77\u2192\t\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t\t// Otherwise, execute dedicatedDeviceCallback\n    80\u2192\t\tdedicatedDeviceCallback(deviceID)\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// allocatedDevices reacts to events in a cache and maintains a set of all allocated devices.\n    85\u2192// This is cheaper than repeatedly calling List, making strings unique, and building the set\n    86\u2192// each time PreFilter is called.\n    87\u2192//\n    88\u2192// All methods are thread-safe. Get returns a cloned set.\n    89\u2192type allocatedDevices struct {\n    90\u2192\tlogger klog.Logger\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\tmutex                     sync.RWMutex\n    93\u2192\tids                       sets.Set[structured.DeviceID]\n    94\u2192\tshareIDs                  sets.Set[structured.SharedDeviceID]\n    95\u2192\tcapacities                structured.ConsumedCapacityCollection\n    96\u2192\tenabledConsumableCapacity bool\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192func newAllocatedDevices(logger klog.Logger) *allocatedDevices {\n   100\u2192\treturn &allocatedDevices{\n   101\u2192\t\tlogger:                    logger,\n   102\u2192\t\tids:                       sets.New[structured.DeviceID](),\n   103\u2192\t\tshareIDs:                  sets.New[structured.SharedDeviceID](),\n   104\u2192\t\tcapacities:                structured.NewConsumedCapacityCollection(),\n   105\u2192\t\tenabledConsumableCapacity: utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.DRAConsumableCapacity),\n   106\u2192\t}\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) Get() sets.Set[structured.DeviceID] {\n   110\u2192\ta.mutex.RLock()\n   111\u2192\tdefer a.mutex.RUnlock()\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\treturn a.ids.Clone()\n   114\u2192}\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) Capacities() structured.ConsumedCapacityCollection {\n   117\u2192\ta.mutex.RLock()\n   118\u2192\tdefer a.mutex.RUnlock()\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192\treturn a.capacities.Clone()\n   121\u2192}\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) handlers() cache.ResourceEventHandler {\n   124\u2192\treturn cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n   125\u2192\t\tAddFunc:    a.onAdd,\n   126\u2192\t\tUpdateFunc: a.onUpdate,\n   127\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: a.onDelete,\n   128\u2192\t}\n   129\u2192}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) onAdd(obj any) {\n   132\u2192\tclaim, _, err := schedutil.As[*resourceapi.ResourceClaim](obj, nil)\n   133\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   134\u2192\t\t// Shouldn't happen.\n   135\u2192\t\ta.logger.Error(err, \"unexpected object in allocatedDevices.onAdd\")\n   136\u2192\t\treturn\n   137\u2192\t}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192\tif claim.Status.Allocation != nil {\n   140\u2192\t\ta.addDevices(claim)\n   141\u2192\t}\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) onUpdate(oldObj, newObj any) {\n   145\u2192\toriginalClaim, modifiedClaim, err := schedutil.As[*resourceapi.ResourceClaim](oldObj, newObj)\n   146\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   147\u2192\t\t// Shouldn't happen.\n   148\u2192\t\ta.logger.Error(err, \"unexpected object in allocatedDevices.onUpdate\")\n   149\u2192\t\treturn\n   150\u2192\t}\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192\tswitch {\n   153\u2192\tcase originalClaim.Status.Allocation == nil && modifiedClaim.Status.Allocation != nil:\n   154\u2192\t\ta.addDevices(modifiedClaim)\n   155\u2192\tcase originalClaim.Status.Allocation != nil && modifiedClaim.Status.Allocation == nil:\n   156\u2192\t\ta.removeDevices(originalClaim)\n   157\u2192\tdefault:\n   158\u2192\t\t// Nothing to do. Either both nil or both non-nil, in which case the content\n   159\u2192\t\t// also must be the same (immutable!).\n   160\u2192\t}\n   161\u2192}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) onDelete(obj any) {\n   164\u2192\tclaim, _, err := schedutil.As[*resourceapi.ResourceClaim](obj, nil)\n   165\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   166\u2192\t\t// Shouldn't happen.\n   167\u2192\t\ta.logger.Error(err, \"unexpected object in allocatedDevices.onDelete\")\n   168\u2192\t\treturn\n   169\u2192\t}\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\ta.removeDevices(claim)\n   172\u2192}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) addDevices(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) {\n   175\u2192\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n   176\u2192\t\treturn\n   177\u2192\t}\n   178\u2192\t// Locking of the mutex gets minimized by pre-computing what needs to be done\n   179\u2192\t// without holding the lock.\n   180\u2192\tdeviceIDs := make([]structured.DeviceID, 0, 20)\n   181\u2192\tvar shareIDs []structured.SharedDeviceID\n   182\u2192\tvar deviceCapacities []structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity\n   183\u2192\tif a.enabledConsumableCapacity {\n   184\u2192\t\tshareIDs = make([]structured.SharedDeviceID, 0, 20)\n   185\u2192\t\tdeviceCapacities = make([]structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity, 0, 20)\n   186\u2192\t}\n   187\u2192\tforeachAllocatedDevice(claim,\n   188\u2192\t\tfunc(deviceID structured.DeviceID) {\n   189\u2192\t\t\ta.logger.V(6).Info(\"Observed device allocation\", \"device\", deviceID, \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   190\u2192\t\t\tdeviceIDs = append(deviceIDs, deviceID)\n   191\u2192\t\t},\n   192\u2192\t\ta.enabledConsumableCapacity,\n   193\u2192\t\tfunc(sharedDeviceID structured.SharedDeviceID) {\n   194\u2192\t\t\ta.logger.V(6).Info(\"Observed shared device allocation\", \"shared device\", sharedDeviceID, \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   195\u2192\t\t\tshareIDs = append(shareIDs, sharedDeviceID)\n   196\u2192\t\t},\n   197\u2192\t\tfunc(capacity structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity) {\n   198\u2192\t\t\ta.logger.V(6).Info(\"Observed consumed capacity\", \"device\", capacity.DeviceID, \"consumed capacity\", capacity.ConsumedCapacity, \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   199\u2192\t\t\tdeviceCapacities = append(deviceCapacities, capacity)\n   200\u2192\t\t},\n   201\u2192\t)\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192\ta.mutex.Lock()\n   204\u2192\tdefer a.mutex.Unlock()\n   205\u2192\tfor _, deviceID := range deviceIDs {\n   206\u2192\t\ta.ids.Insert(deviceID)\n   207\u2192\t}\n   208\u2192\tfor _, shareID := range shareIDs {\n   209\u2192\t\ta.shareIDs.Insert(shareID)\n   210\u2192\t}\n   211\u2192\tfor _, capacity := range deviceCapacities {\n   212\u2192\t\ta.capacities.Insert(capacity)\n   213\u2192\t}\n   214\u2192}\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192func (a *allocatedDevices) removeDevices(claim *resourceapi.ResourceClaim) {\n   217\u2192\tif claim.Status.Allocation == nil {\n   218\u2192\t\treturn\n   219\u2192\t}\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192\t// Locking of the mutex gets minimized by pre-computing what needs to be done\n   222\u2192\t// without holding the lock.\n   223\u2192\tdeviceIDs := make([]structured.DeviceID, 0, 20)\n   224\u2192\tvar shareIDs []structured.SharedDeviceID\n   225\u2192\tvar deviceCapacities []structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity\n   226\u2192\tif a.enabledConsumableCapacity {\n   227\u2192\t\tshareIDs = make([]structured.SharedDeviceID, 0, 20)\n   228\u2192\t\tdeviceCapacities = make([]structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity, 0, 20)\n   229\u2192\t}\n   230\u2192\tforeachAllocatedDevice(claim,\n   231\u2192\t\tfunc(deviceID structured.DeviceID) {\n   232\u2192\t\t\ta.logger.V(6).Info(\"Observed device deallocation\", \"device\", deviceID, \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   233\u2192\t\t\tdeviceIDs = append(deviceIDs, deviceID)\n   234\u2192\t\t},\n   235\u2192\t\ta.enabledConsumableCapacity,\n   236\u2192\t\tfunc(sharedDeviceID structured.SharedDeviceID) {\n   237\u2192\t\t\ta.logger.V(6).Info(\"Observed shared device deallocation\", \"shared device\", sharedDeviceID, \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   238\u2192\t\t\tshareIDs = append(shareIDs, sharedDeviceID)\n   239\u2192\t\t},\n   240\u2192\t\tfunc(capacity structured.DeviceConsumedCapacity) {\n   241\u2192\t\t\ta.logger.V(6).Info(\"Observed consumed capacity release\", \"device id\", capacity.DeviceID, \"consumed capacity\", capacity.ConsumedCapacity, \"claim\", klog.KObj(claim))\n   242\u2192\t\t\tdeviceCapacities = append(deviceCapacities, capacity)\n   243\u2192\t\t})\n   244\u2192\ta.mutex.Lock()\n   245\u2192\tdefer a.mutex.Unlock()\n   246\u2192\tfor _, deviceID := range deviceIDs {\n   247\u2192\t\ta.ids.Delete(deviceID)\n   248\u2192\t}\n   249\u2192\tfor _, shareID := range shareIDs {\n   250\u2192\t\ta.shareIDs.Delete(shareID)\n   251\u2192\t}\n   252\u2192\tfor _, capacity := range deviceCapacities {\n   253\u2192\t\ta.capacities.Remove(capacity)\n   254\u2192\t}\n   255\u2192}\n   256\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/allocation/state\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\"\n    45\u2192\tkubecontainer \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/container\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/status\"\n    49\u2192\tkubetypes \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/types\"\n    50\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/util/format\"\n    51\u2192)\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192// podStatusManagerStateFile is the file name where status manager stores its state\n    54\u2192const (\n    55\u2192\tallocatedPodsStateFile = \"allocated_pods_state\"\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192\tinitialRetryDelay = 30 * time.Second\n    58\u2192\tretryDelay        = 3 * time.Minute\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\tTriggerReasonPodResized  = \"pod_resized\"\n    61\u2192\tTriggerReasonPodUpdated  = \"pod_updated\"\n    62\u2192\tTriggerReasonPodsAdded   = \"pods_added\"\n    63\u2192\tTriggerReasonPodsRemoved = \"pods_removed\"\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192\ttriggerReasonPeriodic = \"periodic_retry\"\n    66\u2192)\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// AllocationManager tracks pod resource allocations.\n    69\u2192type Manager interface {\n    70\u2192\t// GetContainerResourceAllocation returns the AllocatedResources value for the container\n    71\u2192\tGetContainerResourceAllocation(podUID types.UID, containerName string) (v1.ResourceRequirements, bool)\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// UpdatePodFromAllocation overwrites the pod spec with the allocation.\n    74\u2192\t// This function does a deep copy only if updates are needed.\n    75\u2192\t// Returns the updated (or original) pod, and whether there was an allocation stored.\n    76\u2192\tUpdatePodFromAllocation(pod *v1.Pod) (*v1.Pod, bool)\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\t// SetAllocatedResources checkpoints the resources allocated to a pod's containers.\n    79\u2192\tSetAllocatedResources(allocatedPod *v1.Pod) error\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// AddPodAdmitHandlers adds the admit handlers to the allocation manager.\n    82\u2192\t// TODO: See if we can remove this and just add them in the allocation manager constructor.\n    83\u2192\tAddPodAdmitHandlers(handlers lifecycle.PodAdmitHandlers)\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\t// SetContainerRuntime sets the allocation manager's container runtime.\n    86\u2192\t// TODO: See if we can remove this and just add it in the allocation manager constructor.\n    87\u2192\tSetContainerRuntime(runtime kubecontainer.Runtime)\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t// AddPod checks if a pod can be admitted. If so, it admits the pod and updates the allocation.\n    90\u2192\t// The function returns a boolean value indicating whether the pod\n    91\u2192\t// can be admitted, a brief single-word reason and a message explaining why\n    92\u2192\t// the pod cannot be admitted.\n    93\u2192\t// allocatedPods should represent the pods that have already been admitted, along with their\n    94\u2192\t// admitted (allocated) resources.\n    95\u2192\tAddPod(activePods []*v1.Pod, pod *v1.Pod) (ok bool, reason, message string)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\t// RemovePod removes any stored state for the given pod UID.\n    98\u2192\tRemovePod(uid types.UID)\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192\t// RemoveOrphanedPods removes the stored state for any pods not included in the set of remaining pods.\n   101\u2192\tRemoveOrphanedPods(remainingPods sets.Set[types.UID])\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\t// Run starts the allocation manager. This is currently only used to handle periodic retry of\n   104\u2192\t// pending resizes.\n   105\u2192\tRun(ctx context.Context)\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\t// PushPendingResize queues a pod with a pending resize request for later reevaluation.\n   108\u2192\tPushPendingResize(uid types.UID)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\t// HasPendingResizes returns whether there are currently any pending resizes.\n   111\u2192\tHasPendingResizes() bool\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// RetryPendingResizes retries all pending resizes.\n   114\u2192\tRetryPendingResizes(trigger string)\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192type manager struct {\n   118\u2192\tallocated state.State\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192\tadmitHandlers           lifecycle.PodAdmitHandlers\n   121\u2192\tcontainerRuntime        kubecontainer.Runtime\n   122\u2192\tstatusManager           status.Manager\n   123\u2192\tsourcesReady            config.SourcesReady\n   124\u2192\tnodeConfig              cm.NodeConfig\n   125\u2192\tnodeAllocatableAbsolute v1.ResourceList\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\tticker         *time.Ticker\n   128\u2192\ttriggerPodSync func(pod *v1.Pod)\n   129\u2192\tgetActivePods  func() []*v1.Pod\n   130\u2192\tgetPodByUID    func(types.UID) (*v1.Pod, bool)\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192\tallocationMutex        sync.Mutex\n   133\u2192\tpodsWithPendingResizes []types.UID\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\trecorder record.EventRecorder\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192func NewManager(checkpointDirectory string,\n   139\u2192\tnodeConfig cm.NodeConfig,\n   140\u2192\tnodeAllocatableAbsolute v1.ResourceList,\n   141\u2192\tstatusManager status.Manager,\n   142\u2192\ttriggerPodSync func(pod *v1.Pod),\n   143\u2192\tgetActivePods func() []*v1.Pod,\n   144\u2192\tgetPodByUID func(types.UID) (*v1.Pod, bool),\n   145\u2192\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady,\n   146\u2192\trecorder record.EventRecorder,\n   147\u2192) Manager {\n   148\u2192\treturn &manager{\n   149\u2192\t\tallocated: newStateImpl(checkpointDirectory, allocatedPodsStateFile),\n   150\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
54. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git log --all --oneline --grep=\"AllocationMode\\|allocation.*all\\|multi.*node\" | head -20"
}
Output
"82b2a9d543f DRA scheduler: fix another root cause of double device allocation\nf296d4a9a46 DRA scheduler: fix one root cause of double device allocation\ne4b5d3c8b4f DRA scheduler: fix another root cause of double device allocation\naf0e9bb0333 DRA scheduler: fix one root cause of double device allocation\nba81d3040dd DRA scheduler: fix another root cause of double device allocation\n75fd186e7a9 DRA scheduler: fix one root cause of double device allocation\neb7391688ef DRA scheduler: fix another root cause of double device allocation\n6179b6db576 DRA scheduler: fix one root cause of double device allocation\n581ee0a2eca DRA scheduler: fix another root cause of double device allocation\n703642baf65 DRA scheduler: fix one root cause of double device allocation\na0e500ace39 Revert \"DRA: Allow AllocationMode: All from multi-node resource pools\"\n83504f1b02a Merge pull request #134466 from mortent/AllowAllocationModeAllMultinodeResourcePools\n2e534d6da10 DRA: Allow AllocationMode: All from multi-node resource pools\n4bb072512e7 Merge pull request #134446 from aaron-prindle/dv-device-allocation-mode-enum-migration\n9a7dace7347 feat: add +k8s:enum declarative validation to ResourceClaim DeviceAllocationMode\ne5c04392c0a Merge pull request #134276 from aaron-prindle/allocation-mode-enum\n5c4f81743c2 DRA: use v1 API\nf6061605fb5 DRA E2E: run multi-node control plane tests also with two nodes\nffe90820710 DRA allocator: select tests based on supported features\n7b906f204a6 DRA allocator: fork the code"

"commit a0e500ace39ec06de58279a3ca877899d5512709\nAuthor: Patrick Ohly <patrick.ohly@intel.com>\nDate:   Mon Nov 3 13:10:43 2025 +0100\n\n    Revert \"DRA: Allow AllocationMode: All from multi-node resource pools\"\n    \n    This reverts commit 2e534d6da100ef20a048ed44f9ec9997f64676a7.\n    It caused a performance regression, a different fix is needed.\n\n .../internal/allocatortesting/allocator_testing.go | 34 ----------------\n .../internal/experimental/pools_experimental.go    | 45 +---------------------\n .../internal/incubating/pools_incubating.go        | 45 +---------------------\n .../structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go     | 45 +---------------------\n 4 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 163 deletions(-)"

  "command": "git log --all --oneline --all | grep -i \"allow\\|restrict\\|multinode\\|multi-node\" | head -20"
}
Output
"ea3bfd9ea0f feat: Allow wildcard '*' for API groups in audit policy rules\n986020d6bd4 sync check in batch stategy to match AllowMutableSchedulingDirectives and AllowMutablePodResources\n265394c0a5f Add utilities to allow strategy.go files to enable DV naitve validations\n8c33e90e508 Merge pull request #135720 from soltysh/allowed_licenses_link\nbb9bb4d4224 Move away from deprecated term.AllowsColorOutput (#135454)\n3e34de29c49 fixed the loophole that allows user to get around resource quota set by system admin\nbfc44a42d55 Allow to change scheduler_perf threshold data bucket\n365beb2398c Update allowed-thir-party-license-policy.md link\nac90e6a809a Fix: Check defaultBackend in allowRelaxedServiceNameValidation\na66c025dc9e test/e2e_node: Update procMount test to use Restricted PSA level\ncee5b837da9 Resolve lint restriction on BeTrue by introducing Succeed() with contextual error messages\ne986000e6a7 KEP-5607: Allow hostNetwork pods to use user namespaces\n3882f0cf177 allow PV.Spec.NodeAffinity update\n5a7c90fb767 Allow mutable scheduling directives for suspended Jobs\ne5665154f79 kubeadm: add --allow-deprecated-api to 'config validate'\nfd812c5b058 fix: allow job startTime updates on resume from suspended state\ne47053f2fbc fix: allow job startTime updates on resume from suspended state\n7b40e374949 fix: allow job startTime updates on resume from suspended state\n0639f7f40e0 Allow PSA controller tests to handle failure cases as errors\na0e500ace39 Revert \"DRA: Allow AllocationMode: All from multi-node resource pools\""

a/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/experimental/pools_experimental.go\n+++ node\n \treturn false, nil\n }\n \n+type poolIdentifier struct {\n+\tdriver, pool string\n+}\n+\n // GatherPools collects information about all resource pools which provide\n // devices that are accessible from the given node.\n //\n@@ -52,9 +56,46 @@ func nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, node\n // required slices available) or invalid (for example, device names not unique).\n // Both is recorded in the result.\n func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node *v1.Node, features Features) ([]*Pool, error) {\n+\tslicesByPool := make(map[poolIdentifier][]*resourceapi.ResourceSlice)\n+\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\tpoolID := poolIdentifier{\n+\t\t\tdriver: slice.Spec.Driver,\n+\t\t\tpool:   slice.Spec.Pool.Name,\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tslicesByPool[poolID] = append(slicesByPool[poolID], slice)\n+\t}\n+\n+\t// We need to check whether a pool is complete while we have all\n+\t// the slices. Once we discard slices that don't target the node, we\n+\t// no longer have the information needed to find out.\n+\tincompletePools := sets.New[poolIdentifier]()\n+\tfor poolID, slices := range slicesByPool {\n+\t\tcomplete := true\n+\t\tsliceCount := len(slices)\n+\t\tgeneration := slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation\n+\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\t\t// If the number of slices in the pool specified in any of the slices\n+\t\t\t// doesn't match what we found, the pool is most likely being updated\n+\t\t\t// by the controller.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount != int64(sliceCount) {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// If the generation of the pool isn't the same across all slices,\n+\t\t\t// the pool is most likely being updated by the controller. We can't\n+\t\t\t// allocate devices from it.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation != generation {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\t// We still need to keep incomplete pools, since we need to make sure\n+\t\t// all devices available on a node is considered for allocationMode All.\n+\t\tif !complete {\n+\t\t\tincompletePools.Insert(poolID)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\n \tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n \tvar slicesWithBindingConditions []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice\n-\n \tfor _, slice := range slices {\n \t\tif !features.PartitionableDevices && slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n \t\t\tcontinue\n@@ -121,7 +162,7 @@ func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node\n \tresult := make([]*Pool, 0, len(pools))\n \tvar resultWithBindingConditions []*Pool\n \tfor _, pool := range pools {\n-\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = int64(len(pool.Slices)) != pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount\n+\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = incompletePools.Has(poolIdentifier{driver: pool.Driver.String(), pool: pool.Pool.String()})\n \t\tpool.IsInvalid, pool.InvalidReason = poolIsInvalid(pool)\n \t\t// if pool has binding conditions, add the pool to the end of the result\n \t\tif poolHasBindingConditions(*pool) {\ndiff --git have all\n+\t// the slices. Once we discard slices that don't target the node, we\n+\t// no longer have the information needed to find out.\n+\tincompletePools := sets.New[poolIdentifier]()\n+\tfor poolID, slices := range slicesByPool {\n+\t\tcomplete := true\n+\t\tsliceCount := len(slices)\n+\t\tgeneration := slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation\n+\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n+\t\t\t// If the number of slices in the pool specified in any of the slices\n+\t\t\t// doesn't match what we found, the pool is most likely being updated\n+\t\t\t// by the controller.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount != int64(sliceCount) {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// If the generation of the pool isn't the same across all slices,\n+\t\t\t// the pool is most likely being updated by the controller. We can't\n+\t\t\t// allocate devices from it.\n+\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation != generation {\n+\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\t// We still need to keep incomplete pools, since we need to make sure\n+\t\t// all devices available on a node is considered for allocationMode All.\n+\t\tif !complete {\n+\t\t\tincompletePools.Insert(poolID)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\n+\tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n \tfor _, slice := range slices {\n \t\tif !features.PartitionableDevices && slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n \t\t\tcontinue\n@@ -99,7 +140,7 @@ func GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node\n \t// Find incomplete pools and flatten into a single slice.\n \tresult := make([]*Pool, 0, len(pools))\n \tfor _, pool := range pools {\n-\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = int64(len(pool.Slices)) != pool.Slices[0].Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount\n+\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = incompletePools.Has(poolIdentifier{driver: pool.Driver.String(), pool: pool.Pool.String()})\n \t\tpool.IsInvalid, pool.InvalidReason = poolIsInvalid(pool)\n \t\tresult = append(result, pool)\n \t}"

Input
{
  "command": "grep -r \"AllocationMode.*All\\|allocation.*mode.*all\" /workspace/test/e2e/dra --include=\"*.go\" | head -20"
}
Output
"/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourcev1beta1.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourcev1beta1.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourcev1beta2.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,\n/workspace/test/e2e/dra/dra.go:\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAllocationMode:  resourcev1beta2.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount,"
61. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go",
  "limit": 150
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\nCopyright 2024 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage stable\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tresourceapi \"k8s.io/api/resource/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-helpers/scheduling/corev1/nodeaffinity\"\n\tdraapi \"k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/api\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n)\n\nfunc nodeMatches(node *v1.Node, nodeNameToMatch string, allNodesMatch bool, nodeSelector *v1.NodeSelector) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase nodeNameToMatch != \"\":\n\t\treturn node != nil && node.Name == nodeNameToMatch, nil\n\tcase allNodesMatch:\n\t\treturn true, nil\n\tcase nodeSelector != nil:\n\t\tselector, err := nodeaffinity.NewNodeSelector(nodeSelector)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse node selector %s: %w\", nodeSelector.String(), err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn selector.Match(node), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\ntype poolIdentifier struct {\n\tdriver, pool string\n}\n\n// GatherPools collects information about all resource pools which provide\n// devices that are accessible from the given node.\n//\n// Out-dated slices are silently ignored. Pools may be incomplete (not all\n// required slices available) or invalid (for example, device names not unique).\n// Both is recorded in the result.\nfunc GatherPools(ctx context.Context, slices []*resourceapi.ResourceSlice, node *v1.Node, features Features) ([]*Pool, error) {\n\tslicesByPool := make(map[poolIdentifier][]*resourceapi.ResourceSlice)\n\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n\t\tpoolID := poolIdentifier{\n\t\t\tdriver: slice.Spec.Driver,\n\t\t\tpool:   slice.Spec.Pool.Name,\n\t\t}\n\t\tslicesByPool[poolID] = append(slicesByPool[poolID], slice)\n\t}\n\n\t// We need to check whether a pool is complete while we have all\n\t// the slices. Once we discard slices that don't target the node, we\n\t// no longer have the information needed to find out.\n\tincompletePools := sets.New[poolIdentifier]()\n\tfor poolID, slices := range slicesByPool {\n\t\tcomplete := true\n\t\tsliceCount := len(slices)\n\t\tgeneration := slices[0].Spec.Pool.Generation\n\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n\t\t\t// If the number of slices in the pool specified in any of the slices\n\t\t\t// doesn't match what we found, the pool is most likely being updated\n\t\t\t// by the controller.\n\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.ResourceSliceCount != int64(sliceCount) {\n\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If the generation of the pool isn't the same across all slices,\n\t\t\t// the pool is most likely being updated by the controller. We can't\n\t\t\t// allocate devices from it.\n\t\t\tif slice.Spec.Pool.Generation != generation {\n\t\t\t\tcomplete = false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// We still need to keep incomplete pools, since we need to make sure\n\t\t// all devices available on a node is considered for allocationMode All.\n\t\tif !complete {\n\t\t\tincompletePools.Insert(poolID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tpools := make(map[PoolID]*Pool)\n\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n\t\tif !features.PartitionableDevices && slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif nodeName, allNodes := ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.NodeName, \"\"), ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.AllNodes, false); nodeName != \"\" || allNodes || slice.Spec.NodeSelector != nil {\n\t\t\tmatch, err := nodeMatches(node, nodeName, allNodes, slice.Spec.NodeSelector)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to perform node selection for slice %s: %w\", slice.Name, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif match {\n\t\t\t\tif err := addSlice(pools, slice); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to add node slice %s: %w\", slice.Name, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if ptr.Deref(slice.Spec.PerDeviceNodeSelection, false) {\n\t\t\tfor _, device := range slice.Spec.Devices {\n\t\t\t\tmatch, err := nodeMatches(node, ptr.Deref(device.NodeName, \"\"), ptr.Deref(device.AllNodes, false), device.NodeSelector)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to perform node selection for device %s in slice %s: %w\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdevice.String(), slice.Name, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif match {\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := addSlice(pools, slice); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to add node slice %s: %w\", slice.Name, err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Nothing known was set. This must be some future, unknown extension,\n\t\t\t// so we don't know how to handle it. We may still be able to allocated from\n\t\t\t// other pools, so we continue.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// TODO (eventually): let caller decide how to report this to the user. Warning\n\t\t\t// about it for every slice on each scheduling attempt would be too noisy, but\n\t\t\t// perhaps once per run would be useful?\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t}\n\n\t// Find incomplete pools and flatten into a single slice.\n\tresult := make([]*Pool, 0, len(pools))\n\tfor _, pool := range pools {\n\t\tpool.IsIncomplete = incompletePools.Has(poolIdentifier{driver: pool.Driver.String(), pool: pool.Pool.String()})\n\t\tpool.IsInvalid, pool.InvalidReason = poolIsInvalid(pool)\n\t\tresult = append(result, pool)\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/pools_stable.go",
    "numLines": 150,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 212
  },
  "type": "text"
}
